socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, the Seven Great Houses and other regional nobility, priestly elites, urban inhabitants, merchants, peasants, pastoralists, and slaves; strong regional variation existed between the Iranian plateau, Mesopotamia, and frontier oasis zones.

rulingClass.zh: 阿尔萨息王族与帕提亚-伊朗贵族，尤其是拥有骑兵与封地资源的大族。
rulingClass.en: The Arsacid royal family and the Parthian-Iranian nobility, especially the great houses controlling cavalry and landed resources.

succession.zh: 王位通常在阿尔萨息王族内部继承，但并非严格长子继承；贵族、王族支系与军事实力常共同决定继位结果。
succession.en: The throne normally passed within the Arsacid family but not by strict primogeniture; succession was often shaped by nobles, collateral dynasts, and military strength.

structure.zh: 中央权力围绕万王之王的宫廷展开，下辖宫廷官员、财政征收机构、王室直辖省份总督、附庸国君主及地方贵族。帝国整体更像多中心贵族联盟，而非高度官僚化国家。
structure.en: Central authority revolved around the court of the King of Kings, with palace officers, fiscal collection organs, governors in royal provinces, vassal rulers, and local nobles beneath him. The empire functioned more as a multi-centered aristocratic confederation than as a highly bureaucratic state.

function.zh: 负责诏令起草、外交文书、王室档案、朝觐礼仪与宫廷日常行政协调。
function.en: Handled royal edicts, diplomatic correspondence, court archives, ceremonial audiences, and coordination of palace administration.

function.zh: 管理王室收入、土地税、商税、关税、附庸贡物及贵金属储备。
function.en: Managed royal revenue, land taxation, commercial dues, customs, vassal tribute, and precious-metal reserves.

name.zh: 地方总督与属国事务署

function.zh: 联络王室直辖地总督、监督附庸王与地方贵族的义务、协调边疆稳定。
function.en: Liaised with governors of royal lands, monitored obligations of vassal kings and local nobles, and coordinated frontier stability.

name.en: War Office and Cavalry Mobilization Bureau

function.zh: 组织王室近卫、贵族骑兵征召、边境守备、补给与战时集结。
function.en: Organized the royal guard, noble cavalry levies, frontier garrisons, supply, and wartime mobilization.

function.zh: 监督丝绸之路中转、两河与波斯湾商道、驿站、渡口与市场税收。
function.en: Oversaw Silk Road transit, Mesopotamian and Persian Gulf trade arteries, relay stations, river crossings, and market taxation.

function.zh: 协调王室祭祀、火祀场所、地方神庙关系及君权合法性仪式。
function.en: Coordinated royal rites, fire cult establishments, relations with local temples, and ceremonies legitimizing kingship.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖总督、属国国王、城邦议会、神庙机构与地方豪族共同运作。两河地区的希腊化城市常保有一定自治，伊朗内地则更多依托贵族领主与部族首领。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on governors, vassal kings, city councils, temple institutions, and local magnates. Hellenized cities in Mesopotamia often retained some autonomy, while the Iranian interior depended more heavily on noble landlords and tribal chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并不统一，由王室敕令、地方习惯法、贵族裁断、城邦法规及宗教传统共同构成。希腊化城市、巴比伦传统社群与伊朗贵族领地各有不同司法实践。
legalSystem.en: The legal order was not fully uniform, consisting of royal decree, local customary law, aristocratic judgment, city statutes, and religious tradition. Hellenized cities, Babylonian communities, and Iranian noble domains each followed distinct judicial practices.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自土地税、灌溉农业产出、关税、市场税、商队过境费、附庸贡赋及战时征发。中央对全部地区的直接征税能力有限，常需依赖地方贵族与属国代征。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came mainly from land tax, irrigated agricultural output, customs, market dues, caravan transit fees, vassal tribute, and wartime requisitions. The center had limited capacity to tax all regions directly and often relied on local nobles and dependent rulers for collection.

religion.zh: 帝国宗教多元，以伊朗宗教传统为核心，包含早期祆教元素、王室火祀、米特拉崇拜，以及对希腊、巴比伦和地方神祇的并存容纳。宗教政策总体宽容。
religion.en: Religion in the empire was pluralistic, centered on Iranian traditions including early Zoroastrian elements, royal fire cults, and Mithraic worship, while also accommodating Greek, Babylonian, and local deities. Religious policy was generally tolerant.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化缺乏像汉帝国儒学或罗马法学那样统一的官方哲学，更重王权神授、贵族荣誉、家族传统与多地区知识传统的并行。希腊化学术、伊朗史诗记忆与巴比伦天文学同时存在。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life lacked a single official philosophy like Confucianism in Han China or Roman jurisprudence, emphasizing sacral kingship, aristocratic honor, family tradition, and parallel regional knowledge systems. Hellenistic scholarship, Iranian epic memory, and Babylonian astronomy coexisted.

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系多样，官方铸币与铭文常用帕提亚语和希腊语文字形式；行政与商业环境中亦使用阿拉米文书传统，地方上还见巴比伦楔形文字残余与其他区域文字。
writingSystem.en: Writing practices were diverse: official coinage and inscriptions often used Parthian and Greek forms, while Aramaic documentary traditions were common in administration and commerce; remnants of Babylonian cuneiform and other local scripts also survived regionally.

culturalAchievements.zh: 安息文化成就在于连接东西方的中介地位、骑射贵族文化、融合希腊化与伊朗风格的艺术、拱券与伊万式建筑传统的成熟，以及对丝路交流、金属器、织物与宫廷礼仪的推动。
culturalAchievements.en: Parthian cultural achievement lay in its role as intermediary between East and West, its mounted-archer aristocratic culture, art blending Hellenistic and Iranian styles, the maturation of vaulted and iwan-type architecture, and its stimulation of Silk Road exchange, metalwork, textiles, and court ceremonial.

languageFamily.zh: 统治精英主要使用西北伊朗语支的帕提亚语；帝国内还广泛存在阿拉米语、希腊语、中期波斯方言、巴比伦阿卡德语残存及多种地方语言。
languageFamily.en: The ruling elite primarily used Parthian, a Northwestern Iranian language; the empire also widely contained Aramaic, Greek, Middle Persian dialects, residual Babylonian Akkadian traditions, and many local languages.

gdpDescription.zh: 安息帝国经济建立在两河灌溉农业、伊朗高原农牧业、商路转运和区域手工业基础上。其总量在古代世界位居前列，但中央直接控制力不如汉帝国与罗马帝国，故财富分布较分散，且大量资源掌握于贵族与地方势力手中。
gdpDescription.en: The Parthian economy rested on Mesopotamian irrigated agriculture, the mixed agro-pastoral economy of the Iranian plateau, transit trade, and regional crafts. Its overall scale ranked among the largest in the ancient world, though central control was weaker than in Han China or the Roman Empire, so wealth was more dispersed and much of it remained in the hands of nobles and local powers.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、小麦与大麦种植、椰枣园经营、葡萄与畜牧、马匹繁育、丝路转口贸易、纺织、金属器制造、陶器、皮革与驿传服务。
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, wheat and barley cultivation, date-grove production, viticulture and pastoralism, horse breeding, Silk Road entrepôt trade, textiles, metalworking, ceramics, leather production, and relay transport services.

tradeGoods.zh: 丝绸、香料、宝石、马匹、珍珠、地毯、织物、金银器、玻璃器、葡萄酒、椰枣、皮革、奴隶及来自印度洋与中亚的奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Silk, spices, gemstones, horses, pearls, carpets, textiles, gold and silverware, glassware, wine, dates, leather, slaves, and luxury goods from the Indian Ocean and Central Asia.

name.zh: 德拉克马与四德拉克马

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。两河富庶灌区、商路城市与贵族庄园积累了显著财富，而边疆牧民、佃农与小农家庭的物质条件则较为脆弱，易受战事、税负与灌溉失修影响。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously. Prosperous irrigated zones of Mesopotamia, trade cities, and aristocratic estates accumulated substantial assets, whereas frontier pastoralists, tenants, and small farmers lived more precariously and were vulnerable to war, taxation, and failures in irrigation maintenance.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度属于农业地租、贵族封地与商业税并存的混合体。王室控制部分核心税源与贸易节点，但大量生产与征收权掌握于地方贵族、城市与附庸政权。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a hybrid of agrarian rents, aristocratic landed power, and commercial taxation. The crown controlled some core fiscal bases and trade nodes, but much production and tax extraction remained in the hands of local nobles, cities, and vassal polities.

category.zh: 军队、边防与贵族军役补偿
category.en: Army, Frontier Defense, and Noble Military Compensation
Court, Royal Household, and Diplomatic Gifts

category.zh: 道路、驿站、渡口与灌溉维护
category.en: Roads, Relay Stations, River Crossings, and Irrigation Maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备以银币、贵金属器物、贡物与可动用的王室仓储为主。安息财政并非完全货币化，王室可同时依赖实物征收与贵族军事义务，因此账面收入不等同于全部可支配资源。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves consisted primarily of silver coin, precious-metal objects, tribute goods, and mobilizable royal stores. Parthian finance was not fully monetized; the crown also relied on in-kind exactions and noble military obligations, so formal revenue did not equal the full pool of disposable resources.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持宫廷威望、边疆守备与商道安全。在稳定时期避免过度集中征税，以免激怒大贵族与属国；在战争时期则更多依靠临时征发、贡赋追加与贵族军役。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, prioritizing court prestige, frontier defense, and trade-route security. In stable times it avoided excessive central extraction so as not to alienate great nobles and vassals; in wartime it relied more on extraordinary levies, increased tribute, and aristocratic military service.

description.zh: 由王室直属骑兵与宫廷护卫组成，机动性强，是王权最可靠的核心武力。
description.en: Composed of crown-controlled cavalry and palace guards, highly mobile and the most reliable core force of royal power.

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从构成，装备长枪与重甲，是安息军队的突击主力。
description.en: Raised by nobles and their retainers, armed with lances and heavy armor, they formed the shock arm of the Parthian military.

description.zh: 以轻装骑兵和复合弓作战，擅长骚扰、迂回、远程消耗与佯退战术，是安息军事声誉的核心。
description.en: Light cavalry armed with composite bows, skilled in harassment, envelopment, ranged attrition, and feigned retreat tactics; they were central to Parthian military reputation.

description.zh: 主要用于守城、驻防、护送与工程事务，战场机动作用次于骑兵。
description.en: Used mainly for fortress defense, garrison duty, escort, and engineering tasks; less decisive in open mobile warfare than the cavalry.

description.zh: 来自附庸国、边疆部族和地方盟友，兵种构成多样，用于补充边境作战与地方镇压。
description.en: Drawn from vassal states, frontier tribes, and local allies, diverse in composition and useful for frontier campaigns and regional suppression.

commanderInChief.zh: 万王之王帕科鲁斯二世

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓、重甲骑兵装备、优良马种、鳞甲与锁甲、长枪、机动后勤和草原—高原骑战传统见长；攻城技术存在但通常不及罗马军团体系系统化。
technology.en: Military technology excelled in composite bows, cataphract equipment, quality horse breeding, scale and mail armor, lances, mobile logistics, and steppe-plateau cavalry warfare traditions; siegecraft existed but was generally less systematized than that of the Roman legions.

threats.zh: 主要外部威胁来自罗马帝国在亚美尼亚与美索不达米亚方向的压力，以及北方与东北方草原民族的侵扰。内部风险则包括王位争夺、贵族离心和边疆附庸的不稳定。
threats.en: The main external threat came from Roman pressure in Armenia and Mesopotamia, along with incursions by northern and northeastern steppe peoples. Internal risks included dynastic disputes, noble centrifugal tendencies, and instability among frontier vassals.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 匈奴/帕提亚式复合弓，骑射核心武器
description.en: Xiongnu/Parthian composite bow, core mounted archery weapon

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口主要集中于两河流域灌溉农业区、伊朗西部高地、媒体亚、巴比伦尼亚及若干商贸绿洲。人口分布极不均衡，大片高原与荒漠地区地广人稀。
populationDescription.en: The empire’s population was concentrated above all in the irrigated agricultural zones of Mesopotamia, the western Iranian uplands, Media, Babylonia, and selected commercial oases. Population distribution was highly uneven, with vast plateau and desert regions sparsely inhabited.

description.zh: 美索不达米亚古城，空中花园所在地
description.en: Ancient Mesopotamian city, home of the Hanging Gardens

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、大贵族、地方领主与高级祭司；中间层包括城市议员、商人、税吏、手工业者与中小地主；下层则由农民、佃农、牧民、劳役人口与奴隶组成。不同地区还存在希腊化城市公民、巴比伦神庙社群与部族组织等特殊社会形态。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal family, great nobles, local lords, and senior priests; the middle stratum included city councillors, merchants, tax agents, artisans, and middling landholders; the lower stratum comprised peasants, tenants, pastoralists, laborers, and slaves. Distinct social forms also persisted regionally, including Hellenized city citizens, Babylonian temple communities, and tribal organizations.

allies.zh: 安息通常依靠附庸王国与缓冲地带维持势力，尤其在亚美尼亚、阿迪亚贝尼和部分海湾及高加索方向寻求亲安息政权与地方盟友，而非固定意义上的现代同盟体系。
allies.en: Parthia generally relied on vassal kingdoms and buffer zones to sustain influence, especially seeking pro-Parthian rulers and local allies in Armenia, Adiabene, and parts of the Gulf and Caucasus rather than fixed alliances in the modern sense.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是罗马帝国，双方围绕亚美尼亚、幼发拉底河防线与美索不达米亚影响力长期竞争。边疆草原势力和不稳定部族也构成持续威胁。
enemies.en: Its principal adversary was the Roman Empire, with long-term competition over Armenia, the Euphrates frontier, and influence in Mesopotamia. Steppe powers and unstable frontier tribes also posed continuing threats.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策强调维持罗马与东方之间的中介地位、保护丝路和波斯湾贸易、通过扶植附庸王建立缓冲区，并在必要时以机动骑兵战争遏制罗马东进。对东方则重视与中亚商道和印度洋贸易节点的联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized preserving Parthia’s intermediary position between Rome and the East, protecting Silk Road and Persian Gulf commerce, creating buffer zones through client rulers, and using mobile cavalry warfare to check Roman expansion when necessary. To the east, it prioritized links with Central Asian routes and Indian Ocean trade nodes.

era.zh: 双雄帝国时代
era.en: Twin Empires

keyInnovations.zh: 关键优势不在单一发明，而在骑射战术体系、重装骑兵整合、长距离商路组织、驿传与沙漠边疆适应、拱券建筑和多文化商业金融实践。安息在东西技术与奢侈品流通中扮演枢纽角色。
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay less in a single invention than in integrated horse-archer tactics, cataphract warfare, long-distance trade organization, relay systems and desert-frontier adaptation, vaulted architecture, and multicultural commercial-financial practice. Parthia played a pivotal role in transmitting technologies and luxuries between East and West.

strengths.zh: 帝国拥有优越的地缘位置、强大的骑兵军事传统、丰富的两河农业基础、连接罗马与中亚印度贸易的枢纽地位，以及能包容多民族多宗教的统治方式。稳定时期财政稳健，边疆回旋空间大。
strengths.en: The empire possessed an advantageous geopolitical position, a powerful cavalry tradition, the rich agricultural base of Mesopotamia, a pivotal role linking Rome with Central Asian and Indian trade, and a governing style capable of accommodating ethnic and religious diversity. In stable periods its finances were sound and its frontiers offered strategic depth.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于中央集权不足、贵族势力过强、王位继承常有争议、行政一体化有限，以及对附庸和地方精英的依赖较深。面对持久攻城战与系统化步兵作战时，往往不如罗马军制稳固。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in limited centralization, excessive noble power, frequent succession disputes, weak administrative integration, and heavy dependence on vassals and local elites. In prolonged siege warfare and highly systematized infantry operations, it was often less robust than the Roman military system.

outlook.zh: 公元100年前后安息总体稳定，仍是欧亚大陆最重要的大帝国之一。只要王室能够维持与大贵族的平衡、控制亚美尼亚局势并保障商路畅通，帝国将继续繁荣；但若发生继承危机或对罗马战争失利，内部离心问题可能迅速加剧。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE Parthia was broadly stable and remained one of the great empires of Eurasia. So long as the crown maintained balance with the great nobles, managed Armenian affairs, and kept trade routes secure, the empire could continue to prosper; but a succession crisis or major defeat against Rome could quickly intensify centrifugal pressures.

description.zh: 安息控制伊朗高原与两河流域，是罗马与中亚之间的关键帝国。
description.en: Parthia controlled the Iranian plateau and Mesopotamia, serving as the key empire between Rome and Central Asia.

ideology.zh: 阿萨息斯王朝正统，封建帝国统治，骑射军事传统，丝路中间人角色
ideology.en: Arsacid dynastic legitimacy, feudal imperial rule, horse-archer military tradition, Silk Road intermediary role

relationship.zh: 阿萨息斯王室是安息帝国的名义最高权威，但实际权力远不如阿契美尼德或罗马皇帝集中。安息国王自称「万王之王」，但王位继承常受七大贵族家族影响，国王需获得贵族会议的认可。王室通过在附庸王国安插亲属、维持直辖领地和控制主要贸易路线来维系权威。
relationship.en: The Arsacid royal house was the nominal supreme authority of the Parthian Empire, though actual power was far less concentrated than in the Achaemenid or Roman empires. The Parthian king styled himself 'King of Kings,' but succession was often influenced by the Seven Great Noble Families, and the king needed aristocratic council approval. The dynasty maintained authority by installing relatives in vassal kingdoms, holding direct domains, and controlling major trade routes.

description.zh: 阿萨息斯王朝自公元前247年帕尔尼部落首领阿尔萨息斯一世建国以来，统治安息帝国已逾三百年。王朝以伊朗高原和美索不达米亚为核心，建立了一个松散的封建帝国体系，包括直辖领地、附庸王国和半自治贵族领地。与高度集权的罗马帝国不同，安息国王更像是贵族联盟的盟主，其权力取决于与七大家族的平衡关系。帕科鲁斯二世在位时面临王位争夺者的挑战和罗马在东方的持续压力。骑射战术——轻骑兵的远射骚扰与重装铁甲骑兵的冲击——是安息军事声望的基石，卡莱战役的胜利仍令罗马记忆犹新。
description.en: The Arsacid dynasty had ruled the Parthian Empire for over three centuries since Arsaces I, chieftain of the Parni tribe, founded the state in 247 BCE. With the Iranian plateau and Mesopotamia at its core, the dynasty built a loose feudal imperial system comprising direct domains, vassal kingdoms, and semi-autonomous noble territories. Unlike the highly centralized Roman Empire, the Parthian king was more a primus inter pares among aristocratic allies, his power depending on the balance with the Seven Great Families. Pacorus II faced challenges from rival claimants and persistent Roman pressure in the east. Horse-archer tactics — light cavalry's ranged harassment combined with heavy cataphract charges — remained the foundation of Parthian military prestige, and the victory at Carrhae was still fresh in Roman memory.

role.zh: 在位国王，维持帝国统治并应对内部王位争夺与罗马压力
role.en: Reigning king, maintaining imperial rule while managing internal succession disputes and Roman pressure

leader.en: House of Suren, House of Karen, and others

ideology.zh: 贵族封建特权，世袭领地与军事自主，参与国王选举与废立，限制王权
ideology.en: Aristocratic feudal privilege, hereditary domains and military autonomy, participation in royal election and deposition, limiting royal power

relationship.zh: 七大家族（苏连、卡连等）是安息帝国权力结构中与王室并立的核心力量。他们拥有世袭领地、独立武装和在国王选举中的投票权，可以合法地影响甚至决定王位继承。贵族家族提供帝国军队的主力——尤其是重装铁甲骑兵，这使他们在军事上不可或缺。王室与贵族之间是合作与角力并存的关系：强大的国王能驾驭贵族，但弱势的国王则可能被贵族废黜。
relationship.en: The Seven Great Families (Suren, Karen, and others) were forces co-equal with the dynasty in the Parthian power structure. They held hereditary domains, independent armed forces, and voting rights in royal succession, legitimately influencing or even determining the choice of king. The noble houses provided the bulk of the imperial army — especially the heavy cataphracts — making them militarily indispensable. The relationship between the dynasty and nobles combined cooperation and competition: strong kings could manage the nobility, but weak kings risked deposition.

description.zh: 安息帝国的七大贵族家族是其封建体制的基石。苏连家族（控制东部锡斯坦地区）、卡连家族（控制米底西部）以及其他五个大家族各自拥有广大的世袭领地和大量的骑兵武装。他们享有在王位空缺时选举新王的特权——这一制度使安息政治更接近贵族共和而非绝对君主制。苏连家族是帝国最显赫的贵族，其家主曾领兵在卡莱击败克拉苏的罗马军团。这些贵族家族的骑兵部队（尤其是铁甲骑兵）是安息军事力量的核心，但他们的自主性也构成了帝国统一的最大离心力。当王室衰弱时，贵族家族的叛乱和王位争夺是帝国面临的首要威胁。
description.en: The Seven Great Noble Families were the cornerstone of the Parthian feudal system. The House of Suren (controlling eastern Sistan), the House of Karen (controlling western Media), and five other great families each possessed vast hereditary domains and large cavalry forces. They held the privilege of electing a new king when the throne was vacant — a system that made Parthian politics more akin to an aristocratic republic than an absolute monarchy. The Suren family was the most illustrious, having led the army that defeated Crassus' Roman legions at Carrhae. The cavalry forces of these noble houses (especially cataphracts) formed the core of Parthian military power, but their autonomy also constituted the greatest centrifugal force threatening imperial unity. When the dynasty weakened, noble rebellion and succession contests were the empire's foremost threats.

role.zh: 安息最显赫的贵族家族领袖，掌控帝国东部，拥有独立的骑兵武装与外交权力
role.en: Leader of Parthia's most illustrious noble family, controlling the eastern empire with independent cavalry forces and diplomatic authority

ideology.zh: 希腊化城市自治传统，希腊文化保存，商业自由，城市公民自治
ideology.en: Hellenistic urban self-governance tradition, preservation of Greek culture, commercial freedom, civic autonomy

headquarters.zh: 底格里斯河畔塞琉西亚

relationship.zh: 希腊城市群体在安息帝国中保持着显著的自治地位。塞琉西亚是帝国最大的城市之一，拥有约六十万人口，保留了塞琉古时代的希腊化行政体制和城市议会。安息王室给予这些城市相当的自治权——包括内部立法、商业管理和文化活动——以换取税收、贸易合作和政治忠诚。希腊文化在安息帝国中仍然活跃，希腊语被用于铸币和官方铭文。
relationship.en: The Greek city communities maintained notable autonomous status within the Parthian Empire. Seleucia-on-Tigris was one of the empire's largest cities with some 600,000 inhabitants, preserving Hellenistic administrative structures and a city council from the Seleucid era. The Arsacid dynasty granted these cities considerable autonomy — including internal legislation, commercial management, and cultural activities — in exchange for tax revenue, trade cooperation, and political loyalty. Greek culture remained vibrant in the Parthian Empire, with Greek used on coinage and official inscriptions.

description.zh: 安息帝国境内的希腊城市是亚历山大东征和塞琉古王朝时代遗留的文化遗产。底格里斯河畔的塞琉西亚是其中最重要的代表——这座巨型都市是两河流域的商业和文化中心，拥有希腊式的剧场、体育场和公共建筑。安息王朝虽然在塞琉西亚对面建立了新都泰西封，但始终尊重塞琉西亚的城市自治传统。帝国境内还分布着多座希腊化城市，它们维持着希腊语使用、希腊式教育和体育竞赛传统。这些城市是丝路贸易网络的重要节点，其商人阶层在帝国经济中扮演关键角色。然而当安息王权与城市利益冲突时，城市也可能爆发叛乱——塞琉西亚曾多次反抗安息统治。
description.en: The Greek cities within the Parthian Empire were a cultural legacy of Alexander's eastern campaigns and the Seleucid era. Seleucia-on-Tigris was the most important — this massive metropolis was the commercial and cultural center of Mesopotamia, featuring Greek-style theaters, gymnasia, and public buildings. Though the Arsacid dynasty built its new capital Ctesiphon opposite Seleucia, it consistently respected the city's autonomous traditions. Multiple Hellenistic cities were scattered across the empire, maintaining Greek language use, Greek-style education, and athletic competition traditions. These cities were vital nodes in the Silk Road trade network, with their merchant classes playing key roles in the imperial economy. However, when Arsacid interests clashed with civic rights, cities could revolt — Seleucia rebelled against Parthian rule on multiple occasions.

role.zh: 管理帝国最大城市之一的内部事务，维护希腊化传统和商业利益
role.en: Managing internal affairs of one of the empire's largest cities, preserving Hellenistic traditions and commercial interests

name.en: Head of the Gulf Trade Polity

ruler.zh: 阿塔姆贝洛斯／阿波达科斯系国王
ruler.en: King of the Attambelos / Apodakos line

governmentForm.zh: 附属于安息宗主权之下的世袭港口王国，以王室统治、商税财政和河海贸易控制为核心
governmentForm.en: A hereditary port kingdom under Parthian suzerainty, centered on royal rule, customs revenue, and control of river-sea trade

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、港市商人、神庙与书吏、船主与工匠、乡村农民、渔民与劳役人口构成；城市商业阶层在政治与财政中影响尤大
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, port-city merchants, temple personnel and scribes, shipowners and artisans, rural cultivators, fishermen, and laboring dependents; the urban mercantile class held especially strong political and fiscal influence

rulingClass.zh: 王族、地方大族、港口税吏集团以及与安息帝国保持联系的伊朗化贵族共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was formed by the royal family, local magnate houses, port customs officials, and Iranized nobles connected to the Parthian Empire

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室宗支间世袭继承，但需得到宫廷贵族与安息宗主的承认
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within branches of the royal house, but required recognition by court elites and the Parthian overlord

structure.zh: 中央政权规模不大，围绕王宫、港务税关、仓储系统和河口防务运作；地方治理依托城市官员、港口管理者与农村征税代理人
structure.en: The central government was modest in size and operated around the palace, port customs administration, warehouse system, and estuary defense; local governance relied on city officials, harbor administrators, and rural tax agents

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存王室档案、管理外交文书与税契记录
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept royal archives, and managed diplomatic documents and tax contracts

name.en: Harbor Customs and Trade Revenue Office

function.zh: 征收港口关税、航运税、转口税，并监督度量衡与货物申报
function.en: Collected harbor dues, shipping taxes, and transit customs while supervising weights, measures, and cargo declarations

name.en: Granary, Warehousing, and River Transport Office

function.zh: 管理仓库、粮储、码头装卸与底格里斯—幼发拉底水运协调
function.en: Managed warehouses, grain reserves, dock labor, and coordination of Tigris-Euphrates river transport

function.zh: 保管王室金库、支付军费与宫廷开支，并处理币制与贵金属储备
function.en: Held the royal treasury, paid military and court expenses, and handled coinage and precious metal reserves

function.zh: 负责河口巡防、港湾警备、灯火信号和征调船只
function.en: Oversaw estuary patrols, harbor security, beacon signaling, and requisition of vessels

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以首都及其周边港埠、河道据点和农业聚落为核心，依靠任命官员、包税人和地方头人共同维持
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the capital, nearby ports, river stations, and agricultural settlements, maintained through appointed officials, tax farmers, and local headmen

legalSystem.zh: 法律实践融合王命、地方习惯、两河商事传统与安息政治惯例；商贸、航运、债务和仓储纠纷在实际治理中占重要地位
legalSystem.en: Legal practice combined royal decree, local custom, Mesopotamian commercial traditions, and Parthian political usage; disputes over trade, shipping, debt, and storage were especially important in administration

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠关税、港务税、转运税、市场征收费、渔盐收入与周边农地贡赋；对国际贸易课税远重于对农村人口直接征敛
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal system relied chiefly on customs duties, harbor fees, transit tolls, market charges, fishery and salt revenues, and tribute from surrounding farmland; international trade was taxed more heavily than the rural population

religion.zh: 宗教生活呈混合面貌，包含巴比伦—两河传统神祇崇拜、阿拉米与阿拉伯地方祭祀、伊朗宗教影响，以及与希腊化城市文化相联系的神庙实践
religion.en: Religious life was mixed, including Babylonian-Mesopotamian cults, Aramaean and Arabian local worship, Iranian religious influence, and temple practices tied to Hellenistic urban culture

philosophy.zh: 本国并无独立哲学学派，思想文化以实用商贸伦理、宫廷政治智慧、占星历法知识和多语文书传统为主
philosophy.en: The kingdom had no distinct philosophical school; its intellectual culture was shaped by practical mercantile ethics, courtly statecraft, astral-calendar knowledge, and multilingual scribal traditions

writingSystem.zh: 行政与商业环境中并用希腊文书写、阿拉米文书写及其地方变体；钱币铭文常见希腊文，日常实务则多依赖闪米特系书写传统
writingSystem.en: Greek writing, Aramaic writing, and local variants were used in administration and commerce; coin legends commonly appeared in Greek, while everyday practice relied heavily on Semitic scribal traditions

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于塑造了连接美索不达米亚、阿拉伯、印度洋与伊朗世界的港市文明；币制、商业文书、仓储管理、混合宗教与多语交流体现出高度中介性
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievement lay in shaping a port civilization linking Mesopotamia, Arabia, the Indian Ocean, and the Iranian world; coinage, commercial documentation, warehouse management, mixed cults, and multilingual exchange all reveal a highly intermediary culture

languageFamily.zh: 以闪米特语族语言和阿拉米语通用传统为基础，同时受希腊语与伊朗语族强烈影响
languageFamily.en: It was rooted in Semitic languages and the broader Aramaic koine tradition, while being strongly influenced by Greek and Iranian languages

gdpDescription.zh: 作为人口不大的港口王国，卡拉克涅总量有限，但因掌握海湾航线与两河入海口，其单位人口产出在同时代地区政权中偏高；财富集中于贸易、税关、仓储和转口服务，而非广阔农业腹地
gdpDescription.en: As a small-population port kingdom, Characene had limited total output, but because it controlled Gulf shipping lanes and the Mesopotamian maritime outlet, its per-capita productivity was relatively high for a regional polity of the age; wealth was concentrated in trade, customs, warehousing, and entrepôt services rather than in a vast agrarian hinterland

mainIndustries.zh: 国际转口贸易、港口装卸与仓储、河海运输、造船与修船、渔盐业、椰枣与灌溉农业、手工业加工与钱币流通
mainIndustries.en: International entrepôt trade, port handling and warehousing, river-sea transport, shipbuilding and repair, fisheries and salt production, dates and irrigated agriculture, craft processing, and coin circulation

tradeGoods.zh: 输入与转运货物包括印度香料、棉织物、宝石、珍珠、象牙、阿拉伯乳香与没药，以及来自两河和伊朗的谷物、沥青、纺织品、金属器与酒类
tradeGoods.en: Imported and re-exported goods included Indian spices, cotton textiles, gemstones, pearls, ivory, Arabian frankincense and myrrh, along with grain, bitumen, textiles, metalware, and wine from Mesopotamia and Iran

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化极大。港市商人、船主与税务承包者拥有大量流动资本、仓储权益与海外关系；普通农渔家庭财富较低，易受洪水、盐碱与贸易波动影响
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Port merchants, shipowners, and tax contractors held substantial liquid capital, warehousing rights, and overseas connections; ordinary farming and fishing households possessed much less and were vulnerable to floods, salinity, and trade fluctuations

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制属于以王权和港市精英共同主导的商业型混合经济；王室通过关税、仓储和航运监管获取收入，私人商人和家族网络则承担实际贸易组织
economicSystem.en: The economy was a commercial mixed system jointly shaped by royal authority and port-city elites; the crown earned revenue through customs, warehousing, and shipping regulation, while private merchants and family networks carried out most actual trade

source.zh: 市场费、仓储费与航运收费
source.en: Market fees, warehousing charges, and shipping dues

source.zh: 王室产业、渔盐与杂项收入
source.en: Royal estates, fisheries, salt, and miscellaneous income

category.en: Court, royal household, and diplomatic gifts
Maintenance of harbors, embankments, and warehouses
Religious donations, relief, and miscellaneous costs

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模对一小型王国而言相当可观，主要以银币、贵金属储备、香料与珍货库存、以及可迅速变现的商税债权形式存在；其稳定性高度依赖航运畅通
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial for a small kingdom and existed chiefly as silver coin, bullion reserves, stocks of spices and luxury goods, and tax claims that could be quickly monetized; its stability depended heavily on uninterrupted shipping

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏谨慎保守，重在维持港口设施、守备与王室声望，并通过适度盈余应对洪灾、航路风险和地区战争对贸易的冲击
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautious and conservative, emphasizing port infrastructure, defense, and royal prestige while maintaining moderate surpluses to absorb shocks from floods, shipping risks, and regional wars

description.zh: 由宫廷卫队、城防步兵和持矛弓兵组成，负责都城守备、仓库护卫与城门控制
description.en: Composed of palace guards, urban garrison infantry, and spear- and bow-armed troops responsible for the capital, warehouses, and gate control

description.zh: 规模不大，但机动性较强，主要来自贵族和富裕阶层，用于巡逻、护送和应对边缘地区骚扰
description.en: Small in scale but relatively mobile, drawn mainly from nobles and wealthy strata, used for patrols, escorts, and responses to peripheral raiding

description.zh: 以轻型船只和港务武装为主，负责河口、近海与商船航道警戒，打击海盗和走私
description.en: Centered on light craft and armed harbor personnel, tasked with guarding the estuary, nearshore waters, and merchant routes while suppressing piracy and smuggling

description.zh: 在紧急时由城市居民、码头劳工和乡村人口中征集，用于堤岸防守和后方支援
description.en: Raised in emergencies from townsmen, dock laborers, and rural inhabitants for embankment defense and rear-area support

technology.zh: 军事技术总体处于安息体系的地方水平，使用弓矛、鳞甲或皮甲、防御工事、河舟巡逻和信号警戒；不以重大战役军团见长，而以港口控制和机动巡防见长
technology.en: Military technology stood at a local level within the Parthian sphere, using bows, spears, scale or leather armor, fortifications, river craft patrols, and signal warning systems; it excelled not in major field armies but in port control and mobile patrol defense

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自海盗、河口贸易争夺、地方叛乱、阿拉伯部族袭扰，以及在安息与罗马冲突加剧时被迫卷入区域权力竞争
threats.en: Main threats came from piracy, struggles over estuary commerce, local unrest, Arabian tribal raiding, and the risk of being drawn into wider regional competition when Parthian-Roman tensions intensified

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于首都及其港口带、河岸聚落和灌溉农业区；民族与语言构成复杂，包含两河本地居民、阿拉米语社群、阿拉伯人、伊朗因素和希腊化城市人口
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the capital and its port belt, riverbank settlements, and irrigated agricultural zones; its ethnic and linguistic composition was complex, including local Mesopotamian inhabitants, Aramaic-speaking communities, Arabs, Iranian elements, and Hellenized urban populations

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王族与贵族、港市巨商与船主、税吏书吏、神庙人员、工匠与码头劳动者、农民渔民以及依附性劳役人口；奴役和债役现象在港市商业环境中可能存在，但并非社会唯一基础
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the royal family and nobles, great merchants and shipowners, tax officials and scribes, temple personnel, artisans and dockworkers, peasants and fishermen, and dependent laboring populations; slavery and debt servitude likely existed in the port economy, but were not the sole basis of society

allies.zh: 实际上的主要依托为安息帝国；同时与印度西岸、阿拉伯南部和海湾沿岸商人社群维持互利关系
allies.en: Its principal backing lay with the Parthian Empire; it also maintained mutually beneficial relations with merchant communities on the west coast of India, in South Arabia, and around the Gulf littoral

enemies.zh: 没有长期固定的单一死敌，但与海盗集团、敌对部族、争夺航道的地方势力及安息之敌可能发生冲突；在更广范围内需警惕罗马海贸扩张对中介地位的削弱
enemies.en: It had no single permanent mortal enemy, but could clash with pirate groups, hostile tribes, local rivals for shipping routes, and enemies of Parthia; more broadly, it had reason to watch Roman maritime expansion as a challenge to its intermediary position

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以生存和贸易优先为原则：对安息保持忠附，对外洋商人保持开放，对海湾与印度洋航线尽力维持中立安全与税收秩序
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized survival and commerce: loyal subordination to Parthia, openness toward overseas merchants, and efforts to preserve neutrality, security, and taxable order along Gulf and Indian Ocean routes

era.zh: 铁器时代后期的欧亚海陆贸易时代
era.en: Late Iron Age era of Afro-Eurasian maritime and overland trade

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力不在原创发明，而在航运组织、港口工程、仓储管理、度量衡控制、钱币铸造和河海联运体系；对季风航线知识的吸收尤其重要
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay not in original invention but in shipping organization, harbor engineering, warehouse management, control of weights and measures, coin minting, and integrated river-sea transport; especially important was its adoption of monsoon-route knowledge

strengths.zh: 地理位置极其优越，控制两河出海口与海湾贸易节点；财政仰赖高价值贸易而非沉重田赋，城市商业精英活跃，多语文化有利于跨区域往来
strengths.en: It possessed an exceptionally favorable location, controlling the Mesopotamian outlet to the sea and a key Gulf trade node; its finances depended on high-value commerce rather than crushing land taxes, its urban merchant elite was active, and its multilingual culture favored long-distance exchange

weaknesses.zh: 疆域狭小、人口有限、军事规模不大，对航道安全、河口地貌变化、洪水泥沙淤积和外部宗主力量高度依赖；一旦贸易中断，财政将迅速受损
weaknesses.en: It had a small territory, limited population, and modest armed forces, and it depended heavily on secure sea lanes, changing estuarine geography, flood and silt control, and the power of its overlord; if trade were disrupted, fiscal health would deteriorate quickly

outlook.zh: 在100年左右，该国前景总体稳定且繁荣可期，只要安息体系仍能维持对两河与海湾的总体秩序，卡拉克涅便能继续作为连接罗马东方、阿拉伯和印度洋贸易的重要中继王国
outlook.en: Around 100 CE, the kingdom's outlook was generally stable and promising; so long as the Parthian system could preserve broad order in Mesopotamia and the Gulf, Characene could continue to function as an important intermediary kingdom linking the Roman East, Arabia, and Indian Ocean commerce

description.zh: 位于波斯湾北端的卡拉克涅是安息体系下重要的海贸王国。
description.en: At the head of the Persian Gulf, Characene was a significant maritime kingdom within the Parthian sphere.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制下的商路王国，依靠王室、地方精英与商人网络共同统治
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy structured as a caravan-trade kingdom, governed through the crown, local elites, and merchant networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、富裕商人、神庙祭司、城镇自由民、农民、牧民和奴隶组成；城镇商贸阶层在政治与财政中影响显著。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, wealthy merchants, temple priests, urban freemen, peasants, pastoral nomads, and slaves; urban mercantile groups held strong political and fiscal influence.

rulingClass.zh: 纳巴泰王室、佩特拉及主要绿洲城市的贵族家族、商队首领与神庙高层祭司
rulingClass.en: The Nabataean royal family, noble houses of Petra and major oasis towns, caravan magnates, and senior temple priesthood

succession.zh: 王位通常由王室世袭继承，常在近支宗族内传递，并依赖宫廷与地方精英承认
succession.en: The throne was usually inherited within the royal house, often through close dynastic kin, and required acceptance by the court and provincial elites

structure.zh: 中央王廷设于佩特拉，国王通过宫廷官员、地方总督、城镇议事机构和税务代理人治理各绿洲、港口与商路节点。
structure.en: The central court at Petra governed through royal officials, provincial governors, urban councils, and tax agents across oases, ports, and caravan-route stations.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保管王室档案、处理外交文书并监督阿拉姆语文书事务
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept royal archives, handled diplomatic correspondence, and supervised Aramaic-language documentation

function.zh: 征收关税、过境税、市场税和绿洲农业贡赋，重点管理香料与奢侈品贸易收入
function.en: Collected customs, transit tolls, market taxes, and oasis agricultural dues, especially from aromatics and luxury trade

function.zh: 维护蓄水池、渠道、堤坝、岩凿水道、道路和公共建筑，保障佩特拉及沙漠据点供水
function.en: Maintained cisterns, channels, dams, rock-cut conduits, roads, and public works, ensuring water supply for Petra and desert stations

function.zh: 管理红海港口如利乌克·科墨、监督海关与远洋贸易联络
function.en: Administered Red Sea ports such as Leuke Kome and supervised customs and overseas trade contacts

function.zh: 指挥王室卫队、沙漠巡逻、商路护送与要塞守备
function.en: Directed the royal guard, desert patrols, caravan escorts, and garrison defense

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托哈夫拉、赫格拉、博斯拉等中心城镇的总督、驻军长官、税吏与地方名门；对沙漠部族多采取间接统治和盟约控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on governors, garrison commanders, tax officials, and prominent families in centers such as Hegra and Bostra; desert tribes were often managed through indirect rule and treaty relationships.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王令、城市习惯法、商贸契约和宗教誓约，实际运作中高度重视财产、债务、贸易纠纷与水资源使用权。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, urban customary law, mercantile contracts, and religious oaths, with strong practical attention to property, debt, commercial disputes, and water rights.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以商路关税、港口税、市场税、农业贡赋、灌溉地租和附庸部族输纳为主；贸易税收在国家收入中占核心地位。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system centered on caravan customs, port duties, market taxes, agricultural tribute, irrigated land rents, and payments from dependent tribes; trade taxation formed the core of state income.

religion.zh: 纳巴泰宗教以多神崇拜为主，尊奉杜沙拉、乌扎、阿拉特等神祇；神庙、圣坛和岩龛遍布主要城镇，同时受希腊化与邻近闪米特宗教影响。
religion.en: Nabataean religion was predominantly polytheistic, centered on deities such as Dushara, al-'Uzza, and Allat; temples, high places, and rock sanctuaries were widespread, while Hellenistic and neighboring Semitic influences were also strong.

philosophy.zh: 王国并无成体系哲学传统，但其政治文化强调实用主义、商贸理性、宗教合法性以及对多文化交流的开放吸收。
philosophy.en: The kingdom did not possess a formal philosophical tradition, but its political culture emphasized pragmatism, mercantile calculation, religious legitimacy, and openness to multicultural exchange.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用纳巴泰阿拉姆字母书写阿拉姆语文书，这一书写传统后来对阿拉伯字母发展具有重要影响。
writingSystem.en: Administrative and monumental writing primarily used the Nabataean Aramaic script for Aramaic texts, a scribal tradition later important in the development of the Arabic script.

culturalAchievements.zh: 佩特拉岩刻建筑、纪念性墓葬立面、先进的沙漠水利系统、跨地区商贸文化融合以及精致陶器与宗教建筑是其代表成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Its major achievements included Petra’s rock-cut architecture, monumental tomb façades, advanced desert hydraulic engineering, a cosmopolitan trade culture, and fine ceramics and religious architecture.

languageFamily.zh: 西闪米特语系；行政和铭文多用阿拉姆语，日常亦通行阿拉伯化的地方语言成分
languageFamily.en: West Semitic; Aramaic dominated administration and inscriptions, while local speech also contained strong Arabic elements

gdpDescription.zh: 纳巴泰经济规模在阿拉伯北部和南黎凡特属较高水平，依靠过境贸易、香料转运、港口税收、绿洲农业和手工业维持繁荣，但高度依赖区域商路安全与罗马—阿拉伯—印度洋贸易流动。
gdpDescription.en: The Nabataean economy ranked comparatively high for northern Arabia and the southern Levant, drawing prosperity from transit trade, aromatics redistribution, port revenues, oasis agriculture, and crafts, though it depended heavily on secure regional routes and Roman-Arabian-Indian Ocean exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 商队运输、香料与乳香没药转运、铜矿与金属加工、陶器、石建筑营造、灌溉农业、葡萄与枣椰种植、港口贸易服务
mainIndustries.en: Caravan transport, redistribution of incense and aromatics, copper extraction and metalworking, ceramics, stone construction, irrigated agriculture, viticulture and date cultivation, and port trade services

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香料、沥青、铜、葡萄酒、橄榄油、玻璃器、纺织品、染料、珍贵石材、阿拉伯马与奢侈消费品
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, spices, bitumen, copper, wine, olive oil, glassware, textiles, dyes, prized stone, Arabian horses, and luxury consumption goods

householdWealth.zh: 城镇商人和税务承包者财富集中度高，佩特拉与赫格拉上层家庭拥有显著地产、商队股份和储藏财富；普通农牧家庭财产较有限，但在良好水利条件下可维持稳定生计。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was concentrated among urban merchants and tax contractors; upper households in Petra and Hegra held substantial land, caravan shares, and stored wealth, while ordinary farming and pastoral households possessed fewer assets but could maintain stable livelihoods where water systems functioned well.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换和远距离商贸为主导的王室监管经济；国家通过控制绿洲、水源、关隘和港口，对商业利润进行有效截取。
economicSystem.en: A market-oriented, long-distance trading economy under royal supervision; the state extracted profit effectively by controlling oases, water sources, passes, and ports.

source.en: Port duties and maritime trade revenue

source.zh: 市场税、矿产与杂项收入
source.en: Market taxes, mining, and miscellaneous income

category.en: Army, caravan protection, and frontier defense

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备因长期贸易利润而较为充足，既包括金银钱币，也包括可转化财富如香料、贵重器物与应急粮储，但很大程度依赖贸易持续畅通。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was relatively well supplied by long-term trade profits, including coin reserves in gold and silver as well as convertible wealth in aromatics, valuables, and emergency grain stores, though it remained heavily dependent on uninterrupted commerce.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏稳健，以维持商路安全和基础水利为先，避免对商人课以过重负担，同时通过关税和港口征收获取高效现金收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, prioritizing caravan security and hydraulic infrastructure while avoiding excessive burdens on merchants and relying on customs and port collections for efficient cash revenue.

description.zh: 机动主力，适合沙漠侦察、护送商队、快速截击与边境巡逻，熟悉干旱地形。
description.en: The main mobile arm, suited for desert reconnaissance, caravan escort, rapid interception, and frontier patrol in arid terrain.

description.zh: 驻守佩特拉、赫格拉、博斯拉及关隘要地，承担城防、税站保护和道路控制任务。
description.en: Stationed at Petra, Hegra, Bostra, and key passes, they handled urban defense, customs-post protection, and route control.

description.zh: 由附属或结盟部族提供，战时增强兵力，擅长向导、袭扰和沙漠补给。
description.en: Provided by subordinate or allied tribes, these forces strengthened wartime numbers and excelled in guiding, raiding, and desert supply.

commanderInChief.zh: 拉贝勒二世·索特尔国王

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、复合弓、长矛、盾牌、骑乘装备和沙漠要塞防御为主，缺乏罗马那样的大规模攻城体系，但在荒漠机动与补给管理上经验丰富。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, composite bows, spears, shields, riding equipment, and desert-fort defense; it lacked a Roman-style large-scale siege system but was experienced in arid mobility and supply management.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自罗马在叙利亚和犹太行省的战略压力、沙漠部族的不稳定、以及商路改道或受阻导致的防务与财政双重风险。
threats.en: Primary threats came from Roman strategic pressure from Syria and Judaea, instability among desert tribes, and the dual military-fiscal risk posed by diversion or disruption of trade routes.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于佩特拉、赫格拉、博斯拉及若干绿洲、山地聚落和商路据点；居民包括定居城镇人口、农民、牧民和往来商旅。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in Petra, Hegra, Bostra, and various oases, upland settlements, and caravan stations; inhabitants included urban settlers, peasants, pastoralists, and transient merchants.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族居于顶层，其次为富裕商人、祭司和地方名门；中层包括工匠、店主、书吏和小地主；底层为农民、牧民、雇工与奴隶。
socialClasses.en: The royal family and nobility stood at the top, followed by wealthy merchants, priests, and local notable families; the middle ranks included craftsmen, shopkeepers, scribes, and small landholders; the lower strata were peasants, pastoralists, laborers, and slaves.

allies.zh: 无严格意义上的大国盟友，但与部分阿拉伯部族、红海与海湾贸易中介以及若干地方城镇维持实用合作关系。
allies.en: It had no major-power ally in a strict sense, but maintained pragmatic cooperative ties with some Arab tribes, Red Sea and Gulf trade intermediaries, and various local cities.

enemies.zh: 无持续全面战争中的明确宿敌，但罗马帝国是最重要的外部压力来源；与部分沙漠部族及贸易竞争者也存在局部冲突。
enemies.en: It had no single formal enemy in continuous total war, but the Roman Empire was its most important source of external pressure; there were also local conflicts with certain desert tribes and commercial rivals.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以平衡罗马与阿拉伯内陆势力、保障贸易通道、通过婚姻、贡礼、协定和边地控制维持自治为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on balancing Rome and inland Arabian forces, securing trade corridors, and preserving autonomy through marriage ties, gifts, agreements, and frontier management.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期—古典古代商贸文明阶段
era.en: Late Iron Age to Classical antiquity mercantile civilization stage

keyInnovations.zh: 高效沙漠集水与蓄水系统、岩凿引水工程、商路站点后勤组织、精细石刻建筑与成熟的跨区域商贸调度能力
keyInnovations.en: Efficient desert runoff collection and cistern systems, rock-cut water conduits, logistical organization of caravan stations, refined stone-cut architecture, and mature long-distance trade coordination

strengths.zh: 地处阿拉伯—黎凡特—红海交通枢纽，擅长沙漠环境治理与商路税收，首都佩特拉具有强大象征与经济吸引力，王国财政相对稳健。
strengths.en: It occupied a strategic junction linking Arabia, the Levant, and the Red Sea, excelled in desert environmental management and caravan taxation, possessed a highly prestigious and economically attractive capital at Petra, and maintained relatively sound finances.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与兵力有限，对过境贸易依赖过深，缺乏与罗马抗衡的重型军事体系，政治自主性容易受周边帝国挤压。
weaknesses.en: Its population and military resources were limited, it depended too heavily on transit trade, lacked the heavy military system needed to match Rome, and its political autonomy was vulnerable to pressure from surrounding empires.

outlook.zh: 到100年时王国仍保持稳定并具相当繁荣，但其独立前景已受罗马扩张阴影笼罩；若无法持续以外交和商业价值维持缓冲地位，数十年内被并入帝国体系的风险很高。
outlook.en: By 100 CE the kingdom remained stable and fairly prosperous, but its independent future was already overshadowed by Roman expansion; unless it could preserve its buffer role through diplomacy and commercial value, the risk of incorporation into an imperial system within decades was high.

description.zh: 纳巴泰王国控制阿拉伯西北香料与商道，在罗马 annexation 前仍保持独立。
description.en: The Nabataean Kingdom controlled northwestern Arabian trade routes and remained independent shortly before Roman annexation.

name.zh: 萨巴与希木叶尔诸王国
name.en: Saba and Himyarite South Arabian Kingdoms

ruler.zh: 诸南阿拉伯统治者（穆卡里卜或国王）
ruler.en: Various South Arabian rulers (Mukarrib or King)

dynasty.zh: 萨巴、希木叶尔及相关王朝

governmentForm.zh: 多王国君主制，结合部落贵族、神庙机构与灌溉农业行政
governmentForm.en: Multi-kingdom monarchy combining tribal aristocracy, temple institutions, and irrigation-based agrarian administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与部落贵族、神庙祭司、商人、灌溉农业民众、工匠、牧民与奴隶构成；各王国内部保有强烈的氏族与部落认同，城镇与绿洲社会联系紧密。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of royal houses and tribal aristocrats, temple priests, merchants, irrigated farming communities, artisans, pastoralists, and slaves; strong clan and tribal identities persisted within each kingdom, closely linking towns and oasis society.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、部落首领、大土地持有者、神庙祭司集团与控制商路的商人贵族
rulingClass.en: Royal families, tribal chieftains, major landholders, temple priestly groups, and merchant elites controlling trade routes

succession.zh: 王位继承多以王族内部世袭为主，但常受部落联盟、贵族会议与军事支持影响，不同王国间制度并不完全一致。
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within ruling families, but often shaped by tribal alliances, aristocratic councils, and military backing; practices varied among kingdoms.

structure.zh: 南阿拉伯在公元100年前后并非单一统一帝国，而是由萨巴、希木叶尔、哈德拉毛与卡塔班等王国构成。各国通常由君主统治，下设王廷书记官、税吏、灌溉监督、堡寨与商路官员，并依赖地方部落领袖治理山地、高原与沿海地区。
structure.en: Around 100 CE, South Arabia was not a single unified empire but a constellation of kingdoms including Saba, Himyar, Hadramawt, and remnants or successors of Qataban. Each was typically ruled by a monarch, supported by court scribes, tax officials, irrigation supervisors, and officers for forts and trade routes, while relying on local tribal leaders to govern the highlands, plateau, and coastal zones.

function.zh: 起草法令、保存盟约、记录献祭、税务与建筑工程，并以南阿拉伯文字刻写官方铭文。
function.en: Drafted decrees, preserved treaties, recorded offerings, taxation, and construction works, and produced official inscriptions in South Arabian script.

function.zh: 维护堤坝、渠道、分水系统与农田配水，尤其负责马里卜大坝及相关农业工程的组织与修缮。
function.en: Maintained dams, canals, water distribution systems, and field allocation, especially organizing and repairing the Marib Dam and related agricultural works.

function.zh: 征收香料、乳香、没药、葡萄酒、金属器与进口奢侈品关税，监管驿站、商队路线与港口贸易。
function.en: Collected duties on aromatics, frankincense, myrrh, wine, metal goods, and imported luxuries, and supervised caravan routes, stations, and port commerce.

function.zh: 管理国家祭祀、神庙地产、供献与宗教历法，并协调王权与主要神祇崇拜之间的关系。
function.en: Managed state cults, temple lands, offerings, and religious calendars, coordinating relations between royal authority and major deity worship.

name.en: Provincial Forts and Frontier Roads Office

function.zh: 维持堡寨驻军、边境巡逻、山口控制与沿海、沙漠交通安全。
function.en: Maintained garrisons, frontier patrols, pass control, and security along coastal and desert communications.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖王室委任官员与部落首领并行。城市、绿洲和灌溉区由书记官、税吏与水利监督管理；山地和边缘地带则更多通过盟约、朝贡和军事驻防实施间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on both royal appointees and tribal leaders. Towns, oases, and irrigated districts were managed by scribes, tax officials, and water supervisors; uplands and peripheral regions were more often ruled indirectly through alliances, tribute, and military garrisons.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、部落习惯法、宗教誓约与铭文法令为基础。财产、水权、债务、土地边界、商队责任和献祭义务均有较成熟规范，争端可由部落长老、神庙机构或王廷裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on royal commands, tribal customary law, religious oaths, and inscribed decrees. Property, water rights, debt, land boundaries, caravan liability, and sacrificial obligations were regulated with some sophistication, while disputes could be judged by tribal elders, temple institutions, or the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自农产税、灌溉地赋、商路关税、港口税、附庸或部落贡纳、神庙相关征收与战利品。税收既可用实物征纳，也可用银与铸币或称量金属结算。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from agricultural taxes, irrigated land assessments, caravan tolls, port duties, tribute from dependent tribes, temple-related exactions, and war gains. Taxes could be paid in kind or settled in silver and coinage or by weighed metal.

religion.zh: 宗教以多神崇拜为主，重要神祇包括阿尔马卡、阿塔尔、辛和瓦德等；王权、神庙和部落祭祀密切相连，神谕、誓言、献祭与还愿碑铭是公共生活的重要组成部分。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly polytheistic, with major deities including Almaqah, Athtar, Sin, and Wadd; kingship, temples, and tribal worship were closely linked, and oracles, oaths, sacrifices, and votive inscriptions formed an important part of public life.

philosophy.zh: 南阿拉伯没有形成如希腊或印度式的系统哲学传统，其思想核心更体现为神庙伦理、王权合法性、祖先传统、部落荣誉和对水利与丰产的神圣秩序观。
philosophy.en: South Arabia did not develop a systematic philosophical tradition comparable to Greece or India; its intellectual outlook was instead expressed through temple ethics, royal legitimacy, ancestral custom, tribal honor, and a sacred conception of irrigation and fertility order.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古南阿拉伯辅音文字，常见于石刻、青铜铭文与建筑题记；行政和商业记录也可能书写于较易毁损材料上，但保存下来的以碑铭为主。
writingSystem.en: The civilization used the Ancient South Arabian consonantal script, commonly seen in stone inscriptions, bronze texts, and building dedications; administrative and commercial records were also likely written on perishable materials, though surviving evidence is mainly epigraphic.

culturalAchievements.zh: 代表性成就包括大型水利工程与梯田农业、精致石建筑与神庙群、长距离香料贸易网络、发达的铭文学传统，以及将沙漠商路、山地农业与海上贸易结合起来的区域文明模式。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included large-scale irrigation and terrace agriculture, refined stone architecture and temple complexes, long-distance aromatic trade networks, a rich epigraphic tradition, and a regional civilization that integrated desert caravan routes, upland agriculture, and maritime exchange.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系南闪米特分支，主要语言包括萨巴语、米奈语、卡塔班语、哈德拉毛语及早期希木叶尔相关方言
languageFamily.en: Semitic, South Semitic branch, with principal languages including Sabaic, Minaic, Qatabanic, Hadramitic, and early Himyaritic-related dialects

gdpDescription.zh: 南阿拉伯经济规模在当时属区域强国水平，但明显小于罗马与汉等超大型帝国。其财富集中于高价值低体积的乳香、没药和转口贸易，并由灌溉农业提供稳定粮食与税基。
gdpDescription.en: The South Arabian economy ranked as a strong regional economy in its time, though much smaller than the great empires such as Rome and Han. Its wealth was concentrated in high-value, low-bulk aromatics and transit trade, while irrigated agriculture provided a stable food base and tax foundation.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、梯田种植、乳香与没药采集和转运、商队贸易、港口中转、畜牧、织物与皮革、金属加工、石工建筑
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, terrace cultivation, collection and transport of frankincense and myrrh, caravan commerce, port entrepot trade, pastoralism, textiles and leatherwork, metalworking, and stone construction

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香膏、葡萄酒、枣、谷物、蜂蜜、皮革、织物、奴隶、马与骆驼，以及来自印度洋和地中海世界的珠宝、玻璃器、金属器与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, aromatics, wine, dates, grain, honey, leather, textiles, slaves, horses and camels, as well as jewelry, glassware, metal goods, and luxuries from the Indian Ocean and Mediterranean worlds

name.zh: 南阿拉伯银币与称量金属
name.en: South Arabian silver coinage and weighed bullion

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要表现为土地使用权、水权、牲畜、粮仓和家内工具；商旅与贵族家庭则持有金银、进口奢侈品、奴仆和商队股份。财富分布不均，贸易枢纽和灌溉核心区明显富于边缘地区。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was chiefly held in land-use rights, water shares, livestock, stored grain, and domestic tools; merchant and aristocratic households held precious metals, imported luxuries, slaves, and caravan stakes. Wealth was unevenly distributed, with trade hubs and irrigated core regions richer than peripheral zones.

economicSystem.zh: 经济为王权、部落与商人共同运作的混合体系。农业依赖公共水利与地方协作，贸易依赖私人商队和港口中介，国家通过关税、保护、工程与军事保障参与分配。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed system jointly shaped by kingship, tribes, and merchants. Agriculture depended on public waterworks and local cooperation, while trade relied on private caravans and port intermediaries; the state participated through customs, protection, infrastructure, and military security.

source.en: Agricultural taxes and irrigated land assessments
Fines, mineral income, and miscellaneous receipts

category.zh: 神庙、祭祀与合法性支出
外交馈赠、商路保护与储备
category.en: Diplomatic gifts, trade-route protection, and reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由银币、称量金属、谷物储备、香料库存与神庙财富共同构成。不同王国各自掌握财政，不存在统一的全南阿拉伯中央国库，因此此处为整体区域王权与神庙可动员财富的综合估计。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of silver coin, weighed bullion, grain reserves, aromatic stocks, and temple wealth. Since the region was divided among several kingdoms rather than governed by one unified central treasury, this figure is an aggregate estimate of mobilizable royal and temple resources across South Arabia.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守稳健，优先维护灌溉体系、商路安全和王权威望。丰年时积累储备，战事或大坝修缮时则动用金库、神庙资源与附庸贡赋。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy tended toward conservative stability, prioritizing irrigation maintenance, trade-route security, and royal prestige. Surpluses were accumulated in good years, while wars or major dam repairs drew on the treasury, temple resources, and dependent tribute.

description.zh: 由城镇守军、王廷卫队与征召步兵组成，装备矛、剑、匕首、盾牌和弓，负责守卫都城、神庙与主要灌溉设施。
description.en: Composed of urban garrisons, royal guards, and levied infantry, equipped with spears, swords, daggers, shields, and bows, and tasked with defending capitals, temples, and major irrigation works.

description.zh: 由各部落盟友按义务提供，适合山地作战、护送商队和边境巡逻，但忠诚度常首先指向本部族。
description.en: Supplied by allied tribes according to obligation, these troops were suited to upland warfare, caravan escort, and frontier patrol, though their loyalty often remained primarily with their own tribe.

description.zh: 包括少量骑兵与较实用的骆驼骑乘部队，用于沙漠侦察、快速传令、追击与商路控制。
description.en: Included a limited cavalry arm and more practical camel-mounted forces used for desert reconnaissance, rapid communication, pursuit, and route control.

description.zh: 驻守海港、沿海据点与船只停泊地，保护海上贸易并防范来自红海与阿拉伯海方向的袭扰。
description.en: Stationed at harbors, coastal strongpoints, and anchorages, they protected maritime trade and guarded against raids from the Red Sea and Arabian Sea.

commanderInChief.zh: 各王国之王及其受命将领
commanderInChief.en: The kings of the various kingdoms and their appointed generals

technology.zh: 军事技术总体属铁器时代后期水平，广泛使用铁制兵器、弓矛盾牌、堡寨工事和山口防御；海军力量有限，军事优势更多在地形熟悉、商路控制和快速部落动员。
technology.en: Military technology was generally that of the later Iron Age, with widespread use of iron weapons, bows, spears, shields, fortified positions, and pass defenses; naval power was limited, and military advantage lay more in terrain familiarity, route control, and rapid tribal mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南阿拉伯诸王国间竞争、部落叛离、对商路与港口的争夺，以及红海对岸阿克苏姆势力的逐步上升。贸易路线若转向海运，也会削弱内陆王国的战略地位。
threats.en: Principal threats came from rivalry among South Arabian kingdoms, tribal defections, competition for caravan routes and ports, and the gradual rise of Aksum across the Red Sea. A shift of trade toward maritime routes could also weaken the strategic position of inland kingdoms.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约一百万，主要集中于也门高地、灌溉绿洲、瓦迪河谷和沿海贸易城镇。人口分布高度依赖可灌溉土地与季风贸易节点，山地村落与绿洲聚居形态鲜明。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about one million, concentrated mainly in the Yemeni highlands, irrigated oases, wadi valleys, and coastal trading towns. Population distribution depended heavily on irrigable land and monsoon trade nodes, with distinctive upland villages and oasis settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、部落贵族、祭司与大商人；中层包括自由农民、地方首领、手工业者、商队组织者与书记官；下层包括佃作者、劳役民、依附部众与奴隶。社会流动有限，但贸易成功、军事功绩与王权赏赐可提升家族地位。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of royal families, tribal nobles, priests, and major merchants; the middle strata included free farmers, local notables, artisans, caravan organizers, and scribes; the lower strata included tenant cultivators, corvée laborers, dependent clanspeople, and slaves. Social mobility was limited, but commercial success, military service, and royal favor could elevate a family’s standing.

allies.zh: 无稳定统一的整体盟友体系；各王国通常与本地部落、海岸港口势力及部分阿拉伯内陆集团维持临时联盟，并与印度洋商人网络保持务实合作。
allies.en: There was no single stable alliance system for all South Arabia; individual kingdoms typically maintained temporary alliances with local tribes, coastal port powers, and some inland Arabian groups, while sustaining pragmatic cooperation with Indian Ocean merchant networks.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是其他南阿拉伯竞争王国、反叛部落，以及在红海贸易上潜在挑战其地位的阿克苏姆势力；在更广范围内，也面临罗马—埃及海运增长对传统商队贸易的经济竞争。
enemies.en: The main adversaries were rival South Arabian kingdoms, rebellious tribes, and the rising Aksumite power that could challenge them in Red Sea trade; on a broader scale, they also faced economic competition from growing Roman-Egyptian maritime commerce that threatened traditional caravan trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以控制香料贸易、确保商路安全、维持部落附庸关系和争夺港口中转为核心。南阿拉伯诸国对外政策通常务实灵活，倾向通过婚盟、贡纳、关税协定与有限战争维护均势。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling the aromatic trade, securing routes, preserving tribal dependency, and competing for port entrepot access. South Arabian states typically pursued pragmatic and flexible diplomacy, using marriage alliances, tribute arrangements, customs agreements, and limited warfare to maintain balance.

era.zh: 铁器时代后期／古典时代印度洋贸易期
era.en: Late Iron Age / Classical Indian Ocean trade era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型堤坝与配水系统、山地梯田农业、石刻铭文行政传统、香料储运技术、沙漠商队组织与季风海运网络衔接，是其最具代表性的技术与制度成就。
keyInnovations.en: Large dams and water-distribution systems, mountain terrace agriculture, an inscription-based administrative tradition, aromatic storage and transport methods, and the integration of desert caravan organization with monsoon seaborne networks were among its most distinctive technical and institutional achievements.

strengths.zh: 优势在于高价值香料贸易、成熟水利农业、连接红海与印度洋的地理位置、经验丰富的商队网络，以及能把部落社会与王权行政结合起来的治理能力。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in high-value aromatic trade, mature irrigation agriculture, a strategic position linking the Red Sea and Indian Ocean, experienced caravan networks, and an ability to combine tribal society with royal administration.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、王国间内耗、对灌溉系统和气候条件的高度依赖、军事整合能力有限，以及海运兴起对传统陆路贸易的结构性冲击。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included political fragmentation, rivalry among kingdoms, heavy dependence on irrigation systems and climatic conditions, limited military integration, and the structural challenge posed by the rise of maritime trade over traditional overland routes.

outlook.zh: 在公元100年前后，南阿拉伯整体仍保持稳定与富庶，但其长期前景将取决于谁能整合高地与港口、控制红海与印度洋贸易接口，并适应海运增长带来的商业重心转移。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE, South Arabia remained broadly stable and prosperous, but its long-term future would depend on which power could unite the highlands and ports, control the Red Sea–Indian Ocean interface, and adapt to the commercial shift brought by expanding maritime trade.

description.zh: 也门高地与南阿拉伯沿海由多个王国分治，依托乳香贸易与灌溉农业繁荣发展。
description.en: The Yemeni highlands and South Arabian coast were divided among several kingdoms prospering through incense trade and irrigation agriculture.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为中心的区域性贸易王国，结合王室直辖、地方贵族与附属港口网络
governmentForm.en: A monarch-centered regional trading kingdom combining direct royal authority, local nobles, and a network of subordinate ports

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族居于顶层，其下为祭司、地方首领、商人、工匠与农牧民，另有依附劳工与奴隶。高地农业人口构成王国核心，红海沿岸商人阶层因海上贸易而日益重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal house and nobility, followed by priests, local chiefs, merchants, artisans, and agro-pastoral commoners, with dependent laborers and slaves below. The highland farming population formed the core of the kingdom, while Red Sea merchant groups grew increasingly important through maritime trade.

rulingClass.zh: 阿克苏姆王室、高地贵族家族、神庙与地方部族首领
rulingClass.en: The Aksumite royal house, highland noble families, temple elites, and local tribal chiefs

succession.zh: 王位大体实行王室世袭，但早期继承规则并未完全固定，通常依赖王族血统、贵族支持与军事控制的结合
succession.en: Succession was broadly dynastic within the royal house, though early rules were not fully fixed and usually depended on a combination of royal lineage, noble backing, and military control

structure.zh: 阿克苏姆在公元100年前后尚未形成后世高度文书化的帝国官僚制，而是由国王、王廷、地方贵族、港口监督者与贡赋网络共同构成。王权集中于都城及其周边高地，对沿海贸易节点和内陆附庸地区实施间接控制。
structure.en: Around 100 CE, Aksum had not yet developed the highly formalized imperial bureaucracy of later centuries; instead, governance rested on the king, court, regional nobles, port supervisors, and tribute networks. Royal authority was strongest in the capital and surrounding highlands, while coastal trade nodes and inland dependencies were often ruled indirectly.

function.zh: 负责宫廷礼仪、王令传达、使节接待与对贵族关系的协调
function.en: Handled court ceremony, transmission of royal commands, reception of envoys, and coordination with nobles

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、象牙、金属与港口税收，并管理王家仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, ivory, metal dues, and port taxes, and managed royal storage and redistribution

function.zh: 监督阿杜利斯等贸易据点、征收关税、管理商旅通行并维护与阿拉伯及印度洋商人的关系
function.en: Supervised trading stations such as Adulis, collected customs, regulated merchant traffic, and maintained relations with Arabian and Indian Ocean traders

function.zh: 维系高地与边缘地区首领的效忠，协调贡纳、征发与司法仲裁
function.en: Maintained the loyalty of highland and frontier chiefs, coordinating tribute, levies, and judicial arbitration

function.zh: 组织王室亲兵、征召地方武装、储备武器并保护商路与王都
function.en: Organized the royal guard, mobilized regional forces, stored weapons, and protected trade routes and the capital

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依靠受王室承认的部族首领、地方贵族和港口监督官。高地核心区受控较紧，边缘地区以纳贡、结盟和人质制度维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied chiefly on tribal leaders, regional nobles, and port overseers recognized by the crown. The highland core was more tightly controlled, while peripheral zones were held through tribute, alliance, and hostage arrangements.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断和部族仲裁为基础，涉及土地、牲畜、婚姻、血仇与贸易纠纷。港口贸易事务较多通过王室官员和商人习惯进行裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on customary law, royal judgment, and tribal arbitration, covering land, livestock, marriage, blood-feud, and trade disputes. Port commerce was often adjudicated through royal officials and merchant custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自谷物与牲畜贡赋、港口关税、商旅通行税、战利品、附属地区贡纳及部分矿产和手工业征收。税制并非完全统一，而因地区与交通节点而异。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from grain and livestock tribute, port customs, caravan tolls, war booty, tribute from dependencies, and some levies on mining and crafts. Taxation was not fully standardized and varied by region and trade node.

religion.zh: 阿克苏姆在此时仍以本地多神信仰为主，崇拜天神、祖先与具有王权合法性的神灵，并与南阿拉伯宗教传统存在接触。祭祀与王权紧密相连。
religion.en: At this time Aksum remained predominantly polytheistic, venerating sky deities, ancestors, and gods tied to royal legitimacy, while also showing contact with South Arabian religious traditions. Cult and kingship were closely linked.

philosophy.zh: 阿克苏姆没有形成类似希腊或中国式成体系哲学学派，其思想核心体现为王权神圣性、祖先传统、部族义务、商业务实精神与跨海交流带来的文化适应性。
philosophy.en: Aksum did not possess a formalized philosophical school comparable to Greek or Chinese traditions; its intellectual outlook centered instead on sacred kingship, ancestral custom, tribal obligation, mercantile pragmatism, and cultural adaptability shaped by overseas exchange.

writingSystem.zh: 书写主要使用早期古埃塞俄比亚文字传统，并受到南阿拉伯字母影响；在王权、贸易标记和铭文领域开始扩大使用，但识字仍局限于少数精英与书吏。
writingSystem.en: Writing drew on early Epigraphic Ethiopian traditions influenced by South Arabian scripts; its use was expanding in rulership, trade marking, and inscriptions, though literacy remained confined to a limited elite and scribal circle.

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿克苏姆在高地石建筑、王室纪念文化、跨海商贸组织和多区域文化融合方面成就突出。其社会已具备后来建造大型石碑与形成独特高地文明传统的基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Aksum excelled in highland stone construction, royal commemorative culture, overseas trade organization, and multi-regional cultural synthesis. Its society had already laid the foundations for the later erection of monumental stelae and the emergence of a distinctive highland civilization.

languageFamily.zh: 阿非罗-亚细亚语系，主要为闪米特语支的早期埃塞俄比亚语群，并与库施特语族群长期共存互动
languageFamily.en: Afroasiatic, primarily early Ethiopian Semitic speech forms, interacting over time with Cushitic-speaking populations

gdpDescription.zh: 阿克苏姆经济在非洲东北部属于高度活跃的区域性贸易经济体，依托高地产粮区、牧业、象牙与奴隶贸易，以及通往红海和印度洋的转口商业。按人均水平虽不及罗马核心地区，但在撒哈拉以南和红海西岸已极具竞争力。
gdpDescription.en: The Aksumite economy was one of the most dynamic regional trade economies in northeastern Africa, supported by highland agriculture, pastoralism, ivory and slave trading, and entrepôt commerce linking the Red Sea and Indian Ocean. Though below the per-capita levels of the Roman core, it was highly competitive for sub-Saharan and western Red Sea standards.

mainIndustries.zh: 高地农业、畜牧业、象牙采集与加工、黄金与铁资源开采、陶器与金属手工业、驮运贸易、港口转口商业
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, pastoralism, ivory collection and processing, gold and iron extraction, pottery and metal crafts, caravan transport, and port-based entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口象牙、龟甲、犀角、黄金、奴隶、皮革、乳香与农牧产品；进口纺织品、酒、金属器、玻璃器、珠饰、奢侈陶器及来自印度洋世界的香料与精制商品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included ivory, tortoiseshell, rhinoceros horn, gold, slaves, hides, incense, and agro-pastoral products; imports included textiles, wine, metalware, glassware, beads, fine ceramics, and spices and luxury goods from the Indian Ocean world

name.zh: 称量金属与外来钱币并用体系
name.en: Mixed bullion-weight and foreign coinage system

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异较大。高地普通农牧家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜与谷物储备；港口商人和王室亲近贵族则拥有更高的贵金属、进口奢侈品与奴仆数量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied widely. For ordinary highland agro-pastoral families it consisted mainly of land-use rights, livestock, and grain stores; port merchants and nobles close to the crown held much greater stocks of precious metal, imported luxuries, and dependents.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯等值/年
罗马第纳里乌斯等值/年

economicSystem.zh: 以王室主导的贡赋经济与商贸经济并存，国家通过港口关税、附庸贡纳和贵重商品控制介入市场，但民间商人与地方首领在实际流通中仍占重要地位。
economicSystem.en: A royal tribute economy coexisted with a trade economy; the state intervened through port customs, dependency tribute, and control of prestige goods, but private merchants and local chiefs remained important in actual exchange.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯等值/年
罗马第纳里乌斯等值/年
罗马第纳里乌斯等值/年

source.zh: 商路通行费与市场征收
矿产、战利品与杂项收入

category.zh: 地方酋长安抚与外交馈赠
category.en: Subsidies to chiefs and diplomatic gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备以谷物、牲畜、象牙、金属与进口奢侈品混合构成，并非纯粹钱币财政。贵金属储藏主要用于外交、赏赐、贸易支付与危机时期的动员。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury consisted of mixed stores of grain, livestock, ivory, metals, and imported luxury goods rather than a purely monetized reserve. Precious metal holdings were especially important for diplomacy, patronage, trade payments, and emergency mobilization.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健与再分配，依靠贸易繁荣积累盈余，同时通过王室赏赐和粮仓供给维持贵族、军队和地方网络的忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative and redistributive, using commercial prosperity to build surpluses while maintaining the loyalty of nobles, troops, and local networks through royal gifts and granary support.

description.zh: 直属国王的精锐部队，负责宫廷护卫、战时核心突击与政治威慑
description.en: Elite troops directly attached to the king, responsible for palace security, battlefield shock action, and political intimidation

description.zh: 由高地农牧民和地方附庸征召组成，是战争中的主要兵力，擅长山地行动与短期集结
description.en: Drawn from highland agro-pastoralists and local dependents, these formed the main wartime force and were suited for mountain operations and short-term mobilization

description.zh: 由附庸首领率领的辅助武装，用于边境巡逻、护送商队和惩戒叛乱地区
description.en: Auxiliary troops led by dependent chiefs, used for frontier patrols, caravan escort, and punishment of rebellious districts

description.zh: 驻守港口、河谷通道与贸易枢纽的小型驻军，保护税收与物资流通
description.en: Small garrisons stationed at ports, valley corridors, and trade hubs to protect taxation and the flow of goods

technology.zh: 军备以铁制矛、剑、匕首、盾牌、弓箭和皮甲为主，依赖山地行军、地方熟路与商路据点控制。海军力量尚有限，更多体现为港口守备和商船保护能力，而非远洋作战舰队。
technology.en: Armament centered on iron spears, swords, daggers, shields, bows, and leather armor, with effectiveness relying on mountain mobility, local knowledge, and control of trade nodes. Naval power remained limited, expressed more in port defense and merchant protection than in a true blue-water war fleet.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯等值/年

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与库施地区的竞争、红海沿岸港口控制权争夺、南阿拉伯势力变化，以及内陆边缘部族可能发生的反抗。罗马与帕提亚并非直接军事敌人，但其海贸格局会间接影响阿克苏姆安全。
threats.en: Primary threats came from competition with Kushite regions, struggles over Red Sea coastal control, shifts among South Arabian powers, and possible resistance from inland frontier tribes. Rome and Parthia were not direct military enemies, but their maritime trade patterns indirectly affected Aksumite security.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于提格雷高地及其周边肥沃地带，少部分分布于沿海港口、商路节点和边缘附属地区。人口结构以农牧民为主，城市居民占比较低但增长明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Tigrayan highlands and nearby fertile zones, with smaller numbers in coastal ports, trade-route nodes, and peripheral dependencies. The population was predominantly agro-pastoral, while the urban share remained modest but was growing.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室、贵族、祭司、地方首领、商人、工匠、农民、牧民、依附劳工与奴隶。社会流动有限，但成功商人、军功首领和受王室恩宠者可获得更高地位。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the royal family, nobles, priests, local chiefs, merchants, artisans, peasants, pastoralists, dependent laborers, and slaves. Social mobility was limited, though successful merchants, military leaders, and those favored by the crown could rise in status.

allies.zh: 阿克苏姆没有稳定意义上的正式盟友体系，但与部分南阿拉伯港口势力、红海商人群体以及内陆附庸首领保持互利关系。
allies.en: Aksum lacked a permanent formal alliance system, but maintained mutually beneficial relations with certain South Arabian port powers, Red Sea merchant communities, and inland dependent chiefs.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是争夺尼罗河上游与红海贸易影响力的库施势力，以及可能与之竞争港口税源的南阿拉伯和区域性海贸势力。
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were Kushite forces competing for influence over the Upper Nile and Red Sea trade, along with South Arabian and regional maritime powers contesting port revenues.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制贸易通道、扩大红海存在、争取阿拉伯对岸合作并稳固高地核心为主。阿克苏姆倾向通过商业渗透、附庸网络、外交馈赠和有限武力实现扩张，而非进行持续性的大规模征服。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling trade corridors, expanding presence in the Red Sea, securing cooperation across the Arabian shore, and consolidating the highland core. Aksum preferred expansion through commercial penetration, dependency networks, diplomatic gifts, and limited force rather than sustained large-scale conquest.

era.zh: 铁器时代后期与古典时代红海贸易时代
era.en: Late Iron Age and Classical Red Sea trade era

keyInnovations.zh: 梯田与高地农业改良、石建筑与纪念性王室构造、铁器冶炼与兵器制作、长距离驮运组织、季风海贸知识吸收、跨文化商贸管理能力
keyInnovations.en: Terracing and highland agricultural improvement, stone construction and monumental royal building, iron smelting and weapon production, long-distance caravan organization, adoption of monsoon-based maritime knowledge, and cross-cultural commercial management

strengths.zh: 阿克苏姆的优势在于高地农业基础稳固、王室能整合多族群与多贸易节点、地处红海与印度洋贸易枢纽、并拥有象牙和贵重物产等高价值出口品。
strengths.en: Aksum’s strengths lay in its solid highland agricultural base, royal ability to integrate multiple ethnic groups and trade nodes, strategic position on Red Sea and Indian Ocean routes, and access to high-value exports such as ivory and precious products.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括官僚制度仍较初级、对地方首领忠诚依赖较大、沿海与高地之间协调成本高、经济对外贸波动敏感，且军队尚不足以进行远距离持续征服。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included a still relatively undeveloped bureaucracy, heavy dependence on the loyalty of local chiefs, high coordination costs between coast and highlands, sensitivity to fluctuations in foreign trade, and armed forces not yet suited for sustained long-range conquest.

outlook.zh: 在公元100年前后，阿克苏姆前景极为乐观。只要继续掌握阿杜利斯等港口与内陆象牙、黄金来源，并成功吸纳红海商贸收益，王国有望由区域强国进一步成长为东北非和红海世界的重要帝国。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE, Aksum’s outlook was highly favorable. If it continued to control ports such as Adulis and the inland sources of ivory and gold, while successfully capturing Red Sea commercial revenues, the kingdom was well positioned to grow from a regional power into a major empire of northeastern Africa and the Red Sea world.

description.zh: 阿克苏姆在红海贸易中崛起，连接非洲之角、阿拉伯与印度洋世界。
description.en: Aksum was rising through Red Sea commerce, linking the Horn of Africa with Arabia and the Indian Ocean world.

governmentForm.zh: 受罗马庇护的君主制城邦王国，结合希腊化宫廷、地方城市自治与草原贵族联盟政治
governmentForm.en: A Roman-client monarchical city-state kingdom combining a Hellenistic court, local urban self-government, and steppe aristocratic alliance politics

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、希腊化城市贵族、萨尔马提亚与辛多-迈奥提亚骑士贵族、商人、手工业者、农民、渔民、牧民与奴隶构成；沿海城镇偏向希腊城市生活，内陆则保持部族与骑马贵族传统
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, Hellenized urban elites, Sarmatian and Sindo-Maeotian mounted nobles, merchants, artisans, farmers, fishers, pastoralists, and slaves; coastal towns favored Greek urban life while the interior retained tribal and equestrian aristocratic traditions

rulingClass.zh: 提比略-尤利乌斯王室与希腊城邦贵族共同执政，并依赖萨尔马提亚骑兵贵族提供军事支持
rulingClass.en: The Tiberian-Julian royal house ruled together with Greek civic aristocracies while relying on Sarmatian cavalry nobles for military backing

succession.zh: 王位原则上为王朝世袭，通常由王族男性继承，但需获得宫廷贵族、主要城市精英及罗马认可
succession.en: The throne was in principle dynastic and hereditary, usually passing to a male member of the royal house, but effective succession required acceptance by court nobles, major city elites, and Rome

structure.zh: 国王居于中央，宫廷负责财政、外交、军务与王室土地管理；各希腊城市保留议事机构、地方法官与部分税务执行权；边缘地区通过附庸首领、堡垒指挥官和税吏间接统治
structure.en: The king stood at the center; the court handled finance, diplomacy, military affairs, and royal estates; Greek cities retained councils, local magistrates, and some tax-collection powers; frontier zones were governed indirectly through subordinate chiefs, fortress commanders, and tax officials

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存诏书、处理王室文书与对外通信，使用希腊语行政书写
function.en: Drafted decrees, preserved charters, managed royal documentation, and handled foreign correspondence using Greek as the administrative written language

function.zh: 征收关税、港口税、土地与贡赋，管理铸币、王室仓储和盐渔收益
function.en: Collected customs, port dues, land taxes, and tribute; supervised coinage, royal storage, and revenues from salt and fisheries

function.zh: 统筹王室卫队、骑兵贵族动员、要塞补给、边境联络与罗马军事协同
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, mobilization of cavalry nobles, fortress supply, frontier liaison, and military cooperation with Rome

function.zh: 管理黑海港口、航运税、粮食集散、渔业监管与对外商队接待
function.en: Managed Black Sea ports, shipping dues, grain movement, fishery regulation, and reception of foreign merchants

name.zh: 王室地产与附庸事务署
name.en: Royal Estates and Tributary Affairs Office

function.zh: 监督王室土地、谷仓、牧场与附属部族贡纳，调解地方酋长关系
function.en: Oversaw royal lands, granaries, pastures, and tributary obligations of subordinate tribes, and mediated relations with local chieftains

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政呈双重结构：潘提卡派翁、法纳戈里亚等城镇由议会、执政官与税务官管理；亚速海周边与草原边缘则由受封首领、要塞官与王室代理人控制
localAdmin.en: Local administration was dual in structure: towns such as Panticapaeum and Phanagoria were run by councils, magistrates, and tax officers, while the Sea of Azov zone and steppe margins were controlled by invested chiefs, fortress officers, and royal agents

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以希腊城邦法律传统、王室敕令、习惯法及商业契约实践为基础；城市内部偏重书面诉讼，部族地区则更多依赖调解、赔偿与地位秩序
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on Greek civic legal traditions, royal decrees, customary law, and mercantile contract practice; towns favored written litigation, while tribal districts relied more on mediation, compensation, and status hierarchy

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自港口关税、航运税、市场税、土地租税、附庸贡赋、渔盐收入与铸币收益；对城市商贸征税较规范，对部族地区则多以实物和马匹贡纳形式征收
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came chiefly from port customs, shipping dues, market taxes, land rents, tributary payments, fisheries and salt revenues, and mint profits; urban commerce was taxed more systematically, while tribal districts often paid in kind and horses

religion.zh: 宗教高度混合化，希腊诸神崇拜、王室祭祀、地方黑海与迈奥提亚神灵、祖先崇拜以及部分伊朗草原宗教观念并存；统治者常通过神庙捐献与公共祭礼巩固合法性
religion.en: Religion was highly syncretic, combining worship of Greek gods, royal cult observances, local Black Sea and Maeotian deities, ancestor veneration, and some Iranian steppe religious ideas; rulers reinforced legitimacy through temple patronage and public sacrifice

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以希腊化实用伦理、修辞教育、城邦公共德性与王权忠诚为主，并非哲学创新中心，但受希腊文学和罗马政治文化持续影响
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Hellenistic practical ethics, rhetorical education, civic virtue, and loyalty to kingship; it was not a major center of philosophical innovation but remained strongly shaped by Greek literature and Roman political culture

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为希腊字母，行政、铭文、商业契约与王室称号皆多用希腊语书写；部族语言多保留口头传统
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the Greek alphabet; administration, inscriptions, commercial contracts, and royal titulature were mostly written in Greek, while tribal languages remained primarily oral

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就在于将希腊城市建筑、铸币艺术、墓葬壁画、黑海贸易文化与萨尔马提亚骑士象征体系融合，形成独特的边疆希腊化风格
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievement lay in blending Greek urban architecture, coin art, funerary painting, Black Sea mercantile culture, and Sarmatian equestrian symbolism into a distinctive frontier Hellenistic style

languageFamily.zh: 行政与城市文化语言属印欧语系希腊语支；周边精英与草原贵族也使用伊朗语族萨尔马提亚相关语言及本地部族语言
