languageFamily.en: The language of administration and urban culture belonged to the Indo-European Greek branch; surrounding elites and steppe nobles also used Iranian Sarmatian-related languages and local tribal tongues

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在黑海北岸属中等偏强，远小于罗马和帕提亚等大帝国，但凭借谷物出口、渔业、港口税与转口贸易维持稳定繁荣
gdpDescription.en: Its economy was middling but relatively strong for the north Black Sea, far smaller than Rome or Parthia, yet sustained stable prosperity through grain exports, fisheries, port dues, and transit trade

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物生产、葡萄酒与园艺、渔业、盐业、畜牧、陶器、金属加工、船运、铸币与区域转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain production, viticulture and horticulture, fisheries, salt production, pastoralism, pottery, metalworking, shipping, minting, and regional transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口谷物、咸鱼、鱼酱、皮革、奴隶、牲畜与部分手工制品；进口葡萄酒、橄榄油、精制陶器、金属器、奢侈品、织物和罗马制造品
tradeGoods.en: It exported grain, salted fish, fish sauce, hides, slaves, livestock, and some manufactured goods; it imported wine, olive oil, fine pottery, metalware, luxury goods, textiles, and Roman manufactures

householdWealth.zh: 城市精英拥有地产、仓储、船只与税务承包权，生活相对富裕；普通家庭财富多体现为小块土地、牲畜、渔具或手工业工具，受歉收和边境动荡影响较大
householdWealth.en: Urban elites held estates, storage facilities, ships, and tax-farming rights and lived comparatively well; ordinary households possessed small plots, animals, fishing gear, or craft tools, with wealth vulnerable to poor harvests and frontier unrest

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为王室监管下的混合市场经济：城市商业和海运依赖私人与行会活动，关键港口、铸币、关税和部分土地收益则由王室直接控制
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed market economy under royal oversight: urban commerce and shipping depended on private and guild-like activity, while key ports, minting, customs, and some land revenues were directly controlled by the crown

source.zh: 土地税与王室地产收益
source.en: Land taxes and royal estate income

category.zh: 宗教祭祀、外交赠礼与公共工程
category.en: Religious rites, diplomatic gifts, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模不算庞大，但足以应付常年边防、港口维护与宫廷运作；财政稳健高度依赖黑海贸易和粮食顺产
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not enormous but sufficient for routine frontier defense, port maintenance, and court operations; fiscal stability depended heavily on Black Sea trade and successful harvests

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持港口流通、避免对城市精英过度征敛，并以温和盈余储备应对边境袭扰和贸易中断
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, prioritizing port traffic, avoiding excessive exactions on urban elites, and maintaining modest surpluses to withstand raids and trade disruptions

name.zh: 王室骑兵与萨尔马提亚贵族骑队
name.en: Royal Cavalry and Sarmatian Noble Horse

description.zh: 军队核心，为重装或中装骑兵，依赖贵族随从、长枪冲击与骑射能力，适合边境机动作战和威慑附庸部族
description.en: The core of the army, consisting of heavy or medium cavalry relying on noble retinues, lance charges, and horse-archery, well suited to frontier mobility and coercing tributary tribes

description.zh: 由城镇居民、雇佣兵和驻防士兵组成，负责守城、港口治安、城墙和粮仓防护，装备长矛、盾牌、弓与短剑
description.en: Composed of townsmen, mercenaries, and stationed troops, they guarded cities, ports, walls, and granaries, armed with spears, shields, bows, and short swords

name.zh: 附庸部族轻骑与侦察队
name.en: Tributary Tribal Light Cavalry and Scouts

description.zh: 主要用于巡边、侦察、袭扰和通信，战斗力不如王室主力稳定，但在草原和河口地带极具机动优势
description.en: Used chiefly for patrol, reconnaissance, raiding, and communications; less reliable than the royal core but highly mobile in steppe and estuarine terrain

commanderInChief.zh: 萨乌罗马特斯一世国王

technology.zh: 军事技术融合希腊化城防、黑海航运经验与萨尔马提亚骑兵战法，装备包括复合弓、长枪、鳞甲、头盔、城墙与瞭望设施，但缺乏大帝国级攻城能力
technology.en: Military technology blended Hellenistic fortification, Black Sea maritime experience, and Sarmatian cavalry methods, using composite bows, lances, scale armor, helmets, walls, and watch installations, but lacking great-imperial siege capacity

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自草原萨尔马提亚诸部、区域海盗、附庸不稳及黑海贸易受阻；其安全在大战略上依赖与罗马的友好关系
threats.en: Main threats came from steppe Sarmatian groups, regional piracy, unstable tributaries, and disruption of Black Sea commerce; at the grand-strategic level its security depended on good relations with Rome

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于刻赤海峡两岸、克里米亚东部与亚速海周边港口及农业聚落，兼具希腊城市居民、混合化地方社群与草原部族人口
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across both sides of the Kerch Strait, eastern Crimea, and port and farming settlements around the Sea of Azov, including Greek urban dwellers, mixed local communities, and steppe tribal populations

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、城邦议员和大商人；中层为地主、小商人、船主、退役军人与熟练工匠；下层为佃农、雇工、渔民、牧民和奴隶。族群与身份差异明显，但长期交流促成较强混合化
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal house, nobles, civic councilors, and major merchants; the middle stratum included landholders, petty traders, shipowners, veterans, and skilled artisans; the lower stratum comprised tenants, laborers, fishers, herders, and slaves. Ethnic and status distinctions remained marked, yet long interaction produced substantial cultural mixing

allies.zh: 最重要的外部依靠是罗马帝国；同时与部分周边部族和黑海港市维持务实联盟或宗主关系
allies.en: Its most important external support was the Roman Empire; it also maintained pragmatic alliances or suzerain ties with some neighboring tribes and Black Sea ports

enemies.zh: 无长期固定大敌，但与敌对萨尔马提亚部族、海盗势力及偶发叛服的边境群体存在持续摩擦
enemies.en: It had no single permanent great enemy, but faced recurring friction with hostile Sarmatian tribes, pirate groups, and occasionally rebellious frontier communities

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以在罗马保护下维持自治为核心，通过贡礼、婚姻、封赏与贸易网络稳定草原边缘，并避免卷入帝国间大规模冲突
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving autonomy under Roman protection, stabilizing the steppe edge through gifts, marriage, patronage, and trade networks, and avoiding entanglement in large-scale imperial wars

era.zh: 双帝国时代
era.en: Twin Empires

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非原创突破，而是成熟技术的边疆整合：希腊式港口与城防、区域铸币、黑海航海、谷仓储运、复合弓骑兵战术及跨文化商业会计
keyInnovations.en: Its key innovations were less original inventions than frontier integrations of mature techniques: Greek-style ports and fortifications, regional coinage, Black Sea navigation, granary logistics, composite-bow cavalry tactics, and cross-cultural commercial accounting

strengths.zh: 其优势在于地处刻赤海峡交通枢纽，兼有农业腹地、港口税收、希腊城市传统和萨尔马提亚骑兵资源，并受罗马承认与庇护
strengths.en: Its strengths were its position at the Kerch Strait crossroads, its agricultural hinterland, port revenues, Greek urban traditions, Sarmatian cavalry resources, and recognition and protection from Rome

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口与财政规模有限，对贸易航线与丰收依赖很深，且政治平衡需同时安抚城市精英、王室亲信、部族贵族与罗马利益
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited population and fiscal scale, heavy dependence on trade routes and good harvests, and a political balance that had to satisfy urban elites, royal retainers, tribal nobles, and Roman interests at once

outlook.zh: 到100年时王国总体稳定，若黑海贸易保持畅通且王位继承平顺，仍可继续作为罗马北黑海前沿的可靠缓冲国；但一旦草原压力上升或海运衰退，其脆弱性将迅速显现
outlook.en: By 100 CE the kingdom was broadly stable; if Black Sea trade remained open and succession stayed smooth, it could continue as a reliable buffer on Rome's north Black Sea frontier, but rising steppe pressure or maritime decline would quickly expose its fragility

description.zh: 博斯普鲁斯王国是黑海北岸的罗马附庸，融合希腊城市与草原贵族传统。
description.en: The Bosporan Kingdom was a Roman client on the north Black Sea, blending Greek urban life with steppe aristocratic traditions.

governmentForm.zh: 以神圣君权为核心的中央集权王国，王权与神庙体系紧密结合
governmentForm.en: A centralized sacral monarchy in which royal authority was closely linked to the temple system

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、祭司集团、地方官员与军官、城市工匠与商人、农民牧民以及奴婢和战俘构成；尼罗河谷定居农业与草原畜牧社会并存
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal family and nobility, priestly groups, local officials and officers, urban artisans and merchants, peasants and pastoralists, and dependents including slaves and captives; settled Nile agriculture coexisted with savanna pastoral life

rulingClass.zh: 麦罗埃王室、地方贵族、高级祭司与宫廷军事官僚
rulingClass.en: The Meroitic royal house, provincial nobles, senior priests, and court military-bureaucratic elites

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室内部世袭，继承受宫廷共识、王族血统与宗教合法性共同影响，王后与王太后常具重要政治地位
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal house, with succession shaped by court consensus, royal lineage, and religious legitimacy; queens and queen mothers often held major political influence

structure.zh: 政权以国王为中心，依托宫廷、王室亲族、神庙机构及地方总督治理上尼罗河流域与附属边疆；国家控制主要灌溉区、贸易通道与铁冶中心
structure.en: The state was centered on the king and governed the Upper Nile heartland and dependent frontiers through the court, royal kin, temple institutions, and provincial governors; it controlled major cultivation zones, trade routes, and iron-producing centers

function.zh: 管理宫廷仪式、诏令传达、王室地产与高级行政协调
function.en: Managed court ritual, transmission of royal decrees, royal estates, and high-level administrative coordination

function.zh: 监督主要神庙、祭司网络、宗教土地、贡奉和国家祭祀
function.en: Supervised major temples, priestly networks, religious lands, offerings, and state cult ceremonies

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工业品和边贸税，管理仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, manufactured goods, and frontier trade taxes, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 协调诺姆式地方单位、沙漠道路要塞、南部附属部落与边境关系
function.en: Coordinated district-like local units, desert-route forts, southern dependent tribes, and border relations

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、弓箭手、驻军、征召与边防补给
function.en: Organized the royal guard, archers, garrisons, levies, and frontier supply

function.zh: 管理王家作坊、铁冶生产、武器制造与大型建筑工程
function.en: Managed royal workshops, iron production, weapon manufacture, and major building projects

function.zh: 监管尼罗河运输、红海与沙漠商路、象牙金矿和奢侈品流通
function.en: Oversaw Nile transport, Red Sea and desert routes, ivory and gold zones, and luxury-goods circulation

function.zh: 处理地产纠纷、税务案件、刑罚裁断与麦罗埃文和埃及文书记录
function.en: Handled property disputes, tax cases, penal judgments, and records in Meroitic and Egyptian scripts

localAdmin.zh: 地方由王室任命或认可的总督、要塞长官、税吏与神庙代理人共同治理；核心尼罗河地带控制较强，沙漠边缘与南方属地更多依赖附庸关系与驻军监督
localAdmin.en: Local administration was exercised by royally appointed or recognized governors, fort commanders, tax officials, and temple agents; control was strongest in the Nile core, while desert margins and southern dependencies relied more on tributary relations and garrison oversight

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、宗教规范和地方裁判结合而成；地产、灌溉、贡赋、婚姻和商贸争议多由地方官与祭司参与裁断，重大案件上达宫廷
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, customary law, religious norms, and local adjudication; disputes over land, irrigation, tribute, marriage, and trade were often judged by local officials and priests, with major cases referred to the court

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要以实物形式征收，包括谷物、牲畜、金属、手工业品和劳役；边境贸易、商队通行、矿产和附属部族贡纳亦为重要财政来源
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected mainly in kind, including grain, livestock, metals, craft goods, and labor service; frontier trade, caravan transit, mining, and tribute from dependent peoples were also important fiscal sources

religion.zh: 宗教以阿蒙崇拜为核心，同时尊奉伊西斯、阿佩德马克、奥西里斯等神祇；王权被视为神圣秩序的维护者，神庙是经济、政治与文化中心
religion.en: Religion was centered on the cult of Amun while also venerating deities such as Isis, Apedemak, and Osiris; kingship was seen as guardian of sacred order, and temples served as economic, political, and cultural centers

philosophy.zh: 库施并无如希腊或汉地那样成体系的哲学学派，其思想核心体现在王权合法性、祖先传统、神意、祭祀秩序以及尼罗河农业社会的伦理实践中
philosophy.en: Kush did not possess formal philosophical schools like those of Greece or Han China; its intellectual framework centered on royal legitimacy, ancestral tradition, divine will, ritual order, and the ethical practices of a Nile agrarian society

writingSystem.zh: 使用麦罗埃文字，包括表音草书体与纪念碑体，并继续吸收埃及象形传统用于王室与宗教铭文
writingSystem.en: The civilization used the Meroitic script, including cursive and monumental forms, while continuing to draw on Egyptian hieroglyphic traditions for royal and religious inscriptions

culturalAchievements.zh: 麦罗埃时期以金字塔陵墓、砂岩神庙、王室浮雕、阿佩德马克图像、冶铁工艺和独特的麦罗埃书写文化著称；纳塔卡马尼与阿马尼托雷时期尤其留下大量建筑与铭刻
culturalAchievements.en: The Meroitic period was noted for pyramid tombs, sandstone temples, royal reliefs, depictions of Apedemak, ironworking, and a distinctive written culture; the reign of Natakamani and Amanitore in particular left extensive architecture and inscriptions

languageFamily.zh: 主要宫廷语言为麦罗埃语，通常归入东北非语言环境中的尼罗-撒哈拉相关谱系，行政和宗教中亦持续使用埃及传统书写影响
languageFamily.en: The principal court language was Meroitic, generally associated with the northeastern African linguistic environment and often linked to a Nilo-Saharan-related sphere, while administrative and religious practice continued to reflect Egyptian scribal influence

gdpDescription.zh: 库施经济在公元100年前后处于繁荣期，以尼罗河农业、畜牧、黄金与铁冶、象牙和边境转口贸易为基础。其总体规模远不及罗马或汉帝国，但在东北非属于高水平区域强国经济体。
gdpDescription.en: Around 100 CE Kush was in a prosperous phase, with an economy based on Nile agriculture, pastoralism, gold and iron production, ivory, and frontier transit trade. Its overall scale was far below that of Rome or Han China, but within northeastern Africa it ranked as a high-level regional power economy

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、畜牧业、铁冶、黄金开采、陶器、纺织、石工建筑、象牙加工与长途商队贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, iron smelting, gold mining, pottery, textiles, stone construction, ivory working, and long-distance caravan trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口黄金、铁器、象牙、乌木、牲畜、皮革、羽毛、奴隶与异域动物；进口葡萄酒、玻璃器、金属制奢侈品、地中海陶器、纺织品与部分高等级工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included gold, iron goods, ivory, ebony, livestock, hides, feathers, slaves, and exotic animals; imports included wine, glassware, luxury metal goods, Mediterranean ceramics, textiles, and selected high-grade manufactured items

name.zh: 麦罗埃金属货币与实物赋税体系
name.en: Meroitic metal currency and in-kind fiscal system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为粮食储备、牲畜、农具、陶器和家族劳动力；城镇精英则拥有宅邸、金属器、进口奢侈品和更稳定的土地收益
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in grain reserves, livestock, tools, pottery, and family labor; urban elites possessed houses, metal goods, imported luxuries, and more dependable land revenues

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的再分配经济与市场交换并存。王室和神庙控制关键土地、矿产、作坊与税收节点，地方市场和跨境商队则推动奢侈品与战略资源流通。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined state-led redistribution with market exchange. The crown and temples controlled key lands, mines, workshops, and tax nodes, while local markets and cross-border caravans moved luxury goods and strategic resources

source.zh: 附属部族贡纳与牲畜贡赋
source.en: Tribute from dependent peoples and livestock dues

source.zh: 黄金、铁冶与王家作坊收入
source.en: Gold, iron production, and royal workshop income
Customs, route tolls, and transit duties

source.zh: 罚金、王室地产与杂项收入
source.en: Fines, royal estates, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由金属储备、谷仓储粮、牲畜、贡品与神庙网络中的可动员资源构成，不完全表现为铸币现金。麦罗埃繁荣期拥有较稳健的储备能力，但其财政仍受尼罗河水文和边贸波动影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of metal reserves, stored grain, livestock, tribute goods, and mobilizable resources within the temple network, rather than coin alone. In Meroe’s prosperous phase reserve capacity was fairly strong, though finances remained sensitive to Nile conditions and frontier trade fluctuations

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持王权威望、神庙合法性、边疆驻军与商路安全，同时通过仓储与再分配缓冲歉收风险；整体上属于保守而重实物储备的财政体制
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized royal prestige, temple legitimacy, frontier garrisons, and trade-route security, while using storage and redistribution to cushion poor harvests; overall it was a conservative system heavily reliant on in-kind reserves

description.zh: 构成军队骨干，装备长矛、盾牌、短剑和部分铁制武器，用于守卫核心尼罗河区域与攻守城镇
description.en: Formed the backbone of the army, equipped with spears, shields, short swords, and some iron weapons, and used to guard the Nile core and fight around towns and strongpoints

description.zh: 库施自古以弓箭闻名，弓箭手既用于野战也用于边境要塞防御，是对抗北方敌军与沙漠袭扰的重要兵种
description.en: Kush was famous for archery, and its archers served in field warfare and frontier fort defense, making them vital against northern enemies and desert raiders

description.zh: 规模较小，主要用于边境巡逻、侦察、追击与沙漠通道控制
description.en: Smaller in number, they were used mainly for frontier patrol, reconnaissance, pursuit, and control of desert routes

description.zh: 负责保卫王室、首都、神庙中心和关键渡口、要塞与贸易节点
description.en: Responsible for protecting the royal household, the capital, temple centers, and key crossings, forts, and trade nodes

technology.zh: 军队以铁制武器、复合传统弓箭、盾牌、要塞防御和尼罗河运输支援为特点；虽不具罗马军团式重装体系，但在沙漠边境和河谷作战中适应性强
technology.en: The military featured iron weapons, established archery traditions, shields, fortifications, and Nile transport support; although it lacked a Roman legionary heavy system, it was highly adapted to desert-frontier and river-valley warfare

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与罗马控制下埃及的紧张边境关系、沙漠游动部族袭扰、南方与东部边疆整合压力，以及商路安全问题
threats.en: Major threats came from tense border relations with Roman-controlled Egypt, raids by mobile desert peoples, pressures of integrating southern and eastern frontiers, and the security of trade routes

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于尼罗河中上游可耕地带，另有相当数量分布于草原牧区、边疆绿洲和商道节点；人口结构呈现河谷农耕与周边部族世界相互交织的特点
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the arable middle and upper Nile zones, with substantial numbers in savanna pastoral regions, frontier oases, and trade-route nodes; demography reflected an interwoven riverine farming world and surrounding tribal societies

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族和祭司；中层为官员、军官、商人、工匠与神庙管理者；下层为农民、牧民、劳役者与奴婢。边疆附属部族在国家体系中处于半整合状态。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal house, nobles, and priests; the middle included officials, officers, merchants, artisans, and temple managers; the lower included peasants, pastoralists, laborers, and slaves. Frontier dependent peoples were only partially integrated into the state system

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式同盟体系，但与部分南方附属部族、东部商路社群及红海贸易中介保持合作关系
allies.en: There was no stable formal alliance system, but Kush maintained cooperative relations with some southern dependent peoples, eastern trade-route communities, and Red Sea intermediaries

enemies.zh: 无持续全面战争中的宿敌，但与罗马埃及之间长期存在战略戒备，沙漠游动部族与边境掠袭者亦构成经常性对手
enemies.en: Kush had no permanent enemy in full-scale war at this date, but long-term strategic caution existed toward Roman Egypt, while mobile desert peoples and frontier raiders were recurrent adversaries

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持北方边境均势、保护尼罗河与沙漠商路、经营南方贡纳网络并利用自己作为非洲内陆与地中海世界之间中介的地位为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining a balance on the northern frontier, protecting Nile and desert trade routes, managing southern tribute networks, and exploiting Kush’s position as an intermediary between inner Africa and the Mediterranean world

era.zh: 双帝国时代
era.en: Twin Empires

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的铁冶生产、砂岩建筑技术、尼罗河运输组织、弓箭军事传统、灌溉农业管理和麦罗埃文字行政应用构成其技术特色
keyInnovations.en: Its key technical features included developed iron production, sandstone building techniques, Nile transport organization, strong archery traditions, irrigation management, and administrative use of the Meroitic script

strengths.zh: 库施在公元100年前后拥有稳定王权、活跃的金铁资源、重要的中介贸易位置、强韧的宗教合法性和适应本地环境的军政体系，是东北非最重要的区域国家之一
strengths.en: Around 100 CE Kush possessed stable kingship, active gold and iron resources, an important intermediary trade position, resilient religious legitimacy, and military-political systems well adapted to local conditions, making it one of northeastern Africa’s leading regional states

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口和财政基础有限于尼罗河走廊，国家整合程度不如罗马或汉帝国，过度依赖边贸、贡纳与王室神庙网络，并易受气候、水文和贸易转向冲击
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in a population and fiscal base limited to the Nile corridor, lower state integration than Rome or Han China, heavy dependence on frontier trade, tribute, and royal-temple networks, and vulnerability to climatic, hydrological, and trade-route shifts

outlook.zh: 短期内库施仍将维持繁荣并在罗马埃及以南保持独立强权地位；若能继续控制商路与王权继承，则可延续稳定，但长期面临区域贸易重组和边疆控制松动的隐患
outlook.en: In the short term Kush was likely to remain prosperous and preserve its status as an independent power south of Roman Egypt; if it continued to control trade routes and succession, stability could endure, but in the longer run it faced risks from regional trade reorganization and loosening frontier control

description.zh: 库施王国以麦罗埃为中心，维持尼罗河中游强国地位并与罗马埃及互动。
description.en: Centered on Meroe, Kush remained a major middle Nile power interacting with Roman Egypt.

governmentForm.zh: 元首制君主政体，保留共和制度外观的帝国体制
governmentForm.en: Principate monarchy, an imperial system retaining the outward forms of the Republic

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构等级分明，顶层为皇帝与元老贵族，其次为骑士阶层、地方市政精英、自由民平民、被释奴隶与奴隶；各行省内又叠加公民、拉丁权与非公民身份差异。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with the emperor and senatorial aristocracy at the top, followed by the equestrian order, municipal elites, free commoners, freedmen, and slaves; across the provinces these layers were further shaped by differences in citizenship, Latin rights, and non-citizen status.

rulingClass.zh: 以皇帝、元老院贵族、骑士官僚和行省城市显贵构成的帝国统治阶层
rulingClass.en: An imperial ruling class composed of the emperor, senatorial aristocrats, equestrian administrators, and provincial urban notables

succession.zh: 法律上并无固定成文继承法，通常由现任皇帝通过收养、指定与军政精英认可来确立继承人
succession.en: There was no fixed codified law of succession; heirs were typically established through imperial designation, adoption, and acceptance by military and political elites

structure.zh: 中央由皇帝、元老院、财政与宫廷官署共同运作；元老院继续管理部分元老院行省和礼仪事务，皇帝通过直属官员、近卫军长官、财政官与行省总督掌握实际行政、司法与军事权力。
structure.en: The center functioned through the emperor, Senate, fiscal offices, and palace administration; the Senate still managed some senatorial provinces and ceremonial affairs, while the emperor exercised real administrative, judicial, and military authority through direct officials, praetorian prefects, financial procurators, and provincial governors.

function.zh: 负责咨议、荣誉授予、部分立法惯例、监督若干元老院行省及国家礼仪
function.en: Handled deliberation, honors, certain legislative conventions, oversight of some senatorial provinces, and state ceremonial functions

function.zh: 起草敕令、处理请愿、保管档案、传达皇帝命令，并协调中央行政文书
function.en: Drafted edicts, processed petitions, kept archives, transmitted imperial orders, and coordinated central administrative paperwork

function.zh: 管理国库、皇室财产、税收征解、矿山与皇家地产收入
function.en: Managed the treasury, imperial estates, tax collection, and revenues from mines and crown lands

function.zh: 负责地方行政、司法、税务监督、治安与边疆省份军事指挥
function.en: Oversaw local administration, justice, tax supervision, public order, and military command in frontier provinces

name.zh: 罗马城行政与粮食供应署
name.en: Urban Administration and Grain Supply Office

function.zh: 管理罗马城治安、消防、公共建筑、粮食配给与港口运输
function.en: Managed Rome’s policing, fire control, public buildings, grain distribution, and port logistics

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以行省—城市双层结构为主。行省由总督、财务官和下属文吏管理，城市则依靠地方议会、二年官和市政显贵承担税收、司法、公共建设与祭祀责任，帝国以较少中央官员借助地方精英实现广域统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on a province-city dual structure. Provinces were managed by governors, financial officials, and clerks, while cities relied on local councils, magistrates, and municipal elites to handle taxation, justice, public works, and cult obligations; the empire governed vast territories with relatively few central officials by relying on local notables.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合成文法、元老院决议、执政官告示、法学家解释与皇帝敕令，区分罗马公民法与万民法。总督在行省拥有广泛司法裁量，皇帝则是最高上诉来源。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined statutory law, senatorial decrees, magistrates’ edicts, juristic interpretation, and imperial rescripts, distinguishing Roman civil law from the law of nations. Governors exercised broad judicial discretion in the provinces, while the emperor served as the highest source of appeal.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自行省土地税、人头税、关税、矿山与皇家地产收益，以及遗产税、买卖税等间接税。意大利本土税负较轻，行省尤其东部富庶地区承担较大财政份额。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from provincial land taxes, poll taxes, customs duties, mines and imperial estates, as well as indirect taxes such as inheritance and sales taxes. Italy carried a lighter direct tax burden, while the provinces—especially wealthy eastern regions—provided a substantial share of imperial income.

religion.zh: 宗教上以罗马多神信仰和国家祭祀为核心，崇拜朱庇特、马尔斯、维斯塔等神祇，并广泛吸纳希腊、埃及、叙利亚和地方神明；皇帝崇拜在东部和军队中尤为重要，犹太教与早期基督徒社群已存在但仍属少数。
religion.en: Religion centered on Roman polytheism and state cult, venerating deities such as Jupiter, Mars, and Vesta, while widely incorporating Greek, Egyptian, Syrian, and local gods; the imperial cult was especially important in the East and among the military, and Jewish communities and early Christian groups were present though still minorities.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活深受希腊哲学影响，斯多亚主义、中期柏拉图主义、修辞教育与实用法理并行；罗马上层强调德性、责任、节制与公共服务理想，同时也重视法律、行政和军事的务实精神。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was deeply influenced by Greek philosophy, with Stoicism, Middle Platonism, rhetorical education, and practical jurisprudence coexisting; Roman elites emphasized virtue, duty, self-restraint, and public service, while also valuing legal, administrative, and military pragmatism.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母书写拉丁文，帝国东部广泛使用希腊文；官方铭文、法令、军令和商业文书中双语现象常见。
writingSystem.en: The empire primarily used the Latin alphabet to write Latin, while Greek was widely used in the eastern provinces; bilingual practice was common in official inscriptions, decrees, military orders, and commercial documents.

culturalAchievements.zh: 罗马在法学、道路与桥梁、拱券建筑、混凝土工程、公共浴场、引水道、圆形剧场和城市规划方面成就显著。文学方面延续维吉尔、奥维德、李维传统，白银时代拉丁文学、讽刺、修辞与历史写作活跃，帝国文化在地中海世界高度整合。
culturalAchievements.en: Rome achieved distinction in jurisprudence, roads and bridges, arch-based architecture, concrete engineering, public baths, aqueducts, amphitheaters, and urban planning. In literature it carried forward the traditions of Virgil, Ovid, and Livy, while Silver Age Latin literature, satire, rhetoric, and historical writing remained active, and imperial culture was highly integrated across the Mediterranean.

languageFamily.zh: 拉丁语属印欧语系意大利语族，希腊语属印欧语系希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Latin belonged to the Italic branch of the Indo-European family, while Greek belonged to the Hellenic branch of Indo-European

gdpDescription.zh: 帝国经济处于古典世界最发达水平之一，以广泛的农业剩余、城市市场、税收体系、标准化货币、海运网络和跨区域分工为基础。按现代研究常见重建，罗马总产出远超同时代多数政体，但人均水平仍主要受前工业农业约束。
gdpDescription.en: The imperial economy stood among the most developed of the classical world, based on broad agricultural surplus, urban markets, a tax system, standardized coinage, maritime networks, and interregional specialization. In common modern reconstructions, total Roman output greatly exceeded that of most contemporary states, though per capita levels remained constrained by preindustrial agriculture.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、陶器制造、建筑业、采矿冶金、纺织、石材加工、造船、玻璃、军需生产与长途运输
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, wine and olive oil production, pottery, construction, mining and metallurgy, textiles, stoneworking, shipbuilding, glassmaking, military supply production, and long-distance transport

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与域内流通品包括葡萄酒、橄榄油、西班牙金属、加卢姆鱼酱、陶器、玻璃、奴隶和纺织品；进口品包括香料、胡椒、丝绸、珍珠、宝石、象牙、香料木、纸草和东方奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports and internal trade goods included wine, olive oil, Spanish metals, garum fish sauce, pottery, glass, slaves, and textiles; imports included spices, pepper, silk, pearls, gemstones, ivory, aromatic woods, papyrus, and eastern luxury goods.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大。意大利和行省城市精英拥有大片地产、奴隶和商业资本；普通农户与城市平民多为低积蓄状态，易受歉收、税负和债务冲击。埃及、北非和高卢部分地区因农业与贸易繁荣而较为富庶。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Italian and provincial urban elites held large estates, slaves, and commercial capital, while ordinary peasants and urban commoners typically had limited savings and were vulnerable to harvest failure, taxes, and debt. Parts of Egypt, North Africa, and Gaul were relatively prosperous through agriculture and trade.

economicSystem.zh: 以私有财产和市场交换为主、国家强力监管税收与军需的前工业混合经济。城市市场、港口、承包制度和奴隶劳动广泛存在，国家则通过征税、粮食供应、军队采购、道路港口建设和皇家地产经营深度介入。
economicSystem.en: A preindustrial mixed economy centered on private property and market exchange, with strong state involvement in taxation and military supply. Urban markets, ports, contracting systems, and slave labor were widespread, while the state intervened heavily through taxation, grain supply, army procurement, infrastructure, and the management of imperial estates.

source.en: Land tax and provincial direct taxation

source.zh: 人头税与附加地方征收
source.en: Poll tax and related provincial levies
Customs, port dues, and trade taxes

source.zh: 遗产税、销售税及其他间接税
source.en: Inheritance tax, sales tax, and other indirect taxes

category.en: Army pay, supply, and frontier defense

category.zh: 行政、总督体系与官署运作
category.en: Administration, provincial governance, and state offices

category.zh: 罗马城粮食供给与公共福利
category.en: Grain supply and public welfare for the city of Rome

category.zh: 公共工程、道路、港口与建筑
category.en: Public works, roads, ports, and construction

category.zh: 宗教祭祀、庆典、补助与皇室支出
category.en: Religious rites, festivals, subsidies, and imperial household spending

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库与皇帝私人财政并存。帝国积累了可观储备，用于战争、赈济、建筑和皇位更替时期的稳定支出，但其流动性取决于税收征解、铸币和行省输送效率。
treasuryDescription.en: The public treasury and the emperor’s own fiscal resources coexisted. The empire maintained substantial reserves for war, relief, building, and succession-related stabilization, though practical liquidity depended on tax extraction, minting, and the efficiency of provincial remittance.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守稳健，以维持军队支付、城市粮供和公共工程为优先。图拉真时期因扩张、建设与征战而支出上升，但帝国依靠广阔税基、矿山和贸易税维持总体可控的财政平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative and stability-oriented, prioritizing army payment, urban grain supply, and public works. Under Trajan, spending rose because of expansion, construction, and warfare, but the empire maintained an overall manageable balance through its broad tax base, mines, and customs revenue.

description.zh: 由罗马公民组成的重装核心部队，是帝国野战与攻城作战的中坚，装备标准化程度高，纪律严明。
description.en: The heavy infantry core composed of Roman citizens, serving as the backbone of imperial field operations and siege warfare, with highly standardized equipment and strict discipline.

description.zh: 由非公民行省居民组成，提供轻步兵、弓箭手、骑兵和边疆驻军，是补充军团能力的关键力量，服役期满可获公民权。
description.en: Raised from non-citizen provincials, these forces supplied light infantry, archers, cavalry, and frontier garrisons, forming the essential complement to the legions; veterans could gain citizenship upon discharge.

description.zh: 负责皇帝护卫、罗马城治安和政治威慑，包括禁卫军、城市队与部分消防治安力量。
description.en: Responsible for imperial protection, security in Rome, and political deterrence, including the Praetorian Guard, urban cohorts, and associated public-order forces.

description.zh: 负责地中海航道、河川运输、海盗压制与补给护航，在多瑙河、莱茵河和埃及粮运中也具重要作用。
description.en: Handled Mediterranean sea lanes, river transport, anti-piracy operations, and convoy protection, and also played important roles on the Danube, Rhine, and in the Egyptian grain route.

technology.zh: 罗马军队在筑营、攻城器械、道路行军、后勤组织、标准化装备和边防工事方面极为先进。主要武器包括短剑、标枪、长矛、复合弓辅助兵器、鳞甲与锁子甲，攻城塔、弩炮、投石机和工兵技术成熟。
technology.en: The Roman military was highly advanced in camp construction, siege machinery, road-based marching, logistics, standardized equipment, and frontier fortification. Principal arms included swords, pila, spears, auxiliary missile weapons including composite bows, scale and mail armor, while siege towers, ballistae, catapults, and engineering methods were well developed.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自多瑙河地区的达契亚与萨尔马提亚诸部、莱茵河外的日耳曼部族，以及东方与帕提亚帝国的地缘竞争。犹太地区和部分行省也存在周期性叛乱与治安挑战。
threats.en: The chief threats came from Dacia and Sarmatian groups along the Danube, Germanic peoples beyond the Rhine, and geopolitical rivalry with the Parthian Empire in the East. Judea and some provinces also posed periodic risks of rebellion and internal disorder.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 匈奴/帕提亚式复合弓，骑射核心武器
description.en: Xiongnu/Parthian composite bow, core mounted archery weapon

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 罗马投石机，抛射大型石弹
description.en: Roman onager catapult, launching large stone projectiles

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口约六千万，分布于意大利、伊比利亚、高卢、不列颠、巴尔干、希腊、小亚细亚、叙利亚、埃及和北非等地。人口最稠密区域包括尼罗河流域、意大利中部、亚细亚西部、叙利亚城市带及北非沿海农业区。
populationDescription.en: The empire’s population was about sixty million, spread across Italy, Iberia, Gaul, Britain, the Balkans, Greece, Asia Minor, Syria, Egypt, and North Africa. The densest regions included the Nile Valley, central Italy, western Asia Minor, the Syrian urban belt, and the coastal agricultural zones of North Africa.

description.zh: 永恒之城，罗马帝国首都
description.en: Eternal City, capital of the Roman Empire

description.zh: 古代学术中心与地中海大港
description.en: Ancient center of learning and great Mediterranean port

description.zh: 古代叙利亚大都市，早期基督教中心
description.en: Great Syrian metropolis, early Christian center

description.zh: 古代小亚细亚名城，阿尔忒弥斯神庙所在地
description.en: Famed city of Asia Minor, home of Temple of Artemis

description.zh: 北非强大贸易帝国的首都
description.en: Capital of a powerful North African trading empire

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括皇帝与元老贵族、骑士阶层、市政显贵、军人、工商业自由民、农民、被释奴、奴隶和边疆部族附属群体。公民身份在法律、税收与婚姻权利上具有重要意义，但行省精英正逐渐被整合进帝国统治结构。
socialClasses.en: The principal social classes included the emperor and senatorial aristocracy, the equestrian order, municipal notables, soldiers, free artisans and traders, peasants, freedmen, slaves, and subordinate frontier tribal groups. Citizenship mattered greatly for legal, fiscal, and marital rights, but provincial elites were increasingly being integrated into imperial rule.

allies.zh: 罗马较少依赖平等盟友，更常通过附属王国、条约城市和边境缓冲政权维系秩序；阿拉伯、色雷斯及黑海周边若干客户国在外交与防务上依附帝国。
allies.en: Rome relied less on equal allies than on client kingdoms, treaty cities, and buffer polities along its borders; several client states in Arabia, Thrace, and around the Black Sea depended on the empire for diplomacy and defense.

enemies.zh: 最主要的战略对手是帕提亚帝国；此外，多瑙河流域的达契亚人、萨尔马提亚人及莱茵河外的日耳曼部族构成持续边疆压力。
enemies.en: The principal strategic rival was the Parthian Empire; in addition, the Dacians, Sarmatians of the Danubian zone, and Germanic groups beyond the Rhine created persistent frontier pressure.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策结合扩张、威慑与边疆稳定。图拉真时期尤其重视通过军事胜利提升皇帝威望、整顿边界与打通贸易路线；在东方则既维持与帕提亚的势力平衡，也积极利用亚美尼亚和近东附属政权作为战略缓冲。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined expansion, deterrence, and frontier stabilization. Under Trajan, particular emphasis was placed on enhancing imperial prestige through military victory, securing borders, and strengthening trade routes; in the East Rome sought both a balance of power with Parthia and the use of Armenia and Near Eastern client polities as strategic buffers.

era.zh: 古典时代高峰期
era.en: High Classical Era

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的混凝土建筑、拱券与穹顶工程、长距离引水道、标准化道路网、里程与驿站体系、复杂攻城器械、法律行政文书制度、玻璃制造和大型港口仓储系统构成其技术优势。
keyInnovations.en: Its technological strengths included mature concrete construction, arch and dome engineering, long-distance aqueducts, a standardized road network with milestones and posting stations, sophisticated siege machines, legal-administrative documentation systems, glass production, and large-scale port and warehousing infrastructure.

architectureAndEngineering.zh: 建筑与工程技术登峰造极：罗马帝国的建筑工程在公元100年达到巅峰——罗马斗兽场（80年竣工）可容纳5万观众，万神殿（即将于118-128年重建）的穹顶将保持世界最大混凝土穹顶纪录近两千年。水渠系统日供水量达100万立方米。罗马道路网络总长超过8万公里。
architectureAndEngineering.en: Architecture and engineering at their zenith: Roman Empire construction peaks around 100 CE — the Colosseum (completed 80 CE) seats 50,000; the Pantheon (to be rebuilt 118-128) dome will hold the record as the world's largest concrete dome for nearly two millennia. Aqueduct systems deliver 1 million cubic meters of water daily. The Roman road network exceeds 80,000 km.

militaryOrganization.zh: 军事组织与工程全球最完善：罗马军团体制在图拉真时代（98-117年）达到巅峰。每支军团都是自给自足的军事-工程单位，能修建道路、桥梁、堡垒和供水系统。罗马军事工程（攻城术、防御工事、军营建设）标准化程度无与伦比。
militaryOrganization.en: Most refined military organization and engineering globally: the Roman legion system peaks under Trajan (98-117 CE). Each legion is a self-sufficient military-engineering unit capable of building roads, bridges, forts, and water systems. Roman military engineering (siege warfare, fortifications, camp construction) is standardized to an unmatched degree.

overallAssessment.zh: 公元100年的罗马帝国在建筑工程和军事组织方面达到古代世界的最高峰。斗兽场、万神殿、水渠和道路网络展示了无与伦比的工程能力。但罗马的技术优势集中在'应用工程'而非'基础科学'——与同时期的中国相比，罗马在冶金和造纸方面明显落后。罗马工程的真正奇迹在于其规模和标准化——这是一个靠组织能力而非技术创新建立霸权的帝国。
overallAssessment.en: The Roman Empire in 100 CE reaches antiquity's highest peak in architectural engineering and military organization. The Colosseum, Pantheon, aqueducts, and road networks demonstrate unmatched engineering capability. But Roman tech advantage concentrates on 'applied engineering' rather than 'basic science' — compared to contemporary China, Rome clearly lags in metallurgy and papermaking. Roman engineering's true marvel lies in its scale and standardization — this is an empire built on organizational capability rather than technological innovation.

strengths.zh: 罗马在100年左右拥有地中海世界最广阔而整合度最高的政治经济体系之一，具备强大军队、稳固税基、成熟法制、完善交通网络、庞大城市体系与高度同化能力。其海运和道路网络使粮食、军需、税赋与信息可以跨洲流动。
strengths.en: Around 100 CE, Rome possessed one of the broadest and most integrated political-economic systems in the Mediterranean world, with a powerful military, stable tax base, mature legal order, extensive transport networks, large urban systems, and strong assimilative capacity. Its maritime and road networks enabled grain, military supplies, taxes, and information to move across continents.

weaknesses.zh: 帝国高度依赖皇帝个人权威、军队忠诚和行省税收，政治继承机制缺乏制度化，社会贫富悬殊明显，奴隶劳动占比高，边疆漫长而防守成本巨大。若遭遇内战、瘟疫、货币贬值或多线战争，整体稳定性将承受巨大压力。
weaknesses.en: The empire depended heavily on the personal authority of the emperor, army loyalty, and provincial taxation; succession remained weakly institutionalized, social inequality was stark, slave labor was significant, and the long frontiers were costly to defend. Civil war, plague, currency debasement, or multi-front conflict could place severe strain on overall stability.

outlook.zh: 在图拉真统治初期，罗马前景总体强劲，短期内仍将保持扩张能力、财政韧性与地中海霸权。若能控制边疆战争成本并维持精英合作，帝国将在未来数十年继续繁荣；但持续扩张也将增加治理难度和财政负担。
outlook.en: In the early reign of Trajan, Rome’s outlook was broadly strong, and in the short term it was likely to retain expansionary capacity, fiscal resilience, and Mediterranean supremacy. If it could contain the cost of frontier wars and preserve elite cooperation, the empire would continue to prosper for decades, though continued expansion would also increase governing difficulty and fiscal burden.

description.zh: 图拉真治下的罗马帝国处于扩张高峰，统治地中海世界大部并深度整合欧亚非贸易。
description.en: Under Trajan, the Roman Empire stood near its expansionary height, ruling most of the Mediterranean and deeply integrating Afro-Eurasian trade.

ideology.zh: 涅尔瓦-安东尼王朝养子继承制，军事扩张主义，元首制与帝国治理，公共工程与民生
ideology.en: Nerva-Antonine adoptive succession, military expansionism, principate and imperial governance, public works and welfare

relationship.zh: 皇帝家族（domus imperialis）是帝国的绝对权力核心。图拉真作为涅尔瓦-安东尼王朝的第二位皇帝，通过养子继承制获得皇位，开创了后世所称「五贤帝」时代。他以军事扩张和大规模公共建设著称，将帝国疆域推向最大范围。皇帝掌控军队最高指挥权、立法权、财政权和外交决策权，元老院在形式上分享治理权力但实际服从皇帝意志。
relationship.en: The imperial household (domus imperialis) was the absolute center of power. Trajan, the second emperor of the Nerva-Antonine dynasty, gained the throne through adoptive succession, inaugurating what posterity would call the era of the 'Five Good Emperors.' Known for military expansion and massive public construction, he extended the empire to its greatest territorial extent. The emperor held supreme military command, legislative authority, fiscal control, and foreign policy decision-making; the Senate formally shared governance but in practice deferred to the emperor's will.

description.zh: 图拉真时代的罗马帝国正处于权力与声望的巅峰。养子继承制——即在位皇帝选择最有才能的人而非血亲为继承人——解决了朱里亚-克劳狄王朝和弗拉维王朝的继承危机，开创了帝国最稳定、最繁荣的时期。图拉真本人是首位出身行省（西班牙）的皇帝，以卓越的军事才能和亲民的执政风格赢得了「最优秀元首」（Optimus Princeps）的称号。他征服达契亚、远征安息、大规模建设图拉真广场和图拉真浴场，将帝国推向空间领土的最大范围。皇帝家族通过控制军队效忠、赐予公民面包和竞技场娱乐（panem et circenses）以及与元老院维持表面和谐来巩固统治。
description.en: The Roman Empire under Trajan stood at the zenith of its power and prestige. Adoptive succession — in which the reigning emperor chose the most capable man rather than a blood relative as heir — resolved the succession crises of the Julio-Claudian and Flavian dynasties, inaugurating the empire's most stable and prosperous era. Trajan himself was the first emperor of provincial origin (Spain), earning the title 'Optimus Princeps' (Best of Rulers) through military excellence and an accessible governing style. He conquered Dacia, campaigned against Parthia, and built Trajan's Forum and Trajan's Baths on a grand scale, pushing the empire to its maximum territorial extent. The imperial household consolidated rule through military loyalty, bread and circuses (panem et circenses), and maintaining a facade of harmony with the Senate.

role.zh: 在位皇帝，将帝国疆域推向最大范围，被誉为'最优秀元首'
role.en: Reigning emperor who pushed imperial territory to its maximum extent, honored as 'Optimus Princeps'

role.zh: 图拉真的亲属与养子，将继承皇位并转向防御性边疆政策
role.en: Trajan's kinsman and adopted heir, who would inherit the throne and shift to a defensive frontier policy

ideology.zh: 共和传统与元首制共存，元老院治理权威，行省管理，贵族尊严与特权
ideology.en: Coexistence of republican tradition and principate, senatorial governance authority, provincial administration, aristocratic dignitas and privilege

relationship.zh: 元老院贵族是帝国治理体系的高级合作伙伴。在「五贤帝」时代，元老院与皇帝之间达成了历史上最成功的权力默契——元老院承认皇帝的最高权威，皇帝则尊重元老院的尊严和治理参与权。元老阶层管理帝国的重要行省，担任军团统帅和高级法官，并在立法和政策讨论中发挥咨询作用。图拉真与元老院的关系被后世视为帝制下君臣合作的典范。
relationship.en: The senatorial aristocracy was the senior governance partner of the imperial system. During the 'Five Good Emperors' era, the Senate and emperor reached history's most successful power understanding — the Senate acknowledged the emperor's supreme authority while the emperor respected senatorial dignity and governance participation. Senators governed important provinces, commanded legions, served as senior judges, and played advisory roles in legislation and policy. Trajan's relationship with the Senate was later seen as a model of ruler-minister cooperation under imperial rule.

description.zh: 罗马元老院贵族是帝国最古老的政治精英阶层，其传统可追溯至共和国时代数百年。约六百名元老组成的元老院在形式上仍是帝国最高议事机构，掌管部分行省（元老院行省）、参与立法和高级司法。元老阶层的成员多为拥有百万塞斯特斯以上资产的大地主和行省望族，他们通过担任执政官、行省总督和军团统帅等职位构成帝国行政精英。在图拉真时代，来自行省的「新人」元老比例上升，反映了帝国精英的多元化。元老院虽然失去了共和时代的实际权力，但其制度尊严和社会声望仍然至关重要——任何皇帝都不敢公然蔑视元老院。
description.en: The Roman senatorial aristocracy was the empire's oldest political elite, with traditions stretching back centuries to the Republic. The roughly six hundred senators nominally constituted the empire's supreme deliberative body, governing certain provinces (senatorial provinces), participating in legislation, and handling senior judicial matters. Members typically held assets exceeding one million sesterces, drawn from great landowners and provincial notables who formed the administrative elite through posts as consul, provincial governor, and legion commander. Under Trajan, the proportion of 'new men' from the provinces increased, reflecting the diversification of imperial elites. Though the Senate had lost its actual republican-era power, its institutional dignity and social prestige remained crucial — no emperor dared openly flout the Senate.

role.zh: 图拉真时代最著名的元老之一，其书信集记录了皇帝与元老院的合作关系
role.en: One of the most famous senators of Trajan's era, whose letters document the cooperative relationship between emperor and Senate

ideology.zh: 皇帝近卫，军事特权，帝位安全保障，政治影响力
ideology.en: Imperial bodyguard, military privilege, throne security, political influence

relationship.zh: 禁卫军是皇帝最亲近的军事力量，驻扎在罗马城内外，负责皇帝人身安全和首都治安。约九千名精锐士兵组成的禁卫军享有远高于普通军团的待遇——更高的军饷、更短的服役期和更丰厚的退役赏赐。禁卫军长官是帝国权力序列中仅次于皇帝的第二号实权人物。在图拉真治下，禁卫军保持忠诚和纪律，但历史上禁卫军曾多次参与皇帝的废立。
relationship.en: The Praetorian Guard was the emperor's closest military force, garrisoned in and around Rome, responsible for the emperor's personal safety and capital security. Comprising roughly nine thousand elite soldiers, the Guard enjoyed far better terms than regular legionaries — higher pay, shorter service, and more generous retirement bonuses. The Praetorian Prefect was the second most powerful figure after the emperor. Under Trajan, the Guard remained loyal and disciplined, but historically the Praetorians had participated in deposing and installing emperors on multiple occasions.

description.zh: 罗马禁卫军（Cohors Praetoria）是帝国政治中最特殊的军事力量。自奥古斯都设立以来，这支驻扎在罗马城维米纳尔山禁卫军营（Castra Praetoria）的精锐部队既是皇帝的盾牌也是潜在的利剑。禁卫军在公元69年「四帝之年」和此后多次政变中扮演了国王制造者的角色——他们拥立或杀害皇帝的能力使每一位统治者都必须优先确保禁卫军的忠诚。图拉真时代的禁卫军处于相对良好的纪律状态，但其制度性的政治特权——尤其是在皇帝即位时获得的「登基赏赐」（donativum）——使其始终保持着对帝位更替的潜在影响力。禁卫军长官逐渐演变为帝国的首席行政和司法官员，其权力远超军事范畴。
description.en: The Praetorian Guard (Cohors Praetoria) was the most politically distinctive military force in the empire. Since its establishment by Augustus, this elite force garrisoned at the Castra Praetoria on the Viminal Hill served as both the emperor's shield and a potential sword. The Praetorians played the role of kingmaker in the Year of Four Emperors (69 CE) and multiple subsequent coups — their ability to enthrone or assassinate emperors meant every ruler had to prioritize securing their loyalty. Under Trajan, the Guard was in relatively good discipline, but its institutional political privileges — especially the accession donativum — maintained its latent influence over imperial succession. The Praetorian Prefect gradually evolved into the empire's chief administrative and judicial officer, wielding power far beyond the military sphere.

role.zh: 禁卫军最高指挥官，帝国权力序列中仅次于皇帝的实权人物
role.en: Supreme commander of the Praetorian Guard, the second most powerful figure in the imperial power structure after the emperor

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心、联合部族贵族与山地要塞网络的君主制
governmentForm.en: A monarchy centered on the king, supported by tribal aristocracy and a network of mountain fortresses

socialStructure.zh: 社会大致分为贵族阶层、自由民、依附者与奴隶。贵族在军事、宗教和地方治理中具有主导地位，自由民主要从事农业、畜牧、采矿与服兵役。
socialStructure.en: Society was broadly divided into nobles, free commoners, dependents, and slaves. The nobility dominated military, religious, and local political life, while free people were mainly engaged in farming, herding, mining, and military service.

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族与王家亲信，尤其是被称为塔拉博斯特斯的上层贵族
rulingClass.en: The tribal nobility and royal retainers, especially the upper aristocracy known as the tarabostes

succession.zh: 王位继承并非严格固定，多由王族血统、贵族支持与军事威望共同决定
succession.en: Succession was not strictly fixed and was shaped by royal lineage, noble support, and military prestige

structure.zh: 中央权力集中于国王，依靠宗教权威、军事首领和地方部族领袖实施统治。王都及奥勒什蒂耶山地要塞群构成统治核心，边远地区则通过附庸与盟属部族间接控制。
structure.en: Central power was concentrated in the king, who ruled through religious authority, military chiefs, and local tribal leaders. The capital and the Orăștie mountain fortress system formed the core of state power, while outlying regions were controlled indirectly through subordinate and allied tribes.

function.zh: 负责王室决策协商、接待使节、对外谈判以及与周边部族和罗马的关系管理
function.en: Handled royal deliberation, reception of envoys, foreign negotiations, and relations with neighboring tribes and Rome

function.zh: 负责征召战士、统筹边防、维护山地防御工事、储备武器与粮秣
function.en: Managed recruitment, frontier defense, maintenance of mountain fortifications, and storage of weapons and supplies

function.zh: 负责征收农业贡赋、畜产、矿产与战利品，并组织向王室仓储集中
function.en: Collected agricultural tribute, livestock products, mineral output, and war booty, and organized their transfer into royal stores

function.zh: 管理祭司团、圣所礼仪、历法活动与王权合法性的宗教表达
function.en: Oversaw priesthoods, sanctuary rites, calendrical observances, and the religious expression of royal legitimacy

function.zh: 负责金属加工、武器制造、木石工程、山道维护以及仓储体系
function.en: Administered metalworking, weapon production, timber and stone works, mountain routes, and storage facilities

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依赖部族首领、贵族家族与要塞指挥者。王权在核心山区控制较强，在外围地区则通过盟约、贡赋与军事威慑实现统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on tribal chiefs, noble families, and fortress commanders. Royal control was stronger in the core mountain zone, while outer regions were governed through alliance, tribute, and military pressure.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和贵族裁断为主，重视忠诚、军事义务、财产与部族关系。对叛乱、通敌和拒绝贡役的惩处较严。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on customary norms, royal commands, and aristocratic judgment, with emphasis on loyalty, military duty, property, and tribal relationships. Punishments were relatively severe for rebellion, collaboration with enemies, and refusal of tribute or service.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要来自农业与畜牧贡赋、矿山产出、附属部族进贡、贸易抽取以及战争战利品，而非高度货币化的定额税制。
taxationSystem.en: State income came mainly from agricultural and pastoral tribute, mining output, payments from subordinate tribes, levies on trade, and war booty rather than from a highly monetized fixed tax system.

religion.zh: 以达契亚—色雷斯多神信仰为主，敬奉扎尔莫克西斯等神灵，祭司阶层在政治与精神生活中具有重要影响。神圣山地、圣所与天象观念构成宗教核心。
religion.en: Religion was primarily Dacian-Thracian polytheism, including veneration of deities such as Zalmoxis. The priestly class held major influence in political and spiritual life, and sacred mountains, sanctuaries, and celestial ideas formed an important religious core.

philosophy.zh: 思想传统以宗教伦理、战士荣誉、王权神圣性与对死亡的独特观念为主，同时吸收部分希腊化世界的观念与物质文化影响。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was grounded in religious ethics, warrior honor, the sacred character of kingship, and distinctive beliefs about death, while also absorbing some ideas and material influences from the Hellenistic world.

writingSystem.zh: 本土书写使用有限，行政与纪念活动更多依赖口述传统、符号和精英层面对希腊文或拉丁文的有限接触；国家治理并非建立在广泛文书官僚之上。
writingSystem.en: Native writing was limited. Administration and memory relied more on oral tradition, symbols, and limited elite contact with Greek or Latin literacy; governance was not based on an extensive documentary bureaucracy.

culturalAchievements.zh: 山地要塞建筑、石制圣所布局、金银工艺、铁器冶炼、军器制造和复杂防御体系是其主要文化成就。达契亚人在短时期内形成了较强的政治整合能力与鲜明的王都宗教建筑景观。
culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements included mountain fortress construction, stone sanctuary design, gold and silver craftsmanship, iron metallurgy, weapon manufacture, and complex defensive systems. The Dacians also developed strong short-term political integration and a distinctive sacred-royal architectural landscape at the capital.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系，通常归入达契亚—色雷斯语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, usually classified within the Dacian-Thracian branch

gdpDescription.zh: 达契亚经济规模在多瑙河以北诸政体中相当突出，但整体仍以农业、牧业、矿业和战争动员型资源汲取为主。其财富集中度较高，王室与贵族把持金银与盐等关键资源。
gdpDescription.en: The Dacian economy was impressive among polities north of the Danube, but remained based chiefly on agriculture, pastoralism, mining, and war-oriented resource extraction. Wealth was unevenly concentrated, with the crown and nobility controlling key resources such as gold, silver, and salt.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、采盐、金银铜铁开采、冶金、武器制造、木材与陶器生产
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock herding, salt extraction, gold-silver-copper-iron mining, metallurgy, weapon production, timber work, and pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、金银制品、金属锭、牲畜、皮革、蜂蜜、蜡、木材与奴隶输入输出并存，同时从罗马获取奢侈品、酒、器皿与技术性产品
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included salt, gold and silver products, metal ingots, livestock, hides, honey, wax, timber, and slaves, while luxury items, wine, vessels, and technical products were obtained from Rome

name.zh: 混合货币与称量金属体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地、牲畜、粮食储备、工具和亲族网络；贵族家庭则拥有大批牲畜、金属器、武装随从与战利品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in land use rights, livestock, grain stores, tools, and kinship ties; noble households possessed larger herds, metal goods, armed retainers, and war booty.

economicSystem.zh: 以自给农业和部族贡纳为基础，辅以矿业、边境贸易和战利品分配的王权再分配经济
economicSystem.en: A royal redistributive economy based on subsistence agriculture and tribal tribute, supplemented by mining, frontier trade, and the distribution of war booty

category.en: Diplomatic gifts and maintenance of allied tribes

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财政以贵金属储藏、武器库存、粮食仓储和牲畜储备共同构成。因持续备战与修筑工事，100年前后财政承压，但仍保有相当资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury consisted of precious metal reserves, weapon stocks, grain stores, and livestock reserves. Because of continued military preparation and fortress construction, finances were under strain around 100 CE, but substantial resources were still available.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向战时动员，优先保障边防、工事、贵族忠诚和资源集中；当压力上升时，王权会加强贡赋征发与矿产控制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was oriented toward wartime mobilization, prioritizing frontier defense, fortifications, noble loyalty, and resource concentration; when pressure rose, the crown tightened tribute demands and control over mining.

description.zh: 由核心地区步兵与要塞驻军组成，负责山地防御、据点保卫与主力作战，装备长矛、盾牌、剑与著名的法尔克斯弯刀。
description.en: Composed of infantry from the core regions and fortress garrisons, responsible for mountain defense, strongpoint security, and main-force combat, armed with spears, shields, swords, and the famous falx.

description.zh: 由贵族及其随从构成，机动性较强，用于侦察、突击、追击和联络外围部族。
description.en: Made up of nobles and their retainers, this more mobile arm was used for reconnaissance, shock action, pursuit, and liaison with outer tribes.

description.zh: 由各部族在战时提供，训练和装备差异较大，但数量可观，适合防御、伏击与地形依托作战。
description.en: Provided by the tribes in wartime, these troops varied greatly in training and equipment but were numerous and well suited for defense, ambush, and terrain-based fighting.

description.zh: 包括轻装投射兵、山地侦察者和边地辅助兵，常用于骚扰、掩护与复杂地形作战。
description.en: Included light missile troops, mountain scouts, and frontier auxiliaries, commonly used for harassment, screening, and fighting in difficult terrain.

technology.zh: 军备以铁制冷兵器、木石要塞、防御工事和山地战术见长，并吸收部分凯尔特、萨尔马提亚和罗马军备影响。攻城能力有限，野战中更依赖伏击、地形和突然打击。
technology.en: Military technology was strong in iron weapons, timber-stone fortifications, defensive works, and mountain warfare, while also showing Celtic, Sarmatian, and Roman influences. Siege capability was limited, and in open warfare the Dacians relied more on ambush, terrain, and sudden strikes.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自罗马帝国在多瑙河沿线的持续施压与即将升级的远征；此外，北方和东部草原势力及边缘部族的不稳定也构成风险。
threats.en: The greatest threat came from sustained Roman pressure along the Danube and the prospect of an escalating campaign; instability among northern and eastern steppe groups and frontier tribes also posed risks.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 匈奴/帕提亚式复合弓，骑射核心武器
description.en: Xiongnu/Parthian composite bow, core mounted archery weapon

description.zh: 汉军标准配刀，单刃直背，劈斩利器
description.en: Standard Han army saber, single-edged straight back, ideal for slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于喀尔巴阡山及其周边高地、河谷和资源带。核心区人口较密集，山区要塞与周边农业聚落形成互补，边缘地区则以较松散的部族定居为主。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in and around the Carpathian mountains, adjacent uplands, river valleys, and resource zones. The core area was relatively denser, with mountain fortresses linked to surrounding farming settlements, while peripheral regions were dominated by looser tribal habitation.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族与塔拉博斯特斯贵族，中层为自由农牧民、工匠和战士，下层包括依附者、俘虏与奴隶。祭司群体虽然人数不多，但具有很高影响力。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and tarabostes nobility, followed by free farmers, herders, craftsmen, and warriors; lower strata included dependents, captives, and slaves. The priesthood was small in number but highly influential.

allies.zh: 达契亚通过与部分萨尔马提亚和其他多瑙河北岸部族维持松散联盟与临时合作来平衡罗马，但稳定盟友有限。
allies.en: Dacia balanced Rome through loose alliances and temporary cooperation with some Sarmatian and other trans-Danubian tribes, though reliable allies were limited.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为罗马帝国；双方在多瑙河边境长期对峙，战争风险在100年时已非常高。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was the Roman Empire; the two had long faced each other across the Danube, and the risk of war was very high in 100 CE.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以遏制罗马扩张、维护多瑙河北岸主导地位、整合邻近部族并争取战略缓冲地带为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on checking Roman expansion, maintaining predominance north of the Danube, integrating neighboring tribes, and securing strategic buffer zones.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期至罗马帝国盛期的边疆军事时代
era.en: Late Iron Age frontier military age during the high Roman imperial period

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括高水平铁器冶炼、山地环形要塞、石木复合防御工事、金银工艺、储粮与水源管理设施，以及适应山地地形的军事工程。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included advanced ironworking, mountain ring-fortresses, mixed stone-and-timber defensive works, gold and silver craftsmanship, grain and water management facilities, and military engineering adapted to upland terrain.

strengths.zh: 达契亚的优势在于山地防御体系、王权在德凯巴卢斯统治下的较强整合能力、矿产财富、善战贵族与适应复杂地形的军队。
strengths.en: Dacia’s strengths lay in its mountain defensive system, the relatively strong political integration achieved under Decebalus, mineral wealth, a martial nobility, and forces well adapted to difficult terrain.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是行政制度不如罗马成熟、财政与兵员组织的持续性有限、外围部族忠诚不稳、城市与道路网络较弱，并在长期消耗战中处于不利地位。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a less developed administrative system than Rome’s, limited long-term fiscal and manpower organization, unstable loyalty among outer tribes, a weaker urban and road network, and a disadvantage in prolonged wars of attrition.

outlook.zh: 到100年，达契亚仍是罗马北方最危险的对手之一，但随着罗马战略重心重新转向多瑙河，王国正进入生死攸关的对抗阶段。若无法在未来战争中保持联盟和要塞体系，独立前景将迅速恶化。
outlook.en: By 100 CE, Dacia remained one of Rome’s most dangerous northern rivals, but as Roman strategic attention returned to the Danube, the kingdom was entering a decisive phase of confrontation. If it could not preserve its alliances and fortress system in the coming wars, its prospects for independence would deteriorate rapidly.

description.zh: 德凯巴卢斯领导的达契亚是罗马多瑙边境最强对手之一，正处于紧张对抗期。
description.en: Under Decebalus, Dacia was one of Rome’s strongest Danubian rivals and was in a period of mounting confrontation.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族贵族和氏族首领为核心的游牧联盟，按部落与战时联盟运作
governmentForm.en: A nomadic confederative order centered on tribal aristocrats and clan chiefs, functioning through tribes and wartime alliances

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部族和部落联盟为层级，贵族骑士、随从武士、自由牧民及附属人口构成主要阶层，妇女在财产、骑乘与战斗文化中地位较高
socialStructure.en: Society was organized by clan, tribe, and tribal confederation, with noble horse-warriors, retainer fighters, free pastoralists, and dependent populations forming the main strata; women held relatively high status in property, riding, and martial culture

rulingClass.zh: 骑马作战的部族贵族、氏族首领及其随从战士
rulingClass.en: Mounted tribal aristocrats, clan chiefs, and their retainer warriors

succession.zh: 首领职位通常在显贵氏族内世袭，但需获得部族贵族与战士支持，常因联姻、威望与军事成功而变动
succession.en: Leadership was usually hereditary within prominent clans, but required support from tribal nobles and warriors, and often shifted through marriage, prestige, and military success

structure.zh: 萨尔马提亚并非统一国家，而是由雅兹格人、罗克索拉尼人、阿兰人等部群组成的松散联盟。政治权力分散于各部首领手中，重大迁徙、对外战争和缔约通常通过部族会议、贵族协商和临时统帅协调完成
structure.en: The Sarmatians were not a unified state but a loose constellation of groups such as the Iazyges, Roxolani, and Alans. Political authority was dispersed among tribal leaders, while major migrations, external wars, and treaty arrangements were managed through tribal councils, aristocratic negotiation, and temporary war leadership

function.zh: 协商迁徙、盟约、战争、赔偿与贵族争端，代表主要氏族与战士利益
function.en: Deliberated migration, alliances, war, compensation, and aristocratic disputes, representing major clans and warrior interests

function.zh: 执行首领命令、传递消息、守卫营地、征集随从与主持外交接待
function.en: Carried out the chief's commands, relayed messages, guarded camps, mobilized retainers, and handled diplomatic reception

name.zh: 贡赋与战利品分配机构

function.zh: 统计附属部落贡纳、分配牲畜、奴隶、金属器与外来奢侈品，以维系联盟忠诚
function.en: Recorded tribute from subordinate groups and distributed livestock, slaves, metal goods, and imported luxuries to maintain confederate loyalty

function.zh: 与罗马、达契亚、博斯普鲁斯王国、希腊化黑海城市及邻近部族交涉贸易、通行和结盟
function.en: Negotiated trade, passage, and alliance with Rome, Dacia, the Bosporan Kingdom, Hellenized Black Sea cities, and neighboring tribes

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以氏族营地、季节性牧区和河谷据点为基础，由部族首领、家族长老和武装随从管理。征收贡赋、裁决争端和组织防御多依惯例而非固定官僚层级
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on clan encampments, seasonal pastures, and riverine strongpoints, managed by tribal chiefs, family elders, and armed retainers. Tribute collection, dispute settlement, and defense were conducted largely through custom rather than a fixed bureaucracy

legalSystem.zh: 法律以部族习惯法为主，强调血亲关系、赔偿、复仇义务、婚姻契约和牲畜财产权。重大案件由首领与贵族裁决，武力与调解并行
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary tribal law, emphasizing kinship ties, compensation, vengeance obligations, marriage contracts, and rights over livestock. Major cases were judged by chiefs and nobles, with force and mediation operating side by side

taxationSystem.zh: 缺乏统一定额税制，财富主要通过牲畜征纳、附属部落贡赋、贸易抽成、对定居地区的劫掠与罗马边境雇佣军报酬获得
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform fixed tax system; wealth was derived mainly from livestock levies, tribute from subordinate groups, trade shares, raiding on settled regions, and payments for service or diplomacy on the Roman frontier

religion.zh: 宗教以草原多神信仰为主，崇拜天神、火、太阳、祖灵与战神，祭祀常与马、武器、坟冢和誓约仪式相连，并吸收部分伊朗语系与黑海沿岸地方信仰元素
religion.en: Religion was centered on steppe polytheism, venerating sky deities, fire, the sun, ancestral spirits, and war gods; ritual was closely tied to horses, weapons, burial mounds, and oath ceremonies, while also absorbing some Iranic and Black Sea local religious elements

philosophy.zh: 无成体系文字哲学传统，但重视勇武、荣誉、家族忠诚、赠礼关系、游牧自由与首领威望，这些价值构成政治与社会行为准则
philosophy.en: There was no systematic written philosophical tradition, but ideals of valor, honor, kin loyalty, gift exchange, nomadic freedom, and the prestige of leaders formed the governing code of political and social life

writingSystem.zh: 本土社会主要依靠口传传统，无广泛使用的本民族书写系统；在外交和贸易中常借助希腊文、拉丁文或邻近定居文明的文字记录
writingSystem.en: Native society relied chiefly on oral tradition and had no widely used indigenous writing system; in diplomacy and trade, Greek, Latin, or the scripts of neighboring settled civilizations were often used for record-keeping

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨尔马提亚文化以精湛骑术、重装骑兵传统、动物纹饰艺术、金属马具、复合弓、长枪战法及大型库尔干墓葬闻名，对黑海北岸和中欧边疆军事文化影响深远
culturalAchievements.en: Sarmatian culture was noted for superb horsemanship, traditions of heavy cavalry, animal-style art, metal horse gear, composite bows, long-lance tactics, and large kurgan burials, all of which strongly influenced military culture along the north Black Sea and Central European frontiers

languageFamily.zh: 东伊朗语族
languageFamily.en: Eastern Iranian

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以游牧畜产为核心，辅以边境贸易、贡赋、护商、掠夺和雇佣兵收入。物质产出不如大型农耕帝国稳定，但机动性强，能迅速在边境和贸易路线上获取财富
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on pastoral livestock production, supplemented by frontier trade, tribute, protection payments, raiding, and mercenary income. Material output was less stable than that of major agrarian empires, but its mobility enabled rapid wealth extraction along frontiers and trade corridors

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、马匹繁育、皮革加工、毛毡与纺织、金属马具与武器制作、奴隶贸易、边境中介贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse breeding, leatherworking, felt and textile production, manufacture of metal horse gear and weapons, slave trading, and frontier intermediary trade

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牛羊、皮毛、毛毡、奴隶、蜂蜜、蜡、兽皮、武器，以及从黑海和罗马世界获得的酒、金属器、玻璃器和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, cattle, sheep, furs, felt, slaves, honey, wax, hides, and weapons, alongside wine, metalware, glassware, and luxury goods obtained from the Black Sea and Roman world

name.zh: 以物易物并辅以外来钱币

unitName.zh: 罗马第纳尔与地方银币
unitName.en: Roman denarius and local silver coin

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以马群、牛羊、战车、武器、首饰和附属人口衡量；贵族拥有大规模牲畜和战马，普通牧民财产较少且受气候与战乱影响显著
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in horse herds, cattle, sheep, wagons, weapons, ornaments, and dependent persons; nobles possessed large livestock holdings and warhorses, while ordinary pastoralists held less and were more vulnerable to climate and conflict

economicSystem.zh: 以牧业为基础、礼物交换与贡纳分配为纽带的部族经济，兼有市场交换、掠夺再分配和跨边境贸易特征
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on pastoral production and bound together by gift exchange and tribute distribution, while also featuring market exchange, redistributive raiding, and cross-border trade

source.zh: 附属部落贡赋与牲畜征纳
source.en: Tribute from subordinate tribes and livestock levies

source.zh: 罗马边境补助、雇佣与外交馈赠
source.en: Roman frontier subsidies, mercenary pay, and diplomatic gifts

category.en: Chiefly retainers and aristocratic gift distribution

category.zh: 外交馈赠、赎买与结盟成本
category.en: Diplomatic gifts, ransom, and alliance costs

category.zh: 营地迁徙、补给与牲畜维持
category.en: Camp movement, supplies, and herd maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中式国库，财富分散储存在各部首领、贵族家族、牲畜群和贵重金属器物中；可迅速动员，但不易长期集中积累
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized state treasury; wealth was dispersed among tribal leaders, noble households, herds, and precious metal objects. It could be mobilized quickly but was difficult to accumulate in concentrated long-term reserves

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持骑兵追随者忠诚、保障迁徙和分配战利品为核心，偏重再分配而非固定征税，财政稳定性高度依赖对外关系与牧业条件
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining cavalry followers' loyalty, supporting migration, and distributing booty, emphasizing redistribution rather than fixed taxation; financial stability depended heavily on external relations and pastoral conditions

unit.zh: 罗马第纳尔与地方银币
unit.en: Roman denarius and local silver coin

description.zh: 由贵族及其随从组成，装备长枪、鳞甲或札甲，擅长冲击，是萨尔马提亚军力的核心
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, armed with long lances and scale or lamellar armor; they specialized in shock action and formed the core of Sarmatian power

description.zh: 机动性极强，以复合弓和游击战术骚扰敌军、侦察边境并掩护部族迁徙
description.en: Highly mobile riders who used composite bows and skirmishing tactics to harass enemies, scout frontiers, and screen tribal movements

description.zh: 由附属人口和随营人员组成，用于守卫营地、辎重、河渡和临时据点，不是主要作战兵种
description.en: Drawn from dependent populations and camp followers, they guarded encampments, baggage, river crossings, and temporary strongpoints, but were not the main combat arm

description.zh: 来自附属部群或临时盟友，在战时提供额外兵力，但忠诚度与持续性不及核心部族
description.en: Supplied by subordinate groups or temporary allies, they provided additional wartime manpower but were less reliable than core tribal forces

commanderInChief.zh: 战时推举的大酋长或联军统帅
commanderInChief.en: A war leader or high chief chosen for campaigns

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓、长枪、鳞甲、马甲、优良战马和高机动骑兵战术为特色。缺乏成熟攻城器械和持久后勤体系，因此对定居城防目标不占优势
technology.en: Military technology was characterized by composite bows, long lances, scale armor, horse armor, quality warhorses, and highly mobile cavalry tactics. The Sarmatians lacked advanced siege equipment and sustained logistical systems, making them less effective against fortified settled targets

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自罗马多瑙边防反击、达契亚竞争、日耳曼部族压力、草原内部部落冲突，以及东部阿兰等更强游牧集团的重组与扩张
threats.en: Major threats came from Roman counteraction on the Danube frontier, rivalry with Dacia, pressure from Germanic tribes, internal steppe conflicts, and the reorganization and expansion of stronger eastern nomadic groups such as the Alans

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 汉军标准配刀，单刃直背，劈斩利器
description.en: Standard Han army saber, single-edged straight back, ideal for slashing

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于顿河、伏尔加下游到多瑙平原之间的草原和森林草原地带，按部族迁徙分布。定居城市人口极少，多数人生活在可移动营地、车队与季节性牧区中
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the steppe and forest-steppe from the Don and lower Volga to the Danubian plains, distributed through tribal migration patterns. Urban settlement was minimal; most people lived in mobile camps, wagon trains, and seasonal pasture zones

name.zh: 无固定都城；主要人口集中于季节性营地
name.en: No fixed capital; population concentrated in seasonal encampments

socialClasses.zh: 上层为部族贵族与首领家族，中层为自由骑手牧民和手工业者，下层为附属部民、俘虏与奴隶。社会地位与战马数量、家族血统、战功和对首领的追随关系密切相关
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of tribal nobles and chiefly families, the middle of free mounted pastoralists and artisans, and the lower of dependent tribesmen, captives, and slaves. Status was closely linked to numbers of warhorses, lineage, martial success, and ties of following to a chief

allies.zh: 联盟关系多为临时性，部分部群与达契亚、博斯普鲁斯王国、黑海沿岸希腊化城邦或罗马边境指挥官保持互利合作；也常与其他草原部族通过联姻结盟
allies.en: Alliances were often temporary; some groups maintained mutually useful ties with Dacia, the Bosporan Kingdom, Hellenized Black Sea cities, or Roman frontier commanders, and also forged steppe alliances through marriage

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象包括多瑙边境的罗马军、争夺草原控制权的达契亚与日耳曼诸部，以及草原内部竞争的其他萨尔马提亚和阿兰集团
enemies.en: Main enemies included Roman forces on the Danube frontier, Dacians and Germanic groups contesting steppe control, and rival Sarmatian and Alan formations within the steppe world

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策务实灵活，以控制草原通道、维护放牧范围、获取边境贸易利益和借助军事压力换取贡纳或馈赠为主；既可作为掠袭者，也可充当盟友、护商者和雇佣骑兵来源
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and flexible, aimed at controlling steppe corridors, preserving pasture range, securing frontier trade benefits, and using military pressure to obtain tribute or gifts; the Sarmatians could act as raiders, allies, caravan protectors, or suppliers of mercenary cavalry

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期草原军事社会
era.en: Late Iron Age Steppe Military Society

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与实践包括高效骑乘体系、长枪突击战法、复合弓骑射、金属马具、鳞甲防护、移动营地组织和长距离草原通信能力
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and practices included efficient riding systems, long-lance shock tactics, mounted composite-bow warfare, metal horse gear, scale armor, mobile camp organization, and long-distance steppe communication

strengths.zh: 萨尔马提亚诸部拥有卓越骑兵、极高战略机动性、跨草原行动能力和对边疆政治的强大干预力。他们善于利用罗马、达契亚与黑海世界之间的缝隙，通过战争、贸易和雇佣关系积累财富与影响
strengths.en: The Sarmatian confederations possessed excellent cavalry, exceptional strategic mobility, broad steppe operational reach, and strong influence over frontier politics. They were adept at exploiting the spaces between Rome, Dacia, and the Black Sea world to accumulate wealth and influence through war, trade, and military service

weaknesses.zh: 其主要弱点在于政治分裂、缺乏统一财政和官僚机构、对气候与牧场条件敏感、攻城能力薄弱，并且难以长期控制富庶定居地区
weaknesses.en: Their main weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, the lack of unified fiscal and bureaucratic institutions, sensitivity to climate and pasture conditions, weak siege capability, and difficulty in holding wealthy settled regions for long periods

outlook.zh: 约公元100年时，萨尔马提亚世界总体稳定且仍具边疆影响力，但这种稳定建立在部族平衡与外部机会之上。未来数代中，他们仍将左右多瑙与黑海北岸局势，但也会面临阿兰重组、哥特南迁和罗马防线调整带来的更大压力
outlook.en: Around 100 CE, the Sarmatian world was broadly stable and still influential on the frontier, but that stability rested on tribal balance and external opportunity. In the generations ahead, they would continue to shape the Danubian and north Black Sea regions, while facing increasing pressure from Alan consolidation, later Gothic movements, and changes in Roman frontier defense

description.zh: 从顿河到多瑙河草原分布的萨尔马提亚骑马部族持续影响黑海与中欧边疆。
description.en: Sarmatian horse nomads from the Don to the Danube continued to shape the Black Sea and Central European frontiers.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦联盟与氏族治理体系，各部独立，由酋长、战士随从与自由民集会共同决策
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of tribal chiefdoms and clan-based polities, with each tribe independent and governed through chieftains, warrior retinues, and assemblies of free men

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族、部族为核心，分为贵族与酋长家族、自由战士与农民、依附者、奴隶；个人声望建立在武勇、馈赠和追随者数量之上
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kinship, clans, and tribes, divided into noble and chiefly families, free warriors and farmers, dependents, and slaves; personal status rested on valor, gift-giving, and the size of one's following

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族、酋长家族及其战士随从集团
rulingClass.en: Tribal nobles, chiefly lineages, and their warrior retinues

succession.zh: 酋长职位通常在显赫家族内继承，但需获得贵族与自由民集会认可；战时统帅可因威望与战功被推举
succession.en: Chiefly office was usually hereditary within prominent families but required recognition by nobles and assemblies of free men; war leaders could also be elevated by prestige and military success

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构。各部族由酋长、长老会议和自由民集会治理，重大事务如迁徙、战争、结盟、赔偿与审判在公开集会中裁决
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus. Individual tribes were governed by chieftains, councils of elders, and assemblies of free men, with major matters such as migration, war, alliance, compensation, and judgment decided in public gatherings

function.zh: 负责酋长护卫、使者接待、馈赠分配、战利品管理与对外联络，是最接近常设政治核心的机构
function.en: Handled the chief's guard, reception of envoys, distribution of gifts, management of spoils, and external contacts, forming the closest equivalent to a standing political center

function.zh: 由部族显贵、家族首领和有威望的长者组成，负责调解争端、提出政策、监督习惯法执行
function.en: Composed of tribal notables, clan heads, and respected elders, it mediated disputes, proposed policy, and oversaw the enforcement of customary law

function.zh: 批准选举、战争决议、赔偿安排与跨部族盟约，在政治合法性上具有关键作用
function.en: Approved elections, declarations of war, compensation settlements, and intertribal agreements, and served as the key source of political legitimacy

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、神圣誓言和与战争相关的仪式，在若干部族中对决策具有重要影响
function.en: Conducted sacrifices, divination, sacred oaths, and war-related rituals, and in some tribes exercised major influence over decision-making

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理依靠村社、亲族网络和地方首领，缺乏文书化的行政区划；权力按部族、氏族与聚落层级运作
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on villages, kinship networks, and local headmen, without formal written districts; authority operated through tribal, clan, and settlement hierarchies

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为基础，强调血亲责任、赔偿金、誓言、见证和公开裁决；严重冲突可引发私斗或部族报复
legalSystem.en: Based on customary law, emphasizing kin responsibility, compensation payments, oaths, witness testimony, and public judgment; severe disputes could lead to feud or tribal retaliation

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一常设税制。酋长主要依靠贡纳、战利品、牲畜、宴飨义务、罗马馈赠或贸易抽成维持权威
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform standing tax system. Chieftains drew support chiefly from tribute, war booty, livestock, obligations of hospitality, Roman gifts, or shares from trade

religion.zh: 多神信仰与自然崇拜盛行，崇敬天空、战争、生育与命运诸神，也重视圣林、沼泽、泉水和祖先仪式
religion.en: Polytheistic belief and nature worship prevailed, venerating deities of sky, war, fertility, and fate, while also placing importance on sacred groves, marshes, springs, and ancestral rites

philosophy.zh: 无成体系的书面哲学传统，但价值观强调勇武、忠诚、复仇、名誉、亲族义务、命运观念与酋长慷慨
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophical tradition, but values strongly emphasized bravery, loyalty, vengeance, honor, kinship obligation, a sense of fate, and chiefly generosity

writingSystem.zh: 总体上尚无广泛使用的本土书写系统；符号记忆、口传传统和与罗马接触时使用拉丁文或中介书写更为常见，早期符文可能尚处萌芽阶段
writingSystem.en: There was generally no widely used native writing system; mnemonic symbols, oral tradition, and the use of Latin or intermediary scripts in contact with Rome were more common, while early runic forms were at most embryonic

culturalAchievements.zh: 金属工艺、木工、织造、口述史诗、英雄歌谣、宗教仪式和精美的纤维、皮革与琥珀制品体现出高度地方文化活力
culturalAchievements.en: Metalworking, woodworking, weaving, oral epic, heroic song, ritual practice, and finely made fiber, leather, and amber goods reflected strong local cultural vitality

languageFamily.zh: 日耳曼语族早期诸方言
languageFamily.en: Early Germanic dialects of the Germanic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧、狩猎、采集和地方交换为主，货币化程度低，但与罗马边境的贸易为部分地区带来额外财富与奢侈品
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based primarily on subsistence farming, pastoralism, hunting, gathering, and local exchange, with low monetization, though frontier trade with Rome brought added wealth and luxuries to some regions

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧业、林业、采矿与沼铁冶炼、琥珀采集、纺织、陶器、木器和武器制造
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, pastoralism, forestry, mining and bog-iron smelting, amber collection, textiles, pottery, woodworking, and weapon production

tradeGoods.zh: 输出琥珀、毛皮、奴隶、牲畜、蜂蜜、蜡、皮革和部分金属制品；输入罗马酒、玻璃器、金属器、饰品、硬币和陶器
tradeGoods.en: Exports included amber, furs, slaves, livestock, honey, wax, leather, and some metal goods; imports included Roman wine, glassware, metalware, ornaments, coin, and pottery

name.zh: 无统一货币，兼用罗马钱币
name.en: No unified currency, with Roman coin used alongside barter

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、武器、首饰和亲族关系衡量；边境精英因罗马贸易而明显更富
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to land, livestock, grain stores, weapons, ornaments, and kinship ties; frontier elites were notably richer because of Roman trade

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产和部族再分配为基础的混合经济，结合礼物交换、掠夺收益、有限市场交换和边境贸易
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on household production and tribal redistribution, combining gift exchange, gains from raiding, limited market exchange, and frontier trade

source.en: Trade shares and toll-like frontier proceeds

source.zh: 牲畜、宴飨义务与司法赔偿
source.en: Livestock dues, hospitality obligations, and legal compensation

category.en: Maintenance and gifting for warrior retinues

category.zh: 宴会、宗教仪式与政治接待
category.en: Feasts, religious rites, and political hospitality

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库，财富分散保存在各部族酋长的仓储、牲畜群、贵金属饰物与罗马输入品中，具有高度地域分散性
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; wealth was dispersed among the storehouses, livestock herds, precious ornaments, and Roman imports held by individual tribal chiefs, making it highly decentralized

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持酋长威望与追随者忠诚，通过再分配、馈赠、宴飨和战利品分配巩固权力，而非建立制度化征税
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining chiefly prestige and follower loyalty through redistribution, gifting, feasting, and the division of spoils rather than through institutionalized taxation

description.zh: 主力由自由民与精英战士构成，装备矛、盾、短剑、斧与少量投射武器，适合突击、伏击与林地作战
description.en: The main force consisted of free men and elite warriors armed with spears, shields, short swords, axes, and limited missile weapons, well suited for shock combat, ambush, and woodland fighting

name.en: Chiefly retinues and elite household guards

description.zh: 由酋长直接维系的职业化程度较高战士组成，承担护卫、突击和战时核心指挥职能，是最接近常备军的力量
description.en: Made up of more professional warriors maintained directly by chieftains, they served as guards, shock troops, and wartime command cores, and were the closest equivalent to a standing army

description.zh: 数量有限，主要用于侦察、追击、通信和边境袭扰，整体不如草原民族骑兵发达
description.en: Limited in number, they were used mainly for scouting, pursuit, communication, and frontier raiding, and were less developed than steppe cavalry forces

description.zh: 在大战或跨部族结盟时临时征发，训练和装备水平参差不齐，但可在短期内扩大兵力
description.en: Raised temporarily in major wars or intertribal alliances, these men varied in training and equipment but could quickly enlarge total strength

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；战时由最有威望的酋长或联盟首领统军
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; in wartime the most prestigious chieftain or alliance leader took command

technology.zh: 武器以铁制长矛、盾牌、剑、刀斧和简易护具为主，善于利用森林、河流和沼泽地形；缺乏攻城技术与复杂后勤体系
technology.en: Weapons centered on iron spears, shields, swords, seaxes, axes, and simple armor, with strong use of forests, rivers, and marsh terrain; siegecraft and complex logistics were limited

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间冲突、与罗马边境的战争或报复行动，以及东部和东南方向的萨尔马提亚与达契亚势力竞争
