threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal conflict, wars or punitive actions along the Roman frontier, and competition with Sarmatian and Dacian powers to the east and southeast

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于北海沿岸、日德兰、易北河与莱茵河以东、维斯瓦河流域及中欧北部森林地带，聚落规模通常较小，人口密度偏低
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the North Sea coast, Jutland, lands east of the Elbe and Rhine, the Vistula basin, and the forest zones of northern Central Europe, with generally small settlements and low population density

name.zh: 无真正城市；以大型部族聚落和贸易据点为主
name.en: No true cities; large tribal settlements and trade centers predominated

name.zh: 维斯瓦河口琥珀贸易据点

socialClasses.zh: 上层为酋长与贵族家族，其次为战士随从和自由民农牧者，再下为依附者、战俘与奴隶；社会流动性部分取决于战功与追随关系
socialClasses.en: At the top stood chieftains and noble families, followed by warrior retainers and free farming-pastoral households, then dependents, captives, and slaves; some social mobility depended on military achievement and patronage ties

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系；部分边境部族与罗马结盟或受其扶植，另一些则与邻近日耳曼、达契亚或萨尔马提亚群体建立临时同盟
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; some frontier tribes allied with or were supported by Rome, while others formed temporary coalitions with neighboring Germanic, Dacian, or Sarmatian groups

enemies.zh: 无统一国家层面的固定敌国，但许多部族与罗马边防军、敌对日耳曼邻部、达契亚人及萨尔马提亚骑兵存在周期性冲突
enemies.en: There were no fixed enemies at a state level, but many tribes experienced recurrent conflict with Roman frontier forces, rival Germanic neighbors, Dacians, and Sarmatian cavalry peoples

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以现实主义和机会主义为主，围绕贸易、掠夺、迁徙、婚姻联盟、人质交换和罗马边境外交展开
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and opportunistic, revolving around trade, raiding, migration, marriage alliances, hostage exchange, and diplomacy with the Roman frontier

era.zh: 欧洲铁器时代晚期至罗马帝国边疆接触时代
era.en: Late European Iron Age in the age of Roman frontier contact

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的铁器冶炼、木构长屋、纺织与皮革工艺、河海小船、琥珀贸易网络和依地形作战的战术经验是其主要技术特点
keyInnovations.en: Key technological features included mature ironworking, timber longhouses, textile and leather craft, riverine and coastal small craft, amber trade networks, and tactical experience adapted to difficult terrain

strengths.zh: 亲族凝聚力强，战士文化旺盛，地形适应能力出色，社会组织灵活，对罗马边境有持续压力，并能通过贸易吸收外来物资与观念
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in strong kin cohesion, a vigorous warrior culture, excellent adaptation to local terrain, flexible social organization, sustained pressure on the Roman frontier, and the ability to absorb goods and ideas through trade

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、城市体系、书写行政和稳定税收，内部竞争频繁，后勤和攻城能力有限，难以长期维持大规模统一行动
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included the lack of a unified polity, urban system, written administration, and stable taxation, along with frequent internal rivalry and limited logistics and siege capacity, making sustained large-scale coordinated action difficult

outlook.zh: 至100年时整体局势稳定，但罗马边境压力、贸易吸引和部族整合趋势将逐渐促成更大的联盟与迁徙运动，长期看会深刻改变欧洲北部与西部的政治格局
outlook.en: By 100 CE the overall situation was stable, but pressure from the Roman frontier, the attraction of trade, and ongoing tribal consolidation would gradually encourage larger confederations and migrations, ultimately reshaping the political order of northern and western Europe

description.zh: 莱茵河与易北河以东分布着众多日耳曼部族，尚未形成统一国家但对罗马边界影响深远。
description.en: Numerous Germanic peoples lived east of the Rhine and Elbe, lacking a unified state but exerting deep influence on Rome’s frontiers.

name.zh: 高卢与伊比利亚行省社会
高卢—伊比利亚罗马文明

capital.zh: 卢格杜努姆、塔拉科及其他行省中心
capital.en: Lugdunum, Tarraco, and other provincial centers

governmentForm.zh: 帝国统治下的行省—城市共和体制，保留地方元老院、殖民市与自治市机构
governmentForm.en: Provincial-urban republican system under imperial rule, retaining local senates, coloniae, and municipia institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以罗马公民精英、地方贵族、市民、自由民、佃农、奴隶和边疆部族附属群体构成；高卢与伊比利亚地区的旧部族贵族大多已融入罗马城市政治与税收体系。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Roman citizen elites, local aristocrats, townspeople, freedmen, tenant farmers, slaves, and subordinate frontier tribal groups; much of the old Gallic and Iberian nobility had been absorbed into Roman urban politics and the tax system.

rulingClass.zh: 帝国任命的行省总督、骑士与元老阶层、城市议员阶层以及大地产主
rulingClass.en: Imperial-appointed provincial governors, equestrian and senatorial elites, municipal councilors, and great landowners

succession.zh: 最高统治权依罗马帝国的收养、元老院认可与军队拥戴相结合的原则传承；地方层面则多由家族寡头通过市政选举和财产资格延续权力。
succession.en: Supreme authority was transmitted through the Roman imperial blend of adoption, senatorial recognition, and military support; at the local level, power usually continued through family oligarchies via municipal election and property qualifications.

structure.zh: 该区域并非独立国家，而是由高卢与西班牙诸行省组成的罗马西部核心区。最高权力属于皇帝，日常治理由各行省总督、财政官、军区指挥官和城市议会执行；卢格杜努姆是高卢行政与宗教中心，塔拉科是西班牙的重要总督驻地。
structure.en: This region was not an independent state but a western core zone of the Roman Empire composed of the Gallic and Hispanic provinces. Supreme authority lay with the emperor, while routine governance was carried out by provincial governors, financial procurators, military commanders, and city councils; Lugdunum served as an administrative and religious hub for Gaul, while Tarraco was a major gubernatorial center in Hispania.

function.zh: 统辖司法、治安、税务监督、与地方城市议会协调，并向皇帝与罗马中央报告。
function.en: Oversaw justice, public order, tax supervision, coordination with local city councils, and reporting to the emperor and the Roman center.

function.zh: 负责土地税、间接税、矿业收入、关税征收，以及皇室财产管理。
function.en: Managed land taxes, indirect taxes, mining revenues, customs collection, and imperial estates.

function.zh: 维护道路、桥梁、渡口、引水道、公共浴场、城墙及港口设施。
function.en: Maintained roads, bridges, ferries, aqueducts, public baths, walls, and port facilities.

function.zh: 保管法律文书、人口与财产登记，处理上诉、契约、公民身份和地方纠纷。
function.en: Kept legal records, population and property registrations, and handled appeals, contracts, citizenship matters, and local disputes.

name.en: Military Supply and Frontier Coordination Office

function.zh: 统筹驻军粮秣、道路转运、马匹补给、要塞仓储及与莱茵边防的联系。
function.en: Coordinated provisions for garrisons, road transport, horse supply, fortress storage, and links to the Rhine frontier.

localAdmin.zh: 基层以殖民市、自治市和旧部族辖区改造而来的城市共同体为核心，地方议会由富裕地主和商人组成，负责征税、治安、祭祀和公共建设。乡村地区由大庄园、村社和道路站点连接至城市网络。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on coloniae, municipia, and urban communities transformed from older tribal districts. Municipal councils made up of wealthy landowners and merchants handled taxation, policing, cults, and public works. Rural areas were tied to the city network through estates, villages, and road stations.

legalSystem.zh: 实行罗马法与地方习惯法并行但以罗马法为上位原则的制度。公民享有较完备的法律保护，非公民与属民则受较严格的税役和司法约束；总督巡回审判在重大案件中具有决定性作用。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman law with surviving local custom, but Roman law held superior authority. Citizens enjoyed stronger legal protections, while non-citizens and subject peoples faced heavier fiscal and judicial constraints; the governor's circuit courts were decisive in major cases.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括土地税、人头与财产评估、矿山收益、港口与道路关税、市场税和遗产相关征收。许多税项由行省财政官直接管理，部分间接税仍以承包或地方代征方式实施。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included land tax, poll and property assessments, mining income, port and road customs, market duties, and inheritance-related levies. Many taxes were managed directly by provincial fiscal officers, though some indirect taxes were still farmed or locally collected.

religion.zh: 宗教生活以罗马国家祭祀、皇帝崇拜、本地凯尔特与伊比利亚神祇罗马化、以及少量东方宗教并存为特点。基督教在公元100年前后尚极为边缘。
religion.en: Religious life was marked by the coexistence of Roman state cults, the imperial cult, Romanized local Celtic and Iberian deities, and a limited presence of eastern religions. Christianity remained extremely marginal around 100 CE.

philosophy.zh: 公共伦理受罗马实用主义、斯多亚主义和传统市民德性影响，地方精英强调忠于帝国、公共捐献、法律秩序与家族荣誉。
philosophy.en: Public ethics were shaped by Roman pragmatism, Stoicism, and traditional civic virtue; local elites emphasized loyalty to empire, public benefaction, legal order, and family honor.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母与拉丁文；希腊文在少数商业和学术场合可见。地方凯尔特与伊比利亚语言仍在口语层面存在，但官方铭文、法令和契约以拉丁文为主。
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was the Latin alphabet and Latin language; Greek appeared in limited commercial and learned settings. Local Celtic and Iberian languages still survived in speech, but official inscriptions, decrees, and contracts were predominantly in Latin.

culturalAchievements.zh: 高卢与西班牙地区在道路、桥梁、引水道、剧场、竞技场、城墙、浴场和纪念建筑方面成就显著。葡萄酒与橄榄油贸易、镶嵌艺术、拉丁铭文学和城市赞助文化高度发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Gaul and Hispania excelled in roads, bridges, aqueducts, theaters, amphitheaters, walls, baths, and monumental architecture. Wine and olive oil commerce, mosaic art, Latin epigraphy, and civic patronage culture were highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系，以拉丁语为行政和高文化语言，并伴有凯尔特语支、伊比利亚半岛地方语言残存
languageFamily.en: Indo-European family, with Latin as the language of administration and high culture, alongside surviving Celtic branches and residual local languages of the Iberian Peninsula

gdpDescription.zh: 高卢与伊比利亚是罗马西部最富庶区域之一，农业高度商品化，矿业与手工业发达，税基稳固，城市网络密集，并通过大西洋、地中海和内陆河谷与帝国市场紧密连接。
gdpDescription.en: Gaul and Hispania were among the wealthiest regions of the Roman West, with highly commercialized agriculture, strong mining and craft production, a stable tax base, dense urban networks, and close integration into imperial markets via the Atlantic, the Mediterranean, and inland river valleys.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄酒、橄榄油、畜牧、银金铜铁铅矿开采、制陶、腌鱼与鱼酱、纺织、盐业、建筑与运输
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine, olive oil, livestock, extraction of silver, gold, copper, iron, and lead, pottery, salted fish and garum, textiles, salt production, construction, and transport

tradeGoods.zh: 西班牙银与金、贝提卡橄榄油、塔拉科与高卢葡萄酒、陶器、金属器、盐、鱼酱、皮革、奴隶、马匹、琥珀转运品与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Spanish silver and gold, Baetican olive oil, Tarraconensian and Gallic wine, pottery, metalwares, salt, garum, leather, slaves, horses, amber transit goods, and luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 财富分化极大。城市精英和矿业承包者拥有庄园、奴隶和现金储备；一般农户拥有少量土地、牲畜和工具；无地佃户与城市贫民则更依赖劳役、季节工作和施惠网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Urban elites and mining contractors controlled estates, slaves, and cash reserves; ordinary farmers held small plots, livestock, and tools; landless tenants and urban poor depended more on labor, seasonal work, and patronage networks.

economicSystem.zh: 以私有财产、奴隶劳动、佃农经营、市场交换和帝国税收为基础的罗马行省经济。国家通过道路、法制、军队与货币稳定促进商业，但生产核心仍由庄园、矿场、城市作坊与地方市场驱动。
economicSystem.en: A Roman provincial economy based on private property, slave labor, tenant cultivation, market exchange, and imperial taxation. The state promoted commerce through roads, law, the army, and monetary stability, but the productive core remained estates, mines, urban workshops, and local markets.

source.en: Customs, port dues, and market taxes

category.zh: 皇帝祭祀、粮秣储备与应急开支
category.en: Imperial cult, grain reserves, and contingencies

treasuryDescription.zh: 本区积累了可观的税收和现金流，但绝大部分财政并非完全在地方留存，而是服务于帝国整体财政、军费和公共工程。高卢与伊比利亚因矿业和农业出口而拥有较强财政韧性。
treasuryDescription.en: The region accumulated considerable tax income and cash flow, but most finances were not retained purely locally, instead serving broader imperial finance, military needs, and public works. Gaul and Hispania had strong fiscal resilience because of mining and agricultural exports.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体稳健，强调维持税源、改善道路港口、保障军需和鼓励地方精英承担公共捐献。图拉真时期财政尚能在扩张、建设与稳定税制之间取得平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly stable, emphasizing preservation of the tax base, improvement of roads and ports, maintenance of military supply, and encouragement of local elite benefaction. Under Trajan, finances still balanced expansion, building, and a stable tax regime.

description.zh: 主要部署于莱茵防线及其后方基地，负责对日耳曼边境威慑、筑城、巡逻和野战行动。
description.en: Mainly deployed on the Rhine frontier and its rear bases, responsible for deterrence against the Germanic border, fortification, patrols, and field operations.

description.zh: 由高卢、伊比利亚及其他行省征募的非军团部队组成，包括步兵、骑兵、轻装兵和专长部队，是西部边防的骨干。
description.en: Composed of non-legionary troops recruited from Gaul, Hispania, and other provinces, including infantry, cavalry, light troops, and specialist units; they formed the backbone of western frontier defense.

name.en: Urban Garrison and Coastal Security Forces

description.zh: 分布于主要城市、港口和交通节点，承担治安、仓储保护、港口监管和反走私任务。
description.en: Distributed among major cities, ports, and transport nodes, handling security, protection of depots, port oversight, and anti-smuggling duties.

description.zh: 由退伍军人社群、地方民兵和临时征发人员构成，用于道路守卫、补给和紧急防务。
description.en: Made up of veteran communities, local militia, and temporarily levied personnel, used for road guard, supply, and emergency defense.

technology.zh: 军队装备精良，广泛使用锁子甲、分节甲、短剑、投枪、复合工兵器材、攻城器和标准化营地工程。道路网与仓储系统显著提升了西部军队的机动和补给能力。
technology.en: The military was well equipped, widely using mail, segmental armor, short swords, pila, advanced engineering tools, siege equipment, and standardized camp construction. The road network and depot system greatly improved mobility and supply for western forces.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自莱茵彼岸日耳曼部族、北部海岸海盗与走私、以及帝国范围内对军粮和兵员的持续高需求。伊比利亚本土较为安定，但高卢北部和边疆地带始终需要重兵防守。
threats.en: The main threats came from Germanic peoples beyond the Rhine, piracy and smuggling along northern coasts, and the empire-wide constant demand for military grain and manpower. Hispania itself was relatively secure, but northern Gaul and frontier zones required sustained heavy defense.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

description.zh: 匈奴/帕提亚式复合弓，骑射核心武器
description.en: Xiongnu/Parthian composite bow, core mounted archery weapon

description.zh: 汉军标准配刀，单刃直背，劈斩利器
description.en: Standard Han army saber, single-edged straight back, ideal for slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口规模庞大，主要集中于高卢河谷、伊比利亚沿海、贝提卡农业区和若干矿区。整体人口在图拉真时期处于稳定增长与高流动状态，退伍军人安置和城市扩张推动了罗马化深入乡村。
populationDescription.en: The population was large, concentrated mainly in the river valleys of Gaul, the Iberian coasts, the agricultural zone of Baetica, and several mining districts. Under Trajan, population was broadly stable and slowly growing, while veteran settlement and urban expansion drove Romanization deeper into the countryside.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大体包括帝国元老与骑士、行省和城市贵族、退伍军人与中等地主、商人和工匠、自由农、小佃户、自由民、奴隶。高卢和伊比利亚旧部族身份仍有社会记忆，但在城市与法律层面逐渐让位于罗马公民身份与财产等级。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included imperial senators and equestrians, provincial and municipal aristocrats, veterans and middling landowners, merchants and artisans, free peasants, small tenants, freedmen, and slaves. Older tribal identities in Gaul and Hispania still lingered socially, but in urban and legal life they were increasingly displaced by Roman citizenship and property rank.

allies.zh: 作为罗马帝国组成部分，其盟友实质上即帝国内部诸行省与附庸王国；在西部与不列颠、埃及及意大利核心区维持紧密协作。
allies.en: As part of the Roman Empire, its allies were effectively the other imperial provinces and client kingdoms; it maintained close coordination with Britannia, Egypt, and the Italian core in the West.

enemies.zh: 直接敌对者主要是莱茵对岸的日耳曼部族，以及在更广帝国战略层面的达契亚人与帕提亚等强敌。
enemies.en: Its direct enemies were chiefly the Germanic peoples beyond the Rhine, and in broader imperial strategy, major foes such as the Dacians and Parthians.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策服从罗马帝国整体战略：确保西部省份稳定、持续向边疆和远征提供税收与兵员、保护大西洋和地中海贸易线，并以城市化和公民化巩固统治。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy followed overall Roman imperial strategy: keeping the western provinces stable, continuously supplying taxes and manpower to frontiers and campaigns, protecting Atlantic and Mediterranean trade routes, and consolidating rule through urbanization and extension of civic institutions.

era.zh: 罗马帝国盛期的高古典铁器时代
era.en: High Classical Iron Age at the height of the Roman Empire

keyInnovations.zh: 标准化道路和桥梁工程、引水系统、拱券与混凝土建筑、深井采矿和排水技术、规模化陶器生产、军营与仓储后勤体系、货币化税收与文书管理
keyInnovations.en: Standardized road and bridge engineering, aqueduct systems, arch and concrete construction, deep-shaft mining and drainage techniques, large-scale pottery production, military camp and depot logistics, monetized taxation, and documentary administration

strengths.zh: 农业、矿业和城市经济发达，税收能力强，道路港口完善，人口与兵员充足，地方精英高度罗马化并愿意参与治理，是帝国西部最可靠的财政与后勤支柱之一。
strengths.en: It possessed advanced agriculture, mining, and urban economies, strong revenue capacity, excellent roads and ports, ample population and manpower, and local elites highly Romanized and willing to participate in governance, making it one of the most reliable fiscal and logistical pillars of the western empire.

weaknesses.zh: 政治上缺乏独立性，命运受帝国中央与边疆战争牵引；贫富分化明显，部分地区对奴隶劳动和大地产依赖较深，莱茵边境的安全压力长期存在。
weaknesses.en: Politically it lacked independence, with its fate tied to the imperial center and frontier wars; inequality was pronounced, some districts depended heavily on slave labor and large estates, and security pressures along the Rhine remained chronic.

outlook.zh: 在公元100年前后前景极佳。只要帝国中央继续维持军队、道路和货币稳定，高卢与伊比利亚将继续繁荣，并在未来数代内保持西部最重要的税收、贸易和兵员来源地位。
outlook.en: Its outlook around 100 CE was excellent. So long as the imperial center continued to maintain the army, roads, and currency stability, Gaul and Hispania would continue to prosper and remain among the most important western sources of taxes, trade, and manpower for generations.

description.zh: 高卢与伊比利亚已深度罗马化，是帝国西部财政、军队与城市网络的重要支柱。
description.en: Gaul and Hispania were deeply Romanized and formed crucial western pillars of imperial finance, manpower, and urban life.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族君主制与酋邦联盟
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal monarchies and chiefdom confederacies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族与部族关系为核心，贵族武士阶层居上，德鲁伊与祭司掌握宗教与传统知识，自由民包括农民、牧民、工匠与战士，另有依附民、奴隶与战俘。不同地区如加里多尼亚、威尔士与康沃尔的结构略有差异，但总体上以地方性权威和个人效忠为基础。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around kinship, clans, and tribes. A noble warrior elite stood at the top, while druids and ritual specialists held religious and customary authority. Freemen included farmers, pastoralists, artisans, and fighting men, with dependents, slaves, and captives beneath them. Regional variation existed in Caledonia, Wales, and the southwest, but authority generally rested on local power and personal allegiance.

rulingClass.zh: 部族王族、军事贵族与德鲁伊精英
rulingClass.en: Tribal royal families, military aristocrats, and druidic elites

succession.zh: 王位通常在部族显贵家族内部继承或由贵族推举，成年且有战功者更易获得支持，并非严格长子继承。
succession.en: Succession usually remained within leading tribal families or was confirmed by aristocratic selection; mature and militarily successful candidates were often preferred rather than strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 不列吞世界在100年并无统一国家。北方与西方诸部族如塞尔戈瓦人、沃塔迪尼人、德姆奈人、奥陶维斯人、锡勒ures人及多支加里多尼亚部族由各自首领统治，必要时组成临时联盟，以抵御罗马推进或进行跨部族战争。决策多由王庭、贵族议会、战士随从与宗教人士共同影响。
structure.en: The Brittonic world in 100 CE was not a single state. Northern and western tribes such as the Selgovae, Votadini, Damnonii, Ordovices, Silures, and various Caledonian groups were ruled by their own leaders, who could form temporary alliances to resist Roman advances or wage intertribal war. Decisions were shaped by royal households, noble councils, warrior retinues, and religious authorities.

function.zh: 管理首领住所、宴飨、贡赋储存、使者接待与随从武士组织，是部族统治核心。
function.en: Managed the ruler's residence, feasting, tribute storage, reception of envoys, and organization of the warrior retinue; this was the core of tribal rule.

function.zh: 由地方贵族、支系首领与资深战士组成，就战争、盟约、继承与赔偿等重大事务提供决定或共识。
function.en: Composed of local nobles, sub-chiefs, and senior warriors, it decided or advised on war, alliances, succession, and compensation matters.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、口传法律、誓约见证、历法与教育，并在部族争端中担任仲裁角色。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, customary law, oath witnessing, calendrical knowledge, and education, and often arbitrated tribal disputes.

function.zh: 征集牲畜、谷物、金属、皮革与劳役，管理山堡或王廷附近的储藏点。
function.en: Collected cattle, grain, metals, hides, and labor dues, and managed storage points near hillforts or royal centers.

function.zh: 负责召集战士、传递警报、侦察罗马动向并守卫部族边界与山地通道。
function.en: Handled mustering warriors, sending alarms, scouting Roman movements, and guarding tribal frontiers and upland routes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由部族分支首领、聚落长者和贵族家族执行。山堡、谷地聚居点和牧场网络构成实际统治单元，行政更多依赖面对面协商、礼物交换、担保人与血缘关系，而非文书官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by sub-tribal chiefs, settlement elders, and noble families. Hillforts, valley settlements, and pastoral networks formed the real units of rule. Governance relied more on face-to-face negotiation, gift exchange, guarantors, and kinship than on a written bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、赔偿原则、誓言与仲裁为主，杀伤、盗窃、婚姻、边界和牲畜纠纷常通过赔偿和人质安排解决。严重冲突则诉诸械斗、复仇或贵族调停。受罗马接触较多的边缘地区可能逐渐吸收某些罗马法律观念，但核心仍为凯尔特习俗。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on custom, compensation, oaths, and arbitration. Injury, theft, marriage, boundary, and livestock disputes were often settled through payments and hostage arrangements. Severe disputes could lead to feud, armed retaliation, or noble mediation. Border regions with greater Roman contact may have adopted some Roman legal ideas, but Celtic custom remained dominant.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，首领主要通过贡赋、战利品分配、牲畜征收、谷物与手工业产品上贡、宴飨义务和劳役来维持权力。对外贸易通道与金属资源也可为特定部族首领带来额外收入。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system. Rulers derived power chiefly from tribute, distribution of war booty, levies in cattle, dues in grain and craft goods, obligations to provide feasts, and labor service. Trade routes and metal resources could also give certain tribal rulers additional income.

religion.zh: 宗教以凯尔特多神信仰为基础，崇拜与河流、泉水、树林、山丘和战争、丰产、治愈相关的诸神与地方神灵。德鲁伊、预言者和祭司在祭祀、占卜、誓约和记忆传统中扮演重要角色。部分南部与边疆地区在与罗马接触中出现神祇融合现象，但北西部仍以本土仪式为主。
religion.en: Religion was based on Celtic polytheism, with worship of deities and local spirits associated with rivers, springs, groves, hills, war, fertility, and healing. Druids, seers, and ritual specialists played major roles in sacrifice, divination, oath-taking, and the preservation of tradition. In some southern and frontier areas Roman contact encouraged syncretism, but the north and west remained predominantly native in ritual practice.

philosophy.zh: 没有类似地中海世界成体系的哲学学派，思想传统主要体现在德鲁伊的宇宙观、英雄伦理、祖先记忆、口传法俗与诗歌智慧中，重视勇武、名誉、款待、誓言与部族身份。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical school comparable to those of the Mediterranean. Intellectual tradition survived chiefly in druidic cosmology, heroic ethics, ancestral memory, customary law, and poetic wisdom, emphasizing valor, honor, hospitality, oath-keeping, and tribal identity.

writingSystem.zh: 本土文化主要依赖口传。拉丁文字在罗马边境接触区为人所知，但在独立不列吞部族内部未形成广泛行政书写传统。铭文极少，记忆、诗歌与仪式背诵远比书写更重要。
writingSystem.en: Native culture relied primarily on oral transmission. The Latin alphabet was known in zones of Roman contact, but independent Brittonic tribes did not develop a broad administrative writing tradition. Inscriptions were rare, and memory, poetry, and ritual recitation mattered far more than writing.

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就包括精致的金属工艺、珐琅装饰、战士装备、扭颈环与首饰、木工与车轮技术、山堡和环形防御工事、口传史诗与吟游诗传统，以及复杂的社会宴飨文化。与罗马接触也促使部分物质文化吸收地中海器物与风格。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included fine metalworking, enamel decoration, warrior equipment, torcs and jewelry, woodworking and wheeled transport, hillforts and enclosed defenses, oral epic and bardic traditions, and a sophisticated culture of feasting. Contact with Rome also encouraged selective adoption of Mediterranean goods and styles.

languageFamily.zh: 凯尔特语族不列吞支
languageFamily.en: Brittonic branch of the Celtic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧业为主，市场交换有限，财富更多表现为土地控制、牲畜数量、金属器物和人身依附，而非统一货币流通。与罗马不列颠行省的边贸为部分部族带来额外收益，尤其是牲畜、奴隶、皮革、猎物和金属交换。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily based on subsistence agriculture and pastoralism, with limited market exchange. Wealth was expressed more through land control, livestock holdings, metal goods, and personal dependents than through uniform monetary circulation. Border trade with Roman Britain brought additional gains to some tribes, especially through exchange in cattle, slaves, hides, game products, and metals.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧牛养羊、铁器锻造、铜合金工艺、皮革加工、盐和渔猎、木工、车具制作与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, cattle and sheep pastoralism, ironworking, bronze craft, leatherworking, salt and fishing-hunting, woodworking, cart-making, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、羊毛、谷物、盐、奴隶、铁器、猎物、毛皮、铅与少量金银饰品；输入品包括罗马酒、玻璃器、陶器、金属器、珠饰与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, hides, wool, grain, salt, slaves, iron goods, hunting products, furs, lead, and small quantities of gold and silver ornaments; imports included Roman wine, glassware, pottery, metalwares, beads, and luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在牛羊、耕地使用权、粮食储备、住房、工具和亲族支持网络。贵族家庭则以大批牲畜、武器、饰物、宴飨能力和追随者人数显示财富。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was measured mainly in cattle, sheep, access to farmland, grain stores, dwellings, tools, and kin support networks. Noble households displayed wealth through large herds, weapons, ornaments, feasting capacity, and the number of followers they maintained.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、部族贡赋和礼物交换为基础的混合经济，兼具自给农业、掠夺、边贸和酋邦再分配。部分沿海与边境地区更深卷入罗马经济圈，但整体仍属低货币化经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on household production, tribal tribute, and gift exchange, combining subsistence farming, raiding, border trade, and chiefly redistribution. Some coastal and frontier zones were more deeply drawn into the Roman economic sphere, but the economy as a whole remained weakly monetized.

category.zh: 宗教仪式与德鲁伊支持
category.en: Ritual observances and support for druids

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国家金库。各大部族首领分别保有牲畜群、金属器、罗马输入品、粮仓和礼物储备，财富高度分散，且多用于维系政治忠诚而非长期积累。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single state treasury. Major tribal rulers held separate reserves of herds, metal goods, imported Roman items, grain stores, and gift wealth. Resources were highly dispersed and were used mainly to sustain political loyalty rather than for long-term centralized accumulation.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践以再分配为核心：首领收取贡赋后通过宴飨、赏赐、武装保护与婚盟回馈支持者。战争时期更强调迅速动员物资和牲畜，而非制度化预算。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on redistribution: rulers collected tribute and then returned it to supporters through feasts, gifts, protection, and marriage alliances. In wartime, the emphasis was on rapid mobilization of goods and livestock rather than on institutional budgeting.

description.zh: 主力为持矛、盾和剑的步兵，依托地形、突袭与密集冲锋作战，装备差异很大，贵族亲兵质量较高。
description.en: The main force consisted of infantry armed with spears, shields, and swords, fighting through terrain use, ambush, and aggressive charges. Equipment varied greatly, with noble retainers generally better armed.

description.zh: 由王族和贵族维持的核心武装，战斗经验较丰富，承担护卫、突击和指挥任务，是事实上的常备力量。
description.en: These were the core armed followers maintained by kings and nobles, more experienced in combat and responsible for guard duty, shock action, and leadership; they formed the nearest equivalent to a standing force.

description.zh: 使用标枪、投石索与少量弓箭，适于山地、林地和伏击战，在骚扰罗马部队补给线时尤其有用。
description.en: Armed with javelins, slings, and some bows, they were suited for hills, forests, and ambush warfare, especially effective in harassing Roman supply lines.

description.zh: 战车在部分地区仍具声望和战术用途，更多用于首领展示、机动与精锐投送；真正成体系骑兵规模有限。
description.en: Chariots still retained prestige and some tactical use in certain areas, serving especially for elite display, mobility, and transport of champions; true organized cavalry remained limited in scale.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令；战时由最强部族王或联盟首领统率
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; in war, leadership fell to the strongest tribal king or confederate war leader

technology.zh: 军事技术处于铁器时代晚期，使用铁矛、长剑、盾牌、锁子甲与头盔的范围有限，战车在局部延续。工事以山堡、土垒、木栅和天然险阻为主。相较罗马军团，在攻城、工程、纪律和补给上明显落后，但在熟悉地形与游击袭扰方面具有优势。
technology.en: Military technology belonged to the later Iron Age, with iron spears, long swords, shields, and limited use of mail and helmets. Chariots survived in some areas. Fortifications relied on hillforts, earthworks, timber palisades, and natural barriers. Compared with the Roman legions, the tribes were clearly inferior in siegecraft, engineering, discipline, and logistics, but they had advantages in terrain familiarity and guerrilla harassment.

threats.zh: 最主要威胁来自罗马帝国在不列颠的持续推进，尤其是北部边区压力与对部族分化的政治操作。此外，部族间仇杀、资源争夺与联盟破裂也严重削弱抵抗能力。
threats.en: The chief threat came from the continued advance of the Roman Empire in Britain, especially pressure along the northern frontier and Roman manipulation of tribal divisions. In addition, intertribal feuding, competition for resources, and the collapse of alliances seriously weakened resistance.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布以北方低地、苏格兰南部、威尔士山地谷地和英格兰西部边缘为主，聚落多为乡村农牧社区，小型防御聚居点和山堡构成区域中心。人口密度低于罗马控制下的南不列颠。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the northern lowlands, southern Scotland, the valleys of Wales, and the western margins of England. Most settlements were rural farming and pastoral communities, with small defended sites and hillforts serving as regional centers. Population density was lower than in Roman-controlled southern Britain.

name.zh: 无真正统一城市体系；重要中心如特拉普雷因山堡
name.en: No true unified urban system; notable centers included Traprain Law

name.zh: 安格尔西祭祀与聚居中心
加里多尼亚诸山堡网络

socialClasses.zh: 大致可分为王族与显贵、德鲁伊与祭司、贵族战士、自由民农牧者与工匠、依附民以及奴隶或战俘。阶层边界受亲族、战功、婚盟与财富影响而流动，但贵族优势明显。
socialClasses.en: Society can be broadly divided into royal and noble families, druids and ritual specialists, aristocratic warriors, free farming and pastoral commoners with artisans, dependents, and slaves or captives. Boundaries were somewhat fluid through kinship, martial success, marriage alliance, and wealth, but noble advantage remained pronounced.

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系；各部族有时彼此结盟，共同抗击罗马，亦可能与罗马缔结暂时和约或成为其附庸伙伴。
allies.en: There was no durable unified alliance system; tribes at times allied with one another against Rome, though some also made temporary peace with Rome or acted as its subordinate partners.

enemies.zh: 主要外敌为罗马文明在不列颠的总督与军团；同时相邻不列吞部族间也常为牧场、威望和边界发生冲突。
enemies.en: The principal external enemy was Roman rule in Britain under its governors and legions; neighboring Brittonic tribes also frequently fought one another over pasture, prestige, and borders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策高度地方化，核心目标是维护部族独立、争夺边境优势、控制贸易通道并在必要时联合抗罗。部分部族采取灵活策略，在抵抗、议和、交换人质和接受罗马礼物之间反复调整。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was highly localized, centered on preserving tribal independence, gaining advantage along the frontier, controlling trade routes, and forming anti-Roman coalitions when necessary. Some tribes adopted flexible strategies, shifting among resistance, negotiation, hostage exchange, and acceptance of Roman gifts.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期／罗马帝国并存时代
era.en: Late Iron Age / Age of Coexisting Empires

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括铁器锻造、锁子甲的有限使用、战车传统、山堡与土木防御工事、车轮与木工工艺、珐琅金属装饰以及适应湿冷环境的农牧技术。对罗马物资和器型的接触带来部分器物改良，但整体技术体系仍以本土铁器时代传统为核心。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included ironworking, limited use of mail armor, chariot traditions, hillfort and earth-timber defenses, wheel and woodworking skills, enamel metal decoration, and farming-pastoral techniques suited to cool wet environments. Contact with Roman goods and forms brought some improvements in material culture, but the overall technological system remained rooted in native Iron Age traditions.

strengths.zh: 优势在于熟悉山地、森林与沼泽地形，社会动员依托亲族网络迅速，战士文化强烈，地方防御体系分散而顽强，并能在与罗马接触中吸收部分贸易利益和物质技术。
strengths.en: Strengths included intimate knowledge of hills, forests, and marshes, rapid mobilization through kin networks, a strong warrior culture, resilient and decentralized local defenses, and the ability to absorb some trade benefits and material techniques through contact with Rome.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是缺乏统一政权、常设官僚和稳定联盟，内部竞争严重，经济货币化程度低，后勤与攻城能力不足，难以在长期正面战争中与罗马军团抗衡。
weaknesses.en: The main weaknesses were the absence of unified political authority, permanent bureaucracy, and stable alliances; severe internal competition; low monetization; and inadequate logistics and siege capability, making sustained direct war against Roman legions difficult.

outlook.zh: 短期内北方和西方不列吞部族仍可凭借地形、分散抵抗与罗马边境压力维持相当自治，但若无法形成持久联合，其势力将逐步受罗马军事、贸易和政治影响侵蚀。北方加里多尼亚和威尔士高地最有可能长期保持本土结构。
outlook.en: In the short term, the northern and western Brittonic tribes could maintain substantial autonomy through terrain, dispersed resistance, and pressure on the Roman frontier. But without durable political unity, their power would gradually be eroded by Roman military, commercial, and political influence. Caledonia and the Welsh uplands were the areas most likely to preserve native structures for the longest period.

description.zh: 罗马未完全征服的不列颠北部与西部仍由凯尔特部族控制，与帝国边军持续接触和冲突。
description.en: Northern and western Britain remained under Celtic tribal control beyond full Roman rule, with ongoing contact and conflict.

governmentForm.zh: 罗马行省君主制与埃及祭司性王权相结合的神权化总督统治，皇帝兼具法老象征地位，地方行政依罗马法与希腊化城市制度运行。
governmentForm.en: A sacralized provincial monarchy combining Roman imperial rule with Egyptian priestly kingship, in which the emperor held symbolic pharaonic status while local administration operated through Roman law and Hellenistic civic institutions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈多层次结构：顶层为皇帝及其任命的骑士阶层总督与财政官；其下为希腊化城市精英、大神庙祭司、罗马军官与退伍殖民者；中层为村社首领、抄写员、商人、手工业者与船主；底层为广大农民、佃户、码头劳工与奴隶。族群上由埃及人、希腊人、犹太人、罗马人及其他东方商旅共同构成。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified: at the top stood the emperor and his appointed equestrian prefects and financial officers; beneath them were Hellenized urban elites, major temple priesthoods, Roman officers, and veteran settlers; the middle strata included village headmen, scribes, merchants, artisans, and shipowners; the lower strata consisted of peasants, tenants, dockworkers, and slaves. Ethnically it comprised Egyptians, Greeks, Jews, Romans, and other eastern trading communities.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由皇帝代表的罗马中央政权、驻埃及的骑士阶层官员、亚历山大里亚与诺姆城市的希腊语精英，以及与国家财政合作的大型神庙祭司集团构成。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted primarily of the Roman central authority embodied by the emperor, equestrian officials stationed in Egypt, Greek-speaking elites of Alexandria and the nome capitals, and major temple priesthoods cooperating with the fiscal state.

succession.zh: 最高统治权依罗马帝国皇位继承而定，埃及本身无独立王朝继承制；法老身份附属于罗马皇帝，由其即位后自动承继。
succession.en: Supreme authority followed succession to the Roman imperial throne; Egypt itself had no separate dynastic succession, and the pharaonic status was attached to the Roman emperor and passed automatically upon his accession.

structure.zh: 埃及在100年为罗马皇帝直属的特殊行省，由埃及长官统辖，不受元老院控制。中央行政核心设于亚历山大里亚，下辖各诺姆行政治理单位、税务系统、军队驻防体系、港务与粮运机构，并与城市议事会、神庙网络和村社文书体系相互配合。
structure.en: In 100 CE Egypt was a special imperial province directly subject to the Roman emperor and governed by the Prefect of Egypt outside senatorial control. Its administrative core was based at Alexandria and oversaw the nome system, taxation machinery, military garrisons, port and grain transport authorities, while coordinating with city councils, temple networks, and village scribal administration.

name.en: Office of the Prefect of Egypt

function.zh: 统筹全省民政、司法、治安、军务协调及与皇帝的直接联系。
function.en: Directed provincial civil administration, justice, public order, military coordination, and direct communication with the emperor.

function.zh: 征收土地税、人头税、关税与专卖收入，管理国有土地、仓储和账册。
function.en: Collected land taxes, poll taxes, customs duties, and monopoly revenues, while managing state lands, granaries, and fiscal registers.

function.zh: 监督尼罗河谷征粮、仓储、驳船运输及对罗马与地中海市场的出口调配。
function.en: Supervised grain assessment in the Nile Valley, storage, barge transport, and export allocation to Rome and Mediterranean markets.

name.en: Ports and Red Sea Trade Administration

function.zh: 管理亚历山大里亚港、科普特斯至红海商道、贝勒尼凯与米奥斯霍尔摩斯等口岸及对印度洋贸易征税。
function.en: Managed the port of Alexandria, the Coptos-to-Red Sea caravan route, harbors such as Berenike and Myos Hormos, and taxation of Indian Ocean trade.

function.zh: 保存希腊文、拉丁文与埃及文契据，处理诉讼、户籍、地产和行政申诉。
function.en: Maintained Greek, Latin, and Egyptian documentary records and handled litigation, census rolls, landholding registrations, and administrative petitions.

function.zh: 监管获准神庙财产、祭司任命、礼仪秩序，并确保地方宗教不妨碍罗马统治与税收。
function.en: Oversaw authorized temple estates, priestly appointments, and ritual order, ensuring local cults did not interfere with Roman rule or taxation.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以诺姆为核心，由战略官、税务官、抄写员和村社首领层层执行。希腊化城市如亚历山大里亚拥有较强市政机构，乡村则依赖文书、土地丈量员和神庙相关人员完成征税与劳役组织。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the nomes, where strategoi, tax officials, scribes, and village headmen implemented policy. Hellenized cities such as Alexandria possessed stronger civic institutions, while the countryside relied on document clerks, land surveyors, and temple-linked personnel to organize taxation and labor obligations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并行混合：罗马公法支配军政与皇室事务，希腊化城市广泛使用希腊文行政法习惯，民事层面仍保留部分埃及传统契约实践。诉讼常以书面请愿、登记文书和地方裁决为主，皇帝敕令具有最高效力。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was plural and hybrid: Roman public law governed military and imperial affairs, Greek administrative legal practice dominated urban institutions, and some Egyptian contractual traditions survived in civil matters. Litigation commonly proceeded through written petitions, registered documents, and local adjudication, with imperial edicts carrying supreme authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、人头税、灌溉相关征收、港口关税、商业税和若干国家专卖为核心。税收多以谷物、现金和实物并征，尼罗河谷的精密丈量与户籍登记使埃及成为帝国最有效率的税源地之一。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on land tax, poll tax, irrigation-related assessments, port customs, commercial dues, and several state monopolies. Taxes were collected in grain, cash, and kind, and the Nile Valley's precise land surveys and census registration made Egypt one of the empire's most efficient fiscal regions.

religion.zh: 宗教高度多元，传统埃及诸神崇拜、希腊化塞拉比斯与伊西斯信仰、罗马皇帝崇拜、犹太教社群宗教生活并存。皇帝作为法老在官方象征中维系玛阿特式秩序，但实际祭祀由地方神庙与城市宗教机构执行。
religion.en: Religion was highly plural, combining traditional Egyptian cults, Hellenistic worship of Serapis and Isis, the Roman imperial cult, and the communal religious life of Jewish populations. The emperor as pharaoh symbolically upheld a ma'at-like cosmic order, while actual ritual practice was carried out by local temples and urban cult institutions.

philosophy.zh: 思想界受希腊哲学深刻影响，尤以斯多亚主义、中期柏拉图主义和实用修辞教育最为显著；同时埃及祭司传统中的宇宙秩序观、神秘宗教和占星知识亦持续活跃，形成学术与宗教交织的文化气候。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was deeply shaped by Greek philosophy, especially Stoicism, Middle Platonism, and practical rhetorical education; at the same time Egyptian priestly concepts of cosmic order, mystery religion, and astrological learning remained active, creating a climate in which scholarship and religion overlapped.

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系并存使用希腊字母书写的通用希腊文、拉丁文行政文本、世俗埃及语的德摩体文，以及主要用于宗教碑铭的圣书体。公私契据和税册以希腊文最为常见。
writingSystem.en: The writing system was multilingual and multiscript, using Greek alphabetic script for common Greek, Latin for some official texts, Demotic for vernacular Egyptian, and hieroglyphs chiefly for religious inscriptions. Greek was the most common language of contracts, tax records, and administration.

culturalAchievements.zh: 亚历山大里亚仍是地中海世界的重要学术中心之一，在医学、地理、天文学、文献整理和商业知识方面极具影响力。埃及宗教图像与希腊艺术风格融合，产生独特的神像、墓葬肖像和祭祀建筑传统；伊西斯崇拜亦持续向帝国各地传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Alexandria remained one of the Mediterranean world's major intellectual centers, influential in medicine, geography, astronomy, textual scholarship, and commercial knowledge. Egyptian religious imagery fused with Greek artistic forms to produce distinctive cult statues, funerary portraits, and temple traditions, while the cult of Isis continued to spread across the empire.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言分属亚非语系的埃及语与印欧语系的希腊语、拉丁语，其中希腊语为行政和城市精英通用语。
languageFamily.en: The principal languages belonged to the Afroasiatic family in the form of Egyptian and to the Indo-European family in the form of Greek and Latin, with Greek serving as the main administrative and urban elite language.

gdpDescription.zh: 埃及经济在100年处于帝国最繁荣地区之列，核心优势在于尼罗河农业高产、亚历山大里亚转口贸易、红海—印度洋航线利润，以及成熟的税务与仓储体制。若按罗马货币估值，其总产出在各行省中名列前茅。
gdpDescription.en: In 100 CE Egypt's economy ranked among the most prosperous regions of the empire, with core strengths in high-yield Nile agriculture, Alexandria's entrepôt trade, profits from the Red Sea–Indian Ocean routes, and a mature fiscal and granary system. Valued in Roman monetary terms, its total output was among the highest of the provinces.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、亚麻与纺织、莎草纸制造、玻璃与金属加工、河运与海运、香料和奢侈品转口贸易、酒类与橄榄油消费市场、采石与建筑材料生产。
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, flax and textiles, papyrus production, glass and metalworking, river and maritime transport, entrepôt trade in spices and luxury goods, wine and olive oil distribution, and quarrying and building materials.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以小麦、大麦、莎草纸、亚麻布、玻璃器、尼罗河制品为主；进口则包括印度香料、胡椒、象牙、宝石、丝绸、阿拉伯乳香与没药、地中海葡萄酒及橄榄油。
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on wheat, barley, papyrus, linen, glassware, and Nile products; imports included Indian spices, pepper, ivory, gemstones, silk, Arabian frankincense and myrrh, and Mediterranean wine and olive oil.

name.zh: 罗马—亚历山大里亚币制

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。亚历山大里亚商人、船主、税农承包者和神庙相关地主积累可观财富；普通农户多拥有有限牲畜、农具和储粮，生活高度依赖尼罗河水情与税负轻重。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Alexandrian merchants, shipowners, tax contractors, and temple-linked landholders accumulated substantial fortunes, while ordinary peasant households possessed limited livestock, tools, and grain reserves and remained heavily dependent on Nile conditions and tax burdens.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为国家强监管下的市场经济：农业生产多由村社与地主经营，但税收、仓储、航运、专卖和外贸通道受罗马行政深度控制。亚历山大里亚与红海港口则构成高度商业化的交换网络。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a market economy under heavy state supervision: agricultural production was conducted by villages and landholders, but taxation, granaries, shipping, monopolies, and foreign trade corridors were tightly controlled by Roman administration. Alexandria and the Red Sea ports formed highly commercialized exchange networks.

source.zh: 港口关税与红海贸易税
source.en: Port Customs and Red Sea Trade Duties

source.zh: 专卖、国有土地与杂项收费
source.en: Monopolies, State Lands, and Miscellaneous Fees

category.zh: 粮仓、河运与港务基础设施
category.en: Granaries, River Transport, and Port Infrastructure

category.zh: 上缴帝国财政与皇室调拨
category.en: Transfers to Imperial Treasury and Crown Allocations

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散存在于亚历山大里亚金库、区域仓储价值与可快速征调的谷物库存中。埃及的真正财富不仅在金银现款，更在其可持续征收并输送的粮食与贸易税流。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were distributed among the Alexandrian fisc, regional stored value, and grain stocks that could be rapidly mobilized. Egypt's true wealth lay not only in specie but in its continuing ability to extract and ship grain and collect customs revenue.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健征收与物流优先，目标是保证粮食安全输往帝国核心并维持红海贸易收益。政府避免大规模地方自治放权，而依赖精密登记、审计和运输组织来保持高财政效率。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored stable extraction and logistical priority, aiming above all to guarantee grain supply to the imperial core and preserve Red Sea trade revenues. The government avoided extensive local fiscal autonomy and instead relied on detailed registration, audit, and transport organization to maintain high efficiency.

description.zh: 由驻埃及的罗马军团核心构成，负责省内威慑、战略要地把守和必要时的帝国远征支援。
description.en: Formed the core of the Roman military presence in Egypt, responsible for provincial deterrence, key strongpoint defense, and support for imperial expeditions when required.

description.zh: 由辅助军大队组成，驻守尼罗河沿线、沙漠道路、要塞与仓储节点，承担巡逻和治安任务。
description.en: Comprised auxiliary cohorts stationed along the Nile, desert roads, forts, and granary nodes, carrying out patrol and security duties.

description.zh: 用于东部沙漠、红海商路和南部边界快速反应，兼顾通信、侦察与追击。
description.en: Used for rapid response in the Eastern Desert, along the Red Sea routes, and on the southern frontier, handling communication, reconnaissance, and pursuit.

name.en: Riverine, Naval, and Port Defense Forces

description.zh: 负责尼罗河运输护航、港口治安、海岸巡逻及对粮船与商船的保护。
description.en: Protected Nile transport, port security, coastal patrols, and the safety of grain and merchant shipping.

description.zh: 包括城镇守卫、警备人员和危机时征调的地方辅助力量，主要用于后方稳定和设施防护。
description.en: Included town guards, security personnel, and locally mobilized auxiliary forces in times of crisis, chiefly used for internal stability and infrastructure protection.

commanderInChief.zh: 埃及长官（代表皇帝统军）
commanderInChief.en: Prefect of Egypt (commanding in the emperor's name)

technology.zh: 军事技术体现罗马帝国标准化优势，包括军团装备、投射武器、攻城器械、道路与堡垒工事建设、河运保障和文书化补给系统。沙漠作战依赖水站、烽燧和驼队后勤。
technology.en: Military technology reflected Roman standardization, including legionary equipment, missile weapons, siege equipment, road and fortification building, river transport support, and documentary logistics systems. Desert warfare relied on watering stations, signal posts, and camel-caravan supply.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方库施边境摩擦、东部沙漠与红海路线上的部族袭扰、海上盗掠，以及一旦帝国内战爆发时埃及粮仓地位所引发的政治争夺。
threats.en: Principal threats came from friction on the southern Kushite frontier, tribal raiding along the Eastern Desert and Red Sea routes, maritime predation, and the political struggles that could arise over Egypt's role as a grain supplier in the event of Roman civil conflict.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 罗马投石机，抛射大型石弹
description.en: Roman onager catapult, launching large stone projectiles

populationDescription.zh: 人口约七百万，主要集中于尼罗河谷和三角洲高密度农业区。亚历山大里亚为最大都会，另有大量中型诺姆首府与河港城镇。人口结构复杂，埃及乡村人口占绝大多数，城市中希腊化居民、犹太人和外来商旅比例较高。
populationDescription.en: The population was about seven million, concentrated mainly in the densely cultivated Nile Valley and Delta. Alexandria was the largest metropolis, alongside many medium-sized nome capitals and river ports. The demographic profile was complex: rural Egyptians formed the large majority, while Hellenized residents, Jews, and foreign merchants were especially prominent in the cities.

description.zh: 古代学术中心与地中海大港
description.en: Ancient center of learning and great Mediterranean port

description.zh: 古埃及首都，法老权力中心
description.en: Ancient Egyptian capital, center of pharaonic power

description.zh: 古埃及宗教圣城，卡纳克神庙所在地
description.en: Sacred city of ancient Egypt, home of Karnak Temple

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致分为罗马官员与军人、希腊化城市公民与地主、祭司阶层、商人和工匠、村社农民、佃农、劳工与奴隶。法律身份、族群背景和纳税等级共同决定社会机会与负担。
socialClasses.en: Social classes were broadly divided into Roman officials and soldiers, Hellenized urban citizens and landholders, priestly groups, merchants and artisans, village peasants, tenants, laborers, and slaves. Legal status, ethnic background, and fiscal classification together shaped opportunity and burden.

allies.zh: 作为罗马帝国一部分，埃及的盟友即帝国友邦与附庸体系，包括纳巴泰、部分红海与阿拉伯商路伙伴，以及在印度洋贸易中保持往来的南阿拉伯和印度西岸港市。
allies.en: As part of the Roman Empire, Egypt's allies were those of the imperial client and friendly system, including Nabataea, certain Red Sea and Arabian trade partners, and South Arabian and western Indian port polities engaged in Indian Ocean commerce.

enemies.zh: 直接敌对者不多，但在战略上需警惕帕提亚对东部贸易均势的影响、南方库施的边境张力，以及沙漠与海上掠袭势力。
enemies.en: It had few direct enemies of its own, but strategically it had to watch Parthian influence over eastern trade balances, frontier tensions with Kush to the south, and raiding groups in the deserts and at sea.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以服务罗马帝国整体利益为准：保障粮道、控制红海贸易、维持南境稳定、避免地方势力控制亚历山大里亚港，并通过商业而非大规模征服扩大影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed toward the wider interests of the Roman Empire: securing the grain route, controlling Red Sea trade, maintaining southern stability, preventing local powers from threatening Alexandria's harbor, and extending influence more through commerce than through large-scale conquest.

era.zh: 双帝国时代
era.en: Twin Empires

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术优势包括成熟的灌溉与尼罗河洪水测量体系、精细化土地丈量与税册编制、地中海与红海航运知识、玻璃制造、莎草纸文书技术、石材工程，以及亚历山大里亚学术传统中的医学、天文学与地理知识整合。
keyInnovations.en: Key strengths included mature irrigation and Nile flood measurement systems, refined land survey and tax-register methods, Mediterranean and Red Sea navigational knowledge, glass production, papyrus documentation technology, stone engineering, and the Alexandrian scholarly synthesis of medicine, astronomy, and geography.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于尼罗河农业的稳定高产、亚历山大里亚的国际港口地位、红海—印度洋贸易利润，以及高度制度化的征税与粮运能力。文化上兼具埃及传统神圣合法性与希腊—罗马行政效率，使其在100年极为兴盛。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths lay in the stable high productivity of Nile agriculture, Alexandria's position as an international port, profits from the Red Sea–Indian Ocean trade, and highly institutionalized taxation and grain logistics. Culturally it combined Egyptian sacred legitimacy with Greco-Roman administrative efficiency, making it exceptionally prosperous in 100 CE.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于对尼罗河水文和国家运输体系依赖极强，税负沉重易引发乡村压力；同时族群、法律地位与城市特权差异明显，潜在社会摩擦持续存在。其政治命运亦高度受罗马中央局势左右。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were its extreme dependence on Nile hydrology and state transport systems, while heavy taxation could place severe strain on the countryside; ethnic, legal, and civic privilege divisions also created persistent social tensions. Its political fate likewise depended heavily on developments in the Roman center.

outlook.zh: 短期前景十分强劲。只要罗马帝国保持稳定、尼罗河年景正常且红海贸易畅通，埃及将继续作为帝国最富庶、最关键的粮仓与商贸枢纽之一。但长期而言，财政压榨、宗教与族群矛盾，以及帝国政治危机可能削弱这一繁荣基础。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was very strong. So long as the Roman Empire remained stable, Nile inundations were normal, and Red Sea trade flowed, Egypt would continue as one of the empire's richest and most vital granaries and commercial hubs. In the longer term, however, fiscal pressure, religious and ethnic tensions, and imperial political crises could erode that prosperity.

description.zh: 埃及是罗马最富庶的行省之一，掌握尼罗河粮食与红海—印度洋贸易通道。
description.en: Egypt was among Rome’s richest provinces, commanding Nile grain production and Red Sea–Indian Ocean trade routes.

governmentForm.zh: 由村社、氏族与区域性酋邦组成的分散部族政治体制
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal political order composed of villages, clans, and regional chiefdoms

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、年龄等级、农耕村社与区域交换网络为基础；部分地区出现贵族家族、工匠群体与依附人口的分化，但整体仍以地方共同体为核心。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on kinship, age grades, farming villages, and regional exchange networks; some areas showed differentiation into noble lineages, craft specialists, and dependent populations, but local communities remained central.

rulingClass.zh: 地方首领家族、祭司权威、资深长老与控制铁器和贸易节点的显贵
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages, ritual authorities, senior elders, and notables who controlled iron production and trade nodes

succession.zh: 继承方式因地而异，多为氏族内部世袭并由长老认可，部分地区兼有兄终弟及或旁系继承
succession.en: Succession varied by region, usually hereditary within a lineage and validated by elders, with some areas also using fraternal or collateral succession

structure.zh: 无统一国家官僚体系；治理单位主要为村庄、宗族聚落与小型酋邦，酋长在长老会议、祭司和战士首领辅佐下处理司法、分配土地、组织防御和祭祀。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; the main governing units were villages, lineage settlements, and small chiefdoms, where chiefs handled justice, land allocation, defense, and ritual with the aid of councils of elders, priests, and war leaders.

function.zh: 裁决纠纷、确认继承、监督习惯法与调解村社关系
function.en: Resolved disputes, confirmed succession, supervised customary law, and mediated relations among communities

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀、农业仪式、神判与圣地维护，强化首领合法性
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, agricultural ceremonies, ordeals, and maintenance of sacred places, reinforcing chiefly legitimacy

function.zh: 征集谷物、牲畜、铁器与劳役，保管公共粮仓并在歉收时调配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, iron goods, and labor dues, managed communal granaries, and redistributed supplies in poor harvests

function.zh: 管理集市、渡口、林道与河道节点，保护商旅并抽取通行贡礼
function.en: Managed markets, ferries, forest tracks, and river nodes, protected traders, and collected transit tribute

function.zh: 组织民兵、防御劫掠、护卫牲畜与巡查边界地带
function.en: Organized militia, defended against raiding, guarded livestock, and patrolled frontier zones

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理依托村长、氏族长老与区域酋长；河谷、稀树草原与森林地带的制度差异明显，但普遍通过口头传统和人际义务维系统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village heads, lineage elders, and regional chiefs; institutions varied across river valleys, savanna, and forest zones, but rule was generally maintained through oral tradition and interpersonal obligation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、长老裁判、赔偿原则和宗教誓言为核心；涉及婚姻、土地、牲畜、血亲责任和贸易纠纷，多以赔偿、和解或祭祀解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was centered on customary norms, elder arbitration, compensation, and sacred oaths; matters of marriage, land, livestock, kin responsibility, and trade disputes were usually settled through compensation, reconciliation, or ritual.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，主要通过贡赋、礼物交换、集体劳役、收获分成、市场抽成和战时征发维持首领权力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; chiefly authority was maintained mainly through tribute, gift exchange, communal labor, crop shares, market levies, and wartime requisition.

religion.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、地方神灵、自然圣地与祭司仪式为主；部分地区重视铁匠与火的神圣性，宗教与农业周期、降雨和王权合法性密切相关。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, local spirits, sacred natural sites, and priestly ritual; in some areas the sacred character of smiths and fire was emphasized, and religion was closely tied to agricultural cycles, rainfall, and political legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 思想传统主要体现于口述伦理、祖训、长老智慧与群体责任观念，强调亲族互助、土地神圣、勇武、慷慨和对祖先秩序的尊重。
philosophy.en: Intellectual traditions were expressed mainly through oral ethics, ancestral teachings, elder wisdom, and communal responsibility, emphasizing kin solidarity, the sacredness of land, valor, generosity, and respect for ancestral order.

writingSystem.zh: 未见本地普遍使用的文字体系，知识、谱系、法律和神话主要依靠口传、仪式记忆与物质符号传承。
writingSystem.en: No widely used indigenous writing system is attested; knowledge, genealogy, law, and myth were transmitted chiefly through oral tradition, ritual memory, and material symbols.

culturalAchievements.zh: 铁冶炼与锻造技术成熟，形成多样化陶器风格、木雕与身体装饰传统；农业聚落扩展推动了区域交换，口头史诗、祖先谱系和仪式音乐在社会整合中具有重要作用。
culturalAchievements.en: Iron smelting and forging were well developed, alongside diverse ceramic styles, wood carving, and body ornament traditions; expanding farming settlements supported regional exchange, while oral epics, ancestral genealogies, and ritual music played important roles in social integration.

languageFamily.zh: 以尼日尔-刚果语系诸语言为主，萨赫勒地带并存部分尼罗-撒哈拉语系与亚非语系影响
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Niger-Congo languages, with some Nilo-Saharan and Afroasiatic influences present in the Sahel

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业为基础，辅以畜牧、渔猎、采集、铁器生产和区域交换。虽然没有统一市场与国家财政，但尼日尔流域、萨赫勒边缘和森林—草原过渡带已形成活跃的地方贸易网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by herding, fishing, hunting, gathering, iron production, and regional exchange. Although there was no unified market or state fiscal system, lively local trade networks had formed in the Niger basin, Sahelian margins, and forest-savanna transition zones.

mainIndustries.zh: 农耕、畜牧、铁冶炼、锻造、陶器制作、织造、木工、渔业与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Farming, herding, iron smelting, smithing, pottery production, weaving, woodworking, fishing, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、谷物、小米、高粱、薯类、豆类、牲畜、皮革、盐、渔产、陶器、木制品、珠饰、可乐果与森林产物
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, grain, millet, sorghum, tubers, pulses, livestock, leather, salt, fish products, pottery, wood goods, beads, kola nuts, and forest products

name.zh: 物品交换与金属价值并行

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、粮仓储备、牲畜数量、亲族规模、铁制工具和婚姻交换资源；地区差距较大，河谷与贸易节点家庭通常更富裕。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in land-use rights, stored grain, livestock, kin size, iron tools, and resources for marriage exchange; regional disparities were considerable, and households in river valleys and trade nodes were often wealthier.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、亲族再分配、贡赋关系和地方市场交换并存的分散型经济体系
economicSystem.en: A decentralized economy combining village production, kin-based redistribution, tribute relations, and local market exchange

source.en: Corvée labor and public works equivalent

source.zh: 牲畜、铁器与手工业贡纳

category.zh: 首领随从、战士与地方防务
category.en: Chiefly retinues, warriors, and local defense
Ritual, feasting, and political gift distribution
Maintenance of routes, ferries, and markets

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库；财富分散储存在各酋邦粮仓、牲畜群、铁器储备、礼仪珍品和少量金属财富中，更多体现为可动员资源而非铸币储蓄。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single central treasury; wealth was distributed among chiefdom granaries, livestock herds, iron stockpiles, ritual valuables, and small amounts of metal wealth, taking the form of mobilizable resources rather than coined savings.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持稳定、赈济歉收和保障首领再分配能力为目标，强调储粮、礼物政治与灵活征发，而非固定税率。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed at maintaining stability, relieving poor harvests, and preserving chiefly redistributive capacity, emphasizing grain storage, gift politics, and flexible levies rather than fixed taxation.

description.zh: 由酋长直属战士、护卫与精壮随从组成，负责保卫聚落中心、押运贡物和执行突袭。
description.en: Composed of warriors, guards, and able-bodied retainers directly attached to chiefs, responsible for defending settlement centers, escorting tribute, and conducting raids.

description.zh: 由农民在战时或警报时集结，使用长矛、弓箭、木盾和铁制刀具，适合本地防御与小规模冲突。
description.en: Raised from farmers in wartime or alarm, using spears, bows, wooden shields, and iron blades, suitable for local defense and small-scale conflict.

name.zh: 边地骑乘与牲畜护卫队

description.zh: 主要分布于较干燥的萨赫勒边缘，承担侦察、牲畜保护和长距离警戒任务，但规模有限。
description.en: Found mainly on the drier Sahelian margins, they handled scouting, livestock protection, and long-range watch duties, though in limited numbers.

description.zh: 守护河岸聚落、渔业地带和重要渡口，兼负治安与贸易路线保护职责。
description.en: Guarded riverine settlements, fishing zones, and important crossings, also serving policing and trade-route protection roles.

commanderInChief.en: Local chiefs and their war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制矛头、刀剑、箭镞、木盾、皮甲和简易工事为主；不具备大型攻城体系，作战形式偏向伏击、劫掠、守卫与季节性集结。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spearheads, swords, arrowheads, wooden shields, leather protection, and simple fortifications; large-scale siege systems were absent, and warfare favored ambush, raiding, guarding, and seasonal musters.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自地方争战、牲畜劫掠、资源竞争、萨赫勒边缘流动群体冲突以及环境波动造成的人口迁徙压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from local warfare, livestock raiding, competition over resources, conflicts with mobile groups on the Sahelian margins, and migration pressures caused by environmental fluctuation.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于尼日尔河流域、萨赫勒南缘与几内亚森林边缘的村落和小型聚落中，整体密度不高，但在适宜农耕和铁器生产的区域形成较稳定的聚居带。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across villages and small settlements in the Niger basin, the southern Sahel, and the edges of the Guinea forests; overall density was modest, but more stable settlement belts existed in areas suited to farming and iron production.

name.zh: 诺克文化晚期中心聚落
尼日尔河内弯大型河岸聚落
name.en: Large riverine settlements of the Niger Inland Bend

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为首领家族与显贵、祭司和长老、自由农民与牧民、铁匠和手工业者、商旅中介者以及依附人口或俘获者；不同地区分层程度不一。
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into chiefly lineages and notables, priests and elders, free farmers and herders, smiths and craftspeople, trading intermediaries, and dependent people or captives; the degree of stratification varied by region.

allies.zh: 无统一对外同盟；通常与邻近部族、河道聚落和贸易伙伴维持婚姻、互市与防御协作关系
allies.en: There was no unified foreign alliance system; communities usually maintained marriage ties, market relations, and defensive cooperation with neighboring tribes, river settlements, and trade partners

enemies.zh: 无固定共同敌国；冲突对象多为邻近竞争部族、劫掠群体和争夺草场、盐源或渡口控制权的地方势力
enemies.en: There was no fixed common enemy; opponents were usually neighboring rival tribes, raiding groups, and local powers competing for pasture, salt sources, or ferry control

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以地方层面的通婚、互市、贡礼、边界谈判和有限报复性战争为主，重在维持贸易通道与生态资源平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations operated mainly at the local level through intermarriage, market exchange, tribute, boundary negotiation, and limited retaliatory warfare, with the aim of preserving trade routes and ecological balance.

era.zh: 西非铁器时代
era.en: West African Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的本地铁冶炼炉技术、铁制农具与武器、改良陶器烧制、河流利用与区域性农业扩张；在部分地区，冶铁已显著提升了开垦、木工和手工业能力。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature local iron-smelting furnace technologies, iron agricultural tools and weapons, improved ceramic firing, better use of river systems, and regional agricultural expansion; in some areas ironworking had significantly increased capacities in land clearance, woodworking, and craft production.

strengths.zh: 适应多样生态环境，铁器技术推动农业和手工业发展，地方社会韧性强，亲族网络与再分配机制有助于应对歉收和冲突。
strengths.en: These cultures were well adapted to diverse environments, with iron technology supporting agricultural and craft development; local societies were resilient, and kin networks with redistributive mechanisms helped them withstand poor harvests and conflict.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、缺乏统一权威和文字行政，长距离整合能力有限，易受气候波动、地方战争和贸易路线变动影响。
weaknesses.en: Their weaknesses included political fragmentation, the absence of unified authority and written administration, limited long-distance integration, and vulnerability to climatic fluctuation, local warfare, and shifts in trade routes.

outlook.zh: 在100年前后总体稳定，并具备进一步扩展农业聚落、强化区域贸易和形成更复杂酋邦的潜力；长期看，这些基础将为后世西非大型政治体的兴起创造条件。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE these societies were broadly stable and had the potential to expand farming settlements, strengthen regional trade, and develop more complex chiefdoms; in the long run, these foundations would help prepare the rise of later large West African polities.

description.zh: 尼日尔河流域、萨赫勒与几内亚森林带分布着多样化农业与铁器社会，尚未形成后世大帝国。
description.en: The Niger basin, Sahel, and Guinea forest zones contained diverse farming and ironworking societies before the rise of later great empires.

name.zh: 撒哈拉绿洲文明（加拉曼特王国）

governmentForm.zh: 以绿洲城邦网络为基础的君主制王国，王权通过对水源、商路与附属聚落的控制来维系
governmentForm.en: A monarchical kingdom based on a network of oasis centers, with royal authority maintained through control of water, trade routes, and dependent settlements

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与贵族、战士阶层、绿洲农民、工匠与商人、受支配的乡村居民，以及一定数量的奴隶与被掳人口构成；不同绿洲聚落之间保持地方性差异，但整体受加拉马核心统治
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal family and nobles, a warrior stratum, oasis farmers, artisans and merchants, subordinate rural populations, and a notable number of slaves and captives; individual oasis communities retained local distinctions but were broadly dominated by the core centered on Garama

rulingClass.zh: 加拉马王族、绿洲贵族家族、控制灌溉系统与商队路线的部族首领
rulingClass.en: The royal house of Garama, oasis noble families, and tribal chiefs who controlled irrigation systems and caravan routes

succession.zh: 大体为世袭王位继承，可能在王族内部由贵族与地方首领协商确认继承者
succession.en: Broadly hereditary royal succession, likely confirmed within the royal lineage through consultation with nobles and local chiefs

structure.zh: 中央王廷设于加拉马，下辖若干绿洲中心与附属聚落；政权以王室、贵族议事、地方首领和商路据点管理者共同运作，行政能力集中于征税、治水、商路保护与动员劳役
structure.en: The central court was based at Garama and ruled over several oasis centers and dependent settlements; governance operated through the royal household, aristocratic councils, local chiefs, and managers of caravan nodes, with administrative capacity focused on taxation, water management, route security, and labor mobilization

function.zh: 管理王室、使节接待、命令传达与都城日常政务
function.en: Managed the royal household, reception of envoys, transmission of orders, and daily affairs of the capital

function.zh: 维护地下引水渠、井泉、农田分水与劳役调度，是国家权力的核心机构之一
function.en: Maintained underground water channels, wells, field water distribution, and labor drafts, making it one of the core organs of state power

function.zh: 监管跨撒哈拉商队、征收过境税、核查货物并维护驿站与补给点
function.en: Supervised trans-Saharan caravans, collected transit dues, inspected goods, and maintained stations and supply points

function.zh: 联络地方首领、组织贡赋、统计人口与协调治安
function.en: Liaised with local chiefs, organized tribute, recorded population, and coordinated security

function.zh: 征收谷物、椰枣、牲畜与手工业品，并储存王室物资
function.en: Collected grain, dates, livestock, and craft goods, and stored royal supplies

function.zh: 负责王室护卫、边地防卫、骑兵动员与对外袭击或护商行动
function.en: Oversaw the royal guard, frontier defense, cavalry mobilization, and external raiding or caravan-protection operations

localAdmin.zh: 地方统治依托各绿洲据点，由王室任命或认可的地方贵族、村社长老与部族首领共同管理；边远地区更多表现为朝贡与间接控制，而非严密官僚统治
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on oasis centers managed jointly by royal appointees or recognized nobles, village elders, and tribal chiefs; in distant areas control was more tributary and indirect than tightly bureaucratic

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断和部族调解为基础，涉及水权、土地、婚姻、债务、商贸纠纷与掠夺分配；对重大案件由国王或贵族议断
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, royal judgment, and tribal mediation, covering water rights, land, marriage, debt, trade disputes, and division of plunder; major cases were decided by the king or aristocratic councils

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物税和过境税为主，包括农产、牲畜、手工业品、商队关税与附属部落贡赋；劳役也是重要财政资源，尤其用于地下渠道和防御设施维护
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on payments in kind and transit duties, including agricultural produce, livestock, craft goods, caravan customs, and tribute from subordinate tribes; corvée labor was also a major fiscal resource, especially for maintaining underground canals and defensive works

religion.zh: 本地多神信仰占主导，崇拜祖灵、地方神祇与与水源、太阳、土地和王权相关的神圣力量；同时受到地中海世界宗教观念的边缘影响
religion.en: Local polytheistic religion predominated, centered on ancestral spirits, local deities, and sacred forces associated with water, the sun, the land, and kingship; Mediterranean religious influences were present at the margins

philosophy.zh: 未形成成体系的成文哲学传统，价值观以王权合法性、部族荣誉、祖先记忆、治水秩序和长途贸易中的信誉为核心
philosophy.en: There was no fully developed written philosophical tradition; core values emphasized royal legitimacy, tribal honor, ancestral memory, hydraulic order, and reputation in long-distance trade

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用口述传统，可能有限采用利比科-柏柏尔文字或受地中海邻近文明影响的记号体系；正式行政记录规模较小
writingSystem.en: Oral tradition remained dominant, with possible limited use of Libyco-Berber writing or symbolic systems influenced by neighboring Mediterranean cultures; formal administrative record-keeping was modest in scale

culturalAchievements.zh: 最突出的成就是在极端干旱环境中发展出地下引水渠网络、绿洲农业体系、坚固聚落与跨沙漠商路组织能力；墓葬、岩画、陶器和装饰品体现出撒哈拉与地中海交汇的文化特征
culturalAchievements.en: Its greatest achievements were the development of underground water-channel systems, oasis agriculture, fortified settlements, and organizational capacity for trans-desert routes in an extremely arid environment; tombs, rock art, pottery, and ornaments reflect a culture shaped by both Saharan and Mediterranean contacts

languageFamily.zh: 可能属于古代柏柏尔语支，兼有撒哈拉内部多语接触现象
languageFamily.en: Likely part of the ancient Berber branch, alongside multilingual contact with peoples deeper in the Sahara

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在区域层面相当可观，但与罗马或帕提亚等大型帝国相比仍属中小型；财富高度集中在绿洲农业、水利控制和长途转口贸易上
gdpDescription.en: The economy was considerable on a regional scale but remained small to medium compared with major empires such as Rome or Parthia; wealth was heavily concentrated in oasis agriculture, hydraulic control, and long-distance intermediary trade

mainIndustries.zh: 椰枣与谷物种植、畜牧业、地下引水渠维护、陶器与皮革加工、车马与驮运服务、跨撒哈拉贸易中介
mainIndustries.en: Date and grain cultivation, pastoralism, maintenance of underground irrigation channels, pottery and leatherworking, transport services with carts and pack animals, and trans-Saharan trade brokerage

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与转运品包括椰枣、盐、牲畜、皮革、奴隶、象牙、乌木、羽毛和来自撒哈拉南缘的异域物产；输入品包括罗马制陶、金属器、酒、橄榄油、玻璃珠和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports and transit goods included dates, salt, livestock, leather, slaves, ivory, ebony, feathers, and exotic products from the Saharan and sub-Saharan margins; imports included Roman pottery, metal goods, wine, olive oil, glass beads, and luxury items

name.zh: 混合货币与实物交换体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在椰枣树、耕地水权、牲畜、谷物存量和家内劳力；贵族家庭则控制大面积绿洲土地、井渠节点、商队份额和奴隶劳动力
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was measured mainly in date palms, water rights to cultivated plots, livestock, grain stores, and family labor; elite households controlled larger oasis lands, canal nodes, caravan shares, and slave labor

economicSystem.zh: 以绿洲农业和部族—王室控制的商路经济为基础的混合型经济；市场交换存在，但国家收入仍高度依赖贡赋、关税和劳役
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on oasis agriculture and caravan networks controlled by tribal and royal authorities; market exchange existed, but state income still depended heavily on tribute, customs, and labor obligations

source.zh: 农业实物税与水利相关征收
source.en: Agricultural taxes in kind and water-related levies

category.zh: 使节、贸易联络与馈赠外交
category.en: Envoys, trade liaison, and gift diplomacy

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备以谷物、椰枣、牲畜、金属器、外来钱币与奢侈品并存，真正以统一货币计量的部分有限；由于沙漠环境下运输困难，地方仓储比都城金库更重要
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves consisted of grain, dates, livestock, metal goods, foreign coinage, and luxury items, with only part of the treasury existing in standardized monetary form; because transport was difficult in desert conditions, local storage depots were often more important than the capital treasury alone

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体稳健，优先确保水利维护、军事巡护和贵族忠诚；在丰年积累储备，在商路受阻或旱灾时动用库存和贡赋调剂
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, prioritizing irrigation maintenance, military patrols, and aristocratic loyalty; surpluses were accumulated in favorable years and reserves were used to cushion disruptions in trade or drought

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从构成的机动主力，负责护卫商路、快速袭击与镇压地方反叛
description.en: A mobile core composed of nobles and their retainers, responsible for caravan protection, rapid raids, and suppression of local unrest

description.zh: 由农民、城镇居民和附属人口征发而来，承担聚落守备、渠道防护和近距离作战任务
description.en: Raised from farmers, townsmen, and dependent populations, they guarded settlements, protected canals, and fought at close range

description.zh: 分散部署于商路、井站与沙漠边缘，兼任侦察、警戒和向导职责
description.en: Dispersed across caravan routes, wells, and desert margins, they served as scouts, sentries, and guides

technology.zh: 武备以矛、标枪、短剑、皮盾、弓箭和简单护甲为主，兼用马匹和车辆进行沙漠机动；不具备罗马式重装军团水平，但熟悉绿洲与荒漠作战环境
technology.en: Armament centered on spears, javelins, short swords, leather shields, bows, and simple armor, with horses and carts used for desert mobility; it did not match Roman heavy infantry standards but was well adapted to oasis and desert warfare

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自罗马北非边境压力、商路竞争、附属部落叛离、干旱引发的资源冲突，以及撒哈拉内部游动群体对井站和商队的袭扰
threats.en: Principal threats came from pressure along the Roman North African frontier, competition over trade routes, defection of subordinate tribes, resource conflicts caused by drought, and raids by mobile Saharan groups on wells and caravans

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约十万，集中分布于费赞地区绿洲链、地下渠道灌溉区和若干防御性聚落；人口密度极不均衡，绝大多数居民依赖有限水源生存
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 100,000, concentrated in the Fezzan oasis chain, irrigated areas served by underground channels, and a number of defensible settlements; density was extremely uneven, with most inhabitants dependent on scarce water sources

socialClasses.zh: 社会等级包括王族与贵族、骑士与武装随从、祭司或礼仪主持者、商人和工匠、自由农牧民、附庸部落成员、奴隶与战俘；财富与地位通常取决于水权、土地、牲畜和商路控制力
socialClasses.en: Social ranks included the royal family and nobles, mounted retainers and warriors, priests or ritual specialists, merchants and artisans, free farmers and pastoralists, members of tributary tribes, and slaves or war captives; wealth and status generally depended on control of water rights, land, livestock, and caravan routes

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式同盟；与部分撒哈拉部族、南方商路社群及地中海商人保持务实合作关系
allies.en: There were no permanent formal allies in the strict sense; the kingdom maintained pragmatic cooperative ties with certain Saharan tribes, southern trade communities, and Mediterranean merchants

enemies.zh: 与罗马北非边境当局时战时和，也会与竞争性沙漠部族和拒绝纳贡的绿洲集团发生冲突
enemies.en: The kingdom alternated between conflict and accommodation with Roman authorities in North Africa, and also fought rival desert tribes and oasis groups that resisted tribute

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制费赞绿洲网络、维持跨撒哈拉中介地位和在罗马势力与撒哈拉内陆之间保持战略回旋为核心；通过贸易、贡赋、婚姻联系和有限军事行动维系区域影响力
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling the Fezzan oasis network, preserving its intermediary role in trans-Saharan exchange, and maintaining strategic flexibility between Roman power and the Sahara interior; regional influence was upheld through trade, tribute, marriage ties, and limited military action

era.zh: 双帝国时代
era.en: Twin Empires

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术在于地下引水渠系统、深井开掘、干旱环境绿洲农业、沙漠路线导航、车马与驮运组织，以及适应荒漠条件的防御聚落建设
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations lay in underground irrigation channels, deep-well excavation, oasis agriculture under arid conditions, desert route navigation, organized cart and pack transport, and the construction of defensible settlements suited to desert life

strengths.zh: 优势在于掌握关键绿洲和水利节点、熟悉沙漠环境、善于转口贸易并能够在大国之间保持一定自主性；其社会对极端干旱环境具有较强适应力
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in control of key oases and hydraulic nodes, deep familiarity with the desert environment, skill in intermediary trade, and an ability to preserve autonomy between larger powers; its society was highly adapted to extreme aridity

weaknesses.zh: 弱点包括人口与资源基础有限、行政整合程度不高、对水利系统与商路安全高度依赖，以及在面对罗马等大型国家时军事能力相对不足
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included a limited population and resource base, relatively low administrative integration, heavy dependence on irrigation systems and secure trade routes, and comparatively weak military capacity when facing major states such as Rome

outlook.zh: 到公元100年前后，加拉曼特王国总体稳定，并因居于地中海与撒哈拉腹地之间而保持活力；若能维持水利系统与商路控制，其区域地位可持续，但长期仍将受气候波动、商路变迁和罗马边疆政策影响
outlook.en: Around 100 CE, the Garamantian kingdom was broadly stable and remained dynamic because of its position between the Mediterranean and the Sahara interior; if it could maintain its irrigation systems and control of trade routes, its regional standing could endure, though in the long run it remained vulnerable to climatic fluctuation, shifts in caravan traffic, and Roman frontier policy

description.zh: 加拉曼特控制费赞绿洲网络，是地中海与撒哈拉腹地之间的重要中介。
description.en: The Garamantes controlled the Fezzan oasis network and mediated between the Mediterranean and the Sahara interior.

name.zh: 中部墨西哥文明（特奥蒂瓦坎）

ruler.zh: 不详的祭司王或统治议会

rulerTitle.zh: 祭司统治者／城邦统治议会

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教权威、贵族家族与宫殿行政结合的神权化城邦政体
governmentForm.en: A sacralized city-state polity combining religious authority, noble lineages, and palace administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会层级分明，顶层为祭司、军事贵族与统治家族，其下为工匠、商人、建筑劳工与农民，另有外来移民社区与依附人口。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with priests, military nobles, and ruling families at the top, followed by artisans, merchants, builders, and farmers, along with immigrant quarters and dependent populations.

rulingClass.zh: 祭司贵族、宫殿管理者、主要宗庙主持者与掌控黑曜石和贡赋网络的上层家族
rulingClass.en: Priestly nobles, palace administrators, leading temple officiants, and elite families controlling obsidian and tribute networks

succession.zh: 继承方式不明确，可能由贵族宗族协商、宗教认可与政治实力共同决定，而非固定的单一王朝世袭。
succession.en: Succession is unclear; it was likely determined through noble lineage negotiation, religious sanction, and political power rather than fixed dynastic primogeniture.

structure.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎的统治可能由宫殿区与神庙区核心精英共同运作，中央负责礼仪、工程、贸易与周边聚落整合，下设街区和手工业社区管理。
structure.en: Teotihuacan was likely governed by a core elite centered in palace and temple precincts, with central authority overseeing ritual, public works, trade, and the integration of surrounding settlements, alongside neighborhood and craft-community administration.

name.en: Department of Ritual and Calendar Affairs

function.zh: 主持祭祀、天象观测、历法安排与国家性仪式，维系统治合法性。
function.en: Conducted rituals, observed celestial cycles, managed the calendar, and organized state ceremonies to sustain political legitimacy.

function.zh: 负责金字塔、道路、住宅复合体、排水设施与城市扩建工程。
function.en: Managed pyramids, roads, apartment compounds, drainage systems, and urban expansion projects.

name.en: Department of Obsidian and Craft Administration

function.zh: 监管黑曜石开采、作坊生产、器物分配及标准化制造。
function.en: Supervised obsidian extraction, workshop production, artifact distribution, and standardized manufacturing.

function.zh: 征集周边聚落贡赋，保管粮食、纺织品、工艺品与祭祀物资。
function.en: Collected tribute from surrounding settlements and stored grain, textiles, craft goods, and ritual supplies.

name.en: Department of Markets and Long-Distance Exchange

function.zh: 协调市场秩序、商旅往来、外来社区事务与区域贸易网络。
function.en: Coordinated market order, merchant movement, immigrant-community affairs, and regional trade networks.

function.zh: 维持仪式中心安全、护卫精英区、组织战士团体与应对外部威胁。
function.en: Maintained security in ceremonial precincts, guarded elite districts, organized warrior groups, and responded to external threats.

localAdmin.zh: 城市内部可能按大型住宅复合体、街区与族群社区管理；城外腹地则通过附属村落首领、宗教据点和贸易站维系影响力。
localAdmin.en: Within the city, administration likely operated through large apartment compounds, neighborhoods, and ethnic communities; beyond the city, influence was maintained through village headmen, ritual centers, and trade outposts.

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典留存，秩序主要依靠宗教规范、习惯法、精英裁断与社区纪律执行，严重罪行可能受到没收、劳役、流放或祭祀性惩罚。
legalSystem.en: No written law code survives; order was likely maintained through religious norms, customary law, elite adjudication, and community discipline, with serious offenses punished by confiscation, labor, exile, or ritual sanction.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、纺织品、黑曜石制品、建筑劳役及祭祀供品。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base relied primarily on tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, chili, textiles, obsidian goods, construction labor, and ritual offerings.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以雨水、农业、生育、羽蛇、山岳与火之神灵崇拜为核心，祭祀、献供和神圣建筑是公共生活中心。
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on deities of rain, agriculture, fertility, feathered serpents, mountains, and fire; ritual, offerings, and sacred architecture stood at the heart of public life.

philosophy.zh: 尚无后世意义上的成体系哲学，世界观强调宇宙秩序、历法循环、祖先与神灵互动，以及统治者维护天地平衡的职责。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophy in the later literary sense; the worldview emphasized cosmic order, calendrical cycles, interaction with ancestors and deities, and the ruler’s duty to maintain balance between heaven and earth.

writingSystem.zh: 尚未形成完整表音文字体系，可能使用象征性图像、记号、壁画与建筑空间编码传达身份、宗教和政治信息。
writingSystem.en: A fully developed phonetic writing system had not emerged; symbolic imagery, signs, murals, and architectural spatial coding likely conveyed identity, religious meaning, and political messages.

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括巨型都市规划、亡灵大道轴线布局、太阳金字塔早期建设、标准化公寓式住宅、精细黑曜石工艺、壁画艺术与广泛的宗教象征传播。
