culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included monumental urban planning, the axial layout of the Avenue of the Dead, early construction of the Pyramid of the Sun, standardized apartment compounds, fine obsidian craftsmanship, mural art, and the wide spread of religious symbolism.

languageFamily.zh: 语言构成复杂且未可确证，可能包含中部墨西哥多语环境，常被推测与奥托-曼格语系、米赫-索克语系或早期纳瓦语前居民有关。
languageFamily.en: Its linguistic makeup was complex and uncertain, likely reflecting a multilingual Central Mexican environment; scholars often speculate on links with Oto-Manguean, Mixe-Zoquean, or pre-Nahuan populations.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在当时美洲属大型都市经济，基础来自高地农业、黑曜石生产、建筑动员与区域贸易控制，但仍以实物交换和再分配为主。
gdpDescription.en: Its economy was one of the larger urban economies in the Americas at the time, based on highland agriculture, obsidian production, mobilized construction labor, and control of regional exchange, though still dominated by barter and redistribution.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、黑曜石开采与加工、陶器、建筑营造、纺织、宗教手工业与区域转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, obsidian extraction and working, pottery, construction, textile production, ritual crafts, and regional transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石刀片、陶器、玉米、豆类、辣椒、龙舌兰制品、纺织品、贝壳、羽毛、玉石与宗教礼器
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian blades, pottery, maize, beans, chili peppers, maguey products, textiles, shells, feathers, greenstone, and ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为住房空间、储粮、工具、纺织物、陶器和亲族劳动力；精英财富则集中在大型住宅复合体、奢侈品、祭器与对贸易渠道的控制。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of housing space, stored grain, tools, textiles, pottery, and kin labor; elite wealth was concentrated in large residential compounds, prestige goods, ritual objects, and control of exchange routes.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、国家工程动员、市场交换和精英再分配并存的混合经济，尚未形成货币化财政体系。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining household production, state-directed labor mobilization, market exchange, and elite redistribution, without a monetized fiscal system.

source.en: Labor levy and public works service

source.zh: 黑曜石作坊与贸易控制收益
source.en: Obsidian workshops and exchange control revenues

source.zh: 宗教献纳与附属聚落供奉

category.en: Guard forces, warrior groups, and defense

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是金银储藏，而是粮仓、纺织品、黑曜石半成品、礼器和可调动劳力所构成的实物与组织能力储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not chiefly a store of gold or silver, but a reserve of grain, textiles, obsidian semifinished goods, ritual objects, and mobilizable labor capacity.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策侧重通过贡赋和劳役支持持续城市建设、宗教仪式与区域控制，在丰年积储以应对歉收和政治扩张需求。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining urban construction, religious ritual, and regional control through tribute and labor service, storing surpluses in good years to buffer poor harvests and support expansion.

description.zh: 负责宗教中心、精英住宅区、仓储设施和主要道路的守卫。
description.en: Guarded the ceremonial center, elite residential compounds, storage facilities, and major avenues.

description.zh: 由贵族与受训战士组成，用于威慑邻近政体、护卫商路并参与象征性或实际冲突。
description.en: Composed of nobles and trained warriors, they deterred neighboring polities, protected trade routes, and took part in symbolic or real conflict.

description.zh: 在紧急情况下由附属聚落和城市居民临时征发，主要承担后勤、守备和集体作战任务。
description.en: Raised temporarily from dependent settlements and city residents in emergencies, mainly for logistics, garrison duty, and mass combat.

commanderInChief.zh: 统治议会或最高祭司军事首领
commanderInChief.en: Ruling council or supreme priestly war leader

technology.zh: 使用黑曜石刃武器、长矛、投枪器、盾牌、棉甲或厚织物护具；缺乏冶铁、骑兵和攻城机械，但在近战杀伤与仪式化战争中有效。
technology.en: Weaponry included obsidian-edged arms, spears, atlatls, shields, and cotton or thick textile armor; it lacked ironworking, cavalry, and siege engines, but was effective in close combat and ritualized warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自中部高地竞争城邦、资源产地争夺、贸易路线受扰以及周边地区对特奥蒂瓦坎扩张的反制。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival Central Highland polities, struggles over resource zones, disruption of trade routes, and resistance to Teotihuacan expansion in surrounding regions.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

populationDescription.zh: 人口规模在美洲早期都市中极为突出，包含本地居民、工匠、商人、建筑劳工和来自瓦哈卡、玛雅地区及其他中美洲地带的移民社群。
populationDescription.en: Its population was exceptionally large for an early American city, including local residents, artisans, merchants, construction laborers, and immigrant communities from Oaxaca, the Maya region, and other parts of Mesoamerica.

name.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎谷地附属聚落
name.en: Dependent Settlements of the Teotihuacan Valley

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司、贵族、行政首领和高级战士；中层为工匠、商人、专业建筑人员与社区领袖；下层为农民、劳工、仆役和依附人口。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of priests, nobles, administrators, and senior warriors; the middle strata included artisans, merchants, specialized builders, and community leaders; the lower strata comprised farmers, laborers, servants, and dependent peoples.

allies.zh: 无明确的持久同盟体系，更常见的是与周边聚落形成依附、朝贡、婚姻或宗教联系网络。
allies.en: There was no clearly defined permanent alliance system; more common were networks of dependency, tribute, marriage ties, and religious association with neighboring settlements.

enemies.zh: 潜在对手包括中部墨西哥竞争城邦、反抗其贸易影响的高地与低地群体，以及不愿纳入其宗教网络的地方势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included rival Central Mexican city-states, highland and lowland groups resisting its commercial influence, and local powers unwilling to enter its religious network.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以扩展宗教威望、控制黑曜石流通、吸纳外来社群和建立跨区域象征影响力为核心，兼用贸易、婚姻、仪式与有限武力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on expanding religious prestige, controlling obsidian circulation, attracting immigrant communities, and building transregional symbolic influence, using trade, marriage, ritual, and limited force.

era.zh: 中美洲早期古典化都市时代
era.en: Mesoamerican early urbanizing proto-Classic age

keyInnovations.zh: 大规模网格化城市规划、石灰灰泥建筑、标准化公寓复合体、黑曜石专业化生产、排水工程与仪式性纪念建筑整合。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included large-scale gridded urban planning, lime-plaster construction, standardized apartment compounds, specialized obsidian production, drainage works, and integrated ceremonial monumental architecture.

strengths.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎拥有庞大人口、先进都市规划、强大的黑曜石经济、突出的宗教吸引力和跨地区交流网络，是中部墨西哥最具成长性的中心之一。
strengths.en: Teotihuacan possessed a large population, advanced urban planning, a powerful obsidian economy, strong religious magnetism, and broad exchange networks, making it one of the most dynamic centers in Central Mexico.

weaknesses.zh: 其政体结构可能仍在形成中，对周边资源和粮食供给依赖较大，内部多族群整合复杂，且缺乏稳定成文制度与成熟远程军事投送能力。
weaknesses.en: Its political structure was likely still taking shape, it depended heavily on surrounding resources and food supplies, internal integration across multiple ethnic communities was complex, and it lacked stable written institutions and mature long-range military projection.

outlook.zh: 若能持续控制黑曜石产地、整合腹地并以宗教和工程强化中心权威，特奥蒂瓦坎极可能在未来数世纪发展为中美洲最重要的超级都市之一。
outlook.en: If it could continue controlling obsidian sources, integrate its hinterland, and reinforce central authority through religion and public works, Teotihuacan was highly likely to develop into one of the greatest super-cities of Mesoamerica in the coming centuries.

description.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎正在崛起为中部墨西哥最大城市之一，影响黑曜石贸易与宗教网络。
description.en: Teotihuacan was rising into one of Central Mexico’s largest cities, shaping obsidian trade and religious networks.

name.zh: 东非班图与沿海诸政体
name.en: East African Bantu and Coastal Polities

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦与村社联盟，由亲族、年龄组与地方长老共同维系权力
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of tribal chiefdoms and village confederations, with authority maintained through kinship, age-sets, and local councils of elders

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗族、氏族、村社和年龄等级组织为核心，财富主要体现在牛群、土地使用权、铁器、婚姻联姻与随从人数上；沿海与内陆贸易节点开始出现更高地位的中介群体
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on lineages, clans, villages, and age-grade organizations, with wealth expressed chiefly in cattle, land-use rights, iron tools, marriage alliances, and dependents; along coastal and inland trade nodes, higher-status intermediary groups were beginning to emerge

rulingClass.zh: 氏族首领、长老、祭司性人物、富牧主与控制铁冶或贸易通道的地方精英
rulingClass.en: Clan chiefs, elders, ritual specialists, wealthy pastoralists, and local elites who controlled ironworking or trade routes

succession.zh: 通常依血缘与宗族共识继承，多为家族内部传承，但需获得长老支持；部分地区兼顾兄终弟及或旁系继承
succession.en: Succession usually followed lineage and clan consensus, often within a leading family but requiring elder approval; in some areas collateral succession, including brother-to-brother transfer, also occurred

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构，各地区由村社会议、长老会和酋长主持治理；较大的聚落可对周边村落行使松散宗主权，沿海和大湖区贸易节点形成较稳定的区域性权力中心
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; governance was conducted locally through village assemblies, councils of elders, and chiefs. Larger settlements could exercise loose overlordship over nearby villages, while coastal and Great Lakes trade nodes formed more stable regional centers of authority

function.zh: 裁决纠纷、确认继承、协调婚姻、土地与牲畜事务，并在战和问题上向酋长提供意见
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, confirmed succession, coordinated marriage, land, and cattle matters, and advised chiefs on war and peace

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀、降雨仪式、成年礼和王权或酋权合法性仪式，维系共同体神圣秩序
function.en: Oversaw ancestral rites, rainmaking ceremonies, initiation rituals, and ceremonies legitimizing chiefly authority, sustaining the sacred order of the community

function.zh: 登记或记忆牲畜贡纳、婚礼聘礼、战利品分配及向首领进献的物资
function.en: Managed the recording or oral accounting of livestock tribute, bridewealth, spoils distribution, and goods presented to chiefs

function.zh: 维护地方集市、渡口、内陆交换路线和与海岸商人的联络，保障象牙、铁器、牲畜和珠饰流通
function.en: Maintained local markets, river crossings, inland exchange routes, and links with coastal merchants, facilitating the circulation of ivory, iron goods, livestock, and beads

function.zh: 在冲突时动员年龄组战士，组织边界巡逻、牲畜保护、报复袭击与护送商旅
function.en: Mobilized age-set warriors during conflict, organized border patrols, cattle protection, retaliatory raids, and caravan escort

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落和氏族为单位，实际行政由长老、家长和酋长代理人共同承担；行政边界多随水源、放牧地、耕地与亲族网络而定，不具统一划分
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages and clans, with practical governance shared by elders, household heads, and agents of chiefs; administrative boundaries usually followed water sources, grazing lands, arable zones, and kinship networks rather than a uniform territorial scheme

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，以口头传统传承，强调赔偿、和解、牲畜赔付、婚姻义务与血亲责任；严重暴力事件可能引发复仇，需通过长老调解和祭仪恢复秩序
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of customary norms transmitted orally, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, cattle payments, marital obligations, and kin responsibility; serious violence could trigger feud, requiring elder mediation and ritual settlement to restore order

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一定额税制，首领收入主要来自贡赋、牲畜进献、战利品分配、婚礼礼物、市场抽成和对贸易通道的控制
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform assessed tax system; chiefly income came mainly from tribute, cattle gifts, shares of war booty, marriage prestations, market dues, and control over trade routes

religion.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、地方自然神力、降雨祭祀和护佑性灵体信仰为主，不同地区兼有库希特与班图传统；沿海地区开始接触南阿拉伯和印度洋宗教观念，但影响仍有限
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, local nature powers, rainmaking cults, and protective spirit beliefs, varying across Cushitic and Bantu traditions; coastal areas were beginning to encounter South Arabian and Indian Ocean religious ideas, though their impact remained limited

philosophy.zh: 无成体系书面哲学，核心观念强调祖先权威、长幼秩序、共同体互助、牲畜与土地的道德管理，以及人与自然周期之间的平衡
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy, but core ideas stressed ancestral authority, seniority, communal reciprocity, the moral stewardship of cattle and land, and balance between human life and natural cycles

writingSystem.zh: 大多数社会无本地成文文字，政治与法律记忆依赖口传、仪式、歌谣和谱系背诵；沿海极少数接触外来书写传统，但尚未普及
writingSystem.en: Most societies had no indigenous writing system; political and legal memory relied on oral transmission, ritual, song, and recited genealogies. A few coastal communities had limited exposure to foreign scripts, but literacy was not widespread

culturalAchievements.zh: 铁冶技术扩散显著，陶器风格多样，形成稳定的村落农业—牧业生活；珠饰装饰、木器、皮革工艺、口头史诗、谱系记忆和成年礼制度构成重要文化成就，沿海社会逐步纳入印度洋交换网络
culturalAchievements.en: The spread of ironworking was a major achievement, alongside diverse pottery traditions and stable agro-pastoral village life; bead adornment, woodworking, leathercraft, oral epics, genealogical memory, and initiation systems were important cultural accomplishments, while coastal communities were gradually being incorporated into the Indian Ocean exchange network

languageFamily.zh: 以尼日尔-刚果语系中的班图语支和阿非罗-亚洲语系中的库希特语支为主，局部地区亦有尼罗-撒哈拉语系成分
languageFamily.en: The dominant language families were Bantu branches of Niger-Congo and Cushitic branches of Afroasiatic, with some Nilo-Saharan elements in adjoining regions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量以分散的自给农业、牧业、铁器制造和地方交换为基础，货币化程度低；若以外部银币价值粗略折算，总体经济规模不小，但大部分财富并不以货币形式存在，而体现为牲畜、谷物、工具、劳动力和社会关系
gdpDescription.en: Overall output was based on dispersed subsistence agriculture, pastoralism, iron production, and local exchange, with very low monetization. If roughly converted into external silver-coin equivalents, the economy was not insignificant in size, but most wealth existed not in money form but as cattle, grain, tools, labor, and social ties

mainIndustries.zh: 粟类与高粱种植、豆类耕作、畜牧、铁矿开采与冶炼、锻造、陶器、木工、皮革、象牙采集和区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Millet and sorghum cultivation, pulse farming, livestock herding, iron ore extraction and smelting, blacksmithing, pottery, woodworking, leather production, ivory procurement, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 象牙、犀角、龟甲、香料性林产、兽皮、牲畜、铁器、盐、珠饰、陶器、谷物，以及从外部输入的玻璃珠、金属器和少量奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, rhinoceros horn, tortoiseshell, aromatic forest products, hides, livestock, iron tools, salt, beads, pottery, grain, and imported items such as glass beads, metal goods, and small quantities of luxuries

unitName.en: head of cattle / string of beads

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要由谷仓储备、耕作工具、牲畜数量、亲族支持和婚姻网络构成；富裕家庭拥有更多牛群、铁器、附庸和对贸易品的优先获取权
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly measured by grain stores, farming tools, livestock holdings, kin support, and marriage networks; wealthier households possessed larger herds, more iron implements, dependents, and privileged access to trade goods

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯等值/人/年
unit.en: Roman denarii equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产和氏族再分配为主的农业—牧业混合经济，辅以手工业和长短途交换；首领通过礼物、贡纳和贸易中介积累剩余，而非依靠标准化税制与市场货币
economicSystem.en: The economy was an agro-pastoral mixed system centered on household production and clan redistribution, supplemented by craft activity and short- and long-distance exchange; chiefs accumulated surplus through gifts, tribute, and trade intermediation rather than standardized taxation and market currency

source.en: Livestock dues and shares of bridewealth

category.en: Maintenance of chiefly households and retainers
Emergency reserves of grain and livestock

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一中央国库，所谓“财库”更多是分散在各酋长家产中的牛群、谷仓、铁器、珠饰和可调动人力；沿海与贸易节点可能持有少量外来硬币或金属锭，但主要仍为实物储备
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified central treasury; the effective 'treasury' consisted of herds, granaries, iron goods, beads, and mobilizable labor dispersed among chiefly households. Coastal and trade-node elites may have held small amounts of imported coin or bullion, but reserves were predominantly in kind

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政原则重在维持首领威望、保障宴飨与再分配、确保战时动员和稳定贸易关系；在歉收年份，首领若能进行赈济与调解，便能巩固统治合法性
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed primarily at sustaining chiefly prestige, supporting feasting and redistribution, ensuring wartime mobilization, and stabilizing trade relations; in poor harvest years, chiefs who provided relief and mediation could strengthen their legitimacy

unit.en: head of cattle / string of beads

description.zh: 主要由村社和年龄组动员，使用长矛、木盾、短刀和部分铁制武器，适合近距离冲突、劫掠和防御
description.en: Mainly mobilized from villages and age-sets, these warriors used spears, wooden shields, knives, and some iron weapons, suited for close combat, raiding, and local defense

description.zh: 多来自牧业较强地区，机动性较高，负责牲畜防护、报复袭击和边境巡行，但不属于正式骑兵军种
description.en: Drawn largely from more pastoral regions, these forces had higher mobility and were used for cattle protection, retaliatory raids, and frontier patrols, though they were not a formal cavalry arm

description.zh: 由首领亲信和精锐战士组成，负责守卫聚落中心、护送使者和在战斗中充当核心力量
description.en: Composed of trusted followers and elite warriors of the chiefs, they guarded settlement centers, escorted envoys, and formed the core fighting element in battle

description.zh: 部署于海岸登陆点、河渡和贸易通道，负责保护货物、向商旅示威并监控外来往来
description.en: Stationed at coastal landing places, river crossings, and trade routes, they protected goods, displayed force to merchants, and monitored external movement

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；战时由各地酋长或资深战首分别统领
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; in wartime, local chiefs or senior war leaders commanded their own forces

technology.zh: 军备以铁矛头、短刀、斧、木盾和简易护具为主，作战组织依赖年龄组、亲族纽带和熟悉地形的机动；无统一攻城技术，海上军事能力极弱
technology.en: Military equipment centered on iron spearheads, knives, axes, wooden shields, and simple protection, while organization relied on age-sets, kinship, and terrain familiarity. There was no developed siege tradition and naval capability was minimal

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近氏族间的牲畜争夺、土地和水源冲突、贸易通道竞争，以及局部饥荒引发的人口流动；沿海地区则需应对外来商人势力变化和阿克苏姆扩张前期的区域影响
threats.en: The chief threats came from cattle raiding among neighboring clans, disputes over land and water, competition for trade routes, and migration driven by local famine; coastal regions also had to respond to changing foreign merchant influence and the early regional effects of Aksumite expansion

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于东非内陆高地、大湖周边、稀树草原与沿海带，多为中小型村落和季节性聚居点；人口密度总体不高，但农业较稳定地区和贸易节点较为集中
populationDescription.en: The population was widely spread across East African interior uplands, Great Lakes margins, savannas, and the coastal belt, mostly in small to medium villages and seasonal settlements; overall density was low, though more stable farming zones and trade nodes were relatively concentrated

name.zh: 维多利亚湖北岸大型聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层相对有限但已存在差异：上层为首领家族、长老、祭司与富牧主；中层为自由农牧民、工匠和地方商贩；下层包括贫困家户、依附者、战俘及部分家庭奴役人口，但整体尚未形成高度制度化的阶级国家结构
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was limited but real: the upper layer consisted of chiefly families, elders, ritual specialists, and wealthy pastoralists; the middle layer included free farmers, herders, artisans, and local traders; the lower layer included poor households, dependents, war captives, and some domestic servile persons, though no highly institutionalized class-state structure had yet formed

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友体系，各地通常与邻近氏族、沿海中介商人与部分南阿拉伯—印度洋商帮保持互利关系；局部地区也与阿克苏姆前身势力或尼罗河上游群体进行交换与结盟
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; local groups typically maintained mutually beneficial ties with neighboring clans, coastal intermediaries, and some South Arabian–Indian Ocean merchant groups. Certain areas also exchanged and allied with proto-Aksumite forces or Upper Nile communities

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象并非单一文明，而是竞争水草、牲畜与贸易优势的邻近部族；在北部与东北部边缘，也可能与更组织化的库施、阿克苏姆或沙漠边缘势力发生摩擦
enemies.en: The main enemies were not a single civilization but neighboring tribes competing for pasture, cattle, and trade advantage; on northern and northeastern margins, friction could also arise with more organized Kushite, Aksumite, or desert-edge powers

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以务实交换、婚姻联姻、互不侵犯和小规模军事威慑为主；沿海社会倾向保护商路并吸纳外来货物，内陆社会则重视控制象牙、铁器与牲畜的流通节点
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic, relying on exchange, marriage alliances, non-aggression understandings, and limited military deterrence; coastal communities aimed to protect trade routes and absorb foreign goods, while inland societies prioritized control over nodes in the movement of ivory, iron, and livestock

era.zh: 东非铁器时代早期至中期
era.en: Early to Middle East African Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 地方铁冶炉技术、铁制农具与武器、稳定村落农业、牲畜管理技术、陶器烧制、长途交换网络以及沿海与印度洋商路的初步连接
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included local bloomery iron-smelting techniques, iron agricultural tools and weapons, stable village agriculture, livestock management practices, pottery firing, long-distance exchange networks, and early integration of the coast with Indian Ocean routes

strengths.zh: 拥有广泛的人口基础、适应多样生态环境的农牧组合经济、不断扩散的铁器技术和逐步增强的沿海—内陆交换网络；社会韧性强，地方共同体在灾荒或冲突后通常能依靠亲族与再分配恢复
strengths.en: Its strengths included a broad population base, an agro-pastoral economy adapted to varied ecological zones, the continuing spread of iron technology, and strengthening coast-to-interior exchange networks; social resilience was considerable, as local communities could often recover from famine or conflict through kinship and redistribution

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一征税、军令和基础设施；内部冲突频繁，生产剩余有限，书写和行政记录缺乏，因而难以形成跨区域的稳定国家结构，也易受贸易路线变动和气候波动影响
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, the lack of unified taxation, military command, and infrastructure; frequent internal conflict, limited surplus, and the absence of writing and administrative records made durable supraregional state formation difficult, while trade-route shifts and climatic variation could have large effects

outlook.zh: 前景总体稳定并具发展潜力。随着铁器进一步普及、人口增长和印度洋贸易加深，部分沿海和大湖地区可能出现更集中的酋邦与贸易中心；但在100年时，整体仍将长期保持多中心、地方化的发展格局
outlook.en: The outlook was broadly stable and full of developmental potential. As iron technology spread further, population grew, and Indian Ocean trade deepened, some coastal and Great Lakes regions could develop more centralized chiefdoms and trade centers; yet in 100 CE the overall pattern would likely remain multicentered and strongly local for a long time

description.zh: 东非内陆与海岸由多种班图与库施特语族社会构成，并逐步接入印度洋交换网络。
description.en: East Africa’s interior and coasts were home to many Bantu and Cushitic societies gradually connecting to Indian Ocean exchange.

ruler.zh: 蒙特阿尔班的佚名统治者

governmentForm.zh: 以蒙特阿尔班为核心、支配瓦哈卡高地的城市国家与区域霸权政体
governmentForm.en: A city-state and regional hegemonic polity centered on Monte Alban and dominant over the Oaxaca highlands

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈明显等级化，上层由王室、贵族、祭司与军事首领组成，中层为抄写者、工匠、商人和地方行政人员，下层为农民、劳役者与附属社区成员。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with the upper level composed of the royal line, nobles, priests, and military leaders; a middle level of scribes, artisans, merchants, and local administrators; and a lower level of farmers, laborers, and members of subordinate communities.

rulingClass.zh: 蒙特阿尔班王室、世袭贵族、祭司集团及与其结盟的地方精英
rulingClass.en: The Monte Alban royal house, hereditary nobility, priestly elites, and allied local notables

succession.zh: 大致为世袭继承，可能在王室内部择定合格继承人，并获得贵族与祭司认可
succession.en: Succession was broadly hereditary, likely selecting a qualified heir within the ruling family with approval from nobles and priests

structure.zh: 政权以宫殿—神庙复合中心为核心，统治者通过宫廷、祭司机构、军事贵族和附属地方首领实施统治。蒙特阿尔班直接控制核心山谷，并通过朝贡、婚盟、驻防与象征性纪念建筑影响外围聚落。
structure.en: The polity was centered on a palace-temple complex, with the ruler governing through the court, priestly institutions, military nobility, and subordinate local chiefs. Monte Alban directly controlled the core valleys and influenced outer settlements through tribute, marriage alliances, garrisons, and symbolic monumental architecture.

function.zh: 管理宫廷事务、贵族封地、劳役征发、仓储和公共工程协调
function.en: Managed court affairs, noble estates, labor levies, storage, and coordination of public works

function.zh: 主持祭祀、历法、占卜、王权合法化仪式及神庙资产
function.en: Oversaw rituals, calendrical observance, divination, legitimizing ceremonies of rulership, and temple assets

function.zh: 征收粮食、纺织品、手工业品与地方特产，管理储备和再分配
function.en: Collected foodstuffs, textiles, craft goods, and local specialties, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 组织守军、远征、俘虏管理、道路警戒与附属地区威慑
function.en: Organized garrisons, campaigns, management of captives, road security, and deterrence in subordinate regions

function.zh: 负责记事、地名记录、纪念碑铭刻、贡赋清册与精英谱系保存
function.en: Handled record-keeping, place-name documentation, monument carving, tribute registers, and preservation of elite genealogies

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托谷地村社、次级中心和附属首领。核心区可能由蒙特阿尔班任命或认可的贵族管理，外围地区则通过联盟、驻点和贡纳关系间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on valley communities, secondary centers, and subordinate chiefs. Core areas were likely managed by nobles appointed or recognized by Monte Alban, while peripheral areas were ruled indirectly through alliances, outposts, and tribute obligations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、统治者裁断、宗教禁忌和贵族仲裁为基础。对土地、贡赋、婚姻、劳役和叛服关系的规范较为重要，惩罚可能包括赔偿、劳役、降籍、监禁和仪式性处决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, rulings by the ruler, religious taboo, and noble arbitration. Regulation of land, tribute, marriage, labor obligations, and loyalty relations was important, with penalties possibly including compensation, labor service, degradation of status, imprisonment, and ritual execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础主要为贡赋与劳役，而非统一货币税。附属社区定期缴纳玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、陶器、黑曜石制品及其他地方特产，并承担建筑、道路和军事后勤劳役。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal basis relied mainly on tribute and labor service rather than a uniform monetary tax. Subordinate communities regularly delivered maize, beans, chili, cotton cloth, pottery, obsidian goods, and other local specialties, and also performed labor for construction, roads, and military logistics.

religion.zh: 宗教为多神崇拜，重视雨神、玉米生育神、祖先神与山岳、洞穴等神圣地景。祭祀包括献食、焚香、放血、舞仪与在特定场合进行的人祭。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, emphasizing deities of rain, maize fertility, ancestors, and sacred landscapes such as mountains and caves. Rituals included food offerings, incense burning, bloodletting, ceremonial dance, and in certain contexts human sacrifice.

philosophy.zh: 其思想并非成体系哲学，而是宇宙秩序、祖先合法性、历法循环与王权神授观念的结合，强调统治、农业与祭仪之间的平衡。
philosophy.en: Its thought was not philosophy in the formal sense but a combination of cosmic order, ancestral legitimacy, calendrical cycles, and sacral rulership, stressing balance among governance, agriculture, and ritual observance.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期中部美洲文字与历法记号体系，常见于碑刻、地名符号、人物名号和征服纪念。书写尚未达到后世玛雅文字的音节复杂度，但已具明确政治记录功能。
writingSystem.en: An early Mesoamerican writing and calendrical notation system was used, appearing on monuments, place-name signs, personal name glyphs, and conquest memorials. It did not reach the phonetic complexity of later Maya writing but clearly served political record-keeping.

culturalAchievements.zh: 蒙特阿尔班的大型平台与仪式建筑、石刻征服碑铭、球场、精致陶器、墓葬壁龛与灰泥装饰，以及与历法和书写相关的早期国家象征体系，都是其重要成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included the great platforms and ceremonial architecture of Monte Alban, carved conquest monuments, ballcourts, fine ceramics, tomb niches and stucco ornament, and an early state symbolic system tied to calendars and writing.

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系，核心语言为萨波特克诸语
languageFamily.en: Oto-Manguean language family, with Zapotec languages at its core

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以山谷农业、地方手工业和区域再分配为基础，缺乏欧亚式铸币与大牲畜牵引，但在中部美洲范围内属于高度组织化的高地政体经济。估值以可比劳动与物资折算表示，仅为近似重建。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on valley agriculture, local craft production, and regional redistribution. It lacked Eurasian-style coinage and draft-animal traction, but within Mesoamerica it was a highly organized highland polity economy. The estimate is expressed in comparable labor and material values and is only an approximate reconstruction.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类和南瓜种植、陶器制作、纺织、黑曜石加工、石器与建筑石材开采、宗教与礼仪性工艺品生产
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, cultivation of beans and squash, pottery production, textile making, obsidian working, stone tool and construction stone extraction, and production of ritual and ceremonial goods

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、陶器、黑曜石刃器、绿色石材、贝壳饰物、可可少量转运品及礼仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili peppers, cotton cloth, pottery, obsidian blades, greenstone, shell ornaments, limited cacao transit goods, and ceremonial items

name.zh: 无统一铸币，以贡赋与物品价值计量
name.en: No unified coinage; value reckoned through tribute goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、房屋、储粮、陶器、织物和家族劳动力；精英财富则集中于墓葬随葬品、装饰品、附庸控制权和再分配能力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of use-rights to farmland, houses, stored grain, pottery, textiles, and family labor; elite wealth was concentrated in tomb goods, ornaments, control over dependents, and redistributive capacity.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为农业基础上的朝贡—再分配体系，辅以市场交换和长短途贸易。国家与神庙掌握部分剩余产品，精英通过控制劳役、土地与仪式网络获取财富。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a tribute-redistributive order based on agriculture, supplemented by market exchange and short- and long-distance trade. The state and temples controlled part of the surplus, while elites gained wealth by controlling labor, land, and ritual networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非单纯金银储藏，而主要以粮仓储备、织物、器物、礼仪材料和可调配劳役义务形式存在。贵族与神庙也分别掌握相当部分储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a simple stock of precious metal, but consisted mainly of granary reserves, textiles, crafted goods, ritual materials, and mobilizable labor obligations. Nobles and temples also controlled significant reserves separately.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持稳定贡赋流、支持纪念建筑和祭祀、保障核心区仓储，并通过再分配巩固附属社群忠诚。丰年积储，歉年则调拨赈济以防离叛。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to maintain a stable tribute flow, support monument building and ritual, secure storage in the core zone, and reinforce subordinate loyalty through redistribution. Surplus was accumulated in good years and redistributed in poor years to prevent defection.

description.zh: 由与王室关系密切的精英武士组成，负责都城守卫、仪式展示与关键战斗突击。
description.en: Composed of elite warriors closely tied to the royal house, responsible for defending the capital, ceremonial display, and shock action in key engagements.

description.zh: 主体军力来自附属社区和谷地居民的征召，装备投枪、矛、木盾、石刃武器与棉甲或厚织物护具。
description.en: The main military force came from levies drawn from subordinate communities and valley populations, equipped with darts, spears, wooden shields, stone-bladed weapons, and cotton armor or thick woven protection.

description.zh: 部署于交通节点和边缘据点，执行警戒、情报搜集、押送贡赋和压制地方反抗等任务。
description.en: Stationed at transport nodes and peripheral outposts, they performed watch duty, intelligence gathering, tribute escort, and suppression of local resistance.

commanderInChief.zh: 蒙特阿尔班统治者兼最高军事领袖
commanderInChief.en: The Ruler of Monte Alban as supreme military leader

technology.zh: 军事技术以步兵作战为主，使用投枪、矛、棍棒、石刃武器、木盾和棉质或厚布防护；依赖山地据点、防御工事、道路控制和仪式化威慑，而无金属武器、骑兵或大型攻城器。
technology.en: Military technology was primarily infantry-based, using darts, spears, clubs, stone-bladed weapons, wooden shields, and cotton or thick-cloth protection. It relied on hilltop positions, fortifications, control of roads, and ritualized intimidation, without metal weapons, cavalry, or large siege engines.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自瓦哈卡地区内部竞争中心、附属社群的反抗，以及与中部墨西哥和南方邻近势力的边界摩擦。更大的风险是区域网络变化导致的朝贡衰退，而非远距离外敌入侵。
threats.en: The principal threats came from rival centers within Oaxaca, resistance among subordinate communities, and border frictions with neighboring powers in Central Mexico and the south. The greater danger was decline in tribute due to shifting regional networks rather than invasion by distant enemies.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约35万，主要分布于瓦哈卡中央谷地及受蒙特阿尔班影响的周边高地和山间盆地。人口以农村为主，都城及次级礼仪中心集中较多精英、工匠与祭司。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 350,000, concentrated mainly in the Central Valleys of Oaxaca and nearby highlands and intermontane basins under Monte Alban's influence. Most people were rural, while the capital and secondary ceremonial centers concentrated elites, artisans, and priests.

name.zh: 特拉科卢拉谷地聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室、贵族、祭司、武士、书记与工匠、商人与地方中介者、普通农民、附庸社群成员以及战俘和奴役人口。身份、居住位置与墓葬规模明显反映等级差异。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal family, nobles, priests, warriors, scribes and artisans, merchants and local intermediaries, common farmers, members of subordinate communities, and captives or enslaved persons. Status differences were clearly reflected in residence patterns and tomb scale.

allies.zh: 主要依靠瓦哈卡谷地内的附属首领、婚盟贵族和受其保护的次级中心，而非固定意义上的国际同盟。
allies.en: It relied chiefly on subordinate chiefs, marriage-allied nobles, and protected secondary centers within the Oaxaca valleys rather than formal international-style alliances.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为本地区或邻近地区的竞争政治中心、拒绝纳贡的社区，以及在贸易线路与边界控制上与蒙特阿尔班发生冲突的势力。
enemies.en: Its enemies were mainly rival political centers in its own region or nearby areas, communities refusing tribute, and powers that clashed with Monte Alban over trade routes and border control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域霸权维持为核心，通过婚盟、贡纳、纪念碑宣传、宗教威望和有限军事行动巩固控制。其互动范围主要在中部美洲内部，与欧亚诸文明并无直接接触。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining regional hegemony through marriage alliances, tribute, monumental propaganda, religious prestige, and limited military action. Its sphere of interaction remained within Mesoamerica, with no direct contact with Eurasian civilizations.

era.zh: 早期古典期中部美洲高地文明阶段
era.en: Early Classic Mesoamerican highland civilization phase

keyInnovations.zh: 山顶城市规划、大型平台建造、早期文字与历法记号、精细石刻纪念碑、区域性道路与朝贡组织、礼仪球场建筑，以及高度发达的陶器与墓葬工艺。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included hilltop urban planning, construction of large platforms, early writing and calendrical notation, finely carved monumental stonework, regional road and tribute organization, ceremonial ballcourt architecture, and highly developed ceramics and funerary craftsmanship.

strengths.zh: 蒙特阿尔班拥有强大的象征权威、优越的山顶都城防御、成熟的区域朝贡网络、发达的礼仪建筑传统和较高程度的社会组织能力，使其在瓦哈卡长期保持中心地位。
strengths.en: Monte Alban possessed strong symbolic authority, a defensible hilltop capital, a mature regional tribute network, a developed monumental-ritual tradition, and a relatively high degree of social organization, allowing it to maintain centrality in Oaxaca for a long period.

weaknesses.zh: 政权对附属地区忠诚、礼仪合法性和精英协调依赖很深；交通与后勤受山地地形限制，经济以农业剩余和贡赋为主，面对长期干旱、地方离心或贸易路线变化时较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: The polity depended heavily on subordinate loyalty, ritual legitimacy, and elite coordination; transport and logistics were constrained by mountainous terrain, and an economy centered on agricultural surplus and tribute was vulnerable to prolonged drought, local fragmentation, or shifts in trade routes.

outlook.zh: 至100年左右，萨波特克政体总体稳定，仍是瓦哈卡高地最重要的政治与宗教中心之一。短期内可继续维持区域影响力，但中长期将面临地方中心重新崛起与权力网络松动的挑战。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE, the Zapotec polity was broadly stable and remained one of the foremost political and religious centers of the Oaxaca highlands. In the short term it could continue to sustain regional influence, but in the medium to long term it would face the challenge of resurgent local centers and loosening power networks.

description.zh: 瓦哈卡高地的萨波特克城邦体系以蒙特阿尔班为中心，延续古典前期政治传统。
description.en: The Zapotec system of the Oaxaca highlands, centered on Monte Alban, continued an important early Classic political tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教仪式为核心、由地方精英家族控制的神权政体；权力围绕大型礼仪中心、灌溉工程与战俘献祭实践组织。
governmentForm.en: A theocratic polity centered on ritual authority and controlled by local elite lineages; power was organized around major ceremonial centers, irrigation works, and the taking of captives for sacrifice.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显，上层为祭司王、武士贵族与家族首领；中层包括工匠、管理者与专业祭司；下层为农民、渔民、劳役者与战俘。社会义务与地位同宗教职能、治水劳动和贡赋紧密相连。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with priest-kings, warrior nobles, and lineage chiefs at the top; artisans, administrators, and specialist priests in the middle; and farmers, fishers, laborers, and captives below. Status and obligation were closely tied to religious roles, irrigation labor, and tribute.

rulingClass.zh: 主持仪式的祭司贵族、武士首领及掌控水利与土地分配的地方家族
rulingClass.en: Ritual priestly nobles, warrior chiefs, and local lineages that controlled irrigation and land allocation

succession.zh: 继承方式可能以地方世袭家族为基础，并通过宗教合法性、婚姻联盟与对礼仪中心的控制加以确认；尚无统一王朝式继承证据。
succession.en: Succession was likely based on hereditary local ruling houses, validated through ritual legitimacy, marriage alliances, and control of ceremonial centers; there is no evidence for a single unified dynastic succession.

structure.zh: 政权呈区域性分布，由月亮神庙等礼仪中心统合周边谷地聚落。统治依靠祭祀权威、武力威慑、灌溉组织与贡赋网络，各谷地可能由相互关联但相对独立的精英集团治理。
structure.en: The polity was regionally organized, with ceremonial centers such as Huaca de la Luna integrating surrounding valley settlements. Rule depended on ritual authority, military coercion, irrigation management, and tribute networks, while individual valleys were likely governed by related but semi-autonomous elite groups.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、历法仪式、献祭活动与神庙资源分配，维护统治合法性。
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, calendrical rites, sacrificial practices, and the distribution of temple resources, sustaining political legitimacy.

function.zh: 组织修筑与维护灌渠、堤坝和农田分水，协调劳役并监控作物生产。
function.en: Organized the construction and maintenance of canals, embankments, and field water allocation, coordinating labor and monitoring crop output.

function.zh: 征收粮食、纺织品、陶器、金属制品与劳役，管理仓库并向精英和仪式活动再分配。
function.en: Collected food, textiles, ceramics, metal goods, and labor obligations, managing storehouses for redistribution to elites and ritual activities.

function.zh: 组织武装随从、护卫礼仪中心、处理战俘并支撑军事与献祭需求。
function.en: Managed armed retainers, guarded ceremonial centers, handled captives, and supported both military and sacrificial needs.

function.zh: 监管陶器、纺织、金属加工和壁画制作作坊，保障精英消费与宗教用品生产。
function.en: Supervised workshops producing ceramics, textiles, metallurgy, and murals for elite consumption and ritual use.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以谷地、灌溉区和附属村落为基础。各地由地方首领、祭司代理人与工程监督者管理，统治核心通过礼仪中心、仓储和劳役征发维持控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on valleys, irrigation districts, and dependent villages. These were managed by local chiefs, priestly agents, and work supervisors, while the political core maintained control through ceremonial centers, storage facilities, and labor mobilization.

legalSystem.zh: 缺乏成文法证据，秩序主要依靠习惯规范、宗教禁忌、精英裁断和公开惩罚维系。对贡赋、劳役、水权和等级礼制的违背可能受到严厉制裁。
legalSystem.en: There is no evidence for a formal written law code; order was maintained through customary rules, religious taboo, elite judgment, and public punishment. Violations involving tribute, labor duty, water rights, and hierarchical ritual norms were likely punished severely.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要以实物贡赋和徭役形式征收，包括玉米、豆类、棉花、渔产、纺织品、陶器和建筑劳役，而非货币税。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was gathered chiefly through tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, cotton, marine products, textiles, ceramics, and construction labor rather than monetary taxation.

religion.zh: 宗教以神庙礼仪、祖先崇拜、山海与生育丰产神灵、祭司占卜及人祭为核心。统治者借助仪式、献血与俘虏献祭展示与超自然力量的联系。
religion.en: Religion centered on temple ritual, ancestor veneration, deities of mountains, sea, fertility, and abundance, priestly divination, and human sacrifice. Rulers displayed their connection to supernatural forces through ceremonies, blood offerings, and the sacrifice of captives.

philosophy.zh: 莫切没有已知成体系书面哲学，其世界观强调宇宙循环、人与自然力量的交换、统治者的神圣中介角色，以及战争、丰产与祭祀之间的联系。
philosophy.en: The Moche had no known formal written philosophy, but their worldview emphasized cosmic cycles, reciprocity between humans and natural powers, the sacred mediating role of rulers, and the connection between warfare, fertility, and ritual.

writingSystem.zh: 无已证实成熟文字系统；信息传递主要依赖图像叙事、象征符号、建筑布局与口传传统。
writingSystem.en: No fully developed writing system is attested; communication of information relied mainly on visual narratives, symbolic motifs, architectural layout, and oral tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 以极高水准的写实陶器、肖像壶、彩绘壁画、金属工艺和 monumental adobe ceremonial architecture 著称；其图像对战争、仪式、海洋生物和精英生活有高度细致表现。
culturalAchievements.en: The Moche are known for exceptionally accomplished realistic ceramics, portrait vessels, painted murals, metalwork, and monumental adobe ceremonial architecture; their imagery portrays warfare, ritual, marine life, and elite life with remarkable detail.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能与北秘鲁沿海前克丘亚语系或已消失地方语言有关；无直接文字证据可确定。
languageFamily.en: The language situation is uncertain, possibly involving pre-Quechuan or now-lost local languages of Peru’s north coast; there is no direct written evidence to identify it securely.

unit.en: kg grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高强度灌溉农业、渔业和手工业为基础，在安第斯前哥伦布社会中属繁荣水平。财富主要体现在粮食产量、棉纺与精英控制的再分配体系，而非市场货币流通。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on intensive irrigation agriculture, fishing, and craft production, and was prosperous by pre-Columbian Andean standards. Wealth was expressed mainly in food output, cotton textiles, and elite-controlled redistribution rather than market monetization.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、渔业、棉纺织、陶器制作、金属加工、建筑营造与区域性贡赋再分配
mainIndustries.en: Irrigation agriculture, fishing, cotton textiles, pottery production, metallurgy, construction, and regional tribute redistribution

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、南瓜、辣椒、棉花、鱼干、贝壳、精制陶器、铜金属器、纺织品、羽毛与来自高地的石材和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, squash, chili peppers, cotton, dried fish, shells, fine ceramics, copper goods, textiles, feathers, and stone and luxury items obtained from the highlands

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在住房、农具、渔具、衣物、陶器与获得灌溉土地和社区支持的权利。精英财富则体现在金属器、纺织精品、随从、仓储控制权和礼仪用品上。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in dwellings, tools, fishing gear, clothing, ceramics, and rights to irrigated land and community support. Elite wealth was expressed in metal goods, fine textiles, retainers, control of storage, and ritual equipment.

unit.en: kg grain equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以神庙与精英主导的再分配经济为核心，辅以地方交换网络。生产由家户、社区和受统治者指挥的劳役共同完成，市场机制存在但并非主导。
economicSystem.en: The economy was centered on temple- and elite-led redistribution, supplemented by local exchange networks. Production was carried out by households, communities, and ruler-directed labor, with market mechanisms present but not dominant.

source.zh: 高地交换与附属首领献纳

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非金属货币储藏，而是由仓库中的粮食、棉纺品、器物、劳动力调用权和礼仪贵重品构成的综合储备，可用于赈济、工程、祭祀和供养精英。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a hoard of coined money but a composite reserve of stored food, cotton textiles, crafted goods, command over labor, and ritual prestige items, usable for relief, construction, ceremonies, and elite support.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策着重维持灌溉系统、礼仪中心与精英再分配网络，通过集中储备应对旱灾、厄尔尼诺型气候波动和地区冲突。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining irrigation systems, ceremonial centers, and elite redistribution networks, with stored reserves used to manage drought, El Niño-type climate instability, and regional conflict.

description.zh: 由统治者和地方首领直接控制的精锐战士，负责护卫礼仪中心、执行突袭和俘获敌人。
description.en: Elite fighters directly controlled by rulers and local chiefs, responsible for guarding ceremonial centers, raiding, and capturing enemies.

description.zh: 由各谷地农民与社区成员组成，在冲突或工程保卫时临时征集，装备较简陋。
description.en: Farmers and community members from individual valleys, mobilized temporarily during conflict or to defend public works, with relatively simple equipment.

name.zh: 神庙守卫与俘虏押解队

description.zh: 负责神庙安全、仪式秩序和战俘看管，也兼任统治核心的治安力量。
description.en: Responsible for temple security, ceremonial order, and the custody of captives, also serving as an internal security force for the ruling core.

commanderInChief.en: The Priest-King and subordinate warrior chiefs

technology.zh: 武器包括棍棒、矛、投石器、标枪和防护头盔、盾牌与棉甲。军事技术不以大规模会战为主，更偏向仪式化战斗、谷地冲突、俘虏获取和要地控制。
technology.en: Weapons included clubs, spears, slings, darts, along with helmets, shields, and padded cotton armor. Military practice was not centered on large-scale pitched battles but on ritualized combat, valley warfare, captive taking, and control of key sites.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自相邻谷地竞争集团、南方纳斯卡文化圈的边界摩擦、高地群体的资源争夺，以及气候灾害引发的内部动荡。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival neighboring valley polities, frontier friction with the Nazca cultural sphere to the south, competition with highland groups for resources, and internal unrest caused by climatic disasters.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于秘鲁北海岸多个灌溉谷地，依附河谷农业、渔业资源和礼仪中心分布，整体人口规模足以支撑大型土坯建筑与区域性精英统治。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in multiple irrigated valleys on Peru’s north coast, distributed around river-valley agriculture, fisheries, and ceremonial centers; this population base was sufficient to support large adobe monuments and regional elite rule.

socialClasses.zh: 祭司王与贵族、武士阶层、祭司与行政人员、专业工匠、农民与渔民、劳役者、附属社群与战俘
socialClasses.en: Priest-kings and nobles, warrior strata, priests and administrators, specialist artisans, farmers and fishers, laborers, dependent communities, and captives

allies.zh: 不存在现代意义上的正式国际盟友；更常见的是与邻近谷地首领、高地交换伙伴和附属社群形成临时联盟与婚姻联系。
allies.en: There were no formal international allies in the modern sense; more typical were temporary alliances and marriage ties with neighboring valley chiefs, highland exchange partners, and subordinate communities.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是争夺水源、土地与仪式地位的沿海邻近政体，以及与莫切扩张发生冲突的地方集团。
enemies.en: Primary enemies were nearby coastal polities competing for water, land, and ritual status, as well as local groups resisting Moche expansion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制谷地、确保灌溉资源、吸纳贡赋和俘虏、维护长距离交换通道为核心，手段包括战争、威慑、礼物交换、婚姻和宗教象征支配。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling valleys, securing irrigation resources, drawing tribute and captives, and maintaining long-distance exchange routes, using warfare, intimidation, gift exchange, marriage, and religious-symbolic domination.

era.zh: 安第斯早期区域国家形成时期
era.en: Early Andean Regional State Formation

keyInnovations.zh: 大型灌溉渠系、土坯纪念建筑、复杂陶模与写实陶艺、铜及金属工艺、精细纺织与壁画制作技术
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale irrigation canals, monumental adobe architecture, sophisticated mold-made and realistic ceramics, copper and metalworking, and refined textile and mural techniques

strengths.zh: 莫切的优势在于高效灌溉农业、强大的礼仪整合能力、卓越手工业、能够动员大规模劳役，以及通过战争和祭祀巩固统治的意识形态工具。
strengths.en: Moche strengths included efficient irrigation agriculture, powerful ritual integration, outstanding craft production, the ability to mobilize large labor forces, and ideological tools that reinforced rule through warfare and ceremony.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治整合程度有限、各谷地权力分散、对气候与水利系统高度依赖、缺乏文字行政，以及统治合法性过度依赖精英仪式表现。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in limited political integration, fragmented valley-based power, heavy dependence on climate and irrigation systems, the absence of writing for administration, and a ruling legitimacy overly dependent on elite ritual performance.

outlook.zh: 公元100年前后莫切处于上升阶段，若能持续控制灌溉、扩张礼仪中心并整合周边谷地，未来数世纪将继续繁荣；但长期前景将受到厄尔尼诺事件、生态压力与区域竞争的显著制约。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE the Moche were in an ascendant phase; if they could continue controlling irrigation, expanding ceremonial centers, and integrating neighboring valleys, they would likely prosper for centuries, though their long-term future would be heavily constrained by El Niño events, ecological stress, and regional competition.

description.zh: 秘鲁北海岸的莫切诸政体正在形成，以灌溉农业、祭祀中心和精美陶艺著称。
description.en: On Peru’s north coast, early Moche polities were emerging, known for irrigation, ceremonial centers, and fine ceramics.

name.zh: 玛雅前古典晚期诸城邦

capital.zh: 无统一首都，以埃尔米拉多、蒂卡尔等城为核心
capital.en: No single capital; El Mirador, Tikal, and other cities served as major centers

governmentForm.zh: 神权王权结合的城邦体制
governmentForm.en: Theocratic monarchic city-state system

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格等级化结构，上层为王室、贵族与祭司，中层为抄写员、工匠、商人与地方首领，下层为农民、劳役者与奴隶或战俘。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with royal families, nobles, and priests at the top; scribes, artisans, merchants, and local headmen in the middle; and farmers, laborers, and slaves or war captives at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 由各城邦的王室宗族、贵族家族、祭司集团和高级军事领袖组成
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of royal lineages, noble houses, priestly elites, and senior military leaders in each city-state

succession.zh: 通常为王室世袭，以父系继承为主，但在部分城邦也会因婚姻联盟、幼主摄政或王朝争斗而调整
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within royal lineages, generally patrilineal, though marriage alliances, regencies for minors, and dynastic disputes could alter the process in some city-states

structure.zh: 玛雅诸城邦各自独立，由圣主统治；其下设王室宫廷、祭祀机构、贵族议政圈与附属聚落首领体系。大型城邦常控制若干次级中心与乡村腹地，形成区域性政治网络。
structure.en: Maya polities were independent city-states ruled by holy lords, beneath whom stood royal courts, ritual institutions, noble advisory circles, and subordinate settlement chiefs. Large cities often controlled secondary centers and rural hinterlands, forming regional political networks.

function.zh: 管理王室居所、宫廷礼仪、贡品分配、使者接待与王命传达
function.en: Managed royal residences, court ceremony, tribute distribution, reception of envoys, and transmission of royal commands

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、祭献、天文观测、历法制定与吉凶占定
function.en: Oversaw religious rituals, offerings, astronomical observation, calendar keeping, and divination

function.zh: 征收粮食、可可、棉布、玉器、羽毛及劳役，并管理仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected food, cacao, cotton cloth, jade, feathers, and labor obligations, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 组织金字塔、宫殿、堤道、水库、石碑与广场的修建和维护
function.en: Organized the construction and maintenance of pyramids, palaces, causeways, reservoirs, stelae, and plazas

function.zh: 负责战事动员、边界防卫、俘虏处置及对附属聚落和盟邦的监督
function.en: Handled war mobilization, border defense, treatment of captives, and oversight of subordinate settlements and allied polities

function.zh: 记录王统、典礼、贡赋、战争与纪年，负责象形文字文书与碑铭刻写
function.en: Recorded dynasties, ceremonies, tribute, wars, and dates, and maintained hieroglyphic documents and monumental inscriptions

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依靠次级中心首领、村落头人与王室任命或承认的贵族。核心都城通过贡赋、婚盟、仪式中心地位和军事威慑维系对周边的控制，但实际统治深度因地形与政治关系而异。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on secondary-center chiefs, village headmen, and nobles appointed or recognized by royal authority. Core capitals maintained influence over surrounding areas through tribute, marriage alliances, ritual centrality, and military intimidation, though actual depth of control varied by terrain and political ties.

legalSystem.zh: 无统一成文法典，法律与裁判主要基于王权裁断、习惯法、宗教禁忌及贵族调解。盗窃、叛逆、亵渎与贡赋违抗会受到惩罚，俘虏与从属者的地位则受政治和宗教因素影响。
legalSystem.en: There was no single codified legal system; justice rested on royal judgment, customary law, religious taboo, and noble mediation. Theft, rebellion, sacrilege, and refusal of tribute were punishable, while the status of captives and dependents was shaped by political and religious considerations.

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋和劳役为主，而非统一货币税。常见负担包括玉米、豆类、可可、盐、棉布、羽毛、陶器、石材、建筑劳役与军事服役。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on in-kind tribute and labor service rather than a unified monetary tax. Common obligations included maize, beans, cacao, salt, cotton cloth, feathers, pottery, stone, construction labor, and military service.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，崇拜玉米神、雨神查克、太阳神、羽蛇神等众多神祇，并重视祖先崇拜、王权神圣性、献血与祭献仪式。
religion.en: Maya religion was polytheistic, venerating the Maize God, the rain god Chaak, the sun, feathered serpent deities, and many others, while placing strong emphasis on ancestor worship, sacred kingship, bloodletting, and offering rituals.

philosophy.zh: 思想核心在于时间循环、天地层级秩序、王权与宇宙运行的联结，以及通过仪式维持人与神灵、祖先和自然之间的平衡。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core centered on cyclical time, a layered cosmic order, the linkage between kingship and the workings of the universe, and the maintenance of balance among humans, gods, ancestors, and nature through ritual.

writingSystem.zh: 使用成熟发展的玛雅象形文字体系，兼具表意与音节成分，用于碑铭、彩绘器物、宫廷记录与历法纪年。
writingSystem.en: The Maya used a highly developed hieroglyphic writing system combining logographic and syllabic elements, employed on monuments, painted vessels, court records, and calendrical inscriptions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 该时期玛雅低地持续发展大型金字塔建筑、三联金字塔布局、精细灰泥装饰、纪年碑刻、复杂历法与天文观测传统，并在宫廷艺术、玉器、羽饰和陶器绘画方面取得突出成就。
culturalAchievements.en: In this period the Maya lowlands continued to develop large pyramid architecture, triadic pyramid layouts, elaborate stucco decoration, dated monuments, complex calendrical systems, and traditions of astronomical observation, while also excelling in courtly art, jade work, feather regalia, and painted ceramics.

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高强度玉米农业、园圃种植、手工业和区域交换网络为基础。由于缺乏统一货币与近代统计，产值仅为折算估计，但总体显示其为美洲最繁荣的文明区域之一。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on intensive maize agriculture, household horticulture, craft production, and regional exchange networks. Because there was no unified currency and no modern accounting, output is only a conversion estimate, but it indicates one of the most prosperous regions in the ancient Americas.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、可可栽培、盐业、石灰与建筑业、陶器制作、玉器与贝饰加工、纺织与羽饰工艺
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cacao growing, salt production, lime and construction, pottery making, jade and shell working, textiles, and feather craft

tradeGoods.zh: 玉、黑曜石、燧石器、盐、可可、棉布、贝壳、羽毛、陶器、蜂蜜、蜡、木材与奢侈礼品
tradeGoods.en: Jade, obsidian, flint tools, salt, cacao, cotton cloth, shell, feathers, pottery, honey, wax, timber, and prestige goods

name.zh: 无统一货币，兼用可可豆与实物交换
name.en: No unified currency; cacao beans and barter media

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在房屋、耕地使用权、农具、陶器、织物和储粮；精英财富则集中于玉器、羽饰、祭器、侍从、稀有石材和对劳动力与贡赋的支配权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly embodied in dwellings, rights to farmland, tools, pottery, textiles, and stored food; elite wealth was concentrated in jade, feather regalia, ritual objects, attendants, rare stone, and control over labor and tribute.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余、家户生产、精英再分配与长短程交换并存的宫廷主导经济。市场交换可能已在部分中心存在，但整体仍以贡赋、礼物交换和政治控制为核心。
economicSystem.en: The economy was court-led, combining agricultural surplus, household production, elite redistribution, and both short- and long-distance exchange. Market exchange likely existed in some centers, but tribute, gift exchange, and political control remained central.

source.en: Tribute from subordinate and allied communities

treasuryDescription.zh: 玛雅各城邦并无统一国库，储备多以粮食、布匹、玉器、可可、盐与礼仪器物形式分散保存在宫殿仓库和附属仓储点。此处为主要大城邦合计估值。
treasuryDescription.en: The Maya had no single imperial treasury; reserves were dispersed in palace storehouses and subsidiary depots, often held as food, cloth, jade, cacao, salt, and ritual goods. The figure here is a combined estimate for the major flourishing city-states.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重心在于通过贡赋和劳役支持仪式、建筑和战争，以彰显王权合法性。岁入岁出高度依赖农业收成、附庸忠诚和大型工程动员能力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on using tribute and labor to sustain ritual, monumental construction, and warfare in order to display royal legitimacy. Revenue and expenditure depended heavily on harvests, vassal loyalty, and the ability to mobilize labor for major projects.

description.zh: 由王室亲兵、贵族战士和经验丰富的精锐组成，负责保卫都城、护卫君主并在战役中担任突击力量。
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, noble warriors, and experienced elite fighters, they defended capitals, guarded rulers, and served as the shock force in campaigns.

description.zh: 主要由平民男性按需征发，装备长矛、投枪、木盾、石刃武器和飞镖，构成战争中的主体兵力。
description.en: Raised from commoner males when needed, these troops carried spears, javelins, wooden shields, stone-bladed weapons, and darts, forming the bulk of wartime forces.

description.zh: 来自附属聚落和盟邦，承担侦察、搬运、围攻辅助和地区性作战任务。
description.en: Drawn from subordinate communities and allies, they performed scouting, transport, siege support, and local campaigning duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦圣主及其任命的军事首领
commanderInChief.en: Each city-state's Holy Lord and his appointed war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以石器和木器为主，使用燧石或黑曜石刃武器、长矛、投枪器、盾牌、棉甲和简易防御工事，不使用冶铁兵器、骑兵或战车。
technology.en: Military technology was based mainly on stone and wood, using flint or obsidian-bladed weapons, spears, atlatls, shields, cotton armor, and simple fortifications, without iron weapons, cavalry, or chariots.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自玛雅城邦之间的竞争与战争、附庸叛离、贸易通道争夺，以及环境波动对粮食与政治稳定造成的间接压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry and warfare among Maya city-states, defection of subordinate communities, competition over trade routes, and indirect pressure from environmental fluctuations affecting food supplies and political stability.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于玛雅低地的核心都会、次级中心、礼仪聚落与乡村农耕社区。100年时低地总体人口持续增长，大型建筑中心吸引了大量劳动力与精英定居。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across major Maya lowland capitals, secondary centers, ceremonial settlements, and rural farming communities. Around 100 CE the lowlands were still growing in population, with major building centers attracting large concentrations of labor and elites.

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括圣主与王室、世袭贵族、祭司与抄写员、工匠与商人、普通农民、劳役者，以及战俘和奴隶性从属者。社会身份常与血统、仪式角色和对土地与贡赋的控制密切相关。
socialClasses.en: The main social classes included holy lords and royal families, hereditary nobles, priests and scribes, artisans and merchants, ordinary farmers, laborers, and war captives or slave-like dependents. Social rank was closely tied to lineage, ritual role, and control over land and tribute.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友体系；各城邦通过婚姻、共同祭祀、贸易往来和军事合作形成暂时性盟邦与从属网络。
allies.en: There was no permanent unified alliance system; individual city-states formed temporary alliances and subordinate networks through marriage, shared rituals, trade, and military cooperation.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象为竞争性的邻近玛雅城邦，以及在贸易节点和边境地带争夺影响力的地方势力。
enemies.en: The principal enemies were rival neighboring Maya city-states and local powers competing for influence over trade nodes and frontier zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以区域平衡为主，通过联姻、贡品交换、俘虏展示、纪念碑宣传与有限战争来争取威望和霸权，而非建立持久统一帝国。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at regional balance, using marriage alliances, tribute exchange, display of captives, monumental propaganda, and limited warfare to gain prestige and hegemony rather than to build a durable unified empire.

era.zh: 晚前古典期至早期古典期过渡
era.en: Transition from the Late Preclassic to the Early Classic

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与知识成就包括成熟的象形文字书写、长纪历与260日及365日历法结合、精确天文观察、灰泥与石灰建筑技术、大型金字塔和堤道营建、水库与蓄水管理，以及高水平玉器和陶器工艺。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and achievements included mature hieroglyphic writing, the integration of the Long Count with the 260-day and 365-day calendars, precise astronomical observation, plaster and lime building technology, construction of large pyramids and causeways, reservoir and water-storage management, and high-level jade and ceramic craftsmanship.

strengths.zh: 优势在于高度发展的礼仪政治、卓越的建筑与书写传统、广泛的区域交换网络、强烈的王权象征体系，以及能够动员大量劳役修建宏伟城市景观。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in highly developed ritual politics, outstanding architectural and writing traditions, broad regional exchange networks, a powerful symbolic system of kingship, and the ability to mobilize large labor forces for monumental urban landscapes.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治长期分裂、城邦竞争频繁、财政依赖农业与劳役、统治对附庸忠诚和环境条件敏感，且缺乏统一军事与行政体系。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were chronic political fragmentation, frequent city-state rivalry, dependence on agriculture and labor tribute, sensitivity to vassal loyalty and environmental conditions, and the lack of a unified military and administrative system.

outlook.zh: 100年时玛雅文明总体处于兴盛阶段，低地大城仍在扩张，文字、历法和王权仪式持续深化。未来数世纪内，部分前古典巨型中心可能衰退，但新的古典时期王朝城市将崛起，维持玛雅文明的长期活力。
outlook.en: Around 100 CE the Maya world was broadly thriving, with major lowland centers still expanding and traditions of writing, calendrics, and royal ritual deepening. In the coming centuries some great Preclassic centers would decline, but new Classic-period dynastic cities would rise and sustain the long vitality of Maya civilization.

description.zh: 低地玛雅地区遍布兴盛城邦，金字塔建筑、文字与历法传统持续发展。
description.en: The Maya lowlands were filled with flourishing city-states where pyramid building, writing, and calendrical traditions continued to develop.

governmentForm.zh: 以祭祀中心为核心的地区性神权体制，由多个山谷社群在宗教权威下联合
governmentForm.en: A regional theocratic order centered on ceremonial authority, uniting multiple valley communities under religious leadership

socialStructure.zh: 社会由祭司贵族、地方首领、工匠、农民、渔民与劳役群体组成；宗教仪式与水利管理将分散聚落整合为共同体
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of priestly elites, local headmen, artisans, farmers, fishers, and laboring groups; ritual life and irrigation management integrated dispersed settlements into a wider community

rulingClass.zh: 掌控仪式、再分配与圣地建设的祭司家族及地方精英
rulingClass.en: Priestly families and local elites who controlled ritual, redistribution, and the construction of sacred centers

succession.zh: 非严格世袭王位制，祭司权威多由地方显贵家系在宗教声望、亲族关系与地方共识中延续
succession.en: Not a strict dynastic kingship; priestly authority generally continued through prominent local lineages, ritual prestige, kinship ties, and local consensus

structure.zh: 卡瓦奇为最高仪式中心，各河谷聚落保有地方自治，但在大型祭祀、劳役动员、圣地维护与水利事务上接受祭司集团协调
structure.en: Cahuachi served as the supreme ceremonial center. Valley communities retained local autonomy but were coordinated by priestly authorities in major rituals, labor mobilization, sacred-site maintenance, and water management

function.zh: 主持历法、祭典、供奉、祖先仪式与朝圣活动，维护卡瓦奇礼仪秩序
function.en: Oversaw calendrical rites, ceremonies, offerings, ancestor observances, and pilgrimage activities, maintaining ritual order at Cahuachi

function.zh: 组织河谷灌溉、地下水道维护、播种收获安排与旱灾应对
function.en: Managed valley irrigation, maintenance of subterranean aqueducts, planting and harvest scheduling, and drought response

function.zh: 征集贡赋与劳役产出，保管玉米、棉花、豆类、纺织品及祭品，并在节庆与危机时再分配
function.en: Collected tribute and labor output, stored maize, cotton, beans, textiles, and offerings, and redistributed them during festivals and times of need

function.zh: 监管陶器、纺织、染色与礼仪器物生产，组织专业工匠服务宗教中心与精英需求
function.en: Supervised pottery, textile, dyeing, and ritual-object production, organizing skilled artisans for the ceremonial center and elite demand

function.zh: 联络各聚落首领，登记劳动力，安排修建平台、道路、墓葬与地画相关工程
function.en: Liaised with local settlement leaders, registered labor, and arranged work on platforms, roads, burials, and geoglyph-related projects

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托各河谷聚落首领、亲族长者与祭司代理人运作，缺乏统一成文官僚体系，但在贡赋、灌溉和祭祀上具有稳定层级
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through valley headmen, kin elders, and priestly representatives. It lacked a uniform written bureaucracy but maintained stable hierarchies in tribute, irrigation, and ritual

legalSystem.zh: 无成熟成文法典，秩序主要依靠习惯法、亲族调解、祭司裁断与宗教禁忌维持；对冒犯圣地、破坏水利与拒绝劳役的行为尤为严厉
legalSystem.en: There was no mature written law code; order was maintained through customary norms, kin mediation, priestly judgment, and religious taboo. Offenses against sacred places, irrigation works, and communal labor obligations were treated especially seriously

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋和公共劳役为主，内容包括玉米、棉花、豆类、渔产、纺织品及建筑劳动，而非统一货币税
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on tribute in kind and communal labor, including maize, cotton, beans, fish products, textiles, and construction work rather than standardized monetary taxes

religion.zh: 纳斯卡宗教以自然神力、祖先崇拜、祭司仪式与圣地朝圣为核心，重视水、丰产、山地与天象象征；卡瓦奇是重要祭祀中心
religion.en: Nazca religion centered on natural sacred forces, ancestor veneration, priestly rites, and pilgrimage to holy places, with strong emphasis on water, fertility, mountains, and celestial symbolism; Cahuachi was its major ceremonial center

philosophy.zh: 未形成文字化哲学传统，但其世界观强调人与自然、祖灵、土地与水源之间的神圣互惠关系
philosophy.en: No formal written philosophical tradition existed, but its worldview stressed sacred reciprocity among humans, ancestral spirits, land, and water sources

writingSystem.zh: 无已知成熟文字系统，信息主要通过图像、纹饰、仪式表演、口传传统与空间象征表达
writingSystem.en: There was no known mature writing system; information was expressed chiefly through imagery, patterns, ritual performance, oral tradition, and symbolic landscapes

culturalAchievements.zh: 以多彩精细的纺织品、具象与神话主题陶器、复杂地下水道工程以及著名的纳斯卡地画闻名，这些成就体现了高度的仪式组织与审美能力
culturalAchievements.en: The culture was renowned for polychrome fine textiles, ceramics depicting figurative and mythic themes, sophisticated subterranean aqueduct engineering, and the famous Nazca geoglyphs, all reflecting advanced ritual organization and artistic skill

languageFamily.zh: 语言归属尚不确定，可能属于安第斯南海岸已消失的地方语言传统
languageFamily.en: Its language affiliation is uncertain and was likely part of now-lost local linguistic traditions of the south Andean coast

gdpDescription.zh: 纳斯卡经济以灌溉农业、棉花种植、手工业和区域交换为基础，整体属于前货币化、以实物和劳役组织的中等复杂经济；卡瓦奇更多是仪式与再分配中心，而非纯粹商业城市
gdpDescription.en: The Nazca economy rested on irrigated agriculture, cotton cultivation, craft production, and regional exchange. It was a moderately complex pre-monetary economy organized largely through goods and labor; Cahuachi functioned more as a ceremonial and redistributive center than as a purely commercial city

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、棉花生产、纺织、彩陶制作、渔业、驼类运输与区域手工业
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, cotton production, textile weaving, polychrome pottery, fishing, camelid transport, and regional craft industries

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、染织品、陶器、玉米、豆类、葫芦、辣椒、渔产、贝壳、黑曜石及来自高地的驼毛与其他交换品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, dyed textiles, pottery, maize, beans, gourds, chili peppers, fish products, shells, obsidian, and camelid fiber and other exchange goods from the highlands

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、灌溉水源、棉花与粮食储备、纺织工具、陶器、渔具以及亲族网络中的互助能力
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to fields, irrigation water, cotton and grain stores, weaving tools, pottery, fishing gear, and the strength of kin-based reciprocal support

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、地方首领征集、祭司再分配和区域互惠交换并存的礼仪型经济体系
economicSystem.en: A ritual-centered economy combining household production, local elite collection, priestly redistribution, and regional reciprocal exchange

category.zh: 祭祀中心维护与大型仪式
category.en: Ceremonial center maintenance and major rituals

treasuryDescription.zh: 纳斯卡“国库”并非金属货币储备，而主要是分散在圣地仓储与地方储藏设施中的粮食、棉花、纺织品、礼器和可调动劳力
treasuryDescription.en: The Nazca 'treasury' was not a metallic cash reserve but mainly stores of food, cotton, textiles, ritual goods, and mobilizable labor dispersed across sacred and local storage facilities

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持祭祀威望、保障灌溉与在歉收时实施再分配；整体保守，稳定优先于扩张
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining ritual prestige, securing irrigation, and carrying out redistribution in poor harvests; it was broadly conservative, prioritizing stability over expansion

description.zh: 驻守卡瓦奇及重要圣地的常备武装，负责守卫祭司、仓储与仪式活动
description.en: A small standing force stationed at Cahuachi and major sacred sites, guarding priests, storehouses, and ceremonies

description.zh: 由各聚落在冲突或防御需要时征集的农民与地方追随者，主要执行保卫灌溉设施和聚落的任务
description.en: Farmers and local retainers mobilized by settlements during conflict or defense, mainly tasked with protecting irrigation works and communities

description.zh: 依附地方精英和宗教领袖的小规模武装，用于威慑、护送与有限的区域冲突
description.en: Small armed retinues attached to local elites and religious leaders, used for deterrence, escort, and limited regional conflict

commanderInChief.zh: 祭司首领与地方谷地首领共同行使最高指挥
commanderInChief.en: The Priestly Chief together with leading valley headmen exercised supreme command

technology.zh: 武器以投石索、木矛、棍棒、石刃与简易防具为主，军事技术偏地方化，未形成职业化大军
technology.en: Weapons consisted mainly of slings, wooden spears, clubs, stone blades, and simple protection; military technology remained localized and did not support a large professional army

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近谷地竞争、资源与水源争端、局部袭扰以及气候压力导致的社会紧张，而非远程帝国入侵
threats.en: Main threats came from competition among neighboring valleys, disputes over resources and water, local raiding, and social stress caused by climate pressure rather than invasion by distant empires

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在秘鲁南海岸诸河谷与沿海地带，呈分散聚落格局；卡瓦奇虽为著名中心，但常住人口可能有限，更多在节庆时出现大规模集聚
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the river valleys and coastal zones of Peru’s south coast in a dispersed settlement pattern; although Cahuachi was a major center, its permanent population was probably limited, with much larger gatherings during ceremonial seasons

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司家族与地方首领；中间层包括熟练工匠、织工、灌溉组织者与富裕农户；下层为普通农民、渔民与承担公共劳役者，另有因战争或依附关系形成的边缘从属群体
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of priestly families and local chiefs; the middle included skilled artisans, weavers, irrigation organizers, and prosperous farmers; the lower comprised ordinary cultivators, fishers, and communal laborers, along with marginal dependent groups formed through warfare or patronage

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的远程盟友，主要通过与安第斯高地及邻近南海岸群体的交换与礼仪联系维持和平关系
allies.en: There were no fixed long-distance allies in the formal sense; peaceful relations were maintained mainly through exchange and ritual ties with Andean highland groups and neighboring south-coast communities

enemies.zh: 无持续性的单一宿敌，但与邻近谷地社群可能因灌溉、耕地、交通路线与声望竞争发生周期性冲突
enemies.en: There was no single enduring enemy, but periodic conflict could arise with nearby valley communities over irrigation, arable land, routes, and prestige

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以地区网络中的礼仪威望、物资交换和有限防御为主，不追求帝国式扩张，而重在确保朝圣流动与资源通道稳定
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on ritual prestige within regional networks, material exchange, and limited defense. It did not seek imperial expansion, focusing instead on securing pilgrimage flows and resource corridors

era.zh: 早期区域复杂社会时代
era.en: Early regional complex society era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括南海岸灌溉系统、地下水道工程、精细纺织染色技术、多色陶绘工艺以及通过大地景观组织仪式空间的地画设计
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included south-coast irrigation systems, subterranean aqueduct engineering, fine textile dyeing and weaving, polychrome ceramic painting, and geoglyph design that organized ritual space across the landscape

strengths.zh: 纳斯卡的优势在于强大的宗教整合力、精湛手工业、对干旱环境的水利适应能力，以及以卡瓦奇为中心的跨谷地仪式网络
strengths.en: Nazca strengths lay in powerful religious integration, highly skilled craft production, adaptation to arid conditions through irrigation, and a cross-valley ceremonial network centered on Cahuachi

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治集中度有限、对脆弱水源与气候条件依赖很高、缺乏文字行政与大型常备军，面对长期生态波动时抗压能力有限
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited political centralization, heavy dependence on fragile water supplies and climate conditions, the absence of writing-based administration, and the lack of a large standing army, all of which reduced resilience under prolonged ecological stress

outlook.zh: 到公元100年前后，纳斯卡总体稳定，卡瓦奇仍具强大仪式号召力；若灌溉体系与跨谷地合作得以维持，文明将继续繁荣，但未来仍受环境波动与区域竞争制约
outlook.en: Around 100 CE Nazca was broadly stable, and Cahuachi still held strong ceremonial appeal; if irrigation systems and cross-valley cooperation were maintained, the civilization could continue to flourish, though its future remained constrained by environmental fluctuation and regional competition

description.zh: 纳斯卡地区围绕卡瓦奇形成宗教中心与区域社群，以地画和纺织传统闻名。
description.en: The Nazca region formed ritual-centered communities around Cahuachi, renowned for geoglyphs and textiles.

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族与地方共同体为基础的分散部族酋邦体系，各谷地与高原盆地由世袭或半世袭首领统治
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal chiefdom system based on kinship and local communities, with each valley and highland basin governed by hereditary or semi-hereditary chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会以扩大家族、村落共同体与区域性酋邦为核心，农业定居民、驼畜放牧者、工匠与祭司群体相互依存
socialStructure.en: Society centered on extended families, village communities, and regional chiefdoms, with settled farmers, camelid herders, artisans, and ritual specialists forming an interdependent order

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、祭司精英与控制灌溉、梯田和畜群的氏族领袖
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families, ritual elites, and clan leaders who controlled irrigation, terraces, and herds

succession.zh: 多为家族内部继承，通常由首领子嗣、兄弟或同宗支系承继，并需获得地方长老与祭祀团体认可
succession.en: Usually inherited within leading families, typically by a chief's offspring, brothers, or related lineage branches, with recognition from local elders and ritual authorities

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家官僚机构；治理由村社长老会、地方酋长、祭司中心及劳役组织共同构成，区域间通过婚盟、交换与宗教联系维系
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; governance was carried out through village elder councils, local chiefs, ritual centers, and labor-organizing structures, while regions were linked by marriage alliances, exchange, and religious ties

function.zh: 管理耕地分配、劳役协调、储粮与日常纠纷
function.en: Managed land allocation, labor coordination, grain storage, and everyday disputes

function.zh: 主持祭祀、祖先崇拜、季节仪式与农业时令判断
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, ancestor worship, seasonal rites, and agricultural timekeeping

function.zh: 组织梯田修筑、渠道维护、道路与高地储藏设施建设
function.en: Organized terrace construction, canal maintenance, roads, and upland storage facilities

function.zh: 管理羊驼和原驼畜群、季节性转场与毛纺原料征集
function.en: Managed llama and alpaca herds, seasonal transhumance, and collection of fiber raw materials

function.zh: 征集地方贡品，协调不同生态带之间的物资交换
function.en: Collected local tribute goods and coordinated exchange among different ecological zones

function.zh: 在冲突时期召集战士、守卫牧场和通道并组织械斗
function.en: Called up warriors in times of conflict, guarded pastures and routes, and organized local warfare

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理由艾柳式亲族共同体、村落长老和地方酋长负责，较大区域可能围绕仪式中心形成松散支配网络
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested with ayllu-like kin communities, village elders, and local chiefs, while larger areas could form loose networks centered on ritual sites

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依靠习惯法、长老裁决、赔偿制度与宗教禁忌；盗窃、侵占水源、破坏梯田和冒犯祖灵常受严厉谴责
legalSystem.en: Law was based primarily on customary norms, elder arbitration, compensation, and religious taboos; theft, seizure of water, damage to terraces, and offenses against ancestors were strongly condemned

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，首领主要通过劳役、实物贡纳、畜产品、纺织品和节庆供献获取资源
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; chiefs drew resources mainly through labor dues, payments in kind, livestock products, textiles, and ceremonial offerings

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、山岳神灵、太阳与天气崇拜为核心，并重视圣地、墓葬与祭祀中心
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, mountain spirits, and reverence for the sun and weather, with strong importance attached to sacred places, tombs, and ceremonial centers

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成文哲学体系，但普遍强调共同体互助、人与山地环境的平衡、祖先延续与礼仪秩序
philosophy.en: No formal written philosophical system existed, but there was a widespread emphasis on communal reciprocity, balance with the mountain environment, ancestral continuity, and ritual order

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，记忆、口述传统、结绳式前文字记事和图像象征在不同地区可能并存
writingSystem.en: There was no mature writing system; memory, oral tradition, possible proto-recording methods akin to cord notation, and visual symbolism may have coexisted in different regions

culturalAchievements.zh: 在高海拔农业、梯田营建、驼畜饲养、纺织、石工、金属初步使用与区域祭祀建筑方面成就显著，为后起安第斯国家奠定基础
culturalAchievements.en: They achieved notable advances in high-altitude agriculture, terrace building, camelid husbandry, textiles, stonework, early metallurgy, and regional ceremonial architecture, laying the foundations for later Andean states

languageFamily.zh: 语言多样，可能包括早期克丘亚语前身、艾马拉相关语言及其他已失传的地方语言
languageFamily.en: Linguistically diverse, likely including ancestors of Quechuan speech, Aymaran-related languages, and other local languages now lost

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧为主，辅以区域交换；产出受海拔、气候和交通限制，但多生态带互补使整体较稳定
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based chiefly on subsistence farming and herding, supplemented by regional exchange; output was constrained by altitude, climate, and transport, but ecological complementarity made the overall system fairly stable

mainIndustries.zh: 马铃薯和块根作物种植、藜麦与玉米农业、羊驼和原驼牧养、纺织、陶器、石工、初步铜金属加工
mainIndustries.en: Potato and tuber cultivation, quinoa and maize farming, llama and alpaca herding, textile production, pottery, stonework, and early copper metallurgy

tradeGoods.zh: 羊驼毛、纺织品、干肉、玉米、藜麦、盐、黑曜石、贝壳、颜料、金铜饰物和药用植物
tradeGoods.en: Llama fiber, textiles, dried meat, maize, quinoa, salt, obsidian, shell goods, pigments, gold and copper ornaments, and medicinal plants

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、灌溉条件、畜群规模、储粮、纺织品和亲族网络，而非金属货币积累
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to land, irrigation, herd size, stored food, textiles, and kinship ties rather than accumulated coinage

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、亲族再分配、首领征纳和跨生态带互惠交换为主的混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on village production, kin-based redistribution, chiefly exactions, and reciprocal exchange across ecological zones

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金库，而是分散于各地首领和祭祀中心的储粮、纺织品、畜群和礼器储备
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized cash reserve but a dispersed stock of stored grain, textiles, herds, and ceremonial goods held by local chiefs and ritual centers

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持公共储备、抵御歉收和巩固首领威望为核心，偏向实物积累和劳役组织而非货币收支
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining communal reserves, buffering bad harvests, and reinforcing chiefly prestige, favoring accumulation in kind and labor organization over monetary accounting

description.zh: 由各酋邦征召的步战农民和牧民组成，使用投石器、矛、木棒和盾牌
description.en: Composed of levied farming and herding men from individual chiefdoms, using slings, spears, clubs, and shields

description.zh: 首领直属护卫和较有经验的战士，承担保卫仪式中心、牧群和山道的任务
description.en: Direct retainers of chiefs and more experienced fighters, tasked with protecting ceremonial centers, herds, and mountain routes

description.zh: 擅长崎岖地形作战，执行突袭、伏击和谷地防御
description.en: Specialized in rough terrain warfare, carrying out raids, ambushes, and valley defense

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各地酋长分别领兵
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; each regional chief led his own forces

technology.zh: 军事技术以石、木、骨和少量金属武器为主，擅用投石器、高地防御阵地和地形优势，尚无骑兵和成熟攻城体系
technology.en: Military technology relied mainly on stone, wood, bone, and limited metal weapons, with strong use of slings, upland defensive positions, and terrain advantages; there was no cavalry and no developed siege system

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近谷地与高原群体的争地、争水、争牧场冲突，以及区域间资源竞争；不存在跨大陆外敌威胁
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring valleys and highland groups competing over land, water, and pasture, along with regional resource rivalries; there was no transcontinental external threat

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于秘鲁与玻利维亚高地诸谷地、湖区和山间盆地，聚落多为村落和小型区域中心
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the valleys, lake zones, and intermontane basins of the Peruvian and Bolivian highlands, with most settlements being villages and small regional centers

name.en: Proto-highland centers near later Wari regions

name.zh: 的的喀喀湖盆地祭祀中心

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为首领与宗教精英、地方战士与管理者、普通农牧民、工匠及边缘附属群体，但阶层边界较后世国家社会更为流动
