taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自田赋、港口关税、市场税、林产与矿产收益，以及附庸贡纳。部分地区还需承担水利维护与道路劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from land tax, port customs, market dues, forest and mineral income, and tribute from subordinate areas. Some districts also owed labor for irrigation and road maintenance.

religion.zh: 耆那教
religion.en: Jainism

culturalAchievements.zh: 羯陵伽延续了岩刻铭文、洞窟建筑和宗教施舍传统，并在沿海贸易网络中促进了文化交流。其港市文化和工艺生产对东印度沿岸具有区域影响力。
culturalAchievements.en: Kalinga continued traditions of rock inscriptions, cave architecture, and religious patronage, while fostering cultural exchange through maritime trade. Its port culture and craft production influenced the eastern Indian littoral.

languageFamily.zh: 印度-雅利安语支为主，并与东部达罗毗荼及蒙达语族接触
languageFamily.en: Primarily Indo-Aryan, with contact with eastern Dravidian and Munda language groups

philosophy.zh: 耆那教在王室传统中占有重要地位，同时与婆罗门教、地方神祇崇拜并存，形成多元宗教文化。
philosophy.en: Jainism held an important place in royal tradition, while coexisting with Brahmanical practice and local cults in a plural religious culture.

writingSystem.zh: 以婆罗米字母书写的普拉克里特语为主，亦受早期梵语行政与宗教传统影响。
writingSystem.en: Prakrit written in Brahmi script predominated, with some influence from early Sanskrit religious and administrative usage.

gdpDescription.zh: 羯陵伽经济建立在稻作农业、沿海贸易、盐业、林产和手工业之上。作为东印度沿海的重要区域王国，其人均产出高于一般部落地区，但仍低于后期高度货币化的帝国核心地带。
gdpDescription.en: Kalinga’s economy rested on rice agriculture, maritime trade, salt, forest products, and handicrafts. As an important regional kingdom on the east Indian coast, its per-capita output exceeded that of tribal zones, though it remained below the most monetized imperial cores of later periods.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、港口贸易、盐业、纺织、象马贸易、木材与林产、金属手工业
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, port trade, salt production, textiles, elephant and horse trade, timber and forest products, metal crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、棉布、象、象牙、胡椒与香料转运品、木材、宝石、铁器
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, cotton cloth, elephants, ivory, pepper and transshipped spices, timber, gemstones, iron goods

name.zh: 打制银铜币与实物并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富在平原地区主要表现为土地、谷物、牲畜与金属器，在城市则更多体现为货币、库存和商业信用。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth in the plains was held mainly in land, grain, livestock, and metal tools, while in towns it was more often expressed in coin, inventory, and commercial credit.

economicSystem.zh: 属于农业税基与港市商业并重的早期王国经济，国家通过税收、关卡与行会关系调节资源流动。
economicSystem.en: It was an early kingdom economy balancing an agrarian tax base with port-city commerce, with the state regulating flows through taxation, customs points, and ties to guilds.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由金银、铜币、粮仓储备、战象与可征发物资共同构成，兼具金属财富与实物财政特征。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, copper coin, grain reserves, war elephants, and mobilizable goods, combining metallic wealth with an in-kind fiscal base.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持军力、港口税收和灌溉生产，倾向保留适度结余以应对歉收或战争。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military strength, port revenue, and irrigated production, while keeping a moderate surplus for famine or war.

description.zh: 由王家卫队、地方征募兵和城镇守军组成，是军队主体。
description.en: Formed by royal guards, locally levied troops, and town garrisons, making up the bulk of the army.

description.zh: 用于冲击敌阵、威慑边地部落与提升王权声望。
description.en: Used to break enemy lines, intimidate frontier tribes, and enhance royal prestige.

description.zh: 数量有限但适合侦察、追击和护卫商路。
description.en: Limited in number but useful for reconnaissance, pursuit, and protecting trade routes.

technology.zh: 装备以铁制兵器、弓矛、皮甲或布甲、战象与简易攻城器具为主，具备区域王国水平但不属超大型帝国军制。
technology.en: Equipment centered on iron weapons, bows and spears, hide or textile armor, war elephants, and simple siege tools, representing a capable regional kingdom but not a vast imperial military system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自德干与安得拉方向的竞争政权、内陆部族叛离以及对沿海贸易通道的争夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival powers in the Deccan and Andhra regions, defection among inland tribes, and competition over coastal trade routes.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 匈奴/帕提亚式复合弓，骑射核心武器
description.en: Xiongnu/Parthian composite bow, core mounted archery weapon

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中在沿海平原、河谷农区与港市，山区与林地则较为稀疏。整体上属于农业—商业混合型区域社会，城市化程度在东印度诸国中相对较高。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in coastal plains, river valleys, and ports, while hills and forests were more sparsely inhabited. Overall it was an agrarian-commercial regional society with relatively notable urbanization for eastern India.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、祭司与教团、商人行会、工匠、农民、部落民与奴仆性依附人口共同构成社会层次。
socialClasses.en: Royal and noble elites, priests and religious communities, merchant guilds, artisans, peasants, tribal peoples, and dependent servile populations formed the social hierarchy.

allies.zh: 与部分沿海商邦、地方附庸首领及耆那教网络保持友好关系。
allies.en: Maintained friendly relations with some coastal trading polities, subordinate local chiefs, and Jain networks.

enemies.zh: 与邻近德干强权、安得拉势力及争夺边地的山地部族存在潜在敌对。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included nearby Deccan powers, Andhra forces, and hill tribes contesting frontier zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策兼顾维持区域独立、掌控港口贸易与通过婚盟或贡纳稳定周边。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy sought to preserve regional independence, control port commerce, and stabilize neighbors through marriage alliances or tribute.

era.zh: 南亚早期历史时代铁器王国阶段
era.en: Early historic South Asian iron-age kingdom stage

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器农业、港口贸易组织、岩刻铭文、灌溉设施、象兵训练与行会化手工业。
keyInnovations.en: Iron-based agriculture, organized port trade, rock inscriptions, irrigation works, elephant training, and guild-based handicraft production.

strengths.zh: 沿海贸易发达、农业基础稳固、象兵资源突出，且具备区域文化与宗教号召力。
strengths.en: It benefited from strong coastal trade, a solid agrarian base, notable elephant resources, and regional cultural-religious prestige.

weaknesses.zh: 国力仍属中等规模，易受更大德干政权压迫，中央对边地和山区控制力有限。
weaknesses.en: Its power remained moderate in scale, making it vulnerable to larger Deccan states, and central control over hills and frontiers was limited.

outlook.zh: 若能持续控制港口与农税，仍可保持繁荣；但在大国竞争加剧时，王国独立性面临明显压力。
outlook.en: If it retained control over ports and agrarian revenue, it could remain prosperous; however, its independence faced clear pressure when larger powers intensified competition.

description.zh: 羯陵伽是东印度沿海一支以稻作、港贸和象兵闻名、受耆那教与多元地方传统影响的区域王国。
description.en: Kalinga was a regional east Indian coastal kingdom known for rice agriculture, maritime trade, and elephant forces, shaped by Jain and diverse local traditions.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝的潘帕斯诸部
dynasty.en: Pampas tribal groups without a unified dynasty

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族首领和长老协商为主的松散部落社会，没有统一国家机构。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal society guided by clan leaders and elder consultation, without a unified state apparatus.

socialStructure.zh: 潘帕斯地区群体多以亲族、季节性营地和小型部落为单位生活，依靠狩猎、采集与初步园艺。社会分层很弱，个人威望通常来自年长、狩猎成功、仪式知识与调解能力。不同群体之间通过婚姻、交换和偶发冲突维持动态关系。
socialStructure.en: Groups in the Pampas lived in kin-based units, seasonal camps, and small tribes, relying on hunting, gathering, and limited horticulture. Social stratification was weak, and personal prestige usually came from age, hunting success, ritual knowledge, and skill in mediation. Relations among groups were maintained through marriage, exchange, and occasional conflict.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在稳定的贵族阶层，首领多为战猎能力较强或拥有较多亲属支持的人物。其权威依赖说服与馈赠，而非制度化强制。
rulingClass.en: There was no stable aristocratic class; leaders were typically individuals with strong hunting-war ability or broad kin support. Their authority depended on persuasion and gift-giving rather than institutional coercion.

succession.zh: 领导权通常在有威望的家族内部延续，但需经群体认可。若继承者缺乏能力，部众可转而追随其他首领。
succession.en: Leadership often continued within prestigious families, but required community recognition. If an heir lacked ability, followers could shift their allegiance to another leader.

structure.zh: 潘帕斯诸文化没有固定都城，治理以营地议事、首领协调和氏族习惯为基础。重要决策如迁徙、狩猎地分配、婚姻联盟与冲突应对，通常由首领与长老共同商议。
structure.en: Pampas cultures had no fixed capital, and governance rested on camp deliberation, chief coordination, and clan custom. Major decisions such as migration, hunting-ground allocation, marriage alliances, and responses to conflict were usually discussed by chiefs and elders together.

function.zh: 协调部落内部纠纷、婚姻安排、迁徙路线与对外交换。
function.en: Coordinated internal disputes, marriage arrangements, migration routes, and external exchange.

function.zh: 组织集体狩猎、守卫营地并在冲突时集结战士。
function.en: Organized collective hunts, guarded camps, and assembled warriors in times of conflict.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理完全依赖各营地与氏族自管，没有超部落的常设行政单位。跨区域关系通过节庆集会、交换会与联姻维系。
localAdmin.en: Local governance depended entirely on self-management by camps and clans, with no permanent supra-tribal administrative unit. Wider regional ties were sustained through seasonal gatherings, exchange meetings, and marriage links.

legalSystem.zh: 法律是口传习惯法，强调赔偿、和解与群体平衡。严重冲突可通过决斗、交换补偿或分离居住解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was oral customary law, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and group balance. Serious disputes could be resolved by duels, exchange compensation, or separation of residence.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税收，公共事务依靠共享猎物、劳动合作和首领再分配。首领在宴饮和馈赠中巩固其影响力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communal affairs relied on shared game, cooperative labor, and redistribution by chiefs. Leaders reinforced their influence through feasting and gift exchange.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 潘帕斯诸群体发展了适应草原环境的狩猎技术、皮革加工和流动营地组织。其口头传统与仪式活动在维系跨群体认同和生态知识方面十分重要。
culturalAchievements.en: Pampas groups developed hunting techniques adapted to grassland environments, leatherworking, and mobile camp organization. Their oral traditions and ritual activities were crucial in maintaining intergroup identity and ecological knowledge.

languageFamily.zh: 南美洲南锥地区多样语言群，可能包括乔南及相关地方语系
languageFamily.en: Diverse Southern Cone language groups, possibly including Chonan and related local families

philosophy.zh: 信仰以泛灵观念、祖先记忆、自然灵力与萨满式仪式实践为核心。
philosophy.en: Belief centered on animism, ancestral memory, natural spiritual power, and shamanic ritual practice.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，历史、知识与地理记忆通过口述、歌谣和仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; history, knowledge, and geographic memory were preserved through oral tradition, song, and ritual.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎采集和低强度生产为主，市场交换和财富积累有限。虽总量不高，但通过季节迁徙和资源多样化维持了相对稳定的生计。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, gathering, and low-intensity production, with limited market exchange and wealth accumulation. Though modest in total scale, seasonal mobility and diversified resources sustained a relatively stable livelihood.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、皮革加工、石骨器制作、小规模园艺与地方交换
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, leatherworking, stone and bone toolmaking, small-scale horticulture, and local exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、羽毛、石器、骨器、颜料、干肉、贝饰
tradeGoods.en: Hides, feathers, stone tools, bone tools, pigments, dried meat, shell ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为工具、皮张储备、装饰品和亲族互助关系，而非贵金属或土地所有权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in tools, stored hides, ornaments, and kin support relations rather than precious metals or formal landholding.

economicSystem.zh: 属于以生计保障和互惠交换为核心的部落经济，生产目标主要是维持家庭和营地再生产。
economicSystem.en: It was a tribal economy centered on subsistence security and reciprocal exchange, with production aimed mainly at sustaining households and camps.

treasuryDescription.zh: 没有集中财政金库，公共储备主要是皮张、食物、工具和礼物物资，由各营地分散持有。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized fiscal treasury; communal reserves consisted mainly of hides, food, tools, and gift goods held across different camps.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践偏向临时动员和共享储备，在资源波动时优先保障群体生存与关系网络。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice favored temporary mobilization and shared reserves, prioritizing group survival and social networks during resource fluctuations.

description.zh: 以投矛、棍棒、石制或骨制武器进行突袭和近战。
description.en: Conducted raids and close combat using throwing spears, clubs, and stone or bone weapons.

description.zh: 负责守卫家属、物资和临时营地周边。
description.en: Guarded families, supplies, and the perimeter of temporary camps.

technology.zh: 军事装备较为原始，以木石骨器和皮革护具为主，缺乏冶金武器、骑兵和攻城能力。
technology.en: Military equipment was relatively simple, centered on wood, stone, bone weapons, and leather protection, lacking metal arms, cavalry, and siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落的资源争夺、猎场竞争和局部报复性冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes competing over resources, hunting grounds, and localized revenge conflict.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口散布于潘帕斯草原和邻近河谷，多为流动性聚落，少有长期大型定居点。环境承载力限制了人口规模，但草原资源支持了广泛季节迁徙。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across the Pampas grasslands and nearby river valleys in mobile settlements, with few long-term large villages. Environmental carrying capacity limited total numbers, but grassland resources supported broad seasonal movement.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层不固定，主要区分首领家族、普通自由成员、仪式人物与被收纳的外来者或依附者。
socialClasses.en: Social ranking was fluid, mainly distinguishing chief families, ordinary free members, ritual specialists, and incorporated outsiders or dependents.

allies.zh: 盟友通常是通婚部落、交换伙伴和共同狩猎的邻近群体。
allies.en: Allies were usually intermarrying tribes, exchange partners, and neighboring groups that hunted together.

enemies.zh: 没有长期固定敌国，但与争夺猎场和水源的邻部存在周期性敌对。
enemies.en: There were no permanent enemy states, but periodic hostility existed with nearby groups contesting hunting grounds and water.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以保持迁徙自由、交换通道与避免持续性大规模冲突为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on preserving freedom of movement, exchange routes, and avoiding sustained large-scale conflict.

era.zh: 南美南锥地区前陶或早期陶器部落阶段
era.en: Pre-ceramic to early ceramic tribal stage of the Southern Cone

keyInnovations.zh: 草原狩猎组织、皮革加工、季节性营地管理、石骨器组合与口头地理记忆体系。
keyInnovations.en: Grassland hunting organization, leatherworking, seasonal camp management, combined stone-bone toolkits, and orally preserved geographic memory.

strengths.zh: 机动性强、适应草原环境、内部合作灵活，能够在资源分散地区维持生计。
strengths.en: Highly mobile, well adapted to grassland conditions, and flexible in internal cooperation, allowing survival in dispersed-resource environments.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏集中组织、人口规模小、生产与军事实力有限，难以形成大型持久政体。
weaknesses.en: They lacked centralized organization, had small populations, and possessed limited productive and military power, making large durable polities difficult.

outlook.zh: 在没有外来强势国家介入的情况下可长期稳定存在，但难以显著提升复杂度与区域控制力。
outlook.en: In the absence of intrusive stronger states, they could persist stably for long periods, but were unlikely to greatly increase political complexity or regional control.

description.zh: 潘帕斯诸文化是南美草原上以狩猎采集和季节迁徙为基础、由首领和长老协商治理的部落社会。
description.en: The Pampas cultures were tribal societies of the South American grasslands based on hunting, gathering, and seasonal mobility under chief-and-elder consultation.

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族和地域群体为基础的分散部族治理，由长老凭威望、仪式知识与协商主持公共事务。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order based on kin groups and territorial bands, with elders guiding affairs through prestige, ritual knowledge, and consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会通常由地方性氏族、婚姻交换网络与仪式共同体构成，不同群体共享歌线、圣地与季节性活动区域。地位更多来自年龄、狩猎能力、仪式知识与调解纠纷的声望，而非固定官职。男女分工较明确，但在采集、仪式与家庭经济中都具有重要作用。
socialStructure.en: Society was generally organized into local clans, marriage-exchange networks, and ritual communities, with groups sharing songlines, sacred places, and seasonal foraging ranges. Status derived more from age, hunting skill, ritual knowledge, and reputation in mediation than from fixed offices. Gender roles were differentiated, but both men and women were essential to subsistence, ritual life, and household economy.

rulingClass.zh: 并无严格意义上的世袭贵族，核心领导层通常是资深长老、仪式主持者与卓有声望的猎手。其权威主要建立在传统知识、亲族关系与说服能力之上。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict hereditary nobility; leadership usually rested with senior elders, ritual specialists, and highly respected hunters. Their authority depended mainly on traditional knowledge, kin ties, and persuasive ability.

succession.zh: 领导权通常不以正式王位继承，而是在长者群体内部随资历、能力与仪式地位自然转移。若某位长老去世，其影响力常由同系亲族中的资深者或最有威望者承接。
succession.en: Leadership did not pass through a formal throne-like succession, but shifted within elder circles according to seniority, ability, and ritual standing. When an elder died, influence often passed to a senior relative or the most respected member of the relevant kin line.

structure.zh: 政治组织高度地方化，多数事务由营地或地区群体会议处理，重大决定依赖长老协商。跨部族关系主要通过婚姻纽带、仪式集会与习惯法协调，而非固定国家机构。
structure.en: Political organization was highly local, with most affairs handled by camp or regional group councils and major decisions made by elder consensus. Inter-group relations were coordinated through marriage ties, ritual gatherings, and customary law rather than permanent state institutions.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、资源使用、争端调解与对外联络。
function.en: Coordinated movement, resource use, dispute mediation, and external relations.

function.zh: 维护圣地、主持成年礼并传承梦时法则。
function.en: Protected sacred sites, oversaw initiations, and preserved Dreaming law.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以小型营地、氏族领地和季节性聚落为核心，没有常设官僚。各地事务由资深成员和亲族网络共同管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on small camps, clan territories, and seasonal settlements, without a permanent bureaucracy. Affairs were managed collectively by senior members and kin networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口传习俗、梦时神圣传统与赔偿性调解为基础。对婚姻、狩猎边界和禁忌的违反通常通过长老裁断、仪式惩戒或群体补偿处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on oral custom, sacred Dreaming traditions, and compensatory mediation. Violations involving marriage, hunting boundaries, or taboo were usually addressed through elder judgment, ritual sanction, or group compensation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共义务主要表现为共享食物、参与仪式、协助狩猎与履行亲族馈赠。资源再分配依赖社会规范而非强制征收。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public obligations took the form of food sharing, ritual participation, cooperative hunting, and kin-based gift exchange. Redistribution relied on social norms rather than compulsory levy.

religion.zh: 梦时代信仰
religion.en: Dreamtime

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成了极其复杂的歌线体系、圣地网络和生态知识传统，能在多样环境中长期维持可持续生计。岩画、仪式舞蹈和神话叙事构成高度发达的象征文化。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed highly complex songline systems, sacred-site networks, and ecological knowledge traditions, sustaining long-term subsistence across diverse environments. Rock art, ceremonial dance, and mythic narration formed a sophisticated symbolic culture.

languageFamily.zh: 澳大利亚原住民诸语族，以帕马-恩永甘语系为主
languageFamily.en: Australian Aboriginal language families, especially Pama-Nyungan

philosophy.zh: 梦时宇宙观与祖灵秩序
philosophy.en: Dreaming cosmology and ancestral order

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，以口述传统、岩画、身体彩绘与记忆地景传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, rock art, body painting, and mnemonic landscapes.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、捕鱼和季节性资源管理为主，货币化程度极低。产出虽然有限，但在地方生态系统内具有较强适应性与稳定性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, gathering, fishing, and seasonal resource management, with very low monetization. Output was modest, but it was highly adaptive and stable within local ecosystems.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、渔业、石器制作、木器与编织品
mainIndustries.en: Hunting-gathering, fishing, stone toolmaking, woodworking, and basketry

tradeGoods.zh: 石料、赭石、贝壳、木矛、仪式物品
tradeGoods.en: Stone materials, ochre, shells, wooden spears, and ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为工具、武器、装饰品、亲族支持网络及对资源地的使用权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in tools, weapons, ornaments, kin support networks, and rights of access to resource zones.

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族共享、互惠交换与季节性迁徙利用资源为核心的部族生计经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal subsistence economy centered on kin sharing, reciprocal exchange, and seasonal mobility.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中金库，公共储备主要表现为仪式物品、工具、食物共享义务与社会信用。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; public reserves existed mainly as ritual goods, tools, food-sharing obligations, and social credit.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作以临时征集、礼物再分配和危机时互助为主，强调群体平衡而非积累。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice relied on ad hoc contributions, gift redistribution, and mutual aid in times of stress, emphasizing communal balance rather than accumulation.

description.zh: 以矛、投矛器、木盾和棍棒作战，主要承担部族冲突与领地防卫。
description.en: Armed with spears, spear-throwers, wooden shields, and clubs, they handled tribal conflict and territorial defense.

description.zh: 熟悉地形与足迹辨识，用于预警、追踪和小规模袭扰。
description.en: Expert in terrain and tracks, they were used for warning, pursuit, and small-scale raiding.

technology.zh: 军事技术以木矛、石刃、投矛器、火管理和地形知识为核心，不具备金属武器与攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden spears, stone edges, spear-throwers, fire management, and terrain knowledge, without metal weapons or siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近群体间的资源竞争、婚姻纠纷和干旱时期的领地压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from resource competition with neighboring groups, marriage disputes, and territorial pressure during droughts.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于海岸、河谷、草原与沙漠边缘，多为小型流动群体。人口密度很低，但地方生态知识极为深厚。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across coasts, river valleys, grasslands, and desert margins, mostly in small mobile groups. Density was very low, but local ecological knowledge was exceptionally deep.

socialClasses.zh: 无严格阶级制度，主要区分为长老、成年成员、青年与仪式专职者。
socialClasses.en: There was no rigid class system; main distinctions were elders, adult members, youths, and ritual specialists.

allies.zh: 主要通过邻近氏族、婚姻结盟群体与共同仪式网络维持友好关系。
allies.en: Friendly relations were maintained mainly through neighboring clans, marriage-allied groups, and shared ritual networks.

enemies.zh: 无固定外敌，但与相邻群体偶有围绕水源、狩猎地和婚配问题的冲突。
enemies.en: There were no fixed external enemies, though disputes with adjacent groups occasionally arose over water, hunting grounds, and marriage arrangements.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持边界、交换稀有物资和避免长期报复循环为重点。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on maintaining boundaries, exchanging rare goods, and avoiding prolonged cycles of retaliation.

era.zh: 晚期石器时代区域传统
era.en: Regional Late Stone Age traditions

keyInnovations.zh: 投矛器、火生态管理、复杂歌线导航、岩画与石器微型化技术。
keyInnovations.en: Spear-throwers, fire-based landscape management, complex songline navigation, rock art, and microlithic stone technologies.

strengths.zh: 高度适应多样生态环境，社会韧性强，口传知识和追踪能力极为出色。
strengths.en: Highly adapted to diverse environments, socially resilient, and exceptionally skilled in oral knowledge and tracking.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀疏、组织分散、缺乏金属工具和集中资源动员能力。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, fragmented organization, and lack of metal tools or centralized resource mobilization.

outlook.zh: 在无大规模外来冲击的条件下，可继续保持稳定的区域性部族秩序，但难以形成统一国家结构。
outlook.en: Absent major outside disruption, they could maintain stable regional tribal orders, but were unlikely to form a unified state structure.

description.zh: 约公元100年的澳大利亚原住民社会是以梦时传统、亲族网络与季节性迁徙为核心的分散部族文明。
description.en: Around 100 CE, Australian Aboriginal society was a decentralized tribal civilization centered on Dreaming traditions, kin networks, and seasonal mobility.

governmentForm.zh: 以岛屿或沿海群体为单位的松散部族治理，由长老和有威望的猎渔首领共同决策。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal order organized by island or coastal groups, with elders and respected hunting-fishing leaders making decisions jointly.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由小型沿海聚落、亲族群与婚姻联系网络组成，生活重心围绕捕鱼、采集与有限园圃活动。权威通常依附于年龄、航海经验、资源分配能力和宗教声望。群体规模较小，社会分层较弱。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of small coastal settlements, kin groups, and marriage-linked networks, centered on fishing, foraging, and limited garden cultivation. Authority usually rested on age, seafaring experience, skill in resource distribution, and religious prestige. Group size was small and social stratification was weak.

rulingClass.zh: 没有真正的贵族阶层，领导层多为长老、资深航海者与仪式主持人。其影响力来自经验、家族联系和再分配能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no true noble class; leadership lay with elders, experienced navigators, and ritual leaders. Their influence came from experience, family ties, and redistributive ability.

succession.zh: 首领地位一般在相关家族或长老圈中协商继承，不存在严格成文的王位制度。若遭灾变或迁居，新的地方领袖可迅速产生。
succession.en: Chiefly roles were usually inherited or negotiated within relevant families or elder circles, without a formalized throne system. In times of disaster or relocation, new local leaders could emerge quickly.

structure.zh: 治理单位通常是岛屿上的若干聚落联盟，遇到渔场分配、迁徙或防卫问题时召开议事会。行政组织十分简化，主要依靠亲族关系和习惯法运行。
structure.en: The governing unit was usually a loose alliance of several settlements on an island, with councils convened for fishing allocation, movement, or defense. Administration was highly simple and operated mainly through kinship and customary law.

function.zh: 处理资源分配、婚姻协调、迁徙和对外关系。
function.en: Handled resource allocation, marriage coordination, movement, and external relations.

function.zh: 统筹独木舟出航、渔场使用与沿海预警。
function.en: Coordinated canoe expeditions, fishing-ground use, and coastal warning.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理依赖聚落长者与家庭首领，无常备官署。岛屿内部事务多通过公开协商和传统惯例解决。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on settlement elders and household heads, with no permanent offices. Internal island affairs were usually settled through open discussion and customary practice.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是口头习俗，涉及渔场、婚姻、复仇与财物共享。争端多由长老调解，严重冲突可能以迁居或报复性袭击收场。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of oral custom covering fishing grounds, marriage, revenge, and sharing of goods. Disputes were usually mediated by elders, while severe conflicts could end in relocation or retaliatory raids.

taxationSystem.zh: 无制度化税收，公共事务依靠食物贡献、集体劳动和航海活动中的份额分配。
taxationSystem.en: There was no institutionalized taxation; collective affairs depended on food contributions, communal labor, and shared expedition proceeds.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长独木舟航海、近海捕鱼和岛际交流，形成适应海岛环境的生活方式。其贝器、木器和仪式传统体现出稳定的地方文化连续性。
culturalAchievements.en: They were skilled in canoe navigation, coastal fishing, and inter-island exchange, developing lifeways well adapted to island environments. Their shellwork, woodworking, and ritual traditions showed stable local cultural continuity.

languageFamily.zh: 前阿拉瓦克或相关加勒比海早期土著语言群
languageFamily.en: Pre-Arawakan or related early indigenous Caribbean language groups

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖先崇敬
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestral reverence

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口述传统、纹饰、器物样式和仪式歌谣传承记忆。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; memory was transmitted through oral tradition, decorative motifs, artifact styles, and ritual songs.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础是捕鱼、贝类采集、狩猎和有限园圃种植，岛际交换规模较小。总产出不高，但海岸资源可在正常年份提供较稳定供给。
gdpDescription.en: The economic base was fishing, shellfish gathering, hunting, and limited gardening, with small-scale inter-island exchange. Total output was low, but coastal resources could provide relatively stable supplies in normal years.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、采集、狩猎、独木舟制作、贝器加工
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, foraging, hunting, canoe making, and shellcraft

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳、鱼干、盐、木材、石器原料、装饰品
tradeGoods.en: Shells, dried fish, salt, timber, stone raw materials, and ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为独木舟、渔具、贝饰、工具和与邻岛的交换关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly reflected in canoes, fishing gear, shell ornaments, tools, and exchange ties with nearby islands.

economicSystem.zh: 以沿海生计、互惠交换和小范围岛际贸易为基础的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on coastal subsistence, reciprocal exchange, and limited inter-island trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 没有正规国库，公共财富集中于独木舟、储备鱼干、贝饰与首领掌握的馈赠网络。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no formal treasury; public wealth was concentrated in canoes, stores of dried fish, shell ornaments, and gift networks controlled by leaders.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以共享与应急储备为导向，偏重维持航海能力和灾害后的群体恢复。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on sharing and emergency reserves, prioritizing seafaring capacity and group recovery after disasters.

description.zh: 使用木矛、棍棒和石制武器，负责海岸防卫与聚落冲突。
description.en: Armed with wooden spears, clubs, and stone weapons, they defended coasts and fought settlement conflicts.

description.zh: 以轻型独木舟进行短程岛际侦察、掠袭与快速撤离。
description.en: Using light canoes, they conducted short-range inter-island scouting, raiding, and rapid withdrawal.

technology.zh: 军事技术以独木舟、木矛、石刃、棍棒和海岸机动为主，缺少金属和防护装备。
technology.en: Military technology relied on canoes, wooden spears, stone blades, clubs, and coastal mobility, lacking metal and protective armor.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是飓风后资源短缺、岛际竞争以及局部报复性冲突。
threats.en: Main threats were post-hurricane scarcity, inter-island competition, and localized retaliatory violence.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于海岸、河口与渔场附近，聚落规模较小且分散。岛屿环境限制了人口增长，也促成高度依赖海洋资源的生活结构。
populationDescription.en: Population clustered near coasts, estuaries, and fishing grounds, in small dispersed settlements. Island ecology limited population growth and encouraged strong dependence on marine resources.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层有限，主要区分为长老、成年渔猎者、家庭成员和仪式人员。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was limited, with distinctions mainly among elders, adult fishers-hunters, household members, and ritual personnel.

allies.zh: 与邻近岛屿上的通婚群体和交换伙伴保持松散同盟。
allies.en: Loose alliances were maintained with marriage partners and exchange partners on nearby islands.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌人，但局部海域竞争和报复袭击可导致敌对关系。
enemies.en: There was no enduring common enemy, but competition over local waters and retaliatory raids could create hostility.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外重点在于维持航道安全、交换稀缺资源并避免长期岛际战争。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on keeping sea routes safe, obtaining scarce resources through exchange, and avoiding prolonged inter-island warfare.

era.zh: 加勒比早期海岛石器与独木舟传统
era.en: Early Caribbean island stone-tool and canoe traditions

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟航海、贝器加工、鱼叉与网具、海岸资源季节性管理。
keyInnovations.en: Canoe navigation, shellcraft, harpoons and nets, and seasonal management of coastal resources.

strengths.zh: 善于利用海洋环境，机动性强，群体关系灵活，适应岛屿生态。
strengths.en: They were adept in marine environments, mobile, socially flexible, and well adapted to island ecology.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、资源基础脆弱，易受飓风、旱季和岛际冲突影响。
weaknesses.en: Population was small and the resource base fragile, leaving them vulnerable to hurricanes, dry seasons, and inter-island conflict.

outlook.zh: 若气候平稳，可维持小规模稳定的海岛部族社会；但难以形成高集权政治体。
outlook.en: Under stable climatic conditions, they could sustain small but stable island tribal societies, though they were unlikely to form highly centralized polities.

description.zh: 约公元100年的加勒比采集者社会是依托海洋资源、独木舟航海与亲族协商而存在的小型海岛部族体系。
description.en: Around 100 CE, Caribbean hunter-gatherer society was a small island tribal system rooted in marine resources, canoe navigation, and kin-based consultation.

governmentForm.zh: 以森林、湖区和河流流域部族为核心的分散氏族治理，由氏族长老和战时首领共同领导。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized clan-based order centered on forest, lake, and riverine tribes, jointly led by clan elders and wartime chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族联合体和季节性聚落构成，生计依赖狩猎、捕鱼、采集及部分刀耕火种农业。地位通常取决于年龄、狩猎能力、萨满威望和对交换路线的掌握。社会分层存在但不尖锐。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of clans, extended family units, and seasonal settlements, with livelihoods based on hunting, fishing, foraging, and some slash-and-burn cultivation. Status usually depended on age, hunting ability, shamanic prestige, and control of exchange routes. Social differentiation existed but was not sharp.

rulingClass.zh: 领导层由氏族长老、战首和萨满组成，没有成熟的世袭贵族阶层。某些控制河道或皮毛交换的家族在地方上更具影响力。
rulingClass.en: Leadership consisted of clan elders, war leaders, and shamans, without a fully developed hereditary nobility. Some families controlling river routes or fur exchange held greater local influence.

succession.zh: 氏族领导多在同一家族或同一氏族内部由长老认可继承，也可因战功和声望而转移。战时首领往往是临时性职位。
succession.en: Clan leadership was often inherited within the same family or clan with elder approval, though it could shift through military merit and reputation. Wartime leadership was often temporary.

structure.zh: 治理依赖氏族会议与区域集会，平时事务由长老处理，遇到贸易、防卫或迁徙问题时召开更大规模协商。没有固定都城和常设官僚，但在贸易节点可能出现较稳定的领导中心。
structure.en: Governance relied on clan councils and regional assemblies: ordinary matters were handled by elders, while trade, defense, or migration issues prompted larger consultations. There was no fixed capital or permanent bureaucracy, though more stable leadership centers could emerge at trade nodes.

function.zh: 处理亲族关系、资源边界、婚姻安排和对外协商。
function.en: Handled kin relations, resource boundaries, marriage arrangements, and external negotiation.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、治疗并维护神圣传统。
function.en: Conducted rites, divination, healing, and preserved sacred tradition.

function.zh: 协调皮毛、蜡、鱼产与邻近民族的交换活动。
function.en: Coordinated exchange of furs, wax, fish products, and trade with neighboring peoples.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村落、冬季营地和河流节点为单位，由家族首领与长老共同管理。事务多通过集会和口头协议执行。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages, winter camps, and river nodes, managed jointly by family heads and elders. Affairs were enforced largely through assemblies and oral agreements.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以氏族习惯、血亲责任和赔偿原则为基础。关于狩猎地、婚姻、伤害和偷窃的争端通常通过长老裁决、赔偿或宣誓解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on clan custom, kin responsibility, and compensation principles. Disputes over hunting grounds, marriage, injury, and theft were generally settled by elder judgment, compensation, or oath.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税收，公共义务体现为皮毛、鱼产、劳役和节庆供献。贸易节点可能向首领提供固定份额作为保护与中介回报。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified taxation; public obligations took the form of furs, fish products, labor, and festival offerings. Trade nodes might provide fixed shares to leaders in return for protection and mediation.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出适应北方森林与湖沼环境的渔猎技术、木工和皮毛加工传统。其神话歌谣、萨满仪式和口头诗歌为后世芬兰-乌戈尔文化的重要基础。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed fishing-hunting techniques, woodworking, and fur-processing traditions suited to northern forests and lakes. Their mythic songs, shamanic rites, and oral poetry formed important foundations for later Finno-Ugric cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系芬兰-乌戈尔语族
languageFamily.en: Uralic, Finno-Ugric branch

philosophy.zh: 萨满式自然崇拜与祖灵信仰
philosophy.en: Shamanic nature veneration and ancestor belief

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，以口述史诗、咒歌、记忆谱系与器物纹样传承传统。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system; tradition was preserved through oral epics, incantation songs, memorized genealogies, and decorative motifs.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以渔猎、皮毛采集、蜂蜡、林产和小规模刀耕火种农业为主。通过河流与森林路径进行有限长途交换，使其比孤立采集社会略具外贸价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on fishing, hunting, fur gathering, wax, forest products, and small-scale slash-and-burn farming. Limited long-distance exchange via rivers and forest routes gave it somewhat greater external trade value than more isolated forager societies.

mainIndustries.zh: 皮毛采集、渔业、狩猎、木工、蜂蜡与小规模农业
mainIndustries.en: Fur gathering, fishing, hunting, woodworking, wax collection, and small-scale agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、蜂蜡、鱼干、琥珀、木材、兽皮制品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, beeswax, dried fish, amber, timber, and hide products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要包括皮毛储备、渔具、船只、金属进口品、牲畜少量存量及亲族支持。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth mainly consisted of fur stores, fishing gear, boats, imported metal goods, limited livestock, and kin support.

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族共享、季节性生产和皮毛交换为核心的森林边缘部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A forest-frontier tribal economy centered on clan sharing, seasonal production, and fur exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备多为皮毛、鱼干、蜂蜡、金属器和冬季粮料，而非统一现金库。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves consisted largely of furs, dried fish, beeswax, metal goods, and winter provisions rather than a cash treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持冬季生存、防卫准备和贸易节点控制，积累能力有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized winter survival, defensive readiness, and control of trade nodes, with limited capacity for accumulation.

description.zh: 装备长矛、弓箭、木盾和少量金属刃器，负责森林和河岸防卫。
description.en: Equipped with spears, bows, wooden shields, and some metal-edged weapons, they defended forests and riverbanks.

description.zh: 以小舟沿河巡逻，保护贸易路线并监视外来入侵。
description.en: Using small boats, they patrolled rivers, protected trade routes, and watched for intruders.

description.zh: 凭借追踪和森林生存技能执行侦察、伏击和向导任务。
description.en: Using tracking and forest survival skills, they carried out reconnaissance, ambush, and guiding duties.

technology.zh: 军事技术以弓箭、长矛、木舟机动和森林伏击战术为主，部分地区可能接触到少量铁制品。
technology.en: Military technology focused on bows, spears, boat mobility, and forest ambush tactics, with some regions possibly accessing limited iron goods.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近草原或森林民族的袭扰、贸易路线争夺以及严酷气候造成的生存压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from raids by neighboring steppe or forest peoples, competition over trade routes, and survival pressures from harsh climate.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于森林、湖区和河流沿线，聚落小而分散，季节性流动明显。寒冷气候与较低农业生产率限制了人口密度。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in forests, lake districts, and river corridors, in small dispersed settlements with clear seasonal mobility. Cold climate and low agricultural productivity limited population density.

socialClasses.zh: 主要分为氏族长老、萨满、自由家庭成员与依附性较强的边缘成员，没有成熟贵族和平民二元结构。
socialClasses.en: Society was mainly divided among clan elders, shamans, free household members, and more dependent peripheral members, without a mature noble-commoner binary.

allies.zh: 通过婚姻、贸易和共同防卫与邻近芬兰-乌戈尔群体及部分波罗的海周边群体保持合作。
allies.en: Through marriage, trade, and mutual defense, they maintained cooperation with nearby Finno-Ugric groups and some Baltic-area peoples.

enemies.zh: 无统一长期宿敌，但与相邻森林民族、草原边缘势力或争夺河道者常有摩擦。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent enemy, but friction was common with neighboring forest peoples, steppe-edge groups, or rivals for river routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在保障河道交换、维持边界缓冲并避免卷入大型草原冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing river trade, preserving buffer zones, and avoiding entanglement in major steppe conflicts.

era.zh: 北方森林铁器接触期部族文化
era.en: Northern forest tribal culture in an early iron-contact phase

keyInnovations.zh: 弓箭狩猎技术、独木舟与小舟河运、皮毛加工、刀耕火种农业及萨满医治传统。
keyInnovations.en: Bow-hunting techniques, canoe and small-boat river transport, fur processing, slash-and-burn agriculture, and shamanic healing traditions.

strengths.zh: 熟悉森林与湖区环境，皮毛贸易具有价值，机动性和冬季适应能力较强。
strengths.en: They knew forest and lake environments intimately, possessed valuable fur trade potential, and had strong mobility and winter adaptation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、政治分散、农业基础薄弱，难以持续动员大规模兵力。
weaknesses.en: Population was sparse, politics fragmented, and the agricultural base weak, making sustained large-scale mobilization difficult.

outlook.zh: 若贸易路线继续发展，地方首领权力可逐步增强；但在公元100年前后仍以松散部族联合为主。
outlook.en: If trade routes continued to develop, local chiefs could gradually gain power, but around 100 CE the region still remained primarily a loose tribal mosaic.

description.zh: 约公元100年的芬兰-乌戈尔民族是分布于北方森林湖区、依靠渔猎与皮毛交换维生的氏族部族社会。
description.en: Around 100 CE, the Finno-Ugric peoples were clan-based tribal societies spread across northern forests and lake regions, sustained by fishing, hunting, and fur exchange.

dynasty.zh: 北欧铁器时代部族传统

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族会议、宗教祭祀和战时首领制为核心的分散部落联盟。各部落独立治理，在共同威胁或贸易利益面前形成临时联合。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal confederation centered on clan assemblies, religious ceremonies, and wartime chieftainship. Each tribe governed independently, forming temporary alliances against shared threats or for trade interests.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为自由农民、半自由人（thralls的前身）和奴隶。自由人参加部落议会（Thing的前身），贵族地位取决于土地持有、战功和与祭祀传统的联系。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into free farmers, semi-free dependents (proto-thralls), and slaves. Free men attended tribal assemblies (proto-Thing), with noble status determined by landholding, military prowess, and ties to ritual tradition.

rulingClass.zh: 贵族由大农场主、战士首领和祭司组成。其权力来自对土地和牲畜的控制、血统声望和宗教仪式中的角色。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of large landholders, warrior chieftains, and priests. Their power derived from control of land and livestock, lineage prestige, and roles in religious ceremonies.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在特定家族内部传承，但需要部落议会的认可。选举因素与血统因素并存。
succession.en: Chieftainship typically passed within certain families but required assembly approval. Elective and hereditary elements coexisted.

structure.zh: 治理以地方部落议会为中心，首领由长老和自由人推选。无常设官僚，宗教仪式和习惯法维持社会秩序。区域性大会可在危机时协调多个部落行动。
structure.en: Governance centered on local tribal assemblies where chieftains were chosen by elders and freemen. Without a standing bureaucracy, religious ritual and customary law maintained social order. Regional gatherings could coordinate multiple tribes in times of crisis.

function.zh: 裁决纠纷、决定战和、分配资源和认可首领。
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, decided matters of war and peace, allocated resources, and approved chieftains.

function.zh: 主持季节性祭祀、占卜和维护神圣法律传统。
function.en: Conducted seasonal sacrifices, divination, and maintained sacred legal traditions.

function.zh: 组织防御和远征队伍，训练年轻战士。
function.en: Organized defense and raiding parties, trained young warriors.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理依靠家族农庄和村落长老，无成文法规。事务通过口头协议和习惯法解决。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on family farmsteads and village elders without written statutes. Affairs were settled through oral agreements and customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法以血亲责任、赔偿金（wergild前身）和宣誓为核心。严重犯罪可导致放逐。
legalSystem.en: Customary law centered on kin responsibility, compensation payments (proto-wergild), and oaths. Serious offenses could result in outlawry.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收。公共义务以劳役、牲畜贡献和节庆供奉形式存在。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation. Public obligations took the form of labor service, livestock contributions, and festival offerings.

religion.zh: 北欧原始多神信仰
religion.en: Proto-Norse polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展了精湛的铁器锻造、木制船只建造、独特的动物纹样装饰艺术和如尼文字的早期形式。口头史诗传统为后世北欧萨迦文学奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed skilled ironworking, wooden shipbuilding, distinctive animal-style decorative art, and early forms of runic script. Oral epic traditions laid foundations for later Norse saga literature.

languageFamily.zh: 日耳曼语族北日耳曼语支（原始北欧语）
languageFamily.en: Germanic, North Germanic branch (Proto-Norse)

philosophy.zh: 多神崇拜与命运观念
philosophy.en: Polytheistic worship and fatalistic worldview

writingSystem.zh: 如尼文字的早期形式（长枝如尼文前身），主要用于铭文和仪式目的，日常事务依赖口头传统。
writingSystem.en: Early runic script (proto-Elder Futhark), mainly used for inscriptions and ritual purposes; daily affairs relied on oral tradition.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧、渔业、小规模耕作和铁器加工为主。通过波罗的海和北海路线进行琥珀、皮毛和铁器的远程贸易。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on pastoralism, fishing, small-scale agriculture, and ironworking. Long-distance trade in amber, furs, and iron goods was conducted via Baltic and North Sea routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、渔业、铁器锻造、造船、耕作
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, fishing, ironworking, shipbuilding, agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 琥珀、皮毛、铁器、鱼干、蜂蜡、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Amber, furs, iron goods, dried fish, beeswax, slaves

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现为牲畜、土地、铁器工具、船只和珠宝饰品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was reflected in livestock, land, iron tools, boats, and jewelry.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭农庄为基础、辅以区域贸易网络的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on family farmsteads supplemented by regional trade networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备为牲畜、铁器、皮毛和粮食，分散于各首领农庄。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves consisted of livestock, iron goods, furs, and grain, dispersed among chieftains's farmsteads.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策侧重冬季生存和防卫准备，积累能力有限但比纯采集社会更强。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized winter survival and defensive readiness, with greater accumulation capacity than pure forager societies.

description.zh: 装备铁矛、木盾、短剑和弓箭的自由人战士，是军事力量的核心。
description.en: Free men warriors equipped with iron spears, wooden shields, short swords, and bows, forming the military core.

description.zh: 驾驭早期桨船进行沿海突袭和贸易护卫。
description.en: Operating early oared vessels for coastal raids and trade escort.

description.zh: 骑马或步行的侦察力量，负责预警和信息传递。
description.en: Mounted or foot scouts responsible for early warning and message relay.

technology.zh: 铁制武器为主流，包括矛、剑、斧和弓箭。木制圆盾广泛使用，部分精英战士拥有锁子甲。
technology.en: Iron weapons were standard, including spears, swords, axes, and bows. Wooden round shields were common; some elite warriors possessed mail armor.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自日耳曼南部部族的扩张压力、罗马帝国北方边境的军事存在以及部族间的领地争夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from expansion pressure of southern Germanic tribes, Roman military presence on the northern frontier, and inter-tribal territorial competition.

doctrine.zh: 以伏击、突袭和近距离肉搏为主的部族战术。
doctrine.en: Tribal tactics emphasizing ambush, raids, and close-quarters combat.

description.zh: 战士从少年时期开始训练，技能通过氏族传承
description.en: Warriors trained from youth, skills passed through clan tradition

description.zh: 士气坚定，受荣誉文化和宗教信仰驱动
description.en: Firm morale driven by honor culture and religious belief

description.zh: 最常见的近战武器
description.en: Most common melee weapon

description.zh: 以木材和铁箍制成的圆形盾牌
description.en: Round shield made of wood with iron boss

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于斯堪的纳维亚半岛南部和丹麦地区的农庄聚落，以沿海和河谷地带最为集中。北部山区人口稀疏。
populationDescription.en: Population was distributed across farmstead settlements in southern Scandinavia and Denmark, most concentrated in coastal and river valley areas. Northern mountain regions were sparsely populated.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分为部族贵族（大农场主和战士首领）、自由农民、半自由佃户和奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Society was divided into tribal nobles (large landholders and war leaders), free farmers, semi-free tenants, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与邻近日耳曼部族和波罗的海贸易伙伴保持松散联系。与芬兰-乌戈尔人在皮毛贸易中有互利关系。
allies.en: Maintained loose ties with neighboring Germanic tribes and Baltic trade partners. Had mutually beneficial fur trade relations with Finno-Ugric peoples.

enemies.zh: 与南方日耳曼扩张势力及争夺贸易路线的竞争部族存在冲突。罗马帝国是远方的潜在威胁。
enemies.en: Conflicts existed with expanding southern Germanic groups and competing tribes over trade routes. The Roman Empire was a distant potential threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护贸易路线安全和领地完整为核心，通过联姻和季节性集会维持部族间和平。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining trade route security and territorial integrity, using intermarriage and seasonal assemblies to preserve inter-tribal peace.

era.zh: 北欧前罗马铁器时代晚期
era.en: Late Pre-Roman Iron Age in Scandinavia

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器锻造、桨船建造、如尼铭文、动物纹样金属艺术和适应北方气候的农牧技术。
keyInnovations.en: Ironworking, oared ship construction, runic inscriptions, animal-style metalwork, and agricultural-pastoral techniques adapted to northern climate.

strengths.zh: 熟悉海岸和森林环境，铁器技术较为先进，航海能力初步发展，贸易网络连接波罗的海区域。
strengths.en: Intimate knowledge of coastal and forest environments, relatively advanced ironworking, emerging seafaring capability, and trade networks connecting the Baltic region.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、人口稀少、缺乏统一行政和文字记录，难以形成持续的大规模军事行动。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, sparse population, lack of unified administration and written records, making sustained large-scale military campaigns difficult.

outlook.zh: 北欧日耳曼诸部在公元100年处于铁器文化成熟期，贸易和航海能力稳步增长。数百年后，这些传统将为维京时代的崛起奠定基础。
outlook.en: Nordic Germanic tribes around 100 CE were in a mature iron-culture phase, with trade and seafaring capabilities steadily growing. Centuries later, these traditions would lay the foundation for the Viking Age.

description.zh: 公元100年的北欧日耳曼诸部是分布于斯堪的纳维亚半岛和丹麦的铁器时代部族社会，以畜牧、渔业、铁器锻造和波罗的海贸易为生。
description.en: Around 100 CE, the Nordic Germanic tribes were Iron Age tribal societies across Scandinavia and Denmark, sustained by pastoralism, fishing, ironworking, and Baltic trade.

governmentForm.zh: 由长老会议与战时首领共同维系的松散部落治理体制。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal system maintained by councils of elders and temporary war leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、年龄组与狩猎采集营地为基本单位，人口分散在季节性迁徙的聚落中。威望主要来自狩猎能力、祭仪知识与调解纠纷的经验，贫富分化较小。俘获品、牲畜与婚姻交换构成社会关系的重要纽带。
socialStructure.en: Society is organized around kin groups, age grades, and seasonal foraging camps. Prestige comes mainly from hunting skill, ritual knowledge, and experience in mediating disputes, with only limited wealth differentiation. Captured goods, livestock, and marriage exchange form important social ties.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定的世袭贵族，部族长老、著名猎手与萨满构成事实上的领导层。其权力依赖个人威望与追随者支持，而非成文法令。
rulingClass.en: There is no fixed hereditary nobility; tribal elders, renowned hunters, and shamans form the de facto leadership. Their authority depends on personal prestige and follower support rather than written law.

succession.zh: 首领通常由长老会在若干显赫家系中推举，重视年长、能力与母族或姻亲关系。若首领失去声望，部众可转而追随其他领袖。
succession.en: Chiefs are usually selected by the council of elders from among prominent lineages, with age, ability, and maternal or affinal ties valued. If a chief loses prestige, followers may shift their loyalty to another leader.

structure.zh: 治理层级较浅，通常由营地长老会处理日常事务，跨营地问题则由部族集会协商。战争、迁徙与大型祭仪时会出现临时统一指挥，但平时权力分散。
structure.en: Government is shallow in hierarchy: camp elder councils handle daily matters, while inter-camp issues are settled in tribal assemblies. Temporary unified command appears during war, migration, or major rituals, but authority is usually decentralized.

function.zh: 调解纠纷、安排迁徙地与婚姻交换、确认部族共识。
function.en: Mediates disputes, arranges migration grounds and marriage exchange, and confirms tribal consensus.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、分配狩猎队伍、协调防卫与战时动员。
function.en: Conducts rituals, allocates hunting parties, and coordinates defense and wartime mobilization.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理以营地和氏族为单位，由资深长者负责协调。不存在固定官署，决策多在集会、祭仪或分配猎获时形成。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operates at the level of camps and clans, coordinated by senior elders. There are no permanent offices; decisions are typically formed during gatherings, rituals, or the division of game.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和祖先传统为核心，强调赔偿、和解与公开仲裁。严重冲突可能导致逐出营地或血亲复仇。
legalSystem.en: Law is based on customary practice and ancestral tradition, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and public arbitration. Serious conflicts may lead to expulsion from camp or kin-based revenge.

taxationSystem.zh: 无固定税制，首领主要依靠贡献猎获、牲畜、蜂蜜、皮毛与战利品维持威望。战时或祭祀时会集中征集食物与劳役。
taxationSystem.en: There is no fixed tax system; chiefs rely mainly on contributions of game, livestock, honey, hides, and booty to maintain prestige. Food and labor are gathered collectively in wartime or for ritual occasions.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长复杂生态知识、追踪术、草药治疗和节律鲜明的歌舞传统。岩画、身体装饰与仪式性器物体现出高度象征文化。
culturalAchievements.en: They excel in ecological knowledge, tracking, herbal healing, and strongly rhythmic song-dance traditions. Rock art, body ornamentation, and ritual objects reflect a highly symbolic culture.

languageFamily.zh: 多种地方语言并存，可能属尼日尔-刚果语系、尼罗-撒哈拉语系或科伊桑语系支系
languageFamily.en: Multiple local languages coexist, likely belonging to branches of the Niger-Congo, Nilo-Saharan, or Khoisan families

philosophy.zh: 泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜与口述传统主导世界观，强调人与草原、动物和祖先之间的互惠关系。
philosophy.en: Animism, ancestor reverence, and oral tradition shape the worldview, emphasizing reciprocal relations between people, the savanna, animals, and ancestral spirits.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，知识依靠口述、歌谣、记忆地标与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge is transmitted through oral recitation, song, remembered landmarks, and ritual.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、少量放牧和区域性交换为主，市场化程度很低。总产出不大，但在当地生态条件下具有较强的自给韧性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy is based on hunting, gathering, limited herding, and regional exchange, with very low monetization. Total output is modest, but it is resilient and largely self-sufficient within local ecological conditions.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、蜂蜜采集、皮革加工、少量畜牧
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, honey collection, leather working, and limited pastoralism

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、象牙、小型牲畜、蜂蜜、羽毛、药草、石料工具
tradeGoods.en: Hides, ivory, small livestock, honey, feathers, medicinal herbs, and stone tools

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为牲畜、工具、饰物、婚姻关系与可进入的水草资源，而非金属货币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth is expressed mainly in livestock, tools, ornaments, marriage ties, and access to water and grazing resources rather than accumulated coin.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户自给、互惠交换和酋长再分配为核心的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household subsistence, reciprocal exchange, and chiefly redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财富储备多以牲畜、皮毛、干肉、象牙与可用于交换的礼物形式存在，少有集中金属库存。
treasuryDescription.en: Stored wealth exists mainly as livestock, hides, dried meat, ivory, and exchangeable gifts, with little concentrated metal stock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持首领威望和部落团结为先，倾向保留可移动储备以应对旱灾、迁徙与冲突。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice prioritizes chiefly prestige and tribal cohesion, favoring portable reserves to meet drought, migration, and conflict.

description.zh: 以投枪、弓箭、木盾和短矛作战，兼具护卫狩猎与部落防卫功能。
description.en: Armed with throwing spears, bows, wooden shields, and short spears, they serve in both hunting protection and tribal defense.

description.zh: 由经验丰富的猎手和近亲组成，担任突袭、护卫和危机时的核心力量。
description.en: Composed of experienced hunters and close kin, they provide raiding capability, bodyguard service, and a crisis core force.

technology.zh: 军事技术较为原始，以木矛、石骨器、少量铁器和地形熟悉为优势，缺乏攻城与重装能力。
technology.en: Military technology is relatively simple, relying on wooden spears, stone and bone tools, some iron weapons, and terrain familiarity, while lacking siege and heavy equipment.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落争夺水源、牲畜和猎场，以及旱灾导致的人群迁徙冲突。
threats.en: Main threats come from neighboring tribes competing for water, livestock, and hunting grounds, as well as population movements caused by drought.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于热带草原与疏林带，季节性移动明显。寿命受疾病、饥荒与环境波动影响较大，但社区内部凝聚力较强。
populationDescription.en: The population is thinly distributed across savanna and open woodland zones, with marked seasonal mobility. Life expectancy is heavily affected by disease, famine, and environmental fluctuation, but community cohesion is strong.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与祭司、著名猎手、普通家户、附属与俘获者
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual specialists, renowned hunters, ordinary households, dependents and captives

allies.zh: 与邻近通婚部落和交换伙伴维持松散同盟。
allies.en: Loose alliances are maintained with neighboring intermarrying tribes and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺水源、放牧地和象牙贸易路线的邻近部族。
enemies.en: Enemies are mainly neighboring tribes competing over water, pasture, and ivory routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持通婚网络、避免长期战争和确保季节性资源通道为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focuses on preserving marriage networks, avoiding prolonged war, and securing seasonal resource corridors.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期至地方化铁器阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age to localized iron-using stage

keyInnovations.zh: 熟练追踪术、火烧草场管理、植物药利用、皮革与骨角器制作
keyInnovations.en: Advanced tracking, controlled burning of grassland, use of plant medicines, and leather and bone-horn craftsmanship

strengths.zh: 熟悉环境、机动性高、社会弹性强，能在资源波动中迅速调整生计。
strengths.en: Strong environmental knowledge, high mobility, and social flexibility allow rapid adaptation to resource fluctuations.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与物资集中度低，缺乏稳固财政、攻城能力和长期统一指挥。
weaknesses.en: Low concentration of population and resources limits fiscal capacity, siege ability, and sustained unified command.

outlook.zh: 若气候稳定且交换网络延续，可维持长期存续；若遭遇持续旱灾或外来扩张，部落结构将承压。
outlook.en: If climate remains stable and exchange networks continue, the society can endure for long periods; prolonged drought or outside expansion would place heavy strain on tribal structures.

description.zh: 一个依靠狩猎采集、放牧与口述传统维系的热带草原部落联盟。
description.en: A savanna tribal confederation sustained by hunting, gathering, herding, and oral tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 由地方酋长、家长与祭祀权威构成的分散式部落酋邦体制。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal chieftain system composed of local chiefs, household heads, and ritual authorities.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村落、家户与血缘关系为核心，聚落多沿河谷、海岸与山麓分布。男性狩猎、捕鱼和贸易活动较多，女性在纺织、食物加工和家庭祭祀中地位重要。社会分层有限，但首领家系和富裕交易者享有更高声望。
socialStructure.en: Society is centered on villages, households, and kinship, with settlements concentrated along river valleys, coasts, and foothills. Men are prominent in hunting, fishing, and trade, while women hold important roles in weaving, food processing, and household ritual. Stratification is limited, though chiefly lineages and prosperous traders enjoy greater prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要是地方酋长、长老和掌握对外交换资源的家系。其权威来源于分配能力、祭祀威望和与邻近群体的关系。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consists mainly of local chiefs, elders, and lineages controlling external exchange resources. Their authority derives from distributive capacity, ritual prestige, and ties with neighboring peoples.

succession.zh: 首领职位通常在显赫家族内部继承，但须获得长老与村社认可。若继承人能力不足，其他支系领袖可能取而代之。
succession.en: Chiefly positions usually pass within prominent families but require acceptance by elders and the village community. If an heir proves inadequate, another lineage leader may replace him.

structure.zh: 政体以分散村落为基础，缺乏统一的中央官僚机构。地区性事务通过酋长会盟、馈赠交换与婚姻网络协调，战时则形成临时联合。
structure.en: The polity is based on dispersed villages and lacks a unified central bureaucracy. Regional matters are coordinated through meetings of chiefs, gift exchange, and marriage networks, while temporary coalitions emerge in wartime.

function.zh: 协调村落关系、调停纠纷、安排对外贸易与共同狩猎。
function.en: Coordinates village relations, mediates disputes, and arranges external trade and collective hunting.

function.zh: 主持熊祭等重要仪式，管理冬季储粮、渔获与馈赠品分配。
function.en: Oversees major rituals such as bear ceremonies and manages winter stores, fish catches, and the distribution of gifts.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村落为核心，由酋长与家长共同处理生产、分配与祭祀。地区间没有严格层级，更多依赖习俗与协商。
localAdmin.en: Local administration is village-centered, with chiefs and household heads jointly handling production, distribution, and ritual. There is no strict regional hierarchy; custom and negotiation dominate.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，重视血亲责任、赔偿与公开调解。盗窃、伤害与婚姻纠纷通常通过长老仲裁和礼物赔补解决。
legalSystem.en: Law is mainly customary, emphasizing kin responsibility, compensation, and public mediation. Theft, injury, and marital disputes are usually resolved by elder arbitration and compensatory gifts.

taxationSystem.zh: 无常设税收，多通过向酋长贡献鱼干、毛皮、海产和劳役来维持公共事务。大型仪式和对外贸易活动也会形成集中征集。
taxationSystem.en: There is no permanent taxation; public affairs are supported through contributions of dried fish, furs, marine goods, and labor to chiefs. Major rituals and trade expeditions also involve concentrated collection.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在木雕、织物纹样、熊祭仪礼和口头叙事传统方面独具特色。对寒冷海洋与森林环境的适应知识极为发达。
culturalAchievements.en: They are distinctive in wood carving, textile patterns, bear ritual practice, and oral narrative traditions. Their knowledge for adapting to cold maritime and forest environments is highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 阿伊努语系（通常视为孤立语言）
languageFamily.en: Ainu language family (commonly treated as a language isolate)

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰为核心，认为动物、火、河川与工具皆有灵，人与自然之间应保持礼仪性的交换关系。
philosophy.en: At the core is an animistic outlook in which animals, fire, rivers, and tools all possess spirit, and humans must maintain a ritual exchange relationship with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，文化主要通过口述史诗、歌谣、纹样和祭祀仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: There is no indigenous script; culture is transmitted mainly through oral epics, songs, decorative patterns, and ritual ceremonies.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础为渔猎、采集、少量农业和区域贸易，尤其依赖鲑鱼、海兽和毛皮资源。虽然缺乏大规模城市市场，但沿海与岛间交换提升了其人均产出。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rests on fishing, hunting, gathering, limited cultivation, and regional trade, especially in salmon, sea mammals, and furs. While lacking large urban markets, coastal and inter-island exchange raises per-capita output.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、狩猎、毛皮加工、木器制作、纺织、少量粟作与园圃
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, fur processing, woodcraft, weaving, and limited millet cultivation and gardening

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、鱼干、海兽制品、鹰羽、木器、海藻、琥珀样饰物
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried fish, sea-mammal products, eagle feathers, wooden goods, seaweed, and amber-like ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为渔猎工具、冬储食物、毛皮、木屋质量和对贸易网络的控制。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth is expressed chiefly in fishing and hunting gear, winter food stores, furs, house quality, and control of trade networks.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、季节性资源采集和酋长主导交换为特征的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy characterized by household production, seasonal resource harvesting, and chief-led exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要是毛皮、鱼干、海兽油、铁器和贵重木器，并非集中铸币库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves consist mainly of furs, dried fish, marine oil, iron tools, and prized wooden goods rather than a concentrated coin treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在于平衡季节性风险，通过储备食物和维持馈赠网络保障酋长权威。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focuses on balancing seasonal risk, using food storage and gift networks to sustain chiefly authority.

description.zh: 以弓箭、矛和短刀作战，适合林地伏击与沿河防卫。
description.en: Armed with bows, spears, and short blades, they are suited to woodland ambush and river defense.

description.zh: 由首领家系和精锐猎手组成，负责护卫、报复性袭击和维持部落秩序。
description.en: Drawn from chiefly lineages and elite hunters, they serve as guards, raiders, and enforcers of tribal order.

technology.zh: 军事装备以木弓、骨角器、铁制短兵器和皮甲为主，擅长寒地生存与小规模冲突，不具备正规攻城能力。
technology.en: Military equipment is based on wooden bows, bone-horn gear, iron short weapons, and leather armor; they excel in cold-environment survival and small-scale conflict but lack formal siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近岛屿或北方部族争夺渔场、狩猎地和贸易通道，以及与和人文化圈边缘接触带来的压力。
threats.en: Main threats come from neighboring island or northern tribes contesting fishing grounds, hunting territories, and trade corridors, as well as pressure from contacts on the margins of the Wa cultural sphere.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于北海道、库页岛南部及邻近岛屿海岸地带，村落规模普遍较小。聚落分布受鲑鱼洄游、海产资源和冬季环境制约。
populationDescription.en: The population is concentrated mainly in Hokkaido, southern Sakhalin, and nearby island coasts, with generally small village sizes. Settlement patterns are shaped by salmon runs, marine resources, and winter conditions.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家系、祭祀者与富裕交易户、普通渔猎家户、依附者
socialClasses.en: Chiefly lineages, ritual specialists and prosperous trading households, ordinary fishing-hunting households, dependents

allies.zh: 与邻近阿伊努诸村及部分北方海上群体维持互市与通婚关系。
allies.en: They maintain trade and marriage ties with neighboring Ainu communities and some northern maritime groups.

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要是争夺资源的地方部族，偶有与南方边缘农耕群体发生冲突。
enemies.en: Enemies are mainly local tribes competing for resources, with occasional clashes against southern fringe agricultural groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外以控制毛皮和海产交换、维持村落自治和避免长期大规模战争为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centers on controlling fur and marine exchange, preserving village autonomy, and avoiding prolonged large-scale war.

era.zh: 后续绳文—擦文文化过渡时期的北方铁器接触阶段
era.en: Northern iron-contact stage in the post-Jomon to Satsumon transition

keyInnovations.zh: 寒地渔猎技术、独木舟与沿海航行、鱼皮与纤维加工、木雕与仪式器物制作
keyInnovations.en: Cold-region fishing and hunting techniques, dugout canoes and coastal navigation, fish-skin and fiber processing, and wood-carved ritual objects

strengths.zh: 渔猎资源丰富、地方自治灵活、寒地适应能力强，并具备有价值的毛皮贸易优势。
strengths.en: Rich fishing and hunting resources, flexible local autonomy, strong cold-climate adaptation, and valuable fur-trade advantages.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、人口分散、农业基础薄弱，面对更大规模国家势力时较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: The absence of a unified state, dispersed population, and weak agricultural base leave them vulnerable to larger organized polities.

outlook.zh: 短期内可凭借生态专长与海陆交换持续稳定存在，但若南方国家扩张加剧，其独立空间将缩小。
outlook.en: In the short term, ecological specialization and maritime-land exchange support stability, but stronger southern state expansion would narrow their autonomy.

description.zh: 一个以渔猎、毛皮贸易和泛灵祭仪为核心的北方阿伊努部落文化圈。
description.en: A northern Ainu cultural sphere centered on fishing, fur trade, and animistic ritual.

dynasty.zh: 北方塞迦—印度-塞种王系

governmentForm.zh: 由塞种王统治、兼采草原贵族传统与印度化地方行政的王国体制。
governmentForm.en: A kingdom ruled by a Saka king, combining steppe aristocratic traditions with Indianized local administration.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、骑士贵族、佛教僧团、城市商人、乡村农民与工匠组成。西北印度与中亚的族群混合明显，塞种、印度-雅利安人、希腊化居民和地方部族长期共存。城市阶层在税收、贸易和文化传播中扮演重要角色。
socialStructure.en: Society is composed of the royal house, mounted nobility, Buddhist monastic communities, urban merchants, rural cultivators, and artisans. Ethnic blending is pronounced across northwestern India and Central Asia, with Saka, Indo-Aryan, Hellenized inhabitants, and local tribes coexisting over time. Urban groups play a major role in taxation, trade, and cultural transmission.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由塞种王族、地方总督、骑兵贵族和与王权合作的城镇精英构成。佛教僧团虽非世俗贵族，但在合法性与慈善网络方面影响甚大。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class includes the Saka royal house, provincial governors, cavalry aristocrats, and urban elites cooperating with the crown. Buddhist monastic communities are not secular nobles, but they exert major influence through legitimacy and charitable networks.

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族世袭，通常在宗族内部传递，并由军事贵族和高级臣僚承认。王位争夺并不罕见，强势总督或旁支王族可能借兵权继承王统。
succession.en: The throne is in principle hereditary within the royal family and requires recognition by military nobles and senior officials. Succession disputes are not uncommon, and powerful governors or collateral princes may use armed backing to claim the crown.

structure.zh: 王国实行中央王廷与地方总督并存的治理结构，重要城市和交通枢纽受较强控制。税收、司法和军务由王廷委派官员与地方豪族共同运作，呈现出草原王权与定居行政的混合特征。
structure.en: The kingdom uses a mixed structure of central court and provincial governors, with stronger control over major cities and transport nodes. Taxation, justice, and military affairs are managed jointly by royal appointees and local elites, reflecting a blend of steppe kingship and settled administration.

function.zh: 负责诏令、文书、外交和对地方总督的监督。
function.en: Handles edicts, documents, diplomacy, and oversight of provincial governors.

function.zh: 征收地税、商税、关津税，管理道路与市场收益。
function.en: Collects land tax, trade dues, and customs tolls, and manages roads and market revenues.

function.zh: 统筹骑兵、步兵、边境守备和战时征召。
function.en: Coordinates cavalry, infantry, frontier garrisons, and wartime levies.

function.zh: 处理诉讼、契约、城镇治安和寺院土地争议。
function.en: Handles lawsuits, contracts, urban order, and disputes over monastic lands.

localAdmin.zh: 地方多由总督、城镇长官和乡里豪族共同管理，重要佛寺也承担经济与调停功能。王国对税源丰富的城市和干道控制较强，对边远山区和部族地带则较为间接。
localAdmin.en: Local administration is shared by governors, urban magistrates, and rural notables, while major monasteries also perform economic and mediating roles. Royal control is stronger over tax-rich cities and trunk routes, but more indirect in remote hill and tribal zones.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼采印度地方习惯、王命裁断与商贸契约法，城市中也保留部分希腊化文书传统。司法实践重视财产、地租、债务和治安案件。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combines Indian local custom, royal adjudication, and commercial contract law, with some Hellenistic documentary practices surviving in cities. Judicial practice focuses on property, rents, debts, and public order.

taxationSystem.zh: 主要税种包括地税、关税、市场税和附庸贡纳，部分寺院土地享有减免。王国还通过控制商道、驿站和边境关口获取稳定收入。
taxationSystem.en: Main taxes include land tax, customs dues, market levies, and tributary payments, while some monastic lands enjoy exemptions. The kingdom also draws steady income by controlling trade routes, relay points, and frontier checkpoints.

religion.zh: 佛教
religion.en: Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就体现在犍陀罗艺术、佛塔与寺院建设、跨文化钱币铸造以及贸易城市的多语文书传统。王国是中亚与印度文化交流的重要桥梁。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements include Gandharan art, the construction of stupas and monasteries, cross-cultural coin minting, and multilingual urban documentary traditions. The kingdom is an important bridge between Central Asian and Indian civilizations.

languageFamily.zh: 伊朗语族塞种语、印度-雅利安语支俗语、希腊语文书传统并存
languageFamily.en: Saka Iranian, Indo-Aryan vernaculars, and Greek documentary traditions coexist

philosophy.zh: 以佛教为主要公共信仰，同时融合塞种草原传统、印度宗教观念和希腊化城市文化。
philosophy.en: Buddhism serves as the main public faith, while Saka steppe traditions, Indian religious ideas, and Hellenistic urban culture are also blended.

writingSystem.zh: 使用佉卢文与婆罗米文，部分地区和钱币上仍见希腊字母传统。
writingSystem.en: Kharosthi and Brahmi scripts are used, and Greek-letter traditions still appear in some regions and on coinage.

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济建立在灌溉农业、城市手工业与跨区域商路控制之上。得益于塔克西拉一带的交通优势，其商业化程度高于多数周边部族政权。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom's economy rests on irrigated agriculture, urban crafts, and control of long-distance trade routes. Owing to the transport advantages of the Taxila region, it is more commercialized than most surrounding tribal polities.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、金属加工、钱币铸造、纺织、宝石与商旅服务
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, metalworking, coin minting, textiles, gem trade, and caravan services

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、纺织品、香料、宝石、青金石、象牙、葡萄酒、铜铁器、佛教工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, textiles, spices, gemstones, lapis lazuli, ivory, wine, copper and iron goods, and Buddhist craft items

householdWealth.zh: 城市家庭财富多体现为钱币、仓储、店铺和土地租权；乡村家庭则以耕地、牲畜和粮食储备为主。
householdWealth.en: Urban household wealth is reflected in coin holdings, storage, shops, and land rents, while rural households rely mainly on fields, livestock, and grain reserves.

economicSystem.zh: 以王权征税、城市市场交换和地方庄园生产并行的混合型古代王国经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed ancient kingdom economy combining royal taxation, urban market exchange, and local estate production.

category.en: Roads, relay stations, and urban maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由银币、铜币、贵金属器、珠宝与谷物储备构成，部分财富分散保存在王室城市和要塞中。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consists of silver and copper coin, precious metal vessels, jewels, and grain reserves, with part of the wealth dispersed among royal cities and fortresses.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调保持商路畅通和军费稳定，倾向以关税与城市税负支撑王权，同时通过宗教施舍争取合法性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasizes maintaining open trade routes and stable military funding, relying on customs and urban taxes to support royal power while using religious patronage to strengthen legitimacy.

description.zh: 王国核心战力，擅长骑射、冲击和边境机动，是塞种军事传统的延续。
description.en: The kingdom's core force, skilled in mounted archery, shock action, and frontier mobility, continuing the Saka military tradition.

description.zh: 由地方征召兵、守备兵和长枪弓手构成，负责守城、护路和会战支援。
description.en: Composed of levies, garrison troops, and spear-and-bow infantry, they defend cities, protect roads, and support field battles.

description.zh: 部署于山口、关卡和商道据点，兼有侦察、护运与迟滞敌军的职责。
description.en: Stationed at passes, checkpoints, and trade nodes, they serve in reconnaissance, convoy protection, and delaying enemy advances.

technology.zh: 装备包括复合弓、铁制矛剑、鳞甲、马具和要塞防御工事，兼受中亚游牧与印度定居军事传统影响。
technology.en: Equipment includes composite bows, iron spears and swords, scale armor, horse tack, and fortified defenses, influenced by both Central Asian nomadic and Indian settled military traditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自贵霜扩张、印度次大陆诸邦竞争、山地部族袭扰以及商道控制权争夺。
threats.en: Principal threats come from Kushan expansion, competition with other Indian polities, raids by hill tribes, and struggles over trade-route control.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 罗马投石机，抛射大型石弹
description.en: Roman onager catapult, launching large stone projectiles

description.zh: 匈奴/帕提亚式复合弓，骑射核心武器
description.en: Xiongnu/Parthian composite bow, core mounted archery weapon

description.zh: 汉军标准配刀，单刃直背，劈斩利器
description.en: Standard Han army saber, single-edged straight back, ideal for slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于塔克西拉周边河谷、灌溉农业区和商路城市，乡村与市镇联系紧密。族群和语言多样性很高，是典型的边疆混合社会。
populationDescription.en: Population is concentrated in the Taxila region's river valleys, irrigated farming zones, and trade cities, with close ties between countryside and town. Ethnic and linguistic diversity is high, making it a classic frontier hybrid society.

description.zh: 犍陀罗学术与贸易中心
description.en: Gandharan center of learning and trade

socialClasses.zh: 王族与骑兵贵族、总督与官员、僧团与祭司、商人和工匠、农民、附庸部族
socialClasses.en: Royal house and cavalry nobility, governors and officials, monks and religious elites, merchants and artisans, peasants, tributary tribes

allies.zh: 与部分地方城邦、佛教僧团网络和若干附庸部族保持合作关系。
allies.en: The kingdom maintains cooperative relations with certain local city communities, Buddhist monastic networks, and several tributary tribes.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是贵霜势力、敌对地方王公及试图切断商路的山地集团。
enemies.en: Main enemies are Kushan forces, hostile local rulers, and hill groups seeking to disrupt trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以掌控西北印度交通节点、平衡中亚与印度诸强、保护商旅和宣示王室佛教赞助为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centers on controlling northwestern Indian transport nodes, balancing Central Asian and Indian powers, protecting caravans, and proclaiming royal Buddhist patronage.

era.zh: 中亚铁器时代晚期与南亚古典城市文明交汇阶段
era.en: Late Central Asian Iron Age interacting with South Asian classical urban civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑射、双语钱币铸造、犍陀罗佛教艺术、跨山地商道管理
keyInnovations.en: Mounted composite-bow warfare, bilingual coinage, Gandharan Buddhist art, and management of trans-mountain trade routes

strengths.zh: 地处交通枢纽、商业税源丰厚、骑兵机动强，并能整合多元文化资源。
strengths.en: It occupies a key transport hub, enjoys rich commercial tax revenues, fields highly mobile cavalry, and can integrate diverse cultural resources.

weaknesses.zh: 王权对边远地区控制有限，继承争端与强邻压力可能迅速削弱政权稳定。
weaknesses.en: Royal control over remote areas is limited, and succession disputes combined with pressure from stronger neighbors can quickly undermine stability.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍可凭借商路和城市财富维持区域强国地位，但在贵霜持续扩张背景下，其独立性面临明显挑战。
outlook.en: In the short term it can remain a significant regional power through trade routes and urban wealth, but its independence faces clear challenges amid continuing Kushan expansion.

description.zh: 一个以塔克西拉为中心、融合塞种骑马传统与佛教城市文化的西北印度王国。
description.en: A northwestern Indian kingdom centered on Taxila, blending Saka mounted traditions with Buddhist urban culture.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族长老会议和猎队首领协商为核心的松散部族治理结构。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal polity centered on clan elders' councils and consultation with hunting-band leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以小型亲族群体、季节性营地和若干互通婚姻的氏族构成。地位主要取决于狩猎能力、年龄、精神威望与资源分配中的慷慨程度。男女分工较明确，但在严酷环境下家庭合作比严格等级更重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around small kin groups, seasonal camps, and several intermarrying clans. Status depended mainly on hunting skill, age, spiritual prestige, and generosity in distributing resources. Labor was somewhat gendered, but household cooperation mattered more than rigid hierarchy in the severe environment.

rulingClass.zh: 并无固定贵族阶层，实际领导者多为年长的氏族长老、著名猎手与萨满。其权威主要建立在经验、声望和调解能力之上。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed nobility; practical leadership rested with senior clan elders, renowned hunters, and shamans. Their authority was based chiefly on experience, prestige, and skill in mediation.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常由同一家族中的资深成员继承，但并非严格世袭。若后继者缺乏威望，部族可转而推举更有能力的猎手或长老。
succession.en: Leadership often passed to a senior member of the same family, but succession was not strictly hereditary. If an heir lacked prestige, the group could instead elevate a more capable hunter or elder.

structure.zh: 政治组织极为分散，日常事务由各营地自行处理，跨氏族事务则由长老会临时集议。大型迁徙、狩猎协作与纠纷调停时，萨满与资深猎手也会参与决策。
structure.en: Political organization was highly decentralized, with each camp handling daily matters while inter-clan affairs were addressed in ad hoc councils of elders. Shamans and veteran hunters also joined decision-making during major migrations, cooperative hunts, and dispute settlements.

function.zh: 协调营地迁移、资源分配、婚姻安排与争端调解。
function.en: Coordinated camp movements, resource allocation, marriage arrangements, and dispute mediation.

function.zh: 主持祭仪、解释禁忌并组织集体狩猎与防御行动。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, interpreted taboos, and organized collective hunts and defensive action.

localAdmin.zh: 不存在固定地方官制，各季节性营地由家族首领和长者共同管理。较远的群体仅在贸易、婚配或共同狩猎时形成临时协调。
localAdmin.en: There was no fixed local bureaucracy; seasonal camps were managed jointly by household heads and elders. Distant groups coordinated only temporarily for trade, marriage, or collective hunting.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、祖先传统和萨满禁忌为基础。对盗窃、破坏分享规则或婚姻纠纷，通常通过赔偿、公开调解或暂时驱逐解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was grounded in customary practice, ancestral tradition, and shamanic taboos. Theft, violations of sharing norms, and marital disputes were usually resolved through compensation, public mediation, or temporary exclusion.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，首领与萨满依靠礼物、猎获分成和公共储藏中的优先份额维持权威。集体活动时，各家庭需贡献食物、皮毛或劳力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders and shamans were supported through gifts, shares of major kills, and preferential access to communal stores. During collective activities, households contributed food, hides, or labor.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在极寒环境中发展出高效的驯鹿追踪、冰雪行旅与皮毛缝制技术。口述神话、狩猎歌谣和仪式舞蹈构成其主要文化遗产。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed efficient techniques for reindeer tracking, snow travel, and hide sewing in an extreme cold environment. Oral mythology, hunting songs, and ritual dance formed their principal cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 古西伯利亚诸语言与若干孤立语群
languageFamily.en: Paleo-Siberian languages and several language isolates

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵观念和人与动物世界互惠的伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestral spirits, and an ethic of reciprocity between humans and the animal world.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，知识主要通过口述传统、歌谣、神话和仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, myths, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎、采集、渔猎和皮毛加工为主，市场化程度极低。剩余产品有限，但高价值皮毛可在边缘交换网络中充当重要财富。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence hunting, gathering, fishing, and hide processing, with very low marketization. Surplus was limited, but high-value furs served as important wealth in fringe exchange networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、渔猎、采集、皮毛加工、骨角器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, gathering, fur processing, bone and antler tool production

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、兽牙、骨角器、干鱼、琥珀样装饰物
tradeGoods.en: Furs, animal teeth, bone and antler tools, dried fish, amber-like ornaments

name.zh: 以物易物与皮毛价值体系

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在皮毛储备、骨角工具、狩猎武器、雪地交通器具和可交换的装饰物。财富差距存在，但受分享义务约束，不易长期积累。
