householdWealth.en: Household wealth consisted mainly of hide stocks, bone and antler tools, hunting weapons, snow-travel gear, and exchangeable ornaments. Inequality existed, but sharing obligations limited long-term accumulation.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给、氏族共享和小规模互惠交换为特征的采猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A foraging economy characterized by household subsistence, clan sharing, and small-scale reciprocal exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无固定国库，所谓“储备”主要是若干营地保存的皮毛、干鱼、骨器和礼物存量。其价值会随季节、猎获和死亡率迅速波动。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed treasury; the 'reserve' consisted mainly of furs, dried fish, tools, and gift stocks held across several camps. Its value fluctuated rapidly with season, hunting success, and mortality.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政原则重在维持冬季生存、公共分享和首领威望，而非积累货币性盈余。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice prioritized winter survival, communal sharing, and leadership prestige rather than the accumulation of monetary surplus.

description.zh: 由成年猎手组成，使用长矛、弓箭和骨角武器，平时狩猎，战时承担伏击与防卫。
description.en: Composed of adult hunters using spears, bows, and bone-antler weapons; they hunted in peacetime and conducted ambushes and defense in conflict.

description.zh: 负责保护妇幼、储藏与迁徙营地，多在遭遇袭击时临时集结。
description.en: Protected women, children, stores, and migration camps, usually assembling only when attacked.

commanderInChief.zh: 氏族长老与首席猎手共议
commanderInChief.en: Joint direction by clan elders and the foremost hunter

technology.zh: 军事技术接近高寒采猎社会水平，以木矛、骨角矛头、弓箭、皮甲和雪地机动经验为主。缺乏金属武器与攻城能力，但擅长伏击和严寒生存。
technology.en: Military technology remained at the level of an Arctic-subarctic foraging society, relying on wooden spears, bone-antler points, bows, hide protection, and snow mobility. They lacked metal arms and siege capability but excelled in ambush and cold-weather survival.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争夺猎场、极端气候、食物短缺以及疾病。长期战争能力有限，但边境冲突和报复性袭击并不罕见。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring groups competing for hunting grounds, extreme climate, food shortage, and disease. Capacity for prolonged warfare was limited, but border clashes and retaliatory raids were not uncommon.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 人口极为稀疏，散居于冻原、针叶林和河谷地带。季节性迁徙显著，部分群体冬夏营地相距甚远。
populationDescription.en: The population was extremely sparse, scattered across tundra, taiga, and river valleys. Seasonal mobility was pronounced, with some groups maintaining widely separated winter and summer camps.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与萨满、著名猎手、普通家庭成员、依附亲属与俘获外来者。
socialClasses.en: Elders and shamans, renowned hunters, ordinary family members, dependent kin, and captured outsiders.

allies.zh: 无固定盟友，主要与相邻采猎群体维持婚姻、礼物和互助关系。
allies.en: No fixed allies; relations with neighboring foraging groups were maintained mainly through marriage, gift exchange, and mutual aid.

enemies.zh: 无持久国家级敌人，但与争夺河谷、渔场和驯鹿通道的邻近部落时有冲突。
enemies.en: No enduring state-level enemies, though conflicts with nearby tribes over river valleys, fishing grounds, and reindeer routes were recurrent.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略以避战、维持交换网络和确保迁徙路线安全为主。只有在资源压力加剧时才会进行报复性袭击或迁徙扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy emphasized avoiding war, maintaining exchange networks, and keeping migration routes secure. Retaliatory raids or expansion by movement occurred mainly when resource pressure intensified.

era.zh: 晚期石骨器时代的高寒采猎技术阶段
era.en: Late stone-and-bone technological stage of subarctic foragers

keyInnovations.zh: 复合皮衣、骨角鱼叉、雪地行具、季节性储藏、寒地营地构筑。
keyInnovations.en: Layered hide clothing, bone-antler harpoons, snow-travel gear, seasonal storage, and cold-environment camp construction.

strengths.zh: 熟悉严寒生态、机动性强、社会互助紧密、对环境变化反应灵活。
strengths.en: Deep knowledge of cold ecosystems, high mobility, strong mutual support, and flexible adaptation to environmental change.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、生产剩余有限、技术层次低、难以承受连续灾年或大规模冲突。
weaknesses.en: Small population, limited surplus, low technological complexity, and weak capacity to withstand repeated bad years or large-scale conflict.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持稳定生存，但长期发展高度受气候波动、猎物迁徙和邻近群体压力制约。
outlook.en: It could remain viable in the short term, but long-term prospects were strongly constrained by climate shifts, animal migration patterns, and pressure from neighboring groups.

description.zh: 这是一个分散于西伯利亚北方寒地、依靠狩猎渔猎和氏族协作维生的古西伯利亚部族社会。
description.en: This was a dispersed Paleo-Siberian tribal society of the northern Siberian cold zone, sustained by hunting, fishing, and clan cooperation.

governmentForm.zh: 以猎队首领、家族长者和萨满共同维系的松散海猎部族组织。
governmentForm.en: A loose maritime hunting tribal order maintained jointly by hunting-band leaders, family elders, and shamans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由小型海猎家庭群和季节性聚落构成，冬季集中、夏季分散。地位主要取决于捕猎海兽的能力、驾舟经验、物资分享和精神权威。整体分层较弱，但成功猎手常拥有更大影响力。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of small maritime hunting households and seasonal settlements, concentrated in winter and dispersed in summer. Status depended mainly on sea-mammal hunting skill, boating experience, generosity in sharing supplies, and spiritual authority. Stratification was weak overall, though successful hunters often wielded greater influence.

rulingClass.zh: 没有固定统治阶层，权威集中于经验丰富的猎手、长者和主持仪式的萨满。其领导更依赖共识与声望，而非强制权力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed ruling class; authority rested with experienced hunters, elders, and ritual specialists. Leadership relied more on consensus and prestige than coercive power.

succession.zh: 猎队首领通常由其亲属或同一家庭中的优秀猎手接替，但必须获得群体认可。若首领失去捕猎能力或分配不公，地位可被他人取代。
succession.en: A hunting-band leader was often succeeded by a relative or another skilled hunter from the same household cluster, but only with group approval. If a leader lost hunting effectiveness or distributed goods unfairly, another could replace him.

structure.zh: 治理方式以聚落协商和猎队组织为核心，没有常设官僚机构。有关迁徙、猎场使用、婚姻联系和冲突处置的事务，多在冬季共居期由长者会议决定。
structure.en: Governance centered on settlement consultation and hunting-band organization, without a permanent bureaucracy. Matters of migration, hunting-ground use, marriage ties, and conflict management were mostly decided in elders' meetings during winter co-residence.

function.zh: 分配猎场、安排出猎、协调物资分享与对外接触。
function.en: Allocated hunting grounds, scheduled expeditions, coordinated sharing of supplies, and managed outside contacts.

function.zh: 主持与海兽、祖灵和天气相关的仪式，维持群体禁忌秩序。
function.en: Conducted rituals related to sea mammals, ancestral spirits, and weather, and upheld the community's taboos.

localAdmin.zh: 无固定地方行政单位，各聚落由若干家庭联合维持。重要海湾、海冰通道和狩猎点由使用习惯而非正式边界界定。
localAdmin.en: There were no fixed local administrative districts; settlements were maintained by clusters of households. Important bays, sea-ice routes, and hunting spots were defined by customary use rather than formal borders.

legalSystem.zh: 法律建立在口传习惯、禁忌与调停传统之上。对伤害、婚姻纠纷或分享失衡，常通过赔偿、讽刺歌、决斗式竞争或暂时分离来解决。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on oral custom, taboo, and mediation traditions. Injury, marriage disputes, or failures of sharing were often handled through compensation, ridicule songs, competitive duels, or temporary separation.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税收，首领依靠猎获中的优先分配、礼物和集体支持。大型海兽捕获后，家庭须按传统比例向群体分享肉、脂肪与皮革。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders relied on preferential shares of catches, gifts, and communal support. After large sea-mammal hunts, households were expected to distribute meat, blubber, and skins to the group according to tradition.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在极地环境中形成了高水平的皮舟使用、冰上狩猎技巧和保温服装制作传统。口述故事、节律歌谣和象牙骨器装饰体现出鲜明的海猎文化特色。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed advanced traditions of skin-boat use, ice hunting, and insulated clothing production in an Arctic environment. Oral tales, rhythmic songs, and decorated ivory-bone objects expressed a distinctive maritime hunting culture.

languageFamily.zh: 爱斯基摩-阿留申语系早期分支
languageFamily.en: Early branches of the Eskimo-Aleut language family

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵论、海兽灵魂观和维持人与海冰世界平衡的禁忌伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, beliefs in the souls of sea mammals, and a taboo-based ethic of maintaining balance between humans and the sea-ice world.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，历史、航海知识和狩猎规则依靠口述、故事与歌谣传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; history, navigation knowledge, and hunting rules were transmitted through oral narratives, stories, and songs.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以海兽捕猎、渔业、采集和骨角皮革制品为基础，几乎完全服务于生存需求。由于海象牙、优质皮革和骨器具有较高交换价值，边缘贸易虽小但重要。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on sea-mammal hunting, fishing, gathering, and products made from bone, ivory, and skins, serving subsistence needs almost entirely. Because walrus ivory, high-quality skins, and crafted bone goods carried notable exchange value, fringe trade was small but important.

mainIndustries.zh: 海豹与海象捕猎、渔业、皮革加工、骨角象牙器制作、皮舟制造
mainIndustries.en: Seal and walrus hunting, fishing, leather processing, bone-antler-ivory craft production, skin-boat making

tradeGoods.zh: 海象牙、海豹皮、鲸脂制品、骨角工具、干鱼
tradeGoods.en: Walrus ivory, seal skins, whale-blubber products, bone and antler tools, dried fish

name.zh: 以物易物与象牙皮货价值体系

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为皮舟、骨角工具、冬衣、油脂储备、犬具和象牙制品。富裕家庭通常拥有更大的储藏能力和更好的舟具，但仍受共享义务限制。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed through skin boats, bone and antler tools, winter clothing, stores of oil and fat, dog gear, and ivory goods. Better-off households usually had larger storage capacity and superior boats, though sharing obligations still constrained accumulation.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以海猎自给、家庭生产和聚落内部再分配为特征的极地采猎经济。
economicSystem.en: An Arctic foraging economy characterized by maritime subsistence, household production, and redistribution within settlements.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: External exchange of ivory and skins

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 没有集中财政库藏，储备主要是海豹油、干鱼、皮革、象牙和备用舟具。其“财政”本质上是对食物与生存物资的集体掌控。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves consisted mainly of seal oil, dried fish, skins, ivory, and spare boat gear. In essence, their 'finance' was communal control over food and survival goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 资源分配优先保障冬季生存、维持猎队效率并通过共享稳定聚落关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource allocation prioritized winter survival, maintenance of hunting efficiency, and social stability through sharing.

description.zh: 由熟练舟手与海猎者组成，擅长沿岸机动、伏击和保卫猎场。
description.en: Composed of skilled boatmen and sea hunters, adept at coastal movement, ambush, and defense of hunting grounds.

description.zh: 在营地遭袭或资源争端升级时集结，负责护卫家属和储藏。
description.en: Mobilized when camps were attacked or disputes over resources escalated, protecting families and stores.

technology.zh: 武装以骨角矛、鱼叉、弓箭、石刃和皮甲为主，兼具皮舟海上机动能力。其作战优势在于熟悉海冰、海岸线和恶劣天气，而非重装正面战斗。
technology.en: Armament centered on bone-antler spears, harpoons, bows, stone blades, and hide protection, combined with mobility by skin boat. Their fighting advantage lay in mastery of sea ice, coastlines, and harsh weather rather than heavy frontal combat.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括海冰变化、猎物减少、饥荒、局部聚落冲突以及环境造成的高死亡率。战争规模一般很小，但资源危机时冲突会迅速恶化。
threats.en: Primary threats included sea-ice shifts, reduced game, famine, localized settlement conflicts, and environmentally driven high mortality. Warfare was usually small-scale, but crises over resources could escalate quickly.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

populationDescription.zh: 人口规模很小，沿海岸、岛屿和海冰边缘零散分布。聚落通常由数个扩大家庭组成，并随季节和猎物迁移而调整位置。
populationDescription.en: The population was very small, scattered along coasts, islands, and sea-ice margins. Settlements usually consisted of several extended families and shifted location with the seasons and the movements of game.

socialClasses.zh: 资深猎手与长者、萨满、普通海猎家庭成员、年轻从猎者与依附亲属。
socialClasses.en: Senior hunters and elders, shamans, ordinary maritime hunting households, younger apprentice hunters, and dependent kin.

allies.zh: 无稳定正式盟友，但与邻近海猎群体保持婚姻、技术交流和季节性互助。
allies.en: No stable formal allies, though neighboring maritime hunting groups maintained marriage ties, technical exchange, and seasonal mutual support.

enemies.zh: 无长期国家敌人，主要敌对对象是争夺海兽猎场、岸边营地或漂流木资源的邻近群体。
enemies.en: There were no long-term state enemies; principal adversaries were neighboring groups competing for sea-mammal grounds, shoreline camps, or driftwood resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外倾向于维持低强度接触与有限交换，以避免破坏脆弱的生存平衡。只有在关键猎场和冬季资源受威胁时才采取强硬行动。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations tended toward low-intensity contact and limited exchange in order to preserve a fragile survival balance. Forceful action was taken mainly when crucial hunting grounds or winter resources were threatened.

era.zh: 北极海猎石骨器技术阶段
era.en: Arctic maritime stone-and-bone technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 皮舟与猎舟制造、鱼叉系统、保温毛皮服、油灯利用、海冰导航经验。
keyInnovations.en: Skin-boat construction, harpoon systems, insulated fur clothing, oil-lamp use, and sea-ice navigation knowledge.

strengths.zh: 高度适应极地海岸环境，海猎技术精湛，聚落合作紧密，生存知识丰富。
strengths.en: Highly adapted to the Arctic coastal environment, skilled in maritime hunting, cohesive in settlement cooperation, and rich in survival knowledge.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少、剩余薄弱、受气候与猎物波动影响极大，缺乏长期集中政治与军事能力。
weaknesses.en: Extremely small population, thin surplus, heavy dependence on climate and animal fluctuations, and little capacity for long-term centralized politics or warfare.

outlook.zh: 只要海兽资源和海冰节律保持相对稳定，社会可持续延续；但气候异常或迁徙通道变化将迅速带来危机。
outlook.en: So long as sea-mammal resources and sea-ice rhythms remained relatively stable, the society could endure; however, climatic anomalies or shifts in migration routes would quickly produce crisis.

description.zh: 这是一个分布于北极沿海、依靠海兽捕猎与季节性聚落协作维生的古因纽特海猎社会。
description.en: This was a Paleo-Inuit maritime hunting society distributed along Arctic coasts and sustained by sea-mammal hunting and seasonal settlement cooperation.

capital.zh: 无统一都城（各部族有各自环形堡垒中心）
capital.en: No unified capital (each tribe had its own ringfort center)

governmentForm.zh: 以牛财富和亲族关系为基础的分权部族王权制，由地方国王统治小王国（tuath），上层由省级高王松散联结。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal kingship based on cattle wealth and kin relations, with local kings ruling small kingdoms (tuatha), loosely linked by provincial over-kings.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、贵族（flaith）、自由农民（bóaire）、工匠、吟游诗人（fili）和无地附庸组成。牛群数量是衡量地位的核心指标。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of kings, nobles (flaith), free farmers (bóaire), craftsmen, poet-judges (fili), and landless dependents. Cattle holdings were the primary measure of status.

rulingClass.zh: 贵族阶层由拥有大量牛群的氏族首领和战士精英构成，通过客户制（céilsine）维系对下层的控制。
rulingClass.en: The noble class comprised clan chiefs and warrior elites with large cattle herds, maintaining control over subordinates through clientship (céilsine).

succession.zh: 王位在合格王族成员（derbfine，四代以内）中由选举产生，兼顾才能与血统。
succession.en: Kingship was elected from eligible royal kin (derbfine, within four generations), balancing ability and bloodline.

structure.zh: 每个tuath由国王和贵族议事会治理，吟游诗人兼任法官角色。没有常设官僚机构，治理依赖亲族义务和口头法律传统（布列洪法）。
structure.en: Each tuath was governed by a king and a council of nobles, with poet-judges serving legal roles. There was no standing bureaucracy; governance depended on kin obligations and the oral legal tradition (Brehon law).

function.zh: 协助国王决策战争、结盟、土地分配和法律裁决。
function.en: Assisted the king in decisions on war, alliances, land allocation, and legal judgments.

function.zh: 记忆和执行部族法律，裁决纠纷，确定赔偿标准。
function.en: Memorized and enforced tribal law, adjudicated disputes, and determined compensation standards.

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、节气庆典和占卜，维护神圣知识传承。
function.en: Conducted religious rites, seasonal festivals, and divination, preserving sacred knowledge.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以环形堡垒（rath）为中心，由各氏族首领管理，通过口头协议和习惯法执行事务。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on ringforts (raths), managed by clan chiefs, with affairs enforced through oral agreements and customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 布列洪法（Brehon Law）是一套复杂的口头法律体系，以赔偿（éraic）和荣誉价格（lóg n-enech）为核心原则，强调补偿而非惩罚。
legalSystem.en: Brehon Law was a complex oral legal system centered on compensation (éraic) and honor-price (lóg n-enech), emphasizing restitution over punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，贡赋以牛、粮食和劳役形式由客户向领主缴纳，国王从臣属部族获取供奉。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; tribute in cattle, grain, and labor was rendered by clients to lords, and kings received offerings from subject tribes.

religion.zh: 凯尔特多神教与德鲁伊信仰
religion.en: Celtic polytheism and Druidic belief

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出精细的金属工艺（La Tène风格装饰）、口头史诗传统、复杂的法律体系和精美的青铜与黄金饰品。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed refined metalwork (La Tène-style decoration), oral epic traditions, a complex legal system, and exquisite bronze and gold ornaments.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系凯尔特语族盖尔亚支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Celtic, Goidelic branch

philosophy.zh: 德鲁伊自然崇拜与祖灵信仰
philosophy.en: Druidic nature veneration and ancestor belief

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统（欧甘文字尚未广泛使用），依赖口头传承。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system (Ogham script not yet in wide use); reliance on oral transmission.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以牧牛为核心，辅以谷物种植、狩猎和少量手工业。牛群既是财富储存也是交换媒介，对外贸易有限但通过英伦海峡接触罗马不列颠。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on cattle herding, supplemented by grain cultivation, hunting, and limited craft production. Cattle served as both wealth store and exchange medium; external trade was limited but reached Roman Britain across the Irish Sea.

mainIndustries.zh: 牧牛业、谷物种植、金属冶炼与装饰品制造、皮革加工
mainIndustries.en: Cattle herding, grain cultivation, metalworking and ornament manufacture, leather processing

tradeGoods.zh: 牛、皮革、黄金饰品、铜器、猎犬、谷物
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, hides, gold ornaments, copper goods, hunting dogs, grain

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由牛群规模、耕地面积、金属器具和客户数量衡量。一个中等自由农民约拥有七至十头牛。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly by herd size, arable land, metal goods, and number of clients. A middling free farmer typically owned seven to ten cattle.

economicSystem.zh: 以牛群为核心计量单位的部族牧农混合经济，通过客户制和贡赋关系进行资源分配。
economicSystem.en: A tribal agro-pastoral economy using cattle as the core unit of account, with resources distributed through clientship and tribute relations.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共财富以牛群、黄金饰品和铜器形式储存于国王和贵族的堡垒中，而非统一库房。
treasuryDescription.en: Public wealth was stored as cattle herds, gold ornaments, and copper goods in kings' and nobles' forts rather than a centralized treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政围绕维持宴饮声望、武士供养和宗教活动展开，积累能力有限且高度分散。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal activity revolved around maintaining feasting prestige, warrior provisioning, and religious observance, with limited and highly dispersed accumulation.

description.zh: 贵族骑乘战车或骑马作战，装备铁矛、剑和盾牌，是战场核心力量。
description.en: Nobles fighting from chariots or horseback, armed with iron spears, swords, and shields, forming the battlefield core.

description.zh: 装备长矛和投石的自由农民民兵，战时应召集结。
description.en: Free farmer militia armed with spears and slings, mustered when called to war.

description.zh: 凭借对地形的熟悉执行侦察、伏击和牛群劫掠任务。
description.en: Using familiarity with terrain to carry out scouting, ambush, and cattle-raiding missions.

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁质武器、木盾、战车和轻装骑兵为核心。堡垒以环形土石构造为主。牛群劫掠（cattle raid）是常见的部族间冲突形式。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, wooden shields, chariots, and light cavalry. Forts were mainly ring-shaped earthen-stone constructions. Cattle raiding was the typical form of inter-tribal conflict.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族的牛群劫掠和领土争端，以及罗马不列颠可能的侵扰。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes' cattle raids and territorial disputes, and potential Roman British incursions.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于各部族领地，以环形堡垒和散居农庄为主要聚落形态。没有真正意义上的城市，最大的聚落也不过数百人。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across tribal territories, with ringforts and scattered farmsteads as the main settlement pattern. There were no true cities; the largest settlements held only a few hundred people.

name.zh: 克鲁阿汉（康纳赫特中心）

socialClasses.zh: 国王与王族、贵族（flaith）、自由农民（bóaire）、工匠与诗人、半自由附庸（fuidir）。
socialClasses.en: Kings and royal kin, nobles (flaith), free farmers (bóaire), craftsmen and poets, semi-free dependents (fuidir).

allies.zh: 各部族间通过婚姻和宴饮结盟，与不列颠凯尔特部族有文化和贸易联系。
allies.en: Tribes allied through marriage and feasting, with cultural and trade links to British Celtic tribes.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对来自邻近部族的领土和牛群争夺，罗马帝国虽未入侵爱尔兰但构成潜在威胁。
enemies.en: Principal hostility came from neighboring tribes' territorial and cattle disputes; Rome had not invaded Ireland but posed a potential threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持部族平衡、争取高王认可和保持跨海贸易为重，尚无统一外交政策。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on maintaining tribal balance, securing over-king recognition, and sustaining cross-sea trade, without unified foreign policy.

era.zh: 铁器时代（La Tène阶段）
era.en: Iron Age (La Tène phase)

keyInnovations.zh: 铁质工具与武器、环形堡垒建筑、战车技术、精细金属装饰工艺、乳制品加工。
keyInnovations.en: Iron tools and weapons, ringfort construction, chariot technology, refined metalwork decoration, dairy processing.

strengths.zh: 精细的法律传统、强大的口头文化和战士精神、丰富的畜牧资源、与不列颠的贸易联系。
strengths.en: Refined legal tradition, strong oral culture and warrior ethos, rich pastoral resources, and trade links with Britain.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散、部族间频繁内斗、无文字记录能力、缺乏城市化和大规模组织。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, frequent inter-tribal feuding, no written record-keeping, and lack of urbanization and large-scale organization.

outlook.zh: 在罗马帝国未入侵的情况下，爱尔兰部族社会将保持稳定的分散格局，但可能在未来数世纪逐步出现更强大的省级王权。
outlook.en: With Rome not invading, Irish tribal society would maintain its dispersed pattern, though stronger provincial kingships might gradually emerge over coming centuries.

description.zh: 公元100年的爱尔兰是一个由数十个小型部族王国组成的铁器时代社会，以牛群为核心经济支柱，以环形堡垒为聚落中心，拥有复杂的口头法律和深厚的凯尔特文化传统。
description.en: Ireland in 100 CE was an Iron Age society composed of dozens of small tribal kingdoms, with cattle as the economic backbone, ringforts as settlement centers, and a complex oral legal system rooted in deep Celtic cultural tradition.

name.zh: 喀里多尼亚/皮克特前身部族

capital.zh: 无统一都城（各部族有各自山堡）
capital.en: No unified capital (each tribe had its own hillfort)

governmentForm.zh: 以山堡和氏族为核心的分散部族首领制，各部族各自独立，仅在面对外部威胁时进行松散联合。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized chieftainship centered on hillforts and clans, with each tribe independent and loosely confederating only against external threats.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族首领、战士贵族、自由农牧民、工匠和依附人口构成。地位取决于血统、战功和牛群规模。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised tribal chiefs, warrior nobles, free agro-pastoralists, craftsmen, and dependents. Status depended on lineage, martial prowess, and herd size.

rulingClass.zh: 首领和战士精英构成统治阶层，通过战功和财富维系权威。
rulingClass.en: Chiefs and warrior elites formed the ruling class, maintaining authority through martial achievement and wealth.

succession.zh: 首领位通常在同一氏族内传承，但也可能因战功或部族集会决议而更替。
succession.en: Chieftainship usually passed within the same clan, though it could shift through military merit or tribal assembly decisions.

structure.zh: 治理以部族集会和首领议事为基础，无常设官僚。在罗马威胁下部族间进行临时性军事联合。
structure.en: Governance was based on tribal assemblies and chiefly councils, with no standing bureaucracy. Temporary military alliances formed among tribes against Roman threats.

function.zh: 在战时组织跨部族军事协调、确定战略和分配战利品。
function.en: Organized inter-tribal military coordination, set strategy, and allocated spoils in wartime.

function.zh: 裁决氏族间纠纷、管理牧场边界和婚姻事务。
function.en: Adjudicated inter-clan disputes, managed pasture boundaries, and oversaw marriage matters.

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式和占卜，传承神圣知识和部族历史。
function.en: Conducted religious rites and divination, preserving sacred knowledge and tribal history.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以山堡和谷地聚落为单位，由各氏族首领直接管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through hillforts and valley settlements, directly managed by clan chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以部族习惯和长老裁决为基础，强调赔偿和血仇复仇的平衡。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on tribal custom and elder judgment, balancing compensation with blood-feud retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，贡赋以牲畜、谷物和劳役形式向首领缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: No unified tax system; tribute in livestock, grain, and labor was rendered to chiefs.

religion.zh: 凯尔特多神教与自然崇拜
religion.en: Celtic polytheism and nature worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 建造了精密的石质塔楼（broch）和山堡防御体系，发展出独特的几何装饰艺术风格。
culturalAchievements.en: Constructed sophisticated stone tower-houses (brochs) and hillfort defenses, developing a distinctive geometric decorative art style.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系凯尔特语族布立吞亚支（可能含皮克特语元素）
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Celtic, Brythonic branch (possibly with Pictish elements)

philosophy.zh: 德鲁伊自然崇拜与战士荣誉观
philosophy.en: Druidic nature veneration and warrior honor ethic

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，依赖口头传承和象征性石刻。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system; reliance on oral tradition and symbolic stone carvings.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧（牛、羊）和粗放农业为主，辅以狩猎、捕鱼和少量金属加工。与罗马不列颠的非正式贸易提供了部分进口品。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on pastoralism (cattle, sheep) and extensive farming, supplemented by hunting, fishing, and limited metalwork. Informal trade with Roman Britain provided some imports.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、粗放农业、石质建筑（broch）、狩猎与捕鱼
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, extensive farming, stone construction (brochs), hunting and fishing

tradeGoods.zh: 牛、皮革、毛皮、铁器、猎获物
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, hides, furs, ironware, game products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以牛群、农田、石质住宅和金属器具衡量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by cattle herds, farmland, stone dwellings, and metal implements.

economicSystem.zh: 以牧牛为核心的部族自给农牧经济，通过贡赋和亲族义务进行有限的资源再分配。
economicSystem.en: A tribal subsistence agro-pastoral economy centered on cattle, with limited resource redistribution through tribute and kin obligations.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备以牲畜、武器和少量罗马进口品形式分散储存于各山堡首领处。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves in livestock, weapons, and limited Roman imports were dispersed among hillfort chiefs.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政围绕战争准备和首领权威维系展开，在罗马威胁加剧时军事支出比例上升。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal activity centered on war preparation and chiefly authority; military spending rose when Roman threats intensified.

description.zh: 装备长矛、剑和方盾的部族步兵，擅长山地丘陵地形作战。
description.en: Tribal infantry armed with spears, swords, and square shields, skilled in fighting on hilly and mountainous terrain.

description.zh: 贵族驾驶轻型战车或骑马作战，是突击冲锋的核心力量。
description.en: Nobles operating light chariots or fighting on horseback, serving as the shock assault force.

description.zh: 利用对高地和沼泽的熟悉执行伏击、骚扰和情报收集任务，尤其针对罗马军队。
description.en: Using knowledge of highlands and bogs for ambush, harassment, and intelligence gathering, particularly against Roman forces.

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁质武器、山堡防御和游击伏击战术为核心。在对抗罗马军团方面积累了丰富的非正规战经验。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, hillfort defenses, and guerrilla ambush tactics. Extensive irregular warfare experience had been gained from confronting Roman legions.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自罗马帝国从南部不列颠的北扩企图（阿古利可拉远征刚结束不久），部族间争斗也是持续隐患。
threats.en: The greatest threat came from Rome's northward expansion from southern Britain (Agricola's campaign had recently ended); inter-tribal strife was also a persistent concern.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于苏格兰高地、谷地和沿海地区。聚落以小型农牧社区和山堡为主，没有真正的城市。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across the Scottish highlands, glens, and coastal areas. Settlements were mainly small agro-pastoral communities and hillforts, with no true cities.

socialClasses.zh: 部族首领与王族、战士贵族、自由农牧民、工匠、依附人口和奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Tribal chiefs and royal kin, warrior nobles, free agro-pastoralists, craftsmen, dependents, and slaves.

allies.zh: 各喀里多尼亚部族在面对罗马时进行松散联合，与爱尔兰盖尔部族保持一定文化交流。
allies.en: Caledonian tribes loosely confederated against Rome, maintaining some cultural exchange with Irish Gaelic tribes.

enemies.zh: 罗马帝国是最大外部威胁，部族之间也经常发生领土和牲畜争夺。
enemies.en: The Roman Empire was the greatest external threat; inter-tribal territorial and livestock disputes were also frequent.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以抵御罗马北扩为核心，在部族间维持战时临时联盟机制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on resisting Roman northward expansion, with temporary inter-tribal alliance mechanisms in wartime.

era.zh: 铁器时代部族文化
era.en: Iron Age tribal culture

keyInnovations.zh: Broch石质塔楼建筑、铁质工具与武器、山堡防御体系、游击战术。
keyInnovations.en: Broch stone tower-house construction, iron tools and weapons, hillfort defense systems, guerrilla tactics.

strengths.zh: 占据险峻山地地形优势、拥有丰富的游击战经验、部族在抗罗时展现出较强凝聚力。
strengths.en: Commanded advantageous mountainous terrain, possessed rich guerrilla warfare experience, and tribes showed strong cohesion when resisting Rome.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、经济基础薄弱、人口稀少、技术远落后于罗马。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, weak economic base, sparse population, and technology far behind Rome.

outlook.zh: 罗马未能彻底征服喀里多尼亚，未来将修建哈德良长城作为边界。部族社会将继续存在并逐步演变为皮克特政体。
outlook.en: Rome could not fully conquer Caledonia and would later build Hadrian's Wall as a boundary. Tribal society would persist and gradually evolve into Pictish polities.

description.zh: 公元100年的喀里多尼亚是罗马帝国北方边境之外的凯尔特部族联合体，以山堡防御和游击战术著称，刚刚经历阿古利可拉远征的冲击。
description.en: Caledonia in 100 CE was a Celtic tribal confederacy beyond the Roman Empire's northern frontier, renowned for hillfort defenses and guerrilla tactics, having recently weathered Agricola's campaign.

name.zh: 马科曼尼人与日耳曼部落
马科曼尼日耳曼部落联盟

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭王权为核心、贵族议事会为制约的日耳曼部落王国制。国王权力受部落集会（thing）约束。
governmentForm.en: A Germanic tribal kingdom with hereditary kingship at its core, checked by a noble council. Royal power was constrained by the tribal assembly (thing).

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、武士贵族、自由民战士、半自由佃农和奴隶组成。战争功勋和亲从关系（comitatus）是社会流动的关键。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised royal kin, warrior nobles, free warrior-farmers, semi-free tenants, and slaves. Military merit and the retinue system (comitatus) were key to social mobility.

rulingClass.zh: 王族和拥有大量随从（comitatus）的贵族战士构成统治阶层。
rulingClass.en: The royal family and noble warriors commanding large retinues (comitatus) formed the ruling class.

succession.zh: 王位在王族内传承，由贵族议事会认可。若国王无能则可能被废黜或替换。
succession.en: Kingship was inherited within the royal family and confirmed by the noble council. Incompetent kings could be deposed or replaced.

structure.zh: 治理以国王为中心，由贵族议事会辅助。重大事务需经部落集会表决。地方由各分支部族首领管理。
structure.en: Governance centered on the king, assisted by a noble council. Major decisions required tribal assembly approval. Local areas were administered by sub-tribal chiefs.

function.zh: 协助国王处理外交、战争决策和重大纠纷裁决。
function.en: Assisted the king in diplomacy, war decisions, and adjudication of major disputes.

function.zh: 全体自由民参加的大会，表决战争与和平、法律变更和重大事务。
function.en: Assembly of all free men, voting on war and peace, legal changes, and major affairs.

function.zh: 国王的精锐亲卫队，执行军事行动、使节和日常护卫。
function.en: The king's elite personal guard, executing military operations, embassies, and daily protection.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由各分支部族的贵族首领负责，通过定期集会和口头习惯法协调事务。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by noble chiefs of sub-tribes, coordinating affairs through periodic assemblies and oral customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口头习惯法为基础，部落集会为最高裁判机关。赔偿金（wergild）制度用于平息血仇。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on oral customary law, with the tribal assembly as the supreme judicial body. The wergild (compensation payment) system was used to settle blood feuds.

taxationSystem.zh: 无系统性税收，国王收入来自王室领地、战利品、贡赋和自愿供献。
taxationSystem.en: No systematic taxation; royal income came from royal estates, war spoils, tribute, and voluntary offerings.

religion.zh: 日耳曼多神教（沃坦、提尔、弗雷亚等）
religion.en: Germanic polytheism (Wotan, Tyr, Freyja, etc.)

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出精湛的铁器制造技术、独特的日耳曼动物纹装饰风格，以及复杂的口头英雄史诗传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed skilled ironworking, a distinctive Germanic animal-style ornamental tradition, and complex oral heroic epic traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族西日耳曼亚支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Germanic, West Germanic branch

philosophy.zh: 日耳曼荣誉观与命运信仰
philosophy.en: Germanic honor ethic and belief in fate

writingSystem.zh: 如尼文（卢恩字母）用于铭文和宗教用途，尚未广泛用于日常记录。
writingSystem.en: Runic script was used for inscriptions and religious purposes, not yet widely employed for daily record-keeping.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧和粗放农业为主，波希米亚的铁矿资源使其具备较强的武器和工具制造能力。与罗马帝国有显著的边境贸易。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on pastoralism and extensive farming; Bohemia's iron-ore resources gave strong weapons and tool manufacturing capacity. Significant frontier trade existed with the Roman Empire.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、粗放农业、铁器制造、琥珀和毛皮贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, extensive farming, ironworking, amber and fur trade

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、琥珀、毛皮、牲畜、奴隶、蜂蜜
tradeGoods.en: Ironware, amber, furs, livestock, slaves, honey

name.zh: 无本族铸币（使用罗马银币和实物交换）
name.en: No native coinage (used Roman silver coins and barter)

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以牲畜、耕地、武器和罗马银币衡量。贵族还拥有随从和依附人口。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by livestock, arable land, weapons, and Roman silver coins. Nobles also held retainers and dependents.

economicSystem.zh: 以畜牧农业为基础、铁器制造为特色的部落经济，通过罗马边境贸易获取白银和奢侈品。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on agro-pastoralism with iron manufacturing as a specialty, obtaining silver and luxury goods through Roman frontier trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财库包括罗马银币、黄金饰品、铁器储备和牲畜，存放于王室聚落的强化仓储中。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury included Roman silver coins, gold ornaments, iron stockpiles, and livestock, stored in fortified depots at the royal settlement.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持王室亲卫武装和对罗马防御准备为优先，通过边境贸易和战争劫掠补充收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining the royal retinue and Roman defense readiness, supplementing income through frontier trade and war raiding.

description.zh: 装备长矛（framea）、盾牌和铁剑的自由民战士，以楔形阵突击著称。
description.en: Free warrior-farmers armed with spears (framea), shields, and iron swords, renowned for wedge-formation assaults.

description.zh: 国王精锐随从，装备最好的武器和铠甲，誓死效忠。
description.en: The king's elite retainers, armed with the best weapons and armor, sworn to fight to the death.

description.zh: 轻装骑兵执行侦察、骚扰和快速突袭任务。
description.en: Light cavalry conducting reconnaissance, harassment, and rapid raid missions.

technology.zh: 军事技术结合日耳曼传统武器和从罗马边境获取的铁器技术。楔形阵冲锋、森林伏击和快速机动是主要战术。
technology.en: Military technology combined traditional Germanic weapons with iron techniques obtained from the Roman frontier. Wedge-formation charges, forest ambushes, and rapid mobility were the main tactics.

threats.zh: 与罗马帝国的紧张关系是最大威胁（马科曼尼战争即将爆发），同时面临其他日耳曼部落的竞争和萨尔马提亚人的压力。
threats.en: Tensions with the Roman Empire were the greatest threat (the Marcomannic Wars were approaching); competition from other Germanic tribes and Sarmatian pressure also loomed.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于波希米亚盆地及周边森林丘陵地带，以分散的农牧聚落为主。少量较大聚落兼具贸易和防御功能。
populationDescription.en: Population was spread across the Bohemian Basin and surrounding forested hills, mainly in dispersed agro-pastoral settlements. A few larger settlements served trade and defense functions.

name.zh: 马罗波都斯城（波希米亚中心）

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族战士与随从、自由民战士-农民、半自由佃农、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, noble warriors and retainers, free warrior-farmers, semi-free tenants, slaves.

allies.zh: 与夸迪人和部分日耳曼小部落结盟，保持与萨尔马提亚人的复杂关系。
allies.en: Allied with the Quadi and some smaller Germanic tribes, maintaining complex relations with the Sarmatians.

enemies.zh: 罗马帝国是最大对手，同时与切鲁西人残余和其他日耳曼竞争部落存在摩擦。
enemies.en: The Roman Empire was the greatest adversary; friction also existed with Cherusci remnants and other competing Germanic tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策在罗马边境维持紧张的均势，一方面通过贸易获取资源，另一方面为可能的大规模战争做准备。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy maintained a tense balance on the Roman frontier, acquiring resources through trade while preparing for potential large-scale war.

era.zh: 日耳曼铁器时代晚期
era.en: Late Germanic Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 精良铁器制造、罗马式防御工事借鉴、楔形阵战术、如尼文铭刻、边境贸易网络。
keyInnovations.en: Skilled ironworking, adaptation of Roman-style fortifications, wedge-formation tactics, runic inscriptions, and frontier trade networks.

strengths.zh: 强大的武士传统和动员能力、波希米亚铁矿资源、与罗马的贸易带来的技术接触、凝聚力较强的王国组织。
strengths.en: Strong warrior tradition and mobilization capacity, Bohemian iron-ore resources, technological exposure from Roman trade, and a relatively cohesive kingdom organization.

weaknesses.zh: 经济基础仍以粗放农牧为主、政治依赖个人魅力型国王、面对罗马的技术和组织差距明显。
weaknesses.en: Economic base still relied on extensive agro-pastoralism, politics depended on charismatic kingship, and a clear technology and organizational gap existed vis-à-vis Rome.

outlook.zh: 马科曼尼人即将在未来数十年发动对罗马的大规模入侵（马科曼尼战争，166-180年），短期内军事威胁将达到顶峰。
outlook.en: The Marcomanni were about to launch large-scale invasions of Rome in the coming decades (Marcomannic Wars, 166–180 CE), with their military threat peaking in the short term.

description.zh: 公元100年的马科曼尼人是定居于波希米亚地区的强大日耳曼部落王国，拥有较强的铁器制造能力和军事组织，与罗马帝国维持紧张的边境关系，即将成为罗马最危险的对手之一。
description.en: The Marcomanni in 100 CE were a powerful Germanic tribal kingdom settled in the Bohemian region, possessing strong ironworking capacity and military organization, maintaining tense frontier relations with Rome, and about to become one of Rome's most dangerous adversaries.

name.zh: 阿特拉斯山柏柏尔部落
name.en: Berber Tribes of the Atlas Mountains

name.zh: 阿特拉斯山柏柏尔部落联合

ruler.zh: 阿盖利德（部族联盟首领）

dynasty.zh: 诸柏柏尔部族首领世系

capital.zh: 无统一都城（各部族有各自山堡据点）
capital.en: No unified capital (each tribe had its own mountain stronghold)

governmentForm.zh: 以山区部族联合为基础的松散邦联制，各部族保持高度自治，仅在外部威胁时联合行动。部分沿海和平原部族已深度罗马化。
governmentForm.en: A loose confederation based on mountain tribal unions, with each tribe retaining high autonomy and uniting only against external threats. Some coastal and plains tribes had become deeply Romanized.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族首领、战士氏族、自由牧民与农民、工匠和依附部族组成。年长男性和战争英雄享有较高声望。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised tribal chiefs, warrior clans, free pastoralists and farmers, craftsmen, and dependent tribes. Senior males and war heroes enjoyed high prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族和控制山口关隘的家族构成统治核心。
rulingClass.en: Tribal nobles and families controlling mountain passes formed the ruling core.

succession.zh: 首领位在同一家族内选举产生，部族集会可对不称职的首领进行更替。
succession.en: Leadership was elected within the same family; tribal assemblies could replace incompetent chiefs.

structure.zh: 治理以部族集会（djemaa）为核心决策机构，首领执行集会决议。各部族独立管理内部事务，大型联合行动需多部族首领协商。
structure.en: Governance centered on tribal assemblies (djemaa) as core decision-making bodies, with chiefs executing assembly decisions. Each tribe managed internal affairs independently; large-scale joint actions required multi-tribal chief consultation.

function.zh: 决定战争、结盟、资源分配和纠纷裁决。
function.en: Decided on war, alliances, resource allocation, and dispute adjudication.

function.zh: 在对外战争和抵御入侵时协调各部族军事力量。
function.en: Coordinated tribal military forces during external wars and invasion defense.

function.zh: 维护传统信仰、主持仪式和管理神圣场所。
function.en: Maintained traditional beliefs, conducted ceremonies, and managed sacred sites.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以山谷和绿洲聚落为单位，由家族长老通过集会管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through valley and oasis settlements, managed by family elders via assemblies.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以柏柏尔习惯法（azref）为基础，强调集体责任和赔偿原则。受罗马法影响的地区有混合法律实践。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on Berber customary law (azref), emphasizing collective responsibility and compensation. Areas under Roman influence had hybrid legal practices.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，公共义务以牲畜、谷物和劳役形式缴纳。部分罗马化地区向罗马当局缴纳贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: No unified tax system; public obligations were rendered in livestock, grain, and labor. Some Romanized areas paid tribute to Roman authorities.

religion.zh: 柏柏尔传统信仰（自然崇拜、祖灵信仰，部分受罗马和腓尼基影响）
religion.en: Traditional Berber belief (nature worship, ancestor veneration, partly influenced by Roman and Phoenician religions)

culturalAchievements.zh: 建造了适应山区环境的防御性村庄（ksar），发展出精美的纺织、陶器和银饰工艺，保存了独特的口头史诗传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Built defensive mountain villages (ksar) adapted to the terrain, developed fine textile, pottery, and silver ornament crafts, and preserved distinctive oral epic traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 亚非语系柏柏尔语族
languageFamily.en: Afroasiatic, Berber branch

philosophy.zh: 自然崇拜与部族荣誉传统
philosophy.en: Nature worship and tribal honor tradition

writingSystem.zh: 提非纳格文（Tifinagh），古老的柏柏尔字母系统，用于铭文和标记。
writingSystem.en: Tifinagh, an ancient Berber script used for inscriptions and markings.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以山地畜牧（羊、山羊）和谷地灌溉农业为核心，辅以跨撒哈拉贸易中转和手工业。部分平原地区参与罗马经济体系。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on mountain pastoralism (sheep, goats) and valley irrigated agriculture, supplemented by trans-Saharan trade transit and crafts. Some plains areas participated in the Roman economic system.

mainIndustries.zh: 山地畜牧、谷地农业、纺织、银饰制造、跨撒哈拉贸易中转
mainIndustries.en: Mountain pastoralism, valley agriculture, textiles, silver ornament making, trans-Saharan trade transit

tradeGoods.zh: 羊毛纺织品、橄榄油、牲畜、银饰、谷物、皮革
tradeGoods.en: Wool textiles, olive oil, livestock, silver ornaments, grain, leather

name.zh: 无统一铸币（使用罗马货币和实物交换）
name.en: No unified coinage (used Roman currency and barter)

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以牲畜、耕地、银饰和纺织品储量衡量。山口控制权是额外的权力资源。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by livestock, arable land, silver ornaments, and textile stocks. Control of mountain passes was an additional power resource.

economicSystem.zh: 以山地畜牧和谷地灌溉农业为基础的部族经济，通过控制山口和贸易中转获取额外收入。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on mountain pastoralism and valley irrigation agriculture, supplemented by income from controlling mountain passes and trade transit.

source.zh: 贸易中转与山口通行费

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备以牲畜、谷物、银饰和罗马货币形式分散于各部族首领的山堡中。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves in livestock, grain, silver ornaments, and Roman currency were dispersed among tribal chiefs' mountain strongholds.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持山区防御和部族自治为优先，注重山口控制和贸易中转的收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized mountain defense and tribal autonomy, emphasizing income from pass control and trade transit.

description.zh: 擅长山地作战的柏柏尔步兵，装备标枪、短刀和圆盾，利用地形优势进行伏击和防守。
description.en: Berber infantry skilled in mountain warfare, armed with javelins, short swords, and round shields, using terrain advantage for ambush and defense.

description.zh: 以无鞍轻骑闻名的柏柏尔骑手，擅长快速突袭和骚扰战术。
description.en: Berber riders famed for riding without saddles, skilled in rapid raids and harassment tactics.

description.zh: 专门驻守于关键山口和防御工事的常备武装力量。
description.en: Permanent armed force stationed at key mountain passes and defensive works.

technology.zh: 军事技术结合柏柏尔传统山地战术和从罗马学习的防御技术。轻骑兵和标枪投掷是核心优势。
technology.en: Military technology combined traditional Berber mountain warfare with defensive techniques learned from Rome. Light cavalry and javelin throwing were core strengths.

threats.zh: 罗马帝国的控制压力是最大威胁，部分部族已接受罗马统治，内部凝聚力可能因罗马化而瓦解。
threats.en: Roman imperial control pressure was the greatest threat; some tribes had accepted Roman rule, and internal cohesion might erode through Romanization.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于阿特拉斯山脉各谷地、高原和山麓绿洲，以分散的防御性村庄（ksar）和牧营为主要聚落形态。
populationDescription.en: Population was spread across Atlas Mountain valleys, plateaus, and foothill oases, with dispersed defensive villages (ksar) and pastoral camps as the main settlement pattern.

name.zh: 沃利利（罗马化柏柏尔城市）

socialClasses.zh: 部族首领与贵族、战士氏族、自由牧农民、工匠、依附部族和受保护群体。
socialClasses.en: Tribal chiefs and nobles, warrior clans, free pastoralist-farmers, craftsmen, dependent tribes, and protected groups.

allies.zh: 部分部族与罗马帝国建立了附庸或同盟关系，山区部族之间通过婚姻和贸易保持合作。
allies.en: Some tribes established vassal or allied relations with the Roman Empire; mountain tribes maintained cooperation through marriage and trade.

enemies.zh: 罗马帝国的扩张压力是外部主要威胁，部族之间也存在牧场和山口的争夺。
enemies.en: Roman imperial expansion pressure was the main external threat; inter-tribal disputes over pastures and mountain passes also occurred.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策在罗马化合作与山区独立自主之间摇摆，各部族根据自身利益采取不同立场。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy oscillated between Romanized cooperation and mountain independence, with each tribe adopting different stances based on its interests.

era.zh: 铁器时代（受罗马影响的部族文化）
era.en: Iron Age (tribal culture under Roman influence)

keyInnovations.zh: 山区灌溉系统（foggara）、防御性村庄建筑、无鞍骑术、银器与纺织工艺、提非纳格文。
keyInnovations.en: Mountain irrigation systems (foggara), defensive village architecture, bareback riding, silverwork and textile crafts, Tifinagh script.

strengths.zh: 占据险峻山区地形、骑术和山地战能力出众、控制重要贸易通道、文化韧性强。
strengths.en: Commanded rugged mountain terrain, outstanding riding and mountain warfare capability, control of important trade routes, and strong cultural resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散、部分部族已被罗马化造成内部分裂、经济基础薄弱。
weaknesses.en: Highly fragmented politics, some tribes already Romanized causing internal division, and weak economic base.

outlook.zh: 柏柏尔部族将在山区保持数世纪的半独立状态，文化传统具有很强的延续性，但政治统一仍遥远。
outlook.en: Berber tribes would maintain semi-independence in the mountains for centuries, with strong cultural continuity, though political unity remained distant.

description.zh: 公元100年的阿特拉斯山柏柏尔部落是北非山区的半独立部族联合体，以山地战术和牧农经济著称，在罗马帝国的影响圈中保持着不同程度的自治。
description.en: The Atlas Mountain Berber tribes in 100 CE were a semi-independent tribal confederation in the North African highlands, known for mountain warfare and agro-pastoral economy, maintaining varying degrees of autonomy within the Roman Empire's sphere of influence.

name.zh: 诺克后继铁器农耕部族

capital.zh: 无统一都城（分散的农耕聚落群）
capital.en: No unified capital (dispersed farming settlement clusters)

governmentForm.zh: 以农耕聚落和氏族联合为核心的松散部族治理，继承了诺克文化的冶铁和陶器传统。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal governance based on farming settlements and clan unions, inheriting the Nok culture's ironworking and pottery traditions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由长老、铁匠世家、农民和猎手组成。铁匠拥有特殊社会地位，掌握冶炼技术的家族享有权威。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised elders, blacksmith lineages, farmers, and hunters. Blacksmiths held special social status; families controlling smelting technology wielded authority.

rulingClass.zh: 长老群体和铁匠世家构成权力核心，宗教仪式主持者也具有重要影响力。
rulingClass.en: Elder groups and blacksmith lineages formed the power core; ritual officiant figures also held significant influence.

succession.zh: 领导权在同一氏族内由长老协商继承，铁匠技术在家族内秘密传承。
succession.en: Leadership was passed within the same clan through elder consultation; ironworking techniques were secretly transmitted within families.

structure.zh: 治理以村落长老会议和氏族集会为基础，处理土地、婚姻和争端事务。没有超越村落层级的常设政治结构。
structure.en: Governance relied on village elder councils and clan assemblies, handling land, marriage, and dispute matters. There was no permanent political structure beyond the village level.

function.zh: 裁决村落事务、管理土地分配和组织集体劳动。
function.en: Adjudicated village affairs, managed land allocation, and organized collective labor.

function.zh: 垄断冶铁技术、制造工具和武器，在仪式中扮演重要角色。
function.en: Monopolized ironworking technology, produced tools and weapons, and played important roles in ceremonies.

function.zh: 主持农耕祭祀、雨季仪式和祖灵崇拜活动。
function.en: Conducted agricultural rites, rain-season ceremonies, and ancestor worship.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村落为单位，由长老和家族首领通过集会管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the village level, managed by elders and family heads via assemblies.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口头习惯法为基础，长老裁决为主要争端解决方式，辅以祖灵占卜和赔偿制度。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on oral customary law, with elder adjudication as the primary dispute resolution method, supplemented by ancestral divination and compensation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，公共义务以农产、铁器和劳役形式向长老和首领缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: No unified tax system; public obligations were rendered in farm produce, ironware, and labor to elders and chiefs.

religion.zh: 祖灵崇拜与自然精灵信仰
religion.en: Ancestor worship and nature spirit belief

culturalAchievements.zh: 继承了诺克文化的精美赤陶雕塑传统（虽已衰退），发展出独立的冶铁技术体系，是撒哈拉以南最早的铁器制造中心之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Inherited the Nok culture's fine terracotta sculpture tradition (though declining), developed an independent iron-smelting system, and was one of sub-Saharan Africa's earliest iron-manufacturing centers.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系（贝努埃-刚果亚族可能性较大）
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo (likely Benue-Congo branch)

philosophy.zh: 祖灵信仰与铁匠神秘传统
philosophy.en: Ancestor belief and blacksmith mystical tradition

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，依赖口头传承和象征性陶器/铁器纹饰。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; reliance on oral tradition and symbolic pottery/ironware motifs.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以热带农业（薯类、谷物）和冶铁为核心。铁器工具极大提高了农业生产力。少量长途贸易连接森林与萨赫勒地带。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on tropical agriculture (tubers, grains) and iron smelting. Iron tools greatly enhanced agricultural productivity. Limited long-distance trade linked forests with the Sahel.

mainIndustries.zh: 热带农业、冶铁与铁器制造、陶器、狩猎
mainIndustries.en: Tropical agriculture, iron smelting and ironware manufacturing, pottery, hunting

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器工具、陶器、谷物、棕榈油、兽皮
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, pottery, grain, palm oil, animal hides

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以耕地、铁器工具、牲畜和储粮衡量。铁匠家庭通常较为富裕。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by farmland, iron tools, livestock, and grain stores. Blacksmith families were typically better-off.

economicSystem.zh: 以铁器农业为核心的自给部族经济，通过萨赫勒贸易通道进行有限长途交换。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence tribal economy centered on iron-aided agriculture, with limited long-distance exchange through Sahel trade corridors.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备以谷物、铁器锭和牲畜形式分散于各村落长老处。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves in grain, iron ingots, and livestock were dispersed among village elders.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持冶铁生产、村落防御和农业季节性储备为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining iron production, village defense, and agricultural seasonal reserves.

description.zh: 装备铁质矛头、短刀和木盾的农民战士，战时应召集结。
description.en: Farmer-warriors armed with iron spearheads, short swords, and wooden shields, mustered when called to war.

description.zh: 利用森林和萨瓦纳环境执行侦察、伏击和边境巡逻。
description.en: Using forest and savanna environments to conduct reconnaissance, ambush, and border patrol.

description.zh: 驻守于各聚落的常备防卫力量，保护铁匠作坊和谷仓。
description.en: Permanent defense force stationed at settlements, protecting smithies and granaries.

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁质武器为核心优势，森林伏击和快速机动是主要战术。铁器制造能力赋予其对无铁器邻居的军事优势。
technology.en: Military technology had iron weapons as its core advantage; forest ambush and rapid mobility were the main tactics. Iron manufacturing capacity gave a military edge over neighbors without iron.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族的领土争夺和旱季资源竞争。南方班图扩张的移民压力也在逐步显现。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes' territorial disputes and dry-season resource competition. Southward Bantu expansion migration pressure was also gradually appearing.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于尼日利亚中部高原和贝努埃河谷地，以分散的农耕聚落为主。铁器农业支撑了较高的人口密度。
populationDescription.en: Population was spread across the Nigerian central plateau and Benue River valley, mainly in dispersed farming settlements. Iron-aided agriculture supported relatively higher population density.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与氏族首领、铁匠世家、祭司、自由农民、猎手与依附人口。
socialClasses.en: Elders and clan chiefs, blacksmith lineages, priests, free farmers, hunters, and dependents.

allies.zh: 与邻近农耕和牧放部族通过婚姻和铁器贸易保持合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperative relations with neighboring farming and pastoral tribes through marriage and iron trade.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌人，主要冲突来自争夺耕地和铁矿资源的邻近群体。
enemies.en: No long-term unified enemy; main conflicts came from neighboring groups competing for farmland and iron-ore resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重在维持铁器贸易网络和保护冶铁技术垄断。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on maintaining iron trade networks and protecting the ironworking technology monopoly.

era.zh: 西非铁器时代
era.en: West African Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 独立发展的冶铁技术（竖炉炼铁法）、铁质农具、赤陶雕塑传统（衰退中）、热带农业轮作。
keyInnovations.en: Independently developed iron smelting (shaft-furnace technique), iron farming tools, terracotta sculpture tradition (declining), and tropical agricultural rotation.

strengths.zh: 拥有先进的冶铁技术、较高的农业生产力、文化传统深厚、人口基数较大。
strengths.en: Possessed advanced iron technology, relatively high agricultural productivity, deep cultural traditions, and a larger population base.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散、无文字记录、城市化程度极低、对外贸易有限。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, no written records, very low urbanization, and limited external trade.

outlook.zh: 诺克后继文化将为后来的约鲁巴、伊博等西非文明奠定铁器和农业基础，但短期内仍将保持分散的部族格局。
outlook.en: Nok successor cultures would lay the iron and agricultural foundations for later West African civilizations like the Yoruba and Igbo, but would remain in a dispersed tribal pattern in the short term.

description.zh: 公元100年的诺克文化后继者是分布于尼日利亚中部的铁器农耕部族社会，继承了诺克文化的冶铁传统，以竖炉炼铁和热带农业为经济基础，为后来的西非文明奠定了技术基础。
description.en: The Nok culture successors in 100 CE were iron-working farming tribal societies spread across central Nigeria, inheriting the Nok culture's iron-smelting tradition, with shaft-furnace smelting and tropical agriculture as their economic foundation, laying the technological basis for later West African civilizations.

name.zh: 阿扎尼亚海岸贸易社会

capital.zh: 拉普塔（Rhapta，《航海志》记载的贸易港）
capital.en: Rhapta (trading port recorded in the Periplus)

governmentForm.zh: 以沿海贸易港为核心的松散首领制，各港口聚落由当地贸易家族和长老管理，通过阿拉伯南部商人与更广泛的印度洋贸易网络相连。
governmentForm.en: A loose chieftainship centered on coastal trading ports, with each port settlement managed by local trading families and elders, linked to the broader Indian Ocean trade network through South Arabian merchants.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由港口贸易首领、外来商人（阿拉伯人）、本地工匠、渔民和内陆农牧民构成。贸易活动创造了较明显的贫富分化。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised port trading chiefs, foreign merchants (Arabs), local craftsmen, fishermen, and inland agro-pastoralists. Trade created relatively visible wealth stratification.

rulingClass.zh: 控制象牙和龟甲出口的本地首领和阿拉伯商人代理构成权力阶层。
rulingClass.en: Local chiefs controlling ivory and tortoiseshell exports, along with Arab merchant agents, formed the power elite.

succession.zh: 港口首领位在贸易家族内传承，贸易网络控制权是继承的关键资本。
succession.en: Port chieftainship was inherited within trading families; control of trade networks was the key capital for succession.

structure.zh: 治理以各港口的长老议事会为核心，阿拉伯商人代理在贸易事务中具有重要发言权。没有统一的中央政府。
structure.en: Governance centered on elder councils at each port, with Arab merchant agents having significant say in trade matters. There was no unified central government.

function.zh: 管理贸易活动、确定关税和调解商业纠纷。
function.en: Managed trade activities, set tariffs, and mediated commercial disputes.

function.zh: 协调渔船作业、管理海岸资源和维护航行安全。
function.en: Coordinated fishing operations, managed coastal resources, and maintained navigation safety.

function.zh: 维持与内陆部族的贸易关系，组织象牙和龟甲的收购网络。
function.en: Maintained trade relations with inland tribes, organizing ivory and tortoiseshell procurement networks.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以各港口聚落为独立单元，由贸易家族和长老直接管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated with each port settlement as an independent unit, directly managed by trading families and elders.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以本地习惯法为基础，贸易纠纷参照阿拉伯商业惯例。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on local customary law, with trade disputes settled by reference to Arab commercial conventions.

taxationSystem.zh: 收入来源主要为贸易关税和港口使用费，部分以实物形式征收。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from trade tariffs and port fees, some collected in kind.

religion.zh: 非洲传统信仰（祖灵崇拜与自然精灵），部分受南阿拉伯宗教影响
religion.en: African traditional belief (ancestor worship and nature spirits), partly influenced by South Arabian religion

culturalAchievements.zh: 建立了撒哈拉以南非洲最早的海上贸易港口之一，发展出跨文化的贸易实践和航海技术，被记录于希腊文献《厄立特里亚海航海志》。
culturalAchievements.en: Established some of sub-Saharan Africa's earliest maritime trading ports, developed cross-cultural trade practices and navigation skills, and was recorded in the Greek text Periplus of the Erythraean Sea.

languageFamily.zh: 尼罗-撒哈拉语系或班图语族前身（尚不确定）
languageFamily.en: Nilo-Saharan or proto-Bantu (uncertain)

philosophy.zh: 海洋贸易实用主义与祖灵传统
philosophy.en: Maritime trade pragmatism and ancestral tradition

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统；商业记录可能使用阿拉伯文或希腊文。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system; commercial records possibly used Arabic or Greek script.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以印度洋贸易为核心驱动力，出口象牙、龟甲、犀角和香料，进口罗马玻璃器、印度纺织品和铁器。渔业和内陆农业提供基本生计。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was driven primarily by Indian Ocean trade, exporting ivory, tortoiseshell, rhinoceros horn, and spices, importing Roman glassware, Indian textiles, and ironware. Fishing and inland agriculture provided basic subsistence.

mainIndustries.zh: 象牙贸易、龟甲采集、渔业、内陆农业、航海服务
mainIndustries.en: Ivory trade, tortoiseshell collection, fishing, inland agriculture, maritime services

tradeGoods.zh: 象牙、龟甲、犀角、椰子油、香料、鱼干
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, tortoiseshell, rhinoceros horn, coconut oil, spices, dried fish

name.zh: 无本地铸币（使用罗马和南阿拉伯货币）
name.en: No local coinage (used Roman and South Arabian money)

householdWealth.zh: 港口家庭财富以象牙储备、进口奢侈品、渔船和贸易关系网衡量。内陆家庭以耕地和牲畜为主。
householdWealth.en: Port household wealth was measured by ivory reserves, imported luxury goods, fishing boats, and trade networks. Inland households relied mainly on farmland and livestock.

economicSystem.zh: 以印度洋远洋贸易为驱动的港口贸易经济，通过阿拉伯商人中介连接罗马帝国和印度市场。
economicSystem.en: A port trade economy driven by Indian Ocean long-distance commerce, connected to Roman and Indian markets via Arab merchant intermediaries.

treasuryDescription.zh: 港口财库以象牙储备、罗马银币、进口奢侈品和贸易信用形式存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The port treasury consisted of ivory reserves, Roman silver coins, imported luxury goods, and trade credit.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持贸易网络、港口设施和内陆供应链为优先，重视商业外交和与阿拉伯商人的关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining trade networks, port facilities, and inland supply chains, emphasizing commercial diplomacy and relations with Arab merchants.

description.zh: 保护贸易仓库、港口设施和商队安全的常备武装。
description.en: Permanent armed force protecting trade warehouses, port facilities, and caravan security.

description.zh: 熟悉海岸水域的渔民战时组成海上防卫力量。
description.en: Fishermen familiar with coastal waters forming a maritime defense force in wartime.

description.zh: 内陆盟友部族提供的步兵力量，保护贸易通道和内陆据点。
description.en: Infantry provided by inland allied tribes, protecting trade routes and inland outposts.

technology.zh: 军事技术以轻装步兵武器和海岸防御为主，通过贸易获取部分铁器武器。航海技术用于海岸巡逻。
technology.en: Military technology focused on light infantry weapons and coastal defense, with some iron weapons acquired through trade. Navigation skills were used for coastal patrol.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自海盗袭击和内陆部族对贸易通道的争夺。阿拉伯商人势力的过度扩张也是潜在风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from pirate attacks and inland tribal disputes over trade routes. Excessive expansion of Arab merchant influence was also a potential risk.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于东非沿海港口聚落和河口地区，内陆腹地人口稀疏。港口是人口和财富集中地。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in East African coastal port settlements and estuary areas, with sparse inland hinterland population. Ports were centers of population and wealth.

socialClasses.zh: 港口贸易首领与大商人、阿拉伯商人代理、本地工匠与渔民、内陆农牧供应者。
socialClasses.en: Port trading chiefs and major merchants, Arab merchant agents, local craftsmen and fishermen, inland agro-pastoral suppliers.

allies.zh: 与南阿拉伯商人建立了密切的商业伙伴关系，与内陆部族保持象牙和资源供应联系。
allies.en: Established close commercial partnerships with South Arabian merchants and maintained ivory and resource supply links with inland tribes.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌人，主要冲突来自海盗、贸易竞争和内陆资源争夺。
enemies.en: No long-term unified enemy; main conflicts came from piracy, trade competition, and inland resource disputes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持印度洋贸易网络、保障象牙供应链和平衡阿拉伯商人影响力为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining the Indian Ocean trade network, securing ivory supply chains, and balancing Arab merchant influence.

era.zh: 铁器时代海洋贸易文化
era.en: Iron Age maritime trading culture

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟与帆船航海技术、港口建设、象牙加工、跨文化贸易实践、季风航行知识。
keyInnovations.en: Dugout canoe and sailing navigation, port construction, ivory processing, cross-cultural trade practices, and monsoon navigation knowledge.

strengths.zh: 占据印度洋西岸贸易关键节点、象牙和龟甲资源丰富、航海和贸易经验丰富。
strengths.en: Occupied key nodes on the western Indian Ocean trade route, possessed rich ivory and tortoiseshell resources, and had extensive navigation and trade experience.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、军事力量薄弱、高度依赖外来商人、内陆控制有限。
weaknesses.en: Fragmented politics, weak military capacity, heavy dependence on foreign merchants, and limited inland control.

outlook.zh: 阿扎尼亚海岸贸易将在未来数世纪持续发展，最终演变为斯瓦希里海岸城邦文明的前身。
outlook.en: Azanian coastal trade would continue developing over coming centuries, eventually evolving into the precursor of the Swahili Coast city-state civilization.

description.zh: 公元100年的阿扎尼亚是东非沿海的海上贸易社会，以拉普塔港为中心，通过阿拉伯商人中介参与罗马帝国和印度洋的象牙与龟甲贸易，是后来斯瓦希里文明的前身。
description.en: Azania in 100 CE was a maritime trading society on the East African coast centered on the port of Rhapta, participating in Roman and Indian Ocean ivory and tortoiseshell trade via Arab merchant intermediaries, serving as the precursor to the later Swahili civilization.

name.zh: 班图铁器农耕扩张社会

capital.zh: 无统一都城（众多分散的农耕前沿聚落）
capital.en: No unified capital (numerous dispersed agricultural frontier settlements)

governmentForm.zh: 以村落和氏族为核心的分散部族治理。各扩张前沿的聚落群独立运作，通过语言和文化纽带保持松散联系。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal governance based on villages and clans. Settlement clusters on each expansion frontier operated independently, maintaining loose ties through linguistic and cultural bonds.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由村落首领、铁匠、长老、自由农民和新定居者组成。铁匠在开拓新领地中具有核心地位。年龄等级制在部分群体中有所发展。
socialStructure.en: Society comprised village chiefs, blacksmiths, elders, free farmers, and new settlers. Blacksmiths held a central position in pioneering new territories. Age-grade systems developed in some groups.

rulingClass.zh: 氏族长老和首批定居的创始家族享有更高地位和更大的土地分配权。
rulingClass.en: Clan elders and founding families of initial settlement enjoyed higher status and greater land allocation rights.

succession.zh: 首领权在创始家族内传承，分裂后的新聚落由迁出的家族自行建立领导结构。
succession.en: Chieftainship was inherited within founding families; new settlements formed after splits established their own leadership structures from the departing family.

structure.zh: 治理以村落会议和氏族协商为基础。当聚落过大时发生分裂，子群体迁徙建立新聚落。没有超村落层级的稳定政治结构。
structure.en: Governance relied on village meetings and clan consultations. When settlements grew too large, fission occurred and sub-groups migrated to establish new settlements. No stable political structure existed above the village level.

function.zh: 管理土地分配、解决纠纷和组织集体劳动。
function.en: Managed land allocation, resolved disputes, and organized collective labor.

function.zh: 垄断冶铁技术、制造工具并在仪式中扮演重要角色。铁匠是新领地开拓的关键力量。
function.en: Monopolized ironworking, manufactured tools, and played important ceremonial roles. Blacksmiths were crucial to pioneering new territories.

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀、雨季仪式和新领地祝圣。
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, rain-season ceremonies, and consecration of new territories.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以各农耕前沿的聚落群为单位，由创始家族和长老管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through settlement clusters on each agricultural frontier, managed by founding families and elders.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以氏族习惯法和长老裁决为基础。新领地的法律常由迁入群体从母聚落带来并加以调整。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on clan customary law and elder adjudication. Legal norms in new territories were often carried from mother settlements and adapted.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，公共义务以集体农耕劳动、铁器和农产供献形式履行。
taxationSystem.en: No unified tax system; public obligations were fulfilled through collective farming labor, ironware, and agricultural produce offerings.

religion.zh: 班图祖灵崇拜与自然精灵信仰
religion.en: Bantu ancestor worship and nature spirit belief

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出高效的热带刀耕火种和铁器农业体系，创造了丰富的口头传统、音乐和陶器艺术。班图语言的传播是人类历史上最大规模的语言扩散事件之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed efficient tropical slash-and-burn and iron-aided farming systems, created rich oral traditions, music, and pottery art. The spread of Bantu languages was one of the largest linguistic dispersals in human history.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系班图语族
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Bantu branch

philosophy.zh: 祖灵信仰与铁匠神圣传统
philosophy.en: Ancestor belief and sacred blacksmith tradition

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，依赖口头史诗、歌谣和象征符号传承文化。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; culture was transmitted through oral epics, songs, and symbolic motifs.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以刀耕火种农业和冶铁为双核心。新开拓的肥沃土地短期内生产力较高，但随着土壤耗竭需要继续迁移。少量区域间贸易连接不同聚落群。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was dual-centered on slash-and-burn agriculture and iron smelting. Newly pioneered fertile land provided higher short-term productivity, but soil exhaustion necessitated continued migration. Limited inter-regional trade linked different settlement clusters.

mainIndustries.zh: 刀耕火种农业、冶铁与铁器制造、畜牧（部分地区）、狩猎采集
mainIndustries.en: Slash-and-burn agriculture, iron smelting and ironware manufacturing, pastoralism (in some areas), hunting and gathering

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器工具、陶器、谷物、棕榈产品、兽皮
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, pottery, grain, palm products, animal hides

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以耕地面积、铁器工具、牲畜和家族劳动力衡量。新开拓的肥沃土地是最重要的资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by farmland area, iron tools, livestock, and family labor. Newly pioneered fertile land was the most important asset.

economicSystem.zh: 以铁器辅助刀耕火种为核心的前沿农耕经济，通过持续迁移获取新的肥沃土地。
economicSystem.en: A frontier farming economy centered on iron-aided slash-and-burn cultivation, obtaining new fertile land through continuous migration.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备以谷物、铁器锭和种子形式分散于各前沿聚落。流动性是核心特征。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves in grain, iron ingots, and seeds were dispersed across frontier settlements. Mobility was a core characteristic.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以支持冶铁生产和新领地开拓为优先。分裂与迁移模式意味着每个新聚落需重建自身财政基础。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized supporting iron production and new territory pioneering. The fission-migration pattern meant each new settlement needed to rebuild its own fiscal base.

description.zh: 装备铁质矛头和短刀的农民战士，在新领地拓殖中兼任防卫和清理角色。
description.en: Farmer-warriors armed with iron spearheads and short swords, serving both defense and clearing roles in new territory colonization.

description.zh: 利用对森林和草原的熟悉进行新领地勘探、侦察和前沿防御。
description.en: Using forest and savanna knowledge for new territory exploration, reconnaissance, and frontier defense.

description.zh: 驻守于各聚落的常备防卫力量，保护农田和铁匠作坊。
description.en: Permanent defense force stationed at settlements, protecting farmland and smithies.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高指挥官（各聚落群自行组织）
commanderInChief.en: No unified supreme commander (each settlement cluster organized independently)

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁质武器为核心优势，赋予对无铁器狩猎采集民的明显优势。刀耕火种技术也有军事应用（焚烧清理）。
technology.en: Military technology had iron weapons as its core advantage, giving a clear edge over iron-less hunter-gatherer peoples. Slash-and-burn techniques also had military applications (fire-clearing).

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自新领地上的原住民抵抗（科伊桑猎采民、俾格米人等）和不同班图群体间的前沿竞争。热带疾病和环境适应问题也是重大挑战。
threats.en: Main threats came from indigenous resistance in new territories (Khoisan hunter-gatherers, Pygmies, etc.) and frontier competition among different Bantu groups. Tropical diseases and environmental adaptation were also major challenges.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于中非和东非的多条扩张前沿，包括热带雨林边缘、湖区草原和东非高地。聚落密度在新开拓区域较低，在已定居区域逐步增加。
populationDescription.en: Population was widely distributed across multiple expansion frontiers in central and east Africa, including tropical forest margins, lake-region savannas, and East African highlands. Settlement density was lower in newly pioneered areas and gradually increased in established zones.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族首领与创始家族、铁匠世家、祭司与灵媒、自由农民-拓殖者、新加入的依附群体。
socialClasses.en: Clan chiefs and founding families, blacksmith lineages, priests and spirit mediums, free farmer-colonists, and newly incorporated dependent groups.

allies.zh: 同一语言群体的不同聚落间通过婚姻和贸易保持合作，部分班图群体与当地狩猎采集民建立了共生关系。
allies.en: Different settlements of the same linguistic group maintained cooperation through marriage and trade; some Bantu groups established symbiotic relations with local hunter-gatherers.

enemies.zh: 与扩张路线上的原住民群体（科伊桑人、俾格米人、库什特语族群）存在不同程度的冲突，不同班图群体间也可能发生资源竞争。
enemies.en: Varying degrees of conflict existed with indigenous peoples on expansion routes (Khoisan, Pygmies, Cushitic groups); resource competition among different Bantu groups was also possible.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以持续扩张和新领地获取为核心动力，通过同化、驱逐或共生方式与原住民互动。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations were driven by continued expansion and new territory acquisition, interacting with indigenous peoples through assimilation, displacement, or symbiosis.

era.zh: 非洲铁器时代（班图扩张阶段）
era.en: African Iron Age (Bantu Expansion phase)

keyInnovations.zh: 高效冶铁技术（多种炉型）、铁质农具（锄头、斧头）、刀耕火种高效农业体系、香蕉与薯类栽培、陶器制造。
keyInnovations.en: Efficient iron smelting (multiple furnace types), iron farming tools (hoes, axes), efficient slash-and-burn agriculture, banana and tuber cultivation, and pottery manufacturing.

strengths.zh: 铁器技术赋予的军事和农业优势、极强的人口增长和领土扩张能力、适应多种热带环境的经验、丰富的文化传统。
strengths.en: Military and agricultural advantage from iron technology, extremely strong population growth and territorial expansion capacity, experience adapting to diverse tropical environments, and rich cultural traditions.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散、无文字记录、依赖刀耕火种导致的土壤耗竭问题、热带疾病风险。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, no written records, soil-exhaustion issues from slash-and-burn dependence, and tropical disease risks.

outlook.zh: 班图扩张将在未来数世纪继续向南非和马达加斯加推进，最终占据撒哈拉以南非洲的大部分地区，为后来的大津巴布韦等国家奠定基础。
outlook.en: Bantu expansion would continue pushing toward southern Africa and Madagascar over coming centuries, eventually occupying most of sub-Saharan Africa and laying the foundation for later states such as Great Zimbabwe.

description.zh: 公元100年的班图扩张前沿是人类历史上最大规模的农业民族迁徙运动之一，铁器农耕的班图语民族正从西非-中非核心区向东非和南非持续扩散，改变着整个撒哈拉以南非洲的人口和文化格局。
description.en: The Bantu expansion frontier in 100 CE was one of the largest agricultural migration movements in human history, with iron-working Bantu-speaking peoples continuously spreading from their West/Central African homeland toward East and Southern Africa, reshaping the demographic and cultural landscape of the entire sub-Saharan continent.

name.zh: 罗马边疆外自由达契亚部落
name.en: Free Dacian Tribes Beyond the Roman Frontier

ruler.zh: 科斯托博奇等部落首领

dynasty.zh: 诸达契亚部落世系（后德凯巴鲁斯时期）

capital.zh: 无统一都城（分散于喀尔巴阡山区外围）
capital.en: No unified capital (dispersed beyond the Carpathian arc)

governmentForm.zh: 松散的部落联盟制，各部落自治，仅在面对外敌时临时联合。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal confederation with self-governing tribes, uniting temporarily only against external threats.

socialStructure.zh: 塔拉博斯特（戴帽贵族）-科莫伊（自由民）-附庸与奴隶，祭司在社会中仍保有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Tarabostes (cap-wearing nobles) – comati (freemen) – dependents and slaves; the priestly class still held significant social importance.

rulingClass.zh: 部落贵族与祭司共同治理，战时由军事声望最高的首领主导联合行动。
rulingClass.en: Tribal nobles and priests governed jointly; in wartime, the chief with the highest military reputation led joint actions.

succession.zh: 首领职位在贵族家族内传承，受军功和祭司认可影响。
succession.en: Chieftainship passed within noble families, influenced by military achievements and priestly approval.

structure.zh: 罗马征服达契亚核心地区后，边疆外的自由达契亚部落保持分散自治状态，通过首领会议和季节性集会协调事务。
structure.en: After Rome conquered the Dacian heartland, free Dacian tribes beyond the frontier maintained dispersed autonomy, coordinating affairs through chieftains' councils and seasonal assemblies.

function.zh: 协调部落间关系、对外战争与迁徙决策。
function.en: Coordinated inter-tribal relations, external warfare, and migration decisions.

function.zh: 延续扎尔莫克西斯信仰、主持仪式、提供占卜与裁决。
function.en: Maintained the Zalmoxis faith, conducted rituals, and provided divination and adjudication.

function.zh: 组织对罗马边境的警戒和对其他蛮族入侵的防御。
function.en: Organized vigilance along the Roman frontier and defense against other barbarian incursions.

localAdmin.zh: 各部落由本部首领和长老管理，山寨（达瓦）为基本行政单元。
localAdmin.en: Each tribe was managed by its own chiefs and elders, with hill-forts (dava) as the basic administrative units.

legalSystem.zh: 达契亚习惯法，祭司参与裁决，神谕和誓言具有法律效力。
legalSystem.en: Dacian customary law, with priestly adjudication; oracles and oaths had legal force.

taxationSystem.zh: 以谷物、牲畜和矿产实物贡赋为主，无统一货币税制。
taxationSystem.en: In-kind tribute of grain, livestock, and minerals; no unified monetary taxation.

religion.zh: 扎尔莫克西斯崇拜与达契亚传统多神信仰
religion.en: Zalmoxis worship and traditional Dacian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 延续达契亚银器与金饰工艺、山寨建筑传统，在罗马边疆互动中吸收部分罗马物质文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Continued Dacian silver and gold craftsmanship and hill-fort building traditions, absorbing some Roman material culture through frontier interaction.

languageFamily.zh: 达契亚语（色雷斯语支，印欧语系）
languageFamily.en: Dacian (Thracian branch, Indo-European family)

philosophy.zh: 扎尔莫克西斯教义：灵魂不灭、蔑视死亡与道德净化
philosophy.en: Zalmoxian doctrine: immortality of the soul, contempt for death, and moral purification

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，少量借用拉丁或希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: No native script; limited borrowing of Latin or Greek letters.

gdpDescription.zh: 以喀尔巴阡外围的农牧业为基础，辅以矿产开采和与罗马边境走私贸易。经济水平低于被罗马征服的达契亚核心地区。
gdpDescription.en: Based on agriculture and pastoralism in the outer Carpathian region, supplemented by mining and smuggling trade with the Roman frontier. Economic level was lower than the Roman-conquered Dacian heartland.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、山地畜牧、铁矿与金银矿开采、皮毛加工
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, highland pastoralism, iron and gold-silver mining, and fur processing

tradeGoods.zh: 金银矿石、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、毛皮、牲畜；从罗马边境获取陶器与金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Gold and silver ores, honey, beeswax, furs, and livestock; obtained pottery and metalware from the Roman frontier

name.zh: 实物交换与仿罗马银币
name.en: Barter and imitation Roman silver coins

householdWealth.zh: 贵族家庭积累金银饰品与牲畜；普通自由民拥有耕地和小型畜群。
householdWealth.en: Noble households accumulated gold and silver ornaments and livestock; ordinary freemen owned farmland and small herds.

economicSystem.zh: 以部落再分配和实物交换为主的混合经济，罗马边境贸易提供了有限的货币化渠道。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy dominated by tribal redistribution and barter, with the Roman frontier trade providing limited monetization.

treasuryDescription.zh: 部落储备以金银矿产和实物为主，分散保存于各山寨要塞中。
treasuryDescription.en: Tribal reserves consisted mainly of gold-silver minerals and goods in kind, dispersed across hill-forts.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以实物贡赋和矿产开采为财政基础，战利品按军功分配，祭司阶层享有免赋特权。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was based on in-kind tribute and mining, with war spoils distributed by merit; the priestly class enjoyed tribute exemptions.

description.zh: 装备曲刃短剑（西卡）、长矛和盾牌的部落战士，擅长山地防御战。
description.en: Tribal warriors armed with the curved sica, spears, and shields, skilled in mountain defensive warfare.

description.zh: 以标枪和长矛为武器的轻骑兵，用于侦察、袭扰和追击。
description.en: Light cavalry armed with javelins and lances, used for reconnaissance, raiding, and pursuit.

description.zh: 驻守各达瓦山寨的常备防卫力量。
description.en: Permanent garrison force stationed at the dava hill-forts.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅（各部落首领分别指挥）
commanderInChief.en: No unified supreme commander (each tribal chief commanded independently)

technology.zh: 延续达契亚铁器锻造传统，曲刃短剑（西卡）为标志性武器，山地防御经验丰富，但整体军事技术逊于罗马军团。
technology.en: Continued Dacian ironworking traditions with the curved sica as the signature weapon; extensive mountain-defense experience, but overall military technology was inferior to Roman legions.

threats.zh: 罗马帝国的边境压力和潜在扩张是最大威胁，同时面临萨尔马提亚游牧骑兵的袭扰和其他日耳曼部落的竞争。
threats.en: Roman frontier pressure and potential expansion were the greatest threats, alongside Sarmatian nomadic cavalry raids and competition from other Germanic tribes.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

populationDescription.zh: 约五十万人口分布于罗马达契亚省边疆以外的喀尔巴阡山区外围和摩尔达维亚平原，包括科斯托博奇人、卡尔皮人等多个达契亚部落。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000 people were distributed beyond the Roman Dacian provincial frontier across the outer Carpathian region and Moldavian plains, including Costoboci, Carpi, and other Dacian tribes.

socialClasses.zh: 部落贵族（塔拉博斯特）、祭司、自由民（科莫伊）、依附民、战俘奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Tribal nobles (tarabostes), priests, freemen (comati), dependents, and war-captive slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分萨尔马提亚部落和日耳曼部落保持临时联盟，偶尔与罗马达契亚省进行边境贸易。
