allies.en: Maintained temporary alliances with some Sarmatian and Germanic tribes, and occasionally engaged in frontier trade with Roman Dacia.

enemies.zh: 罗马帝国（潜在的征服威胁）、萨尔马提亚罗克索拉尼人（竞争草场）、其他日耳曼部落（领土争夺）。
enemies.en: Roman Empire (potential conquest threat), Sarmatian Roxolani (pasture competition), and other Germanic tribes (territorial rivalry).

foreignPolicy.zh: 在罗马边疆保持警惕和灵活平衡，避免直接对抗罗马主力，同时通过联盟和贸易获取生存空间。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained vigilance and flexible balancing along the Roman frontier, avoiding direct confrontation with Roman main forces while securing survival space through alliances and trade.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期达契亚部落文明
era.en: Late Iron Age Dacian tribal civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 曲刃短剑锻造、金银矿冶炼、达瓦山寨建筑、部分罗马技术借鉴（陶器、金属加工）。
keyInnovations.en: Curved sica forging, gold-silver ore smelting, dava hill-fort architecture, and partial adoption of Roman techniques (pottery, metalworking).

strengths.zh: 矿产资源、山地防御优势、达契亚战士传统、扎尔莫克西斯信仰的凝聚力。
strengths.en: Mineral resources, mountain-defense advantage, Dacian warrior traditions, and the cohesion of the Zalmoxis faith.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂、罗马军事压力、缺乏文字和官僚体系、人口相对稀少。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, Roman military pressure, lack of writing and bureaucracy, and relatively sparse population.

outlook.zh: 自由达契亚人将在未来几个世纪持续抵抗罗马扩张，卡尔皮人等部落将在3世纪对罗马边疆构成严重威胁，最终在民族大迁徙中融入其他民族。
outlook.en: Free Dacians would continue resisting Roman expansion in coming centuries; tribes like the Carpi would pose serious threats to the Roman frontier in the 3rd century, eventually merging into other peoples during the Migration Period.

description.zh: 公元100年的自由达契亚人是罗马帝国达契亚省边疆以外的盖塔-达契亚部落群，包括科斯托博奇、卡尔皮等部落，他们在喀尔巴阡山区外围和摩尔达维亚平原保持独立，延续着达契亚的战士传统和扎尔莫克西斯信仰。
description.en: The Free Dacians in 100 CE were Geto-Dacian tribal groups beyond the Roman Dacian provincial frontier, including the Costoboci, Carpi, and others, who maintained independence in the outer Carpathian region and Moldavian plains, continuing Dacian warrior traditions and the Zalmoxis faith.

governmentForm.zh: 以“大人物”制度为核心的分散部族社会。各山谷独立运作，领导者通过个人威望和财富再分配获得地位。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed tribal society centered on the 'Big Man' system. Each valley operated independently, with leaders gaining status through personal prestige and wealth redistribution.

socialStructure.zh: 以氏族和山谷为基本单位。“大人物”通过组织盛宴和猪群交换获得威望。社会相对平等，但猪群规模和仪式知识决定地位。
socialStructure.en: Organized by clan and valley. Big Men gained prestige through organizing feasts and pig exchanges. Relatively egalitarian, but pig herd size and ritual knowledge determined status.

rulingClass.zh: “大人物”和仪式长老。
rulingClass.en: Big Men and ritual elders.

succession.zh: 不世袭，须通过个人能力获得地位。
succession.en: Not hereditary; status had to be earned through personal ability.

structure.zh: 没有中央政权，各山谷独立运行。猪群交换仪式是跨部族政治协调的核心。
structure.en: No central authority; each valley operated independently. Pig exchange ceremonies were the core of cross-tribal political coordination.

function.zh: 处理纠纷和部族间关系。
function.en: Handled disputes and inter-tribal relations.

function.zh: 管理园艺周期和祖先祭祀。
function.en: Managed horticultural cycles and ancestor rituals.

localAdmin.zh: 各山谷由地方“大人物”和长老自治。
localAdmin.en: Each valley was self-governed by local Big Men and elders.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法，纠纷通过赔偿或仪式性战争解决。
legalSystem.en: Customary law; disputes resolved through compensation or ritual warfare.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，以互惠义务为基础。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation; based on reciprocal obligations.

religion.zh: 祖先崇拜与万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Ancestor worship and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 高地园艺系统进一步完善，甘薯尚未传入前芋头和山药是主要作物。文化和语言多样性极为丰富，不同山谷间保持着数百种独立语言。
culturalAchievements.en: Highland horticultural systems further improved; taro and yam were the main crops before sweet potato's later arrival. Cultural and linguistic diversity was extremely rich, with hundreds of independent languages maintained across different valleys.

languageFamily.zh: 跨新几内亚语系为主
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Trans-New Guinea languages

philosophy.zh: 以祖先灵力、自然精灵和互惠义务为核心的宇宙观
philosophy.en: A cosmology centered on ancestral power, nature spirits, and reciprocal obligations

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统
writingSystem.en: No writing system

gdpDescription.zh: 以沼泽排水园艺农业和猪群饲养为基础。猪群是核心财富形式和社会交换的媒介。
gdpDescription.en: Based on swamp drainage horticulture and pig husbandry. Pig herds were the core wealth and medium of social exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 园艺农业、猪群饲养、石器制造、盐业、羽毛和贝壳加工
mainIndustries.en: Horticultural farming, pig husbandry, stone tool making, salt production, feather and shell working

tradeGoods.zh: 猪、芋头、盐块、石斧、鸟羽装饰、贝壳
tradeGoods.en: Pigs, taro, salt blocks, stone axes, bird feather ornaments, shells

householdWealth.zh: 以猪群数量和园艺地面积为主要衡量标准。
householdWealth.en: Measured mainly by pig herd size and garden plot area.

economicSystem.zh: 以猪群交换和互惠义务为核心的自给农业经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence agricultural economy centered on pig exchange and reciprocal obligations.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以猪群、贝壳和盐块形式储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as pig herds, shells, and salt blocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持猪群交换网络和仪式义务为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining pig exchange networks and ritual obligations.

description.zh: 各氏族的勇士。
description.en: Clan warriors.

description.zh: 冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during conflict.

technology.zh: 石制和木制武器（石斧、木矛、弓箭），仪式性战争为主。
technology.en: Stone and wood weapons (stone axes, wooden spears, bows); ritual warfare predominated.

threats.zh: 山谷间的部族冲突。
threats.en: Inter-valley tribal conflicts.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

populationDescription.zh: 约三十万人分布于新几内亚高地各山谷，人口因农业生产力提高而稳定增长。
populationDescription.en: About 300,000 people distributed across New Guinea highland valleys, with steady growth from improving agricultural productivity.

socialClasses.zh: “大人物”、仪式长老、普通农民。
socialClasses.en: Big Men, ritual elders, ordinary farmers.

allies.zh: 通过猪群交换仪式与相邻山谷保持联盟。
allies.en: Maintained alliances with neighboring valleys through pig exchange ceremonies.

enemies.zh: 与竞争山谷的部族间存在周期性仪式战争。
enemies.en: Periodic ritual warfare with competing valley tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持猪群交换网络和领土完整为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining pig exchange networks and territorial integrity.

era.zh: 新几内亚高地新石器农业时代
era.en: New Guinea Highland Neolithic Agricultural Era

keyInnovations.zh: 沼泽排水技术完善、芋头栽培集约化、石斧制造、猪群管理体系成熟。
keyInnovations.en: Refined swamp drainage, intensified taro cultivation, stone axe manufacturing, and mature pig management systems.

strengths.zh: 最古老的独立农业传统之一，高效的高地园艺，密集的社会交换网络。
strengths.en: One of the oldest independent agricultural traditions, efficient highland horticulture, and dense social exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 极度政治分散，无金属和文字，与外部世界基本隔绝。
weaknesses.en: Extremely politically fragmented, no metals or writing, and essentially isolated from the outside world.

outlook.zh: 高地社会将在隔离中持续稳定发展，直到近现代。
outlook.en: Highland society would continue developing stably in isolation until the modern era.

description.zh: 巴布亚高地农耕社会是公元100年新几内亚高地以沼泽排水农业和“大人物”制度为特征的部族社会，拥有约三十万人口。
description.en: The Papuan Highland Farmers were a tribal society of about 300,000 in the New Guinea highlands around 100 CE, characterized by swamp drainage agriculture and the Big Man system.

governmentForm.zh: 以汤加和萨摩亚为核心、正在向东太平洋扩张的波利尼西亚酋邦体系。世袭酋长通过玛纳和塔布体系维持权威。
governmentForm.en: A Polynesian chiefdom system centered on Tonga and Samoa, expanding into the eastern Pacific. Hereditary chiefs maintained authority through mana and tabu systems.

socialStructure.zh: 严格的等级社会。世袭酋长家族（阿利基）占据顶层，航海家和仪式专家地位崇高，平民从事农渔业，最底层为仆从。
socialStructure.en: A strictly stratified society. Hereditary chiefly families (ariki) at the top, navigators and ritual specialists held high status, commoners worked agriculture and fishing, and servants were at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭酋长家族。
rulingClass.en: Hereditary chiefly families.

succession.zh: 长子继承制，以玛纳血统传承为依据。
succession.en: Primogeniture, based on mana lineage.

structure.zh: 汤加已形成复杂的多层酋邦，图伊汤加（汤加之王）的地位正在上升。萨摩亚的法诺（酋长会议）制度成熟。新殖民的东部岛屿正在形成独立酋邦。
structure.en: Tonga had formed a complex multi-tiered chiefdom, with the Tu'i Tonga (King of Tonga) gaining prominence. Samoa's fono (chiefly council) was mature. Newly colonized eastern islands were forming independent chiefdoms.

function.zh: 处理岛屿事务、贡赋和对外关系。
function.en: Handled island affairs, tribute, and external relations.

function.zh: 维护航海传统和宗教仪式。
function.en: Maintained navigation traditions and religious ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 各岛由酋长家族管理，下设次级酋长。
localAdmin.en: Each island managed by chiefly families, with sub-chiefs below.

legalSystem.zh: 以塔布体系为核心的法律规范。
legalSystem.en: Legal norms centered on the tabu system.

taxationSystem.zh: 以初穂献纳和集体劳役为基础。
taxationSystem.en: Based on first-fruit offerings and collective labor.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神信仰（唐格阿洛亚、图等诸神）
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism (Tangaloa, Tu, and other deities)

culturalAchievements.zh: 波利尼西亚航海术达到巅峰水平，航海家能够跨越数千公里的开阔海域发现和定居新岛屿。口传文学（谱系吟诵、创世神话）日益丰富。树皮布、木雕和纹身艺术高度发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Polynesian navigation reached its peak, with navigators able to discover and settle new islands across thousands of kilometers of open ocean. Oral literature (genealogical chants, creation myths) grew richer. Bark cloth, woodcarving, and tattooing art were highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 波利尼西亚语族（南岛语系大洋洲语族）
languageFamily.en: Polynesian languages (Austronesian, Oceanic branch)

philosophy.zh: 以玛纳、塔布和阿图阿为核心的波利尼西亚宇宙观
philosophy.en: A Polynesian cosmology centered on mana, tabu, and atua

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口传谱系和航海歌谣传递知识
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge transmitted through oral genealogies and navigation chants

gdpDescription.zh: 以园艺农业（芋头、面包果、椰子）、渔业和岛屿间贸易为核心。酋长主导的再分配经济。新殖民岛屿的开发正在扩大经济总量。
gdpDescription.en: Centered on horticulture (taro, breadfruit, coconut), fishing, and inter-island trade. Chief-directed redistribution economy. Development of newly colonized islands was expanding the economic total.

mainIndustries.zh: 园艺农业、渔业、独木舟建造、树皮布制作、珍珠贝和贝壳加工
mainIndustries.en: Horticultural farming, fishing, canoe building, bark cloth making, pearl shell and shell working

tradeGoods.zh: 芋头、鱼干、贝壳饰品、树皮布、独木舟、羽毛装饰
tradeGoods.en: Taro, dried fish, shell ornaments, bark cloth, canoes, feather ornaments

name.zh: 实物交换与酋长再分配

householdWealth.zh: 以独木舟、猪群和园艺地为衡量标准。酋长家族控制大部分资源。
householdWealth.en: Measured by canoes, pig herds, and garden plots. Chiefly families controlled most resources.

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配为核心的等级化岛屿经济。
economicSystem.en: A hierarchical island economy centered on chiefly redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以贝壳、树皮布和食物储备形式保存。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as shells, bark cloth, and food reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以支持航海远征和酋长权威为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Voyaging expeditions and chiefly authority took priority.

description.zh: 酋长的常备武装。
description.en: The chief's standing armed force.

description.zh: 冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during conflict.

technology.zh: 木制和石制武器（棍棒、矛、投石索），精于独木舟海战。
technology.en: Wood and stone weapons (clubs, spears, slings), skilled at canoe naval warfare.

threats.zh: 岛屿间的酋邦竞争和新殖民地的防御需求。
threats.en: Inter-island chiefdom competition and defense needs of new colonies.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 约五万五千人分布于西波利尼西亚（汤加、萨摩亚）和新近殖民的东波利尼西亚岛屿。人口在新岛屿的开发中快速增长。
populationDescription.en: About 55,000 people across western Polynesia (Tonga, Samoa) and newly colonized eastern Polynesian islands. Population was growing rapidly through new island development.

socialClasses.zh: 世袭酋长、次级酋长、航海家、仪式专家、平民、仆从。
socialClasses.en: Hereditary chiefs, sub-chiefs, navigators, ritual specialists, commoners, servants.

allies.zh: 汤加与萨摩亚保持紧密联盟，新殖民岛屿与母岛保持联系。
allies.en: Tonga and Samoa maintained close alliance; newly colonized islands maintained ties with mother islands.

enemies.zh: 岛屿间的酋邦领土竞争。
enemies.en: Territorial competition among island chiefdoms.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以继续东向太平洋扩张和维持岛屿间联盟网络为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on continuing eastward Pacific expansion and maintaining inter-island alliance networks.

era.zh: 波利尼西亚远航扩张时代
era.en: Polynesian Voyaging Expansion Era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型远洋双体独木舟、星象和洋流精密导航、园艺集约化、树皮布制造、鱼钩制造技术。
keyInnovations.en: Large oceanic double-hulled canoes, precise stellar and current navigation, agricultural intensification, bark cloth manufacturing, and fishhook technology.

strengths.zh: 人类历史上最卓越的远洋航海能力，成熟的酋邦社会组织，丰富的海洋资源，持续扩张的领土。
strengths.en: The most outstanding oceanic navigation in human history, mature chiefdom organization, rich marine resources, and continually expanding territory.

weaknesses.zh: 人口分散于广大海域，各岛屿间联系可能因距离而减弱，缺乏金属和文字。
weaknesses.en: Population dispersed across vast ocean, inter-island connections could weaken with distance, lacking metals and writing.

outlook.zh: 波利尼西亚人将在未来几个世纪继续向东扩张，最终抵达夏威夷、新西兰和复活节岛，完成人类最后的大规模海洋殖民。
outlook.en: Polynesians would continue expanding eastward in coming centuries, eventually reaching Hawaii, New Zealand, and Easter Island, completing humanity's last great oceanic colonization.

description.zh: 波利尼西亚酋邦是公元100年从西波利尼西亚心脏地带向东太平洋扩张的航海文明，代表着人类历史上最伟大的远洋探险传统。
description.en: The Polynesian Chiefdoms were a seafaring civilization expanding from the western Polynesian heartland into the eastern Pacific around 100 CE, representing the greatest oceanic exploration tradition in human history.

governmentForm.zh: 以卡瓦奇仪式中心为核心的松散政治联合体。
governmentForm.en: A loose political confederation centered on the Cahuachi ceremonial complex.

socialStructure.zh: 仪式精英、工匠和农渔民分层明显。
socialStructure.en: Clear stratification among ritual elites, artisans, and farmer-fishers.

rulingClass.zh: 纳斯卡仪式-政治领主
rulingClass.en: Nazca ritual-political lords

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 创造了闻名于世的纳斯卡线条地画，精美的多彩陶器达到南美陶艺巅峰。
culturalAchievements.en: Created the world-famous Nazca Lines geoglyphs; exquisite polychrome pottery reached the pinnacle of South American ceramic art.

languageFamily.zh: 早期克丘亚语
languageFamily.en: Early Quechuan

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 灌溉农业、渔业和高水平手工业。
gdpDescription.en: Irrigated agriculture, fishing, and high-quality craft production.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、渔业、陶器、纺织
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, fishing, pottery, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 彩绘陶器、纺织品、鱼干
tradeGoods.en: Polychrome pottery, textiles, dried fish

householdWealth.zh: 精美陶器、纺织品和仪式建筑。
householdWealth.en: Fine pottery, textiles, and ritual architecture.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 干旱、厄尔尼诺、水源枯竭。
threats.en: Drought, El Niño, water depletion.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

populationDescription.zh: 秘鲁南部纳斯卡河谷。
populationDescription.en: Nazca Valley of southern Peru.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 纳斯卡线条绘制、地下引水渠（puquios）、高级彩陶。
keyInnovations.en: Nazca Lines construction, underground aqueducts (puquios), advanced polychrome pottery.

strengths.zh: 工艺水平极高，灌溉技术先进。
strengths.en: Outstanding craftsmanship, advanced irrigation.

weaknesses.zh: 沿海沙漠环境脆弱，人口有限。
weaknesses.en: Fragile coastal desert environment, limited population.

outlook.zh: 纳斯卡文明正处鼎盛，但环境退化将成为未来隐患。
outlook.en: Nazca civilization was at its peak, but environmental degradation would become a future threat.

description.zh: 纳斯卡文明以举世闻名的纳斯卡线条和精美彩陶著称，是安第斯海岸文明的杰出代表。
description.en: Nazca Civilization was renowned for the world-famous Nazca Lines and exquisite polychrome pottery, a remarkable example of Andean coastal civilization.

governmentForm.zh: 游猎营地的分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: Dispersed tribal society in mobile hunting camps.

socialStructure.zh: 高度平等的扩展家庭。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian extended families.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 北方森林生存技术。
culturalAchievements.en: Boreal forest survival.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡/阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan/Algonquian

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 驯鹿狩猎和捕鱼。
gdpDescription.en: Caribou hunting and fishing.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、毛皮
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, furs

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、肉干
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried meat

householdWealth.zh: 独木舟和猎具。
householdWealth.en: Canoes and gear.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 冬季饥荒。
threats.en: Winter famine.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

populationDescription.zh: 亚寒带森林。
populationDescription.en: Subarctic forests.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 雪鞋、独木舟。
keyInnovations.en: Snowshoes, canoes.

strengths.zh: 环境适应力。
strengths.en: Adaptation.

weaknesses.zh: 密度极低。
weaknesses.en: Extremely low density.

outlook.zh: 将持续。
outlook.en: Would persist.

description.zh: 亚北极森林猎人以驯鹿为生。
description.en: Subarctic Hunters living by caribou.

name.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者
北美太平洋沿岸采集者

governmentForm.zh: 复合采集社会。
governmentForm.en: Complex foraging societies.

socialStructure.zh: 等级分明。
socialStructure.en: Stratified.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 围猎技术成熟。
culturalAchievements.en: Mature communal hunting techniques.

languageFamily.zh: 苏语/阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Siouan/Algonquian

populationDescription.en: Arctic coastline.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 皮划艇、鱼叉。
keyInnovations.en: Kayaks, harpoons.

strengths.zh: 海洋资源利用。
strengths.en: Marine resource use.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少。
weaknesses.en: Very small.

outlook.zh: 将延续。
outlook.en: Would persist.

description.zh: 北极海洋猎人。
description.en: Arctic Marine Hunters.

governmentForm.zh: 以部落首领和长老议事为核心的松散部落联盟
governmentForm.en: A loose confederation of tribes governed by chiefs and elder councils

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族和部落为基本单元，畜牧和高原农业为生计基础。武士阶层受尊崇，普通牧民构成人口主体。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized by clans and tribes, relying on pastoralism and highland farming. Warriors held high status, while ordinary herders formed the majority.

rulingClass.zh: 部落首领、战士贵族和祭司长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs, warrior nobles, and priestly elders

succession.zh: 首领通常在有威望的家族中推选产生
succession.en: Chiefs were typically elected from prestigious lineages

structure.zh: 无中央集权体系，各部落自行管理内部事务，通过季节性集会和贸易网络维持松散联系。
structure.en: No centralized system existed; each tribe managed its own affairs, maintaining loose ties through seasonal assemblies and trade networks.

function.zh: 协调部落间纷争与对外军事行动
function.en: Coordinated inter-tribal disputes and external military actions

function.zh: 负责防御、劫掠和保卫牧场
function.en: Handled defense, raiding, and protection of pastures

localAdmin.zh: 各部落以氏族长老自治，定居点由首领直接管理。
localAdmin.en: Each tribe was self-governed by clan elders; settlements were managed directly by chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法为主，重视血亲复仇和赔偿调解。
legalSystem.en: Customary law prevailed, emphasizing blood vengeance and compensatory mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 以牲畜和谷物贡纳为主。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute in livestock and grain.

religion.zh: 羌族多神信仰和自然崇拜
religion.en: Qiang polytheism and nature worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 羌人以游牧歌谣、祭祀仪式、石碉建筑和羊毛纺织著称。
culturalAchievements.en: The Qiang were known for pastoral songs, sacrificial rituals, stone watchtower construction, and wool weaving.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系羌语支
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Qiangic branch

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵论与祖先崇拜
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor veneration

writingSystem.zh: 无本族文字，依赖口传传统。
writingSystem.en: No native script; reliance on oral tradition.

grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 以高原畜牧为主，辅以粗放农业、狩猎和毛皮贸易。
gdpDescription.en: Based on highland pastoralism, supplemented by extensive farming, hunting, and fur trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、农业、狩猎、纺织
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, farming, hunting, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 牦牛、羊毛、皮革、药材、青稞
tradeGoods.en: Yaks, wool, hides, medicinal herbs, highland barley

householdWealth.zh: 以牲畜数量和牧场使用权衡量财富。
householdWealth.en: Wealth measured by livestock count and pasture access.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 部落自给型畜牧经济，部分与汉朝边境互市。
economicSystem.en: Self-sufficient tribal pastoral economy, with some frontier trade with Han China.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持6个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 6 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

populationDescription.zh: 约80万人，分散于青藏高原东部和河西走廊周边。
populationDescription.en: Around 800,000 people scattered across the eastern Tibetan Plateau and edges of the Hexi Corridor.

militaryDescription.zh: 以骑兵和山地步兵为主的部落武装。
militaryDescription.en: Tribal forces mainly consisting of cavalry and mountain infantry.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

diplomaticStatus.zh: 与东汉政权冲突频繁，部分部落接受汉朝统辖。
diplomaticStatus.en: Frequent conflict with the Eastern Han, though some tribes accepted Han suzerainty.

description.zh: 高原防御性建筑技术
description.en: Defensive highland architectural technique

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、诸附属部落为臂膀的半游牧王国
governmentForm.en: A semi-nomadic kingdom centered on royal authority with subordinate tribal groups

socialStructure.zh: 王族和贵族掌握草场和贸易控制权，游牧民构成人口主体，绿洲定居农民提供粮食。
socialStructure.en: The royal and noble families controlled pastures and trade; nomads formed the population majority, with settled oasis farmers providing grain.

rulingClass.zh: 王族及游牧贵族
rulingClass.en: Royal family and nomadic aristocracy

succession.zh: 王位在王族内部传承，长子或有力者优先。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family, favoring the eldest son or the most powerful.

structure.zh: 国王统领中央事务，五小王分治各部，绿洲城市由总管管辖。
structure.en: The king directed central affairs; five lesser kings governed regional divisions, with city governors overseeing oasis towns.

function.zh: 处理外交、贡赋征收和军事调度
function.en: Handled diplomacy, tribute collection, and military coordination

function.zh: 管理丝路中亚段的商旅通行与关税
function.en: Managed caravan passage and tariffs along the Central Asian Silk Road segment

localAdmin.zh: 五小国分别管辖，城镇由总管治理，牧区由部落首领自治。
localAdmin.en: Five sub-kingdoms administered their own domains; towns were governed by overseers, while pastoral zones were self-governed by tribal chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法与王命并行。
legalSystem.en: Customary law and royal edicts operated in parallel.

taxationSystem.zh: 牲畜贡赋、过境商旅税和农产品征收。
taxationSystem.en: Livestock tribute, transit trade taxes, and agricultural levies.

religion.zh: 萨满教与琐罗亚斯德教并存
religion.en: Shamanism and Zoroastrianism coexisted

culturalAchievements.zh: 康居人融合草原游牧文化和中亚绿洲文明，善于骑射和金属加工。
culturalAchievements.en: Kangju people blended steppe nomadic culture with Central Asian oasis civilization, skilled in mounted archery and metalworking.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系伊朗语族东伊朗语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Iranian, Eastern Iranian

philosophy.zh: 草原天神信仰与善恶二元论
philosophy.en: Steppe sky-god worship and dualistic concepts of good and evil

writingSystem.zh: 使用粟特文字的早期形式。
writingSystem.en: Used an early form of Sogdian script.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 依靠丝路过境贸易、畜牧和绿洲灌溉农业。
gdpDescription.en: Relied on Silk Road transit trade, pastoralism, and oasis irrigated agriculture.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、过境贸易、灌溉农业、冶金
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, transit trade, irrigated farming, metallurgy

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、毛皮、玉石、丝绸（转运）
tradeGoods.en: Horses, furs, jade, silk (transit)

householdWealth.zh: 财富取决于牲畜规模和贸易参与度。
householdWealth.en: Wealth depended on herd size and trade participation.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 贸易中转型经济，兼有畜牧和绿洲农业。
economicSystem.en: Trade-transit economy combined with pastoralism and oasis farming.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持7个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 7 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

populationDescription.zh: 约150万人，分布于锡尔河流域和中亚草原。
populationDescription.en: About 1.5 million people across the Syr Darya basin and Central Asian steppe.

militaryDescription.zh: 以骑兵为核心的草原军事力量，善于快速机动和弓骑射术。
militaryDescription.en: Steppe military forces centered on cavalry, skilled in rapid maneuver and mounted archery.

militaryTechnology.zh: 复合弓、骑射术、青铜与铁制甲胄
militaryTechnology.en: Composite bows, mounted archery, bronze and iron armor

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀
description.en: Refined iron sword: Roman gladius or Han ring-pommel dao

diplomaticStatus.zh: 丝路中亚段重要中转国，与东汉和贵霜均有往来。
diplomaticStatus.en: A major transit state on the Central Asian Silk Road, engaging with both the Han and the Kushan Empire.

description.zh: 中亚旱地灌溉技术
description.en: Arid-land irrigation techniques in Central Asia

governmentForm.zh: 在罗马与安息之间保持自治的高加索王国
governmentForm.en: A Caucasian kingdom maintaining autonomy between Rome and Parthia

socialStructure.zh: 国王和贵族掌控土地，自由农民和山区部落构成人口基础，希腊文化影响城市精英。
socialStructure.en: The king and nobility controlled land; free peasants and mountain tribes formed the population base, while Greek culture influenced urban elites.

rulingClass.zh: 王族和地方贵族领主
rulingClass.en: Royal family and regional noble lords

succession.zh: 王位在王族内传承，受罗马和安息两大势力影响。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family, influenced by both Roman and Parthian powers.

structure.zh: 王权统领全国，下设地方贵族总管和城市官员。
structure.en: Royal authority governed the nation, supported by regional noble governors and city officials.

function.zh: 处理国政与外交
function.en: Managed state affairs and diplomacy

function.zh: 征召与统领军队
function.en: Recruited and commanded the army

localAdmin.zh: 地方由贵族领主管辖，山区部落保有较大自治权。
localAdmin.en: Regions were governed by noble lords; mountain tribes retained significant autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 王令与地方习惯法并行。
legalSystem.en: Royal edicts and local customary law operated in parallel.

taxationSystem.zh: 土地税、贸易通行费和贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: Land tax, trade tolls, and tribute.

religion.zh: 高加索多神教与琐罗亚斯德教影响
religion.en: Caucasian polytheism with Zoroastrian influence

culturalAchievements.zh: 融合高加索本土文化与希腊、波斯元素，以石雕、金银器和葡萄酒酿造闻名。
culturalAchievements.en: Blended Caucasian native culture with Greek and Persian elements; renowned for stone carving, gold and silverwork, and winemaking.

languageFamily.zh: 南高加索语系卡特韦利语族
languageFamily.en: South Caucasian (Kartvelian) language family

philosophy.zh: 多神信仰与波斯二元论影响下的精神世界
philosophy.en: Polytheistic belief influenced by Persian dualism

writingSystem.zh: 使用阿拉米文和希腊文，本族文字尚在形成中。
writingSystem.en: Used Aramaic and Greek scripts; a native writing system was still developing.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 农业、畜牧与高加索过境贸易。
gdpDescription.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, and Caucasus transit trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、冶金、葡萄酒酿造
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, metallurgy, winemaking

tradeGoods.zh: 葡萄酒、金属制品、皮革、木材
tradeGoods.en: Wine, metalwork, leather, timber

householdWealth.zh: 以土地和牲畜为主要财产。
householdWealth.en: Land and livestock as principal property.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 农牧结合的地方经济，受罗马与安息贸易网络影响。
economicSystem.en: Mixed agro-pastoral economy influenced by Roman and Parthian trade networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持6个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 6 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

populationDescription.zh: 约70万人，集中于库拉河谷和高加索山南麓。
populationDescription.en: About 700,000 people concentrated in the Kura River valley and southern Caucasus foothills.

militaryDescription.zh: 以重骑兵和山地步兵为主的王国军队。
militaryDescription.en: Kingdom forces mainly of heavy cavalry and mountain infantry.

militaryTechnology.zh: 重甲骑兵、山地要塞防御
militaryTechnology.en: Heavy armored cavalry, mountain fortress defense

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效
description.en: Han-era repeating and single-shot crossbows, accurate and effective

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

diplomaticStatus.zh: 罗马与安息争夺的缓冲国。
diplomaticStatus.en: A buffer state contested between Rome and Parthia.

description.zh: 铁器和青铜器加工传统
description.en: Iron and bronze metalworking tradition

governmentForm.zh: 高加索东部的小型附庸王国
governmentForm.en: A small vassal kingdom in the eastern Caucasus

socialStructure.zh: 贵族领主控制土地，山区部族自治，平原定居农民耕种灌溉田地。
socialStructure.en: Noble lords controlled lands; mountain clans self-governed, while settled lowland farmers cultivated irrigated fields.

rulingClass.zh: 王族和部落首领贵族
rulingClass.en: Royal family and noble tribal chiefs

succession.zh: 王位在阿尔沙基德家族内传承。
succession.en: The throne passed within the Arsacid family.

structure.zh: 王权统领，部族首领在山区保有自治权。
structure.en: Royal authority governed, with tribal chiefs retaining autonomy in the mountains.

function.zh: 国政与对外关系
function.en: State affairs and foreign relations

function.zh: 征召和指挥
function.en: Recruitment and command

localAdmin.zh: 平原由王室官员管理，山区由部族首领自治。
localAdmin.en: Lowlands managed by royal officials; mountains self-governed by tribal chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 王令与部落习惯法并行。
legalSystem.en: Royal edicts and tribal customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 土地税和贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: Land tax and tribute.

religion.zh: 高加索多神教和月神崇拜
religion.en: Caucasian polytheism and moon-god worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 融合本地高加索文化、波斯和希腊元素。
culturalAchievements.en: Blended local Caucasian culture with Persian and Greek elements.

languageFamily.zh: 东北高加索语系
languageFamily.en: Northeast Caucasian languages

philosophy.zh: 多神信仰与自然崇拜
philosophy.en: Polytheism and nature worship

writingSystem.zh: 使用阿拉米文。
writingSystem.en: Used Aramaic script.

gdpDescription.zh: 灌溉农业、畜牧和高加索东部过境贸易。
gdpDescription.en: Irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, and eastern Caucasus transit trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、渔业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, fishing

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、鱼、皮革、木材
tradeGoods.en: Grain, fish, leather, timber

householdWealth.zh: 以耕地和牲畜为主。
householdWealth.en: Land and livestock as main property.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 农牧混合经济。
economicSystem.en: Mixed agro-pastoral economy.

treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 6 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

populationDescription.zh: 约50万人。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000 people.

militaryDescription.zh: 以轻骑兵和山地弓箭手为主。
militaryDescription.en: Mainly light cavalry and mountain archers.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 投掷用重标枪（罗马）或长戈矛（汉）
description.en: Heavy javelin (Roman pilum) or long spear (Han mao)

description.zh: 大型长方形盾牌，龟甲阵核心
description.en: Large rectangular shield, core of testudo formation

description.zh: 罗马环片甲或汉代铁札甲
description.en: Roman lorica segmentata or Han lamellar iron armor

description.zh: 平原灌溉系统
description.en: Lowland irrigation systems

governmentForm.zh: 以贸易城市为核心的松散城邦联合体
governmentForm.en: A loose federation of trade-oriented city-states

socialStructure.zh: 商人贵族掌握城市权力，工匠和农民构成中下层，以丝路商贸闻名。
socialStructure.en: Merchant aristocrats held city power; artisans and farmers formed the middle and lower tiers, renowned for Silk Road commerce.

rulingClass.zh: 商人贵族和城市领主
rulingClass.en: Merchant aristocrats and urban lords

succession.zh: 城邦首领多在望族中推选产生。
succession.en: City-state leaders were typically chosen from prominent families.

structure.zh: 各城市自治，由领主和贵族委员会共治。
structure.en: Each city was autonomous, co-governed by lords and aristocratic councils.

function.zh: 管理集市、仓储和商旅事务
function.en: Managed markets, warehousing, and caravan affairs

function.zh: 维护城墙与守军
function.en: Maintained city walls and garrisons

localAdmin.zh: 城市由领主和长老管理，乡村由地方豪族治理。
localAdmin.en: Cities were administered by lords and elders; rural areas by local magnates.

legalSystem.zh: 商法与习惯法结合。
legalSystem.en: Commercial law combined with customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 商业税、土地税和过境费。
taxationSystem.en: Commercial taxes, land taxes, and transit fees.

religion.zh: 琐罗亚斯德教为主，佛教渐入
religion.en: Predominantly Zoroastrian, with growing Buddhist influence

culturalAchievements.zh: 粟特人是欧亚大陆最活跃的商人群体，精于多语言交流、金银细工和壁画艺术。
culturalAchievements.en: Sogdians were Eurasia's most active merchant community, skilled in multilingual communication, gold and silverwork, and mural painting.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系伊朗语族粟特语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Iranian, Sogdian

philosophy.zh: 琐罗亚斯德教善恶二元论与商业务实主义
philosophy.en: Zoroastrian dualism combined with commercial pragmatism

writingSystem.zh: 粟特文（源自阿拉米字母）
writingSystem.en: Sogdian script (derived from Aramaic alphabet)

gdpDescription.en: A core Silk Road hub, relying on long-distance trade, irrigated agriculture, and fine craftsmanship.

mainIndustries.zh: 长途贸易、灌溉农业、金银加工、纺织
mainIndustries.en: Long-distance trade, irrigated farming, gold and silverwork, textiles

tradeGoods.zh: 丝绸、玻璃、宝石、香料（转运）、金银器
tradeGoods.en: Silk, glass, gems, spices (transit), gold and silverware

householdWealth.zh: 商人家庭以贸易网络和不动产为核心财富。
householdWealth.en: Merchant households centered wealth on trade networks and real estate.

economicSystem.zh: 以商业贸易为驱动的城邦经济。
economicSystem.en: A commerce-driven city-state economy.

description.zh: 丝绸之路明珠，帖木儿帝国首都
description.en: Pearl of the Silk Road, Timurid capital

militaryDescription.zh: 城邦联合守军和雇佣骑兵。
militaryDescription.en: Combined city-state garrisons and mercenary cavalry.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 精锻铁剑，罗马短剑或汉代环首刀

description.zh: 汉代连弩与单射弩并用，精准有效

diplomaticStatus.zh: 名义上受贵霜帝国管辖，实际高度自治。

treaties.en: Maintained trade agreements with the Kushans

description.zh: 贯通东西方的商业组织体系
description.en: Commercial organizational system linking East and West

governmentForm.zh: 以昆弥为最高统治者的游牧联盟王国
governmentForm.en: A nomadic confederate kingdom headed by the Kunmi

socialStructure.zh: 王族和贵族掌控牧场和贸易权，普通牧民随季节迁徙。
socialStructure.en: The royal family and nobles controlled pastures and trade rights; common herders migrated seasonally.

rulingClass.zh: 王族和部落贵人
rulingClass.en: Royal family and tribal nobles

succession.zh: 昆弥位由王族内部继承。
succession.en: The Kunmi position was inherited within the royal family.

structure.zh: 昆弥统领全国，各翼由部落首领分管。
structure.en: The Kunmi led the nation; wings were managed by tribal chiefs.

function.zh: 决策与外交
function.en: Decision-making and diplomacy

function.zh: 骑兵征召与指挥
function.en: Cavalry recruitment and command

localAdmin.zh: 部落首领分管各区。
localAdmin.en: Tribal chiefs administered their respective zones.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法。
legalSystem.en: Customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 牲畜贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: Livestock tribute.

religion.zh: 萨满教和天神崇拜
religion.en: Shamanism and sky-god worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 草原游牧文化，以金制动物纹饰和骑术闻名。
culturalAchievements.en: Steppe nomadic culture, known for gold animal-style art and horsemanship.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系吐火罗语或突厥-伊朗混合语
languageFamily.en: Possibly Tocharian or Turkic-Iranian mixed language

philosophy.zh: 天神信仰与萨满传统
philosophy.en: Sky-god worship and shamanic traditions

writingSystem.zh: 无本族文字。
writingSystem.en: No native script.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、马匹贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse trading

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、毛皮、乳制品

title.en: Emperor Trajan Ascends the Roman Throne

description.zh: 图拉真于公元98年成为罗马皇帝，开创了"五贤帝"时代的黄金时期。他推行大规模公共工程建设，修筑图拉真广场和图拉真柱，罗马帝国在其统治下达到领土最大范围。
description.en: Trajan became Roman Emperor in 98 CE, inaugurating the golden age of the "Five Good Emperors." He launched massive public works including Trajan's Forum and Column, and under his rule the Roman Empire reached its greatest territorial extent.

description.zh: 东汉宦官蔡伦于公元105年向汉和帝献上改良造纸术，使用树皮、麻头、破布和鱼网等原料制纸。这项发明极大降低了书写材料的成本，对知识传播和文明发展产生了深远影响。
description.en: Eastern Han court eunuch Cai Lun presented improved papermaking techniques to Emperor He in 105 CE, using bark, hemp, rags, and fishing nets as raw materials. This invention dramatically reduced the cost of writing materials and profoundly impacted knowledge dissemination and civilizational development.

description.zh: 罗马皇帝图拉真在两次达契亚战争后（101-102年和105-106年）彻底征服达契亚王国，将其纳入罗马行省。达契亚的金银矿产为罗马国库注入了巨额财富。
description.en: Emperor Trajan conquered the Dacian Kingdom after two wars (101-102 and 105-106 CE), incorporating it as a Roman province. Dacia's gold and silver mines injected vast wealth into the Roman treasury.

title.zh: 纳巴泰王国被罗马吞并

description.zh: 罗马帝国于公元106年吞并纳巴泰王国，将其改为阿拉伯行省。佩特拉从独立贸易王国的首都转变为罗马省会，丝绸之路西端的贸易格局因此发生重大变化。
description.en: The Roman Empire annexed the Nabataean Kingdom in 106 CE, reorganizing it as the province of Arabia Petraea. Petra transformed from the capital of an independent trading kingdom into a Roman provincial city, significantly altering trade patterns at the western end of the Silk Road.

title.en: Gan Ying's Mission Toward the Roman Empire

description.zh: 东汉西域都护班超派遣甘英出使大秦（罗马帝国），甘英到达波斯湾后因安息人阻挠未能渡海前往。这是中国与罗马直接接触的最早尝试之一，虽未成功但反映了丝绸之路的活跃交流。
description.en: Eastern Han Protector-General Ban Chao sent Gan Ying on a mission toward Da Qin (the Roman Empire). Gan Ying reached the Persian Gulf but was dissuaded from crossing by Parthian intermediaries. Though unsuccessful, this was one of the earliest Chinese attempts at direct contact with Rome, reflecting active Silk Road exchanges.

description.zh: 图拉真率罗马军团东征帕提亚帝国，攻占亚美尼亚和美索不达米亚，于116年占领帕提亚首都泰西封。罗马帝国疆域短暂达到历史最大范围，但图拉真在117年去世后罗马即放弃了东方征服地。
description.en: Trajan led Roman legions eastward against the Parthian Empire, conquering Armenia and Mesopotamia, and in 116 CE captured the Parthian capital Ctesiphon. The Roman Empire briefly reached its maximum extent, but Rome abandoned the eastern conquests after Trajan's death in 117 CE.

title.zh: 哈德良继位推行防御政策
title.en: Hadrian Succeeds Trajan, Adopts Defensive Policy

description.zh: 哈德良继承罗马帝位后放弃图拉真的东方征服，转而推行巩固边防的战略。他下令修筑横贯不列颠的哈德良长城，标志着罗马帝国从扩张转向防御的战略转变。
description.en: Hadrian succeeded to the Roman throne and abandoned Trajan's eastern conquests, adopting a strategy of border consolidation. He ordered the construction of Hadrian's Wall across Britain, marking Rome's strategic shift from expansion to defense.

title.zh: 贵霜帝国迦腻色伽盛世
title.en: Kanishka's Golden Age of the Kushan Empire

description.zh: 贵霜帝国在迦腻色伽大帝统治下进入全盛期，控制了从中亚到恒河流域的广大地区。迦腻色伽大力扶持大乘佛教，召开第四次佛教结集，促进了佛教向中亚和中国的传播。
description.en: The Kushan Empire entered its zenith under Emperor Kanishka, controlling vast territories from Central Asia to the Ganges plain. Kanishka patronized Mahayana Buddhism, convening the Fourth Buddhist Council and promoting Buddhism's spread toward Central Asia and China.

title.en: Silk Road Trade Reaches Its Peak

description.zh: 公元2世纪初，连接汉帝国与罗马帝国的丝绸之路陆路和海路贸易达到空前繁荣。中国丝绸、罗马玻璃、印度香料和中亚宝石通过贵霜和安息领土流通，形成了古代世界最大的贸易网络。
description.en: In the early 2nd century CE, Silk Road land and maritime trade linking the Han and Roman empires reached unprecedented prosperity. Chinese silk, Roman glass, Indian spices, and Central Asian gems circulated through Kushan and Parthian territories, forming the ancient world's largest trade network.

description.zh: 东非之角的阿克苏姆王国在红海贸易中崛起，控制了从阿杜利斯港到内陆高原的广大领土。阿克苏姆发展出独特的方尖碑建筑和吉兹文字，成为连接罗马、印度和非洲内陆的贸易枢纽。
description.en: The Aksumite Kingdom of the Horn of Africa rose through Red Sea trade, controlling vast territory from the port of Adulis to the interior highlands. Aksum developed distinctive obelisk architecture and the Ge'ez script, becoming a trade hub connecting Rome, India, and interior Africa.

description.zh: 犹太人在巴尔·科赫巴的领导下发动反罗马大起义（132-136年），短暂建立了独立政权。罗马帝国出动大军镇压，最终摧毁犹太人在巴勒斯坦的政治存在，大量犹太人被驱逐出故土。
description.en: Jews launched a major revolt against Rome under Bar Kokhba (132-136 CE), briefly establishing an independent state. Rome deployed massive forces to crush the uprising, ultimately destroying the Jewish political presence in Palestine and expelling large numbers from their homeland.

description.zh: 东汉科学家张衡于公元132年发明了世界上第一台地震仪器——候风地动仪，能够检测远方地震的方向。张衡还在天文学和数学方面做出了重要贡献，计算出圆周率近似值。
description.en: Eastern Han scientist Zhang Heng invented the world's first seismoscope around 132 CE, capable of detecting the direction of distant earthquakes. Zhang Heng also made significant contributions to astronomy and mathematics, calculating an approximation of pi.

description.zh: 南印度的萨塔瓦哈纳王朝在德干高原维持着强大的统治，控制着连接印度东西海岸的重要贸易路线。该王朝以其精美的佛教石窟寺（如阿旃陀石窟）和活跃的罗马贸易闻名。
description.en: The Satavahana dynasty of southern India maintained powerful rule across the Deccan Plateau, controlling vital trade routes connecting India's east and west coasts. The dynasty was renowned for its exquisite Buddhist cave temples (such as Ajanta) and active Roman trade connections.

title.en: An Shigao Arrives in China to Translate Buddhist Sutras

description.zh: 安息国王子安世高来到东汉都城洛阳，成为中国最早系统翻译佛经的译师之一。他翻译了大量小乘佛教经典，为佛教在中国的传播奠定了重要基础。
description.en: An Shigao, a Parthian prince, arrived at the Eastern Han capital Luoyang and became one of the earliest systematic translators of Buddhist scriptures in China. He translated numerous Hinayana Buddhist texts, laying an important foundation for Buddhism's spread in China.

description.zh: 墨西哥中部的特奥蒂瓦坎在公元2世纪进入快速扩张期，太阳金字塔和月亮金字塔相继建成，城市人口可能超过十万。特奥蒂瓦坎成为美洲最大的城市和中美洲文化的辐射中心。
description.en: Teotihuacan in central Mexico entered a period of rapid expansion in the 2nd century CE, with the Pyramids of the Sun and Moon constructed and the city's population possibly exceeding 100,000. Teotihuacan became the largest city in the Americas and a radiating center of Mesoamerican culture.

title.en: Deepening of the Xiongnu North-South Split

description.zh: 匈奴自公元48年分裂后，南匈奴归附汉朝成为边境屏障，北匈奴在鲜卑和汉军的共同打击下逐渐西迁。北匈奴的西迁对中亚乃至欧洲的民族格局产生了深远的连锁反应。
description.en: After the Xiongnu split in 48 CE, the Southern Xiongnu submitted to Han as a frontier buffer while the Northern Xiongnu gradually migrated westward under combined Xianbei and Han pressure. This westward migration created profound chain reactions across Central Asian and eventually European ethnic configurations.

description.zh: 公元165年起，从帕提亚战场返回的罗马军队带回了大规模疫病（可能是天花），被称为"安东尼瘟疫"。疫病在罗马帝国全境蔓延，造成数百万人死亡，严重削弱了帝国的人口和军事力量。
description.en: From 165 CE, Roman soldiers returning from Parthian campaigns brought back a massive epidemic (possibly smallpox), known as the Antonine Plague. The disease spread across the entire Roman Empire, killing millions and severely weakening the empire's population and military strength.

description.zh: 东南亚的扶南王国凭借其优越的地理位置，成为印度洋与南海贸易的关键中转站。扶南的港口奥克欧（Oc Eo）出土了罗马金币和印度商品，证明了该地区与远方文明的广泛贸易联系。
description.en: The Kingdom of Funan in Southeast Asia, leveraging its advantageous geographic position, became a key transit point for Indian Ocean and South China Sea trade. Roman gold coins and Indian goods excavated at Funan's port of Oc Eo attest to extensive trade connections with distant civilizations.

title.en: Roman Envoys Reach the Eastern Han Court

description.zh: 据《后汉书》记载，公元166年大秦（罗马帝国）国王安敦派遣使者经海路抵达东汉，献上象牙、犀角和玳瑁。这是中国史书中记载的罗马与中国之间最早的直接接触，虽然可能是私人商人假托官方之名。
description.en: According to the Hou Hanshu, envoys from Da Qin (the Roman Empire) sent by King Andun (likely Marcus Aurelius) arrived at the Eastern Han court by sea in 166 CE, presenting ivory, rhinoceros horn, and tortoiseshell. This is the earliest recorded direct contact between Rome and China, though the envoys may have been private merchants claiming official status.

title.zh: 东汉宦官与外戚斗争激化
title.en: Eunuch-Consort Clan Struggles Intensify in Eastern Han

description.zh: 东汉朝廷中宦官集团与外戚势力的斗争日益激烈，"党锢之祸"（166-169年）中大批士人被禁锢。朝政腐败和中央权威的衰落为后来的黄巾起义和三国分裂埋下了祸根。
description.en: Struggles between eunuch factions and consort clans at the Eastern Han court intensified, with the "Partisan Prohibitions" (166-169 CE) leading to mass purges of scholars. Court corruption and declining central authority sowed the seeds for the later Yellow Turban Rebellion and Three Kingdoms fragmentation.

description.zh: 日耳曼部落联盟（以马科曼尼人为首）大举入侵罗马帝国多瑙河边境，甚至深入意大利北部。马可·奥勒留皇帝被迫亲自率军在前线作战多年，暴露了帝国北方边境的脆弱性。
description.en: A Germanic tribal coalition led by the Marcomanni launched a massive invasion of the Roman Empire's Danube frontier, penetrating into northern Italy. Emperor Marcus Aurelius was forced to personally command armies at the front for years, exposing the vulnerability of the empire's northern frontier.
